REALLY salty, but i think its supposed to be that way. My review isnt influenced by that alone.  If you want to check it out as a tourist spot, by all means go. You'll enjoy your pancakes, but I'm not in a rush to wait so long for an averahe breakfast when there's so many amazing options around.

I would normally give the toscana spa at the peppermill five stars but it was to crowded and annoying to deal with loud obnoxious women. Ladies it's a spa I come here to get away from stress when you come in as a group keep it quiet this isn't animal house for god sakes! So it added to my irritation oh yeah the women's elevator was broken better luck next time. Don't go on a Saturday it's just to much.

Higher end (but not stuffy) breakfast/brunch place that is consistent with the quality that I'd expect from this new mixed-use development.  We came right at the Sunday brunch rush. Wait for a table of four was 20 minutes to get a table and then probably 30 more minutes for food. Everything came out hot and delicious. Kid's menu available with protein and carb breakfast and lunch type options. Slings and highchairs available. And Steven, our server, was friendly and took wonderful care of us. We will definitely be back!

A few years ago, there used to be a Bar Louie in Clay Terrace. I loved it! Especially the $5 burgers and drink specials. Came here this past Saturday night.   - The band was quite good and started around 9:30pm - The food was good. Bar pub food with Tots! - The drinks were ok, I liked my Moscow Mule but the Browned lime was disappointing for a $9.50 drink  - Parking is mostly street parking or you can park in the Church parking lot across the street. Cars go quickly at night down that corner. Be careful! - Service was meh, it took a while for someone to get to us at bar. There was one bartender who was pretty on it. If I was able to get his attention, we were good. You kinda had to flag down otherwise. Or be ok with waiting 15 min for a drink.   I will probably be back, but wouldn't go out of my way. I want to see how brunch is.

Good thing we had a reservation or else we would be waiting a good 1-2 hours. The food was great but the service was a bit slow but it was super busy. Theres a good range of side dishes that come with the bbq.   The entrees were delicious. Got the seafood pancakes and japchae. definately worth it!

The service by waitress Jenea'was excellent . She was well versedof all menu items,happy to explain the typds of oyster dishes.The crab cakes were excellent,  Fried picles w Creole Ranch Dressing was Great.The Gumbo,Red Beans,Jambalaya were Delicious. The Desserts-- Bread pudding and slu e of Key lime Pie were just Awesome. The omly drawback was tge luke warm coffee was a disappointment.  Overall a Great Experience, i will be back.

Money ... money ... who doesn't love money?  While visiting Philly, I thought it'd be fun to see where the coins are made.  The mint is pretty small and you walk around on your own -- no guided tours.  You'll get to see the machines making coins and the entire production from A - Z.  The souvenir shop also has some cool items for sale.

Rum Fish Grill gave me music for all of my senses. I made a reservation ahead of time. It's pretty handy that you can do it directly through yelp.com using the open table site. I wasn't quite sure what was to come because we planned outdoor activities before hand, and due to crazy rain we would be having, I had to have a plan a and b. I set up two reservations online. One at 5:00 and one at 5:45pm. The day of at 5 with the crazy storm upon us, we used our 5pm reservation and cancelled the other reservation online. Worked perfectly.  We had no wait. We had a perfect spot in front of the fish tank, and all was well. Our waiter was helpful. He broke down the menu for us, said I asked tons of questions which I do, and he answered them very well. Hubby picked a N.Y. Strip and I picked the grilled jerk swordfish w papaya rum butter sauce.  Guess what happens shortly after that?  Electricity went out! That's never happened to us at a restaurant. Ok. What's next? We just chilled a bit. We received table lamps, and the menus were lit.  Everyone had their calm professional demeanor with us. I'm sure they were really freaking out to death. Much of the surrounding businesses were out from the storm. Someone said someone hit a light pole. No idea. Florida is weird. It could be the case. Lights came on 15 minutes later, and food had not stopped coming out so I guess they were fine in the kitchen. Cool! Management was so professional. Went to every single table, apologized for any inconvenience. I thought that was really nice that they did that. While we were waiting, we were still able to see the fish swimming in the tank. There was light there. Weird feeling to have no electricity, but it worked out fine, and there were no mishaps. Our food came out soon. my hubby's steak came with truffled mashed potatoes, & he changed the veggie option to coconut rice. It was perfect. Seriously, it was juicy and flavorable, and the right temperature and color.  My jerk swordfish was very good with the papaya rum butter sauce. I think it's more of a salsa to me w cubes of cut up papaya. I would ask for extra if I were you. It's that good. I was concerned that the jerk seasoning would be spicy, but w each bite of the sauce it was perfect. My waiter also said, if it ended up being spicy, he would make sure to make me a new one. He was really good and helpful. My dish came w coconut rice and red beans. It was a plate which I could not make at home so it proved to be an excellent choice. I washed it down with a "tanked"cocktail with tropical juices, mango rum, and bitters. Perfect. We were celebrating my birthday weekend. Hubby let my waiter know about my birthday, and after we finished our entrees, my happy birthday dessert came with salted caramel ice cream and lava cake. Yes that was perfect too. Afterwards... ahh, we had a great dinner and great service, we had to kill time at the restaurant. With the pouring rain, it was fine, we checked out the cave. You don't have to eat at the restaurant to see the fishes. Just go through the cave. There are so many picture opportunities. Everything is bright and pretty. The sting ray is smiling from the ceiling. I guess he likes the edges. The nurse shark is small.  It looks like it's like a stuffed animal because of its shape. Check out the irridescent colorful fish and the see through school of fish family. All the descriptions of the fish are there. You will not have to guess. Before you leave, you can see if there's a band in the bar, or watch some sports on the flat screen tv. Lastly, shop for some Guy Harvey apparel, or make an appointment to swim in the tank. This is a great place for a nice occasion, or just a night out. Children are welcome and will love the fish tank. You don't need to sit to close, with sitting a bit further you can better see the top of the tank. I am happy to have finally checked out Rum Fish Grill. Food is tasty, service was great, and fish tank, and Guy Harvey art was vibrant and fun.
Just a great music venue. We hung out in the back with a beer and had a blast. Music all over New Orleans, but thanks to this venue we catch the band when the tour in New York.

I took a friend to this Korean spot because she was curious about Korean food.  Bimbimbop was inedible, fried chicken with panko was not a real chicken filet, but akin to chicken McNuggets, or mystery meat.  The wings were just OK.  This was an awful meal, and I think my friend will likely skip the Korean food for a long time to come.

I can customize my rolls?!? YAY! I'm pregnant and the waitress was so nice and patient with going over all of the cook rolls.  In addition to their available cooked rolls, I added whatever I wanted in them- mango, avocado, sauce, tamago egg... I called the shots.    I love that their ceiling is really high and that their location is in a not so busy area. I will definitely be coming back for more after I give birth to try out their raw fish.

I recently stayed at The Saint with my sister for a few nights and loved it.  The minute we walked in we knew we'd loved it because of the amazing decor and the amazing hospitality. Right from the first time we walked in, we felt so welcome with the doorman and the front desk girls. SUPER!!! FRIENDLY!!!   The hotel lobby and entrance all looked cool with great paintings and decor.  Just like their other locations, they have these oversized chairs that are great for taking fun pictures so before we even went up to see our room we already took a ton of cool pictures.  The chairs make you feel so miniature.  The hotel also has a really nice bar in the lobby.  It's decorated with lots of red chandeliers hanging over the bar and they serve some really great drinks.  If you're ever there, you need to make sure you go for there happy hours drinks (WAY cheaper).  At night the bar does get pretty busy and every night they have a live band so it was nice to sit and listen to them but it does get a little loud.  When we finally decided to go up to our room, we entered the elevator and we were so surprised and thought it was funny that they have a television that is always playing Classic Jazz (how cool is that?).  When the elevator doors open, the entire hallway was all dark with  blue lights; at first it reminded me of the movie The Shinning LOL  Our room was really roomy and nice.  At first I didn't like that our room was at the back of the building where you have no view (just looking at another building right there) but later i was happy to be in the back because when you get the rooms along Canal Street it is really loud with all the traffic and street action; the view wasn't worth it so we kept our back room.  But I will say I was so happy that the hotel was willing to change us if we wanted.  The location was so good; really close to everything we needed like best spot to get the trolleys, on the edge of the French Quarter, nearby so much more.

One of my favorite spots for morning coffee. The device here is excellent. Such a cute place to sit back and relax. I've had several of their hot and cold beverages and my favorite is the white chocolate mocha on ice. Delicious!!!  This business will be seeing a lot of me.

This park is a hidden gem. Didnt even know that it exists because it is located behind the wall. This is where you can see a panoramic view of New Orleans CBD. It's amazing. I'm sure when the weather warms up a bit, it is going to be beautiful with flowers and all that!

I've been having a hard time finding a good brunch spot, until I found Jones.  Unfortunately for me, Jones is a 3 hour drive.   So I will not be frequenting it - but one can dream.  We sat upstairs, which I recommend - a little quieter & you can watch everyone below you.  We were there around 11:30 on a rainy Saturday, and were sat immediately.  Service was fine, and fast.  Decor is super cute.  I had the French toast with tater tots.  Tater tots, you ask?  Yes, because where I'm from, they are not quite ubiquitous.  My Philly friend (aka tour guide) also had the French toast, and my husband had the avocado sandwich with fries.   The French toast was thick and tasty, and probably the biggest piece of French toast I've ever seen.   I enjoyed the tater tots.  My husband liked his sandwich and fries, but the fries went cold fast.    Perhaps a second location in DC or Boston is needed?

My family and I were in Nashville for the first time and needed some BBQ before we were out of time and had to return home. This place was next to our hotel so we tried it. The ribs were the best: Tender and juicy and great flavor. The sides were also very good. Probably Not the best BBQ in Nashville but Satisfied the last item on the list.

You guys, I finally made it to Milktooth!  I have to say this place is amazing and I don't think my review will be any different than anyone else's.  So I'm just gonna get my complaints out of the way now so I can move on: 1.  You're almost too popular because I had to wait, outside in the rain, for an hour to be seated with my party.  (But I keep a commitment so I did it) 2. I don't like the disorganization of the coffee area or the prices of such. ($4 for a tiny cup? Come on, it's morning and mama needs some energy) also, I was practically shunned for asking for some cream and *gasp* sweetener for my iced coffee.  I'm a lady who knows what she wants and I really try to lay off the suga, so gimme my Splenda packet and we'll be ok.    On to the good stuff.  Despite how busy they were, service was great.  I almost didn't feel cool enough to be there.  Everyone in their locally made apparel and handlebar mustaches, but I had on my best Hayes & Taylor shirt and crammed myself in anyways.  Of course I tried the bacon.  Do you consider that bacon?  It was half the size of a small pig, but none the less it was delicious!!  I had the pigs in a poncho to eat.  Soooo amazing!  That avocado cream sauce was just the perfect topping and the pickled veggies! I dream about being caught in a rainstorm wearing a poncho like that...   I'll be back, and for my own selfishness I hope you are less busy :)

This was excellent. We came for dinner and got conch croquettes, crispy dirty rice arancini, hamachi, broiled shrimp, semolina gnocchi, and curried goat. Everything was good, but the arancini and curried goat stood out for me. The arancini was somewhat unexpected, as it was a last second throw-in to our order, but the flavor really jumped and the dipping sauce it came with was excellent as well.   Obviously, though, the main event was the curried goat. The meat itself was outstanding, but the gnocchi and the sauce could have been a great entree on its own. (To some extent, it overshadowed the semolina gnocchi, which was also very good, but more plain.)   My wife also loved the broiled shrimp, and while I liked them too, I'm more turned off by having to do the work of peeling them.  Overall, an excellent dinner, and I was really torn between 4 and 5 stars.

It's become the place you go to before a game in south Philly.  I have always had a good experience here.  Crab fries are a staple.  Lobster white pizza is always great.  Mussels in white garlic sauce, yes please, I will take all the bread you can give me to eat this delicious broth.  My favorite of all time at Chickie's is the McGooby sandwich.  A great roll, with chopped onion and pickle.  They then top that with steamed and peeled shrimp.  On top of that goes some crab fries, and on top of that goes the patented Chickie's cheese sauce.  It is a great balance of cold and hot in a seafood sandwich.  Can it complete with the lobster rolls of New England, maybe... I would like to think so.  My service has always been great here, and there are always beer specials to be had.

This place is great! They always have fresh sushi and creative options. I love trying all the new rolls they always taste great! It's also byo which is wonderful!!! It's got a great atmosphere and the servers always treat you wonderfully. Highly recommend for sushi.

Was really disappointed when I received my tofu Provençale this last time.  Both the potatoes and tofu came out incredibly dry...no olive oil to be found on the plate.  Their Provençale had previously been a blend of olive oil, herbs, tomatoes and garlic sautéed to perfection.  I've always asked for this dish without onions.  Today the olive oil with Herbs de Provence, garlic etc. was completely missing.  The waitress told me she had no idea why they did that.  It was just dry fried tofu with a bit of tomato and herbs. I was so disappointed that this excellent dish had been botched.  She took it back and returned with what tasted like a lesser olive oil and nearly raw garlic added to my existing plate.  I guess I won't be returning, which is too bad because I adored that dish.

Went here for a dinner date with my fiancé on a Sunday, glad I reserved it because all the Colts fans came pouring in! The only thing I didn't like here were the rude hostesses who, when we checked in, told us we needed to be done by 6pm. Rude! Once we got seated everything got better. Our waitress was nice, attentive and had good suggestions. We ordered the lamb porterhouse which was great, but the Izzy Style NY Strip was even better! Best steak I've ever had. The cracked pepper flavor and seared crispy texture on the outside on this steak did it for me. For sides we had garlic mashed potatoes and asparagus, and appetizers we had grilled slab bacon (very thick cut!). The drinks here are expensive but worth it bc they are fancy and on the strong side. I had the Limoncello Todd Collins. Definitely recommend this place to everyone (just ignore the rude hostesses)!

Being a clean eater that strays away from meat and gluten makes it a little difficult to go out to eat. Finding someone to dine with at a vegetarian restaurant can be difficult so I often find myself saying "I'll just get a salad" when going out to eat with friends. At Blue Sage there were many options for me and I couldn't have been more pleased.  Before I get to the food I would first like to compliment our server, Charlee. She was very kind and well educated on the menu. This is important because I can't eat gluten and get very nervous when dining at a restaurant for the first time. She made my first experience at Blue Sage fantastic!!  For starters, she offered a delicious UNSWEETENED raspberry iced tea, which is almost impossible to find! We also got an appetizer, the Tacos Verduras. The amount of texture and flavor piled high on a homemade blue corn taco was more than satisfying. I split this with a friend but was tempted to put in another order as my meal.   Instead of doing that I went for the miso pumpkin soup. The miso broth was jam packed with flavor and even developed a slight creamy texture. The soup was hearty and had medium, bite sized chunks of cauliflower and pumpkin which was accompanied but the punch of flavor possessed by scallions. I was In love.    Overall, I was extremely satisfied with the service, flavor, portions and prices. I am so excited to go back to Blue Sage and try more of their menu!

Came on a Sunday night.  Not very crowded but very loud in the upstairs dining.  No worries sound panels are on the way.  Very creative wine list and menu.  It seemed that managers were  the servers, perhaps new hires are coming.     Now the food, dont miss the fried cornbread worth every bite.  Also the cauliflower is worth it.  Didnt love the pork thought it was a bit over cooked.  loved the farro.  Enjoyed the scallop risotto too.   All in all really liked this place but think it can continue t improve.

Met some friends for coffee/snacks here on 5/30. First, it's in Broadripple so parking is a nightmare.  First - this place is just a little too precious for my taste. Everyone in white ruffled aprons and glitter on all the food. It's very juvenile for me and not really interesting as much as irritating. But that's a personal preference. I will say that the bench seating inside is extremely uncomfortable.  Coffee drinks were good and the lemon bar I had was tasty. My friends each had a scone and said they were good but lacked flavor.  So....I'd probably go back here again if someone suggested it as a meet up spot...but it is unlikely I would go here of my own volition.
First time in here did a all day long shopping spree with a friend and I enjoyed my time spent in this store. It's small but easy to maneuver around.

I am very particular with my cupcakes. I like a moist cake with a sticky frosting. I know that's not popular. Most cupcakes have the frosting that hardens.   The Flying Cupcake is probably right in the middle -- the frosting isn't sticky, but it's definitely not hard. It's creamy and luscious!   I went on Small Business Saturday, an Amex-sponsored event. The Flying Cupcake had samples out, and not just pieces of cake with icing. Little mini cupcakes! One per customer.   I was with another adult and two children. We purchased four cupcakes and cut them into quarters, and were even given four mini cupcakes to sample. So we cut those up too.   Needless to say, I got to try a LOT of cupcakes that day. :)   For me, the red velvet was probably my least favorite. Just too sugary for my tastes, but I can see the appeal.   My favorite was a golden cake with white icing - nice a simple. So good!

Delivery is my specialty. lol. I love ordering-out when I'm way too busy at work. Either late lunch or dinner. I have ordered the veggie dumplings fried and steam. It is really good. My fave is the Beef Cho Ho Fun extra spicy. They are just as good as Chinatown food. I've had many other menu items from here and have never been disappointed.  From String Beans in Garlic sauce to Fried Chicken. The only thing is the delivery sur charge. lol. $1.50 is not much to ask but if your a good tipper then that just sucks.  This would be a highly recommend place to order take-out. I get my deliveries to Brewerytown and they are pretty quick.

1 Tippling Place: Come for the cocktails, stick around for the giant zebra head and attentive service. Less pomp than some of the other fancy schmancy cocktail spots, I'll continue tippling at Tippling Place for happy hours, dates, and any booze-gathering you can think to put together.   Brighter than Ranstead Room, easier to dress for than Hop SIng, and cheaper than Franklin Mortgage, 1 Tippling is the place I go to when I want a cocktail without making a whole "thing" about it. Grab some casual appetizers (bacon wrapped dates, artichoke dip), enjoy a few of the happy hour offerings (big fan of the fish bowl punch, a steal at $5), and chat with your buddies around the accessible tables that don't mandate a strict dress code to stand/sit around... You'll leave with a saucy buzz, and a smile on your face from the attentive and knowledgeable staff.   Bathrooms are trendy and always clean, no formal "coat check" but a bunch of hangers just waiting to hold onto your outerwear for any length of time, and all the chotskies one could hang on a wall without drifting into TGIFridays territory, 1TP is a solid spot that I've gradually begun to make a regular in my circulation of must-drink bars...

My only complaint here is always the temperature of the building. It always seems stuffy and warm, no matter what time of year we go. Other than that we thoroughly enjoyed the new Reel West Exhibit, contemporary art, and especially all of the Native American paintings. I love the old art-o-matic machine on the second floor, next to the elevator. It's an old cigarette machine that for $5 you can pull a knob and it will dispense the art of your choice. I chose the silver or gold plated earrings knob and got a cute pair of dangly  sterling silver earrings designed by an artist!!! Other options were necklaces, watercolor painting, some kind of fortune cookie and many more. All in all, it was a great way to spend the day with my family. The lower level is an interactive play and learning area, fun for kids and adults. Also if you park in the underground garage you can get validation for parking at the ticket counter so that parking is free, so don't let parking prices posted deter you from visiting!!!
Nice atmosphere, good food & service, very enjoyable lunch.  I also liked that they have gluten free options.

We really enjoyed our meal here. Inside staff was great and attentive. Young gentleman doing hostess at door could use some additional training on customer service and friendliness. He lacked both.  Mac and cheese was to die for!absolutely mouthwatering. The Pork belly was ok, would prefer a bit more crunch/crispness. Crab Gnochi was good as well, although a bit cold when it arrived.  Aurora Nealand was performing with her band royal roses, they were outstanding!

What a gem!!!!! I love a good shrimp taco!!! These are definitely an authentic shrimp taco. Im usually not a huge fan of corn tortillas but these were actually very good!!!! I love how you can select your own toppings and the pickled onions were such a good choice. The jamaica was good too. Staff was very friendly as well. If I lived in Philly this would be my good to place
Awesome noodle bowls. Chicken wings and brussel sprouts were delicious too. Nice drink selection with good service. Only downfall is it is very tiny.

Awesome hardware store for city limitations. There's always someone around to answer questions and help you find what you need, and they're knowledgable enough to trust. They also stock certain less conventional items that apartment dwellers don't even know they need until it's too late. Every square inch of space is occupied and used to capacity. I see some folks saying that they're overpriced, but no independent hardware store can beat box giants like home depot, and you're paying to keep their real estate in RITTENHOUSE of all places. Very fair for what they are.
Great hiking trail. Lots of steep hills and awesome views. Had a nice work out while enjoying the sights.

Delicia was great! Their cocktails are handcrafted and delicious - try the Fire & Ice! They made vegetarian accommodations for me, and it was wonderful. I definitely recommend this spot for a perfect date night or night out with friends. It's a nicer place with a down to earth feel. I'll be back!
Sterilized .. commercialized.. canned.. what more can I say?  Wasn't impressed with the beer or cocktail menus..

Excellent food. A nice change from all the Cajun/Creole (although I love it too). Nice French inspired menu. Zucchini roulade was crazy good.  Lovely, CLEAN atmosphere, wonderful attentive service and the local lore was fun!

I have passed by this place several time and dined at the Binford Cafe next door to it so I thought I would step out of my Hank's Smoked Brisket and Fat Dan's comfort zone for some Black Diamond Barbecue.  I was highly dissapointed that they were out of Brisket so I tried the rib tips with hot barbecue sauce, mac & cheese and greens.  For what I paid I felt like I got a really small portion of tips that had a great smokey taste to them but were over done or older.  The last tip that I ate was on point though.  It was very tender with great smoke flavor.  The hot barbecue sauce was spicy and flavorful without being overly hot.  The mac & cheese was just plain old mac & cheese.  The greens were very good and exactly how I like them.  I tried a friends baked beans because he kept raving about them and I will say they were pretty awesome with tons of brown sugar flavor.  All in all they were A-OK but for what I spent there I would stick with my other two go to's.  I may stop by again to see how the brisket stacks up though.
This place is awesome. Best salad I've ever had! Haven't gotten past the signature salad to be able to try anything else on the menu

This is my second time dining here for brunch and I highly recommend this place for any occasion. The brunch menu and buffet consists of a great variety of breakfast and lunch entrees as well as some desserts. The endless mimosa is a nice perk they add to the $33 brunch. Make a reservation for sure, especially if you hate sitting at the bar top. The staff is friendly and extremely accommodating and I will most definitely return here in the future!

Ordered wings and basket of fries. The wings I got the Hot flavor.  The wings were perfect. I was pretty upset about the fries. It was stale and cold. We asked for fresh fries and it still came out even harder and added more seasoning to it. Usually everything is always so perfect.  I'm assuming it was just a busy Sunday and they just couldn't keep everything super fresh? :/  In place for the cold fries, they did give a free dessert  Thank you.. we will still come back on a slower day.

I went here for a corporate dinner. Like the northside Harry and Izzy's, there's nothing really "wrong" with this place except their steaks are not USDA prime. You can look up the difference in price between USDA prime and choice.  The issue is that other steakhouses in its price range use USDA prime meat for its steaks. There is a definite difference in quality between prime and choice - it's not a gimmick. Prime is significantly more tender because it's better marbled - there is more fat interspersed in the lean meat.  For the prices they are charging, they need to serve prime.

Happy Hour here is outstanding. $5 drinks and $5 select apps. Our favorite was the squash blossom quesadilla. The chicken flautas were okay and the goat cheese empanadas were tasty. Their signature margarita comes from a machine--which was offputting. However, they do not skimp on the tequila as one would think! Order it and enjoy.

Yum yum. This is a cute little place located in the heart of broad ripple. We came about an hour before closing. We got 2 regular Cuban sandwich's and the rice, bean, pork mixture. The staff was friendly. The restaurant is clean and I love the decor.   The food was really good. The sandwich was so filling I had to take the other half home. I could have went w the mini. We will be back for sure.
This is a bar not a 4+ star eatery. Know that going in. Food so-so. Loud and noisy. We endured lunch
Good farm to table option when you are trying to stay healthy. Hard to do when you eat out! I had their classic, no bun and added a fried egg. Had sautéed spinach as my side. Will be back for sure!

Excellent Car Wash, slow but thorough service performed by a friendly and professional staff. Prices are a little high but worth it for the result,  Full service washes range from about $15 to $40 depending on the service you want, the more you spend the better the wash (tire shine, interior dressing, etc)  Because I get my car washed about 10 times per month I pay $50 per month for the pass which gives me unlimited platinum washes which normally cost $30 each, that would normally cost me $300 per month but I pay $50 each month and can go as much as I want at no cost !! Wish they had this deal back in the day up in New York when I lived there

First time at this local restaurant. The meats were tasty but served cold. Collard greens were unusual but not in a good way. Garlic bread and cole slaw lack flavor. The fried green tomatoes were good. The waitress brought our food out, already cold but did not bring us plates or utensils. Had to ask for sauces too. She didn't bring straws with the drinks. She forgot my wine altogether. Food and service need work!
Went during restaurant week. Enjoyed the variety of the pre fixe menu. I'm only giving them 4 stars because the service could've been better. I would absolutely return for the hangar steak!

finally caught up with Spot burgers at their new brick & mortar in brewerytown...  the SpOt burger is uniquely delicious! with an amazing combination of flavor & texture (slaw & pickles anyone?). love the soft potato roll.  aside from the burger, this might be the best cheesesteak in Philly?! cheese fries are also a must. will definitely be back to eat my way through the menu!

Great food, but took forever to come out of the kitchen, and it was not a busy night.  My friend and I sat at a high top in the bar area and it was very loud and difficult to talk.  The guacamole was fantastic and I would definitely eat here again.

I've been here at least 10 times, and have somehow never written a review!   I freaking love Phat Bites.    Very hipster/loungy atmosphere.  Graffiti and art everywhere.  Friendly staff.  But let's get to the important part...the food.    The Turkey Wrap. I keep coming back for it.  Roast Turkey. Green Apple. Cranberries. Goat Cheese. Cheddar. Dijon. Tomato. Lettuce. Mayonnaise.  I'm not sure I ever had such an adventuerous combination of food before in a sandwich.  But, it works!  And has become one of my all-time favorite sandwiches/wraps.  Hummus Envy.   First off...the hummus at Phat Bites is the bomb.  So many different flavor options, and all so good.  This is a platter of pita chips, and your choice of at least two hummus flavors.  They top it with pesto, balsamic reduction, goatcheese, red peppers, and MAGIC.  Holy Crap its so good.    Kickin' Chickn Salad.   Your breath will be kickin' after you eat this, but its so worth it!   I could just drink that homemade ranch dressing from the cup its so good.    I've tried many of their other sandwiches/salads, but these were my top 3.  Pretty much anything you order here is bound to be good.  I highly recommend you give the place a try if you haven't already!

Wow.  I bought an assortment of 6 cupcakes for a dinner party I was attending. We got their cookies and cream, chocolate peanut butter, red velvet, double salted caramel, caramel apple, and chocolate raspberry.   At the dinner party, we cut each cupcake in fourths so that we could all sample each and every one.   Not a single stinker in the bunch.  Seriously. Every last one was 5-star fabulous.  The frosting is plentiful, but isn't overly sweet or thick.  The frosting is made with real butter, so they recommend refrigerating the cupcakes up to 2-3 hours prior to serving and then taking out of the fridge and allowing to sit at room temperature.   Some of the cupcakes are filled (omg...amazing).  All of the ingredients tasted incredibly fresh.  For example, the chocolate raspberry tasted of fresh raspberries (not sickly sweet fake raspberry flavoring), the caramel apple tasted of crisp fresh apples, and the chocolate peanut butter tasted of real peanut butter (unlike the inside of your typical peanut butter cup!).    The variety is astounding.  It took me a bit to choose 6 flavors because there were just so many to pick from. I didn't count--but seriously--SO many!   The pricing seemed reasonable for quality, carefully prepared, (not to mention beautiful) specialty cupcakes.  We got the half dozen for roughly $17.   The service was incredibly friendly and efficient. And, there is seating so you could certainly come and get a cupcake to eat right then and there.   Amazing variety meets pure decadent deliciousness!  Get to it!

My friend from out of town is a burger connoisseur so I thought this place would be the perfect place to take him for a casual lunch. The burger of the month at the time included pork belly, an edamame spread, and some sweet and tangy asian sauce that was amazing! I'd heard a lot about the duck fat fries so we ordered them, but to be honest I was a little disappointed at how bland they were. It was a bit emptier at that time of day so the service was fine, but I wouldn't necessarily come back.  I've also been here for the bar at night, which I prefer. Limited seating but really delicious drinks!

My new favorite spot! Fantastic food and service. So good that I keep thinking about my next visit. We got the samurai omasake and it did not disappoint - everything was so fresh and flavorful. Chef Rexley crafted each piece with care and precision and his staff provided awesome service.  So glad that there's finally a real sushi joint in the area. Tampa Bay was seriously lacking in that department and  Sushi Sho can definitely fill that void. Can't wait to be back!

I wish I lived closer to Brick House Tavern because it's really a great spot to be at! If you are looking for a nice place to chill on the relaxing patio while enjoying drinks and food on a beautiful day, this is it! I also enjoy coming for dinner and sitting inside, watching the game on 1 of their many TVs and mingling with friends. For food recommendations, I suggest the black and bleu burger, which had an outstanding flavor with the perfect combo of seasoned beef, savory bleu cheese crumbles and crispy, salty bacon on top! The mac and cheese is also a tasty treat, with bacon crumbles and bread crumbs mixed in for crunchy, cheesy goodness. If you go with a group of people, order a tall beer bong filled with your favorite brew, which never disappoints!

Definitely a hit or miss kind of place. Because I live close by, I always take my friends here for a bit of Asian fusion instead of making the trek to Chinatown. I feel like half the menu items are amazingly delicious (such as the Fried Chicken Tantan) and the other half is very mediocre (Coconut Curry Noodles). Cheu has a modern Asian vibe with some cool artsy decor scattered throughout the small interior. It's always been crowded when I've gone, so expect to wait no matter what time you stop by. Here's a quick summary of my thoughts:  (+) -Fried Chicken Tantan - unique, warm, soupy goodness -Crispy Sweet Potato Rangoon - so good, you'll want to order more -ambience -noise level -prices are pretty fair, considering the location  (-) -some of the food like the Coconut Curry Noodles and the Brisket Ramen (unremarkable) -service is sometimes harsh since it gets so crowded -wait time -crowded interior - you're going to be pretty close to all of the other diners

Came here with 2 foodie friends and we had an amazing feast. it was worth the visit  We ordered 8 items between us! For appetizers we had: 1) Pickled Pig's Heart (I was hoping it would be a the whole heart, but it was sliced like deli meat; nonetheless, it was very tasty, served with grilled broccoli on farm bread, all seated on a mildly spicy pepper jelly); 2) Sweet potato nachos (served with blue cheese, jalapenos and housemade catsup, very unique presentation and, very tasty); 3) Steamed pork buns (very tender pork belly, served on chinese buns (think "kong bah bao" buns) with a delicious XO sauce, also served with a housecured kimchi (for that "umami" factor).   For entrees, we got: 1) Portochino (housemade pork and pancetta sausage, served on a bed of braised kale and roasted radishes in a red wine ham sauce, yummy!); 2) Gulf Fin Tuna (fresh whole tuna, perfectly done, honestly it wasn't particularly special, the only taste that stood out was the duck egg yolk that gave the fish a unique flavor); 3) Breakfast (I think my friend would have kissed the chef for the corn flour blinis, with whipped maple pig butter, it was very very good pancakes); 4) Bacon wrapped meatloaf; 5) Pork tenderloin (a bit on the salty side,but very tender!)    For dessert, we got: 1) homemade apple pie with zuchinni bread ice cream, 2) chocolate and peanut butter cup (chocolate mousse in a chocolate ring, on a peanut butter nougat disc), 3) trio of ice cream including the zuchinni bread, rhubarb sorbet and vanilla ice crean.   To be honest, we regretted staying for dessert, it was a disappointing end to the meal. But it did not spoil our experience, I will still be back! (just skip dessert)

By far one of my favorite Malaysian spots !  Roti Cani is on point the chicken is tender and the potato is soft, the curry isn't spicy just a slight kick. The bbq beef & chicken satay sticks with peanut sauce is amazing ! It's a much try and trust me you will not regret it ! I usually get the prawn mee when I come here it's my go to. The soup base is a little more spicer than other places i've been to but I love it ! They can add a little more veggies and meat into it. Service here has always been great never had a problem.

This place is within walking distance. A few years ago an old coworker would bring these donuts into the office every Friday like clockwork. I left that company and hadn't had them in years. I finally ordered a dozen yesterday to take to a fantasy football draft party. They were a hit.   Very tasty, reasonably priced and friendly service.

Could have been better. Dinner seemed a little rushed - it was a Thursday night so it was not extremely busy for them, I attribute it more to the server not being dialed in to his customer - which isn't great for a somewhat romantic setting. (I often wonder why the server hasn't noticed that we haven't even opened our menus yet.)  The food was pretty good, pasta bolognaise and a salad to start. But my criticism is this, which I hope the owners read & heed: we were at an outside table and it was a lovely evening. The sun had set and there was plenty of light thanks to the street lights in the borough, but at some point they threw on the overhead spotlights which was an immediate rude awakening. The table of females behind us gasped collectively as well and actually screamed when the burst of light brought bugs to our tables and specifically a beetle into the hair of one of them. And what seemed worse was that when we all asked that they kill the offensive lighting, the answer twice was that they didn't want to "appear closed" to potential customers. Not only was this strange because clearly with people inside and out at tables eating it appeared quite open but it was annoying because the focus was more on the would-be customer rather than their actual customers (who were now unwitting participants in their marketing scheme).

Nice little cafe/restaurant on South. We sat in the front window ledge area, they have tables in back as well as a few seats outside in the back (too hot these days in the summer when it is 95+) and at least one table outside in the front typically.  Staff could not have been nicer, but at the same time not being in your business the whole time. My tea was just OK, but the wacky sounding hawaiian coffee with hazelnut was very good, better than it sounds.  We had the dill scrambled eggs with feta, which was OK but not great. Good quality, just not to our particular liking/taste. Also had the combo meal I think called the lot or something (scrambled eggs, bacon, french toast, etc.). Eggs were good but the bacon was incredible, probably the best I've had for breakfast in the city; not too crispy, the right amount of fat, etc.  Prices were pretty reasonable, the other patrons were pretty friendly, overall a nice time for breakfast without some crazy wait like you might get at some of the other brunch/breakfast places that have a rep in the city.  If you haven't been, give it a try, it is pretty good and the people are pretty nice all around.

This place is amazing.  In general, the vibe alone is 5 + stars.  The back courtyard can be crowded but I appreciate that they limit the people in the wine room.  So you're not knocking anything over or getting too irritated with the crowd.  You can buy cheese and meats and thene bring it to the counter with your wine.  They will make a platter for you and bring it to your table.  In addition, you can order some amazing food from the window in the back.  First come first serve on the tables but there are also standing tables and an upstairs cocktail bar.  The live music was incredible.  The place is just buzzing!  I loved it and I can't wait to come back and visit again!

So after a few months of driving by this place on the way to Kroger, I decided to give it a try. We arrived mid Sunday morning and I'm guessing that's not the best time to show up: there was a line of people (some were outside). It looked like they had pieces of paper with typed numbers to prioritize hungry diners. I strongly recommend they upgrade to a text alert system. It's much more efficient.   Anyways, since it was for a table of two, it was about a 10 minute wait before we were seated. It's a quaint spot with lots of food photos and paintings of Indiana sports teams. I really liked the atmosphere: chill and inviting. The host was very accommodating in making sure we got a table away from the door (it was a little chilly). Once seated, we made our orders: Mexican skillet, golden house frittata, and crêpe with fruit. I'm not sure if it was short on staff or too many diners, but it was worth the wait. I really enjoyed the crêpe. It's very rare when you have a crêpe with just the right amount of syrup. It wasn't too sugary or saucy and the fruit was fresh. The frittata, though slightly burnt around the edges, was also very tasty and the pancakes were very scrumptious.   All in all, I really enjoyed the food and the service. I really wished I remembered the waitress' name for proper recognition. This is certainly my new greenwood breakfast spot. The location is perfect for a good meal followed by a grocery run. I'd certainly go again (probably before or after the apparent Sunday morning rush).

Four visits with great food and beer! We had one problem with a tough tuna app and it was taken care of manager apologized and it was off our bill. Servers great, seating outside and in. Much needed restaurant of this type in this location. Love the onion soup, meat loaf and pizza's. We will be regulars!
One of my favorite Greek spots. Love the traditional Greek salad (good size) and you must try the spread and chips. It's a feta garlic dip with toasted pita slices. Awesome. I'm hooked on this place.

This place had pretty average reviews but I went there anyway. LIke previous reviewers have mentioned, the salsa is pretty spectacular. My friend actually drank the left over lime juice when we finished the fresh veggies. There were more chips left, so we ordered another one. The waitress brought it, but at no time did she tell us it was $1.50. We had assumed it was free refills, we found out that assumption was wrong when we got the bill.   I ordered the choose 2 combo. Tamali with shredded beef and enchilada with chorizo. I was pretty full from the chips so I only ate the tamali. The shredded beef was spot on and the corn filling was great too. Nothing special about the enchilada, so I just packed it up to bring home. The accompanying rice wasn't too bad, the refried beans were dry and looked like paste.   My friend ordered the Carribean Burrito with ground beef. They weren't given the option of shredded, just chicken or beef. He said everythign else was good, except the beef. It was dry and poorly seasoned.

Flexible atmosphere!  I love the comfortable atmosphere here.  You can sit at the bar and watch sports, or a romantic table for two, or a large comfortable table surrounded by friends.  The lighting and decor fit many different moods depending on your occasion.    You may as well start with an appetizer because it will take you awhile to get through the large variety of menu choices.  I was on a date with my husband so it was a romantic evening.  I had the meatloaf with mashed potatoes and corn succotash.  It was delicious and the portion was huge.  I made myself stop halfway through so I could have room for cheesecake.  After all that is the most important course for a date night.  The second half of my food went into a to go box and we ordered cheesecake.  Godiva Chocolate Cheesecake was this visit's winner.  It was amazing.  The service was great.  We can't wait for our next visit!  After all there are so many dishes and cheesecakes to experience.

Had plans to check out #Collingswood Mayfair and was happy to walk out the door to see this truck blocking my st off of Haddon Ave.  This was almost my first meal of the day, but since I heard all these greet things I decided to hold off. N glad I did cos after walking both ends of the fair it was hot and my first meal of the day was a sub par pulled pork sandwich from the Lumpys Bbq stand.  I've been outside for almost 2 hours and needed another meal before heading home and hit up this truck. I love cheese curds and this may b the best around.  One order was I think $9 but worth it for the 8/9 pieces I got with the srirachia mayo I think. Real good way to end the afternoon

I'm so glad that Mt Juliet has an authentic Indian restaurant.  We try to eat here at least once every couple of weeks.  The prices aren't bad at all and the food is amazing and so flavorful.  We really enjoy the garlic naan and have enjoyed every dish we've tried, from the biryanis to the chicken tikka masala to curry and more.    They take pride in making great, authentic Indian food and will make it as mild to as hot as you want it.  i have Indian coworkers that bring me food occasionally, and the taste is almost identical to their homemade dishes.
We had the renowned fish because burgers are too greasy,  that was an expensive mistake,

BEST BRUNCH IVE EVER HAD in Florida.  I loved the service, the food was so delicious, and the actual restaurant was so nice. They had indoor and a sort off outdoor area with fans, trees, and umbrellas  which is where I sat. They have a full bar, but I ordered a chocolate iced coffee, which isn't on the menu, but my waiter made himself, and it was the best thing ever!
The braised pork bowl came with a pretty pitiful amount of pork but it did taste pretty good. Had a taiwan beer for $3.50 which is pretty cool.

PF Changs is a pretty good place for a date, but the food is nowhere near authentic Chinese and the price is up there. I do enjoy the honey chicken, but for $15 your just paying for the atmosphere of the establishment. Service was fast and friendly

Excellent donuts! Came here three times because the first two tries we missed the brûlée cinnamon roll. We finally got it and it did not disappoint. We also tried two sliders and they were tasty! Yummy chocolate chip cookie topped with salt just the way I like it! Great egg and bacon biscuit sandwich. Would be bad if we lived here. Friendly staff. Seems like a fun place to work.

Marathon does not seem like a five-star restaurant on the surface, but when you dig deep and go there regularly, you realizw why.  The food is so delicious and so consistent there. Never a surprise. Their salads, sandwiches, and brunch items are some of my favorites in the city. Screw Stephen Starr and Jose Garces. A great home-cooked meal awaits you at Marathon!

Sandwich was quite good  We stopped in to grab some food, unsure what we wanted.  The order taker was patient as we scanned the menu board.  I finally settled on their Steak and White Cheddar Panini, and found it to be quite good.  I very much like that they list the calories for each item.  The place was clean and inviting and their offer to sell me a bakery item with my sandwich for only 99 cents tempting.  You order at one place and pick up your food at another.  They gave us a buzzer so we would know when it was ready, which only took a few minutes.

Family and I came here to try To-Kai because it was part of the UPromise Dining rewards Program.  Although pricey, we were hapy to find a new place to make it a regular in our list of restaurants.  The food for the family is below:   Japanese Mojito: 5 Stars.  Refreshing and not too stiff!  I probably could have had many of these, but stuck with one.  3 piece Dumpling Soup: 2 Stars.  Bland, dumplings I could find anywhere, did not finish.  Mango Soft Shell Crab Salad:  5 Stars.  The best food of the night.  The description is Mango, Crab Meat, Seaweed Salad, Lettuce, Avocado Salad and Masago on the Bottom, Whole Soft Shell Crab on Top.  This could have been a meal by itself.  Extremely tasty and just the right amount of sizzling crab on top with an amazing sauce.  Duck Rolls: 4 Stars.  Wonderful with no grease taste - Deep Fried Roasted Duck and Taro Finished with Sweet Soy and Honey Wasabi Sauce.  I expected duck meat pieces, but it was more of a hamburger meat texture.  Still, very tasty!  I Phone 4S Roll: 3 Stars.  Felt like it was warmer-than-room temperature which was a bit off putting.  Also, I had so much food that I probably did not enjoy it as much as I should have.  Crunchy Spicy Crab with Seared Sea Scallop on top.    Teriyaki Bento Box Salmon:  3 Stars.  Comes with Salad, Soup, Rice, California Roll, Shrimp Tempura, Shumai, Fruit.  Wife said there was too much tempura in the box with the vegetables, and did not finish it.  However, the salmon was very tasty, and she did not like the California Roll.  Fruit was 1/2 an orange.  The tempura shrimp was really nice, though.  Overall, it came to $96 for 2 adults and 1 kid including tip, so for lunch this was expensive!  There are a lot of choices, so I'll be back to try others.  They have a hibachi on the one side which I'd like to try, too.  To-Kai also has 6 party tray specials from $34 to $80 for catering!  Service for our empty restaurant was ok.  Too long to take our orders; just right to get the food and drinks.

What everyone else said about this laundromat is true: it is exceedingly clean, well kept, safe, priced right and the people who work here are friendly and helpful. What more can you want from a laundromat?  I came here to get my duvet washed. I could have washed it here myself, but as someone smarter than me recently said: "Ain't no one got time for that." So I dropped it off with the friendly launderette who was sitting at the counter. She took my info, told me when I could pick it up, and asked me what kind of detergent to use. (She listed off like five different kinds. I chose unscented Tide.) Three hours and $19 dollars later, I have a clean, fresh, nicely folded duvet ready to be put back on my bed. My clean duvet also came in a nice plastic bag with a handle. It's a dang miracle, I tell you.  If my washer and dryer ever conk out, this will be the place I visit to wash and dry my laundry. In the mean time, I like knowing that I have a place where I can drop off random things that are too big for my washer, and they will come back sparkling (and it won't cost me a fortune).

Our server was wonderful! she was attentive, sweet, knowledgeable of the food and its preparations. This place operates like clockwork.  The host was welcoming and the support staff was attentive and aware what was going on around them.  Staff gets a 5 star or (Starr)..lol Now lets talk about the food. First off, our meals were not bad. They were average.  They did however, lack flavor.  It might sound strange to say this, but our food and the space lacked identity. I feel like all Starr restaurants are known to have an identity and the food reflects that identity. This was definitely a weak point. This place was "good" but not what I assumed a Starr restaurant would be about.  We did attend there brunch a few time and thought it was very good, our meals at dinner were uneventful and a little  disappointing.

I ate here a very long time ago. One of Philadelphia's oldest, original Italian restaurants. Food was delicious. I especially remember loving the meatballs and you can judge a good Italian place by two things:  meatballs and marinara!  Highly recommend if you are in town.

Two stars so far...... We finally got to go here last night after I talked them up to my friends. We called ahead to make sure they were wheelchair accessible since one of my friends was in a chair (one of those 400lb motor car ones).  When I got there we were the 1st ones and we got some summer rolls (which were okay, but no where near as good as Pho Grand IMO). I also got the Thai iced coffee and thought it was a little too sweet for me.  When my friends got there we noticed there was a step to get in and we asked for a ramp.  They had no clue what we were talking about.  We reminded them that we already called ahead and they said they were accessible but I don't think they even know what that means.  We had to leave and were given no apology whatsoever. We ended up walking down the street in the rain to The King and I who did have a ramp.  What a mess of a night.

Yolklore challenges assumptions that a counter-service restaurant can't deliver thoughtful, elevated food. I was so impressed with my frittata and my husband raves about the Billy the Kid. All the ingredients were top notch and all dishes were plated beautifully. Delicious. Great kid options available. My son devoured his little dipper pancakes. The restaurant is a bit small, so you may struggle to find a table during busy weekend hours, but the staff is very welcoming and helpful.

Still loving this place, but feel like I need to bring an extra container for the excess cream chesse. Not complaining that they give you a lot of product, but I also dont want to die from a cream cheese heart attack!!

Great restaurant!  Just ate here for breakfast this morning after having eaten here the other night for dinner (which was our first night in New Orleans).  For dinner the other night, the service was excellent.  For breakfast this morning, somehow it was even better.  I'll start with this morning's breakfast.  Our server, Jackson, was completely on top of his game.  I cannot say enough how much I appreciated not only the excellent service he provided us, but I could tell from observing him with other tables that he is an outstanding server, because I noticed other people trying to order items not really on the menu, and in each case he was extremely accommodating.  You don't always find that attitude and approach in restaurants these days.  But Jackson just offered to "make it happen," as he put it himself.  That's awesome in and of itself.  In our case, we did go just a bit outside the menu but nothing too outlandish - my wife ordered scrambled eggs, hash browns, plain and simple, and that combination just didn't happen to be on the menu but Jackson was happy to oblige.  For me, the fried chicken on a biscuit with tabasco honey was terrific!  Man, that tabasco honey was great!  And so was the fried chicken, moist, and tasty.  And the biscuit itself was great, too.  And those GRITS, they are also great. Do not touch them.  Don't add butter, pepper, or salt.  Just eat them as is because they have plenty of flavor.  An outstanding breakfast at Luke.  Nice ambiance too.  And I haven't even gotten to dinner.  I must say service was excellent for dinner, and I had shrimp and grits, which was very good, but for the star of the dinner show was the happy hour oysters and shrimp.  SO fresh and delicious and you can't beat the happy hour prices.  Plus those French 75s are delicious and also on the happy hour menu!  Like is a FINE restaurant!

I've been wanting to try this place since it opened and finally got a chance on my 5th wedding anniversary! While I take my baby son out to many restaurants, this isn't the kind of place I wanted to spend a bunch of money at and have to worry about him. So we came child-free. We made a reservation, which was a good idea. It was packed! We were seated upstairs, and as some others mention, it was LOUD. It didn't help that one party seated near us was quite obnoxious - to the tune of everyone turning around and staring. While it wasn't a romantic ambiance, it was still an enjoyable meal.  We started with the roasted cauliflower and the fries with ketchup and aioli. Both were delicious. The cauliflower came with golden raisins and gremolata. The fries were thin, salty, and had delicious dipping sauces. We cleaned our plates and washed them down with beer (him) and pineapple agua fresca (me). They have a huge list of wine and spirits here, and even a sommelier. I'm abstaining from alcohol currently but my husband enjoyed his pour. We continued our meal with the burger (him) and ratatouille (me). He enjoyed the burger with more fries, though said it was a little unimaginative. I LOVED the ratatouille. It had penne pasta, eggplant, zucchini and summer squash in a red pepper tomato sauce and lots of cheese. It came with a huge nest of arugula on time, which I removed (yuck, my least favorite green). I wanted to lick my plate. The flavors were intense. I'm not even a huge fan of eggplant unless it's very well prepared, like here. We finished with desserts, the pumpkin tart with maple ice cream and the oat financier cake with caramel apples and creme fraiche. Both were again, amazing. The oat cake was so flavorful and moist, perfectly complimented by the apples and creme. My husband almost didn't want us to order two to share because he was so full, but he couldn't stop eating them either. For two drinks, two apps, two entrees and two desserts, the bill was about $75 before tip. Not bad at all for this quality of meal.   Service was on point all night. They have a lot of staff and seem to take somewhat of a team approach. I saw many people delivering food and helping out other than servers. We rarely had empty water glasses or dirty dishes on our table for more than a minute.   Overall, a great experience and I will be back. They start serving brunch soon and I plan on trying it. Hopefully the crowds that we saw also enjoyed it and it stays around. Great addition to Maplewood!
I never thought I'd be back but after two company lunches I must say the food, service and overall experience was so much better.

UPDATE: They have a jumbo size now, thus eliminating the pettiness of my complaint at having no drink left by the time I arrive at my destination after purchasing a regular boba.   I come here almost weekly, and am still trying to figure out the sugar quantities in these drinks, it's either too much or too little, and appears to differ greatly based on who is making your drink. I still thoroughly enjoy the Jasmine Green Milk Tea with regular boba, and am still struggling to find a new flavor due to my inability to order outside of this.

This was my second experience at Syberg's and much better than my first.  My first dinning experience was at the Syberg's on Dorsett where I ate the veggie pizza.  That was not the best pizza nor best experience.  Recently, my lunch on market was better.  I ordered the Syberg's Veggie Burger, which impressed me.  The thickness of the patty was similar to a juicy, thick burger.  Although it was not what anyone would categorize as juicy, but it definitely possessed the grilled flavor of a burger.  The bread, however, was nothing special and really not worth eating.  I would recommend sticking to the "Syberg's" labeled items and having a dish that includes the famous Syberg's sauce.

Well they got rid of my fish sandwich only have fillets so I properly won't be back again. $17 for a fillet for lunch was much more then I expected. Completely disappointed!  Food good yes but not what I came for. This reason two stars. People get a fish sandwich back in the menu!!!!

Brunchies is just what we needed in a Lutz! This is the perfect spot for breakfast or brunch. The guava stuffed pancakes are delicious and more than enough to share.  You can't go wrong with any of the omelettes.  Brunchies also offers a selection of mimosas.
Comfy reclining seats. Didn't go there for the food...movie theater was enjoyable. Clean bathrooms! Suggestion - buy a new broom to clean up the theater.  Broom was dirty. We will come back again.

Comes to my job all the time.  I was hungry.  I didn't want to leave and lose my good parking spot.  It was not too bad.  Big ass burritos for cheap and quick.  I really want to try the Frenchy Toast but next time.

I found the food here decent, but the headache of weaving through the ever-expansive line stretching out the front door is not quite worth the heady taco experience.   The main issue lies within the compact entrance which leaves little to no room for people to migrate into a manageable clump. Instead, we piled up like herded sheep around the register, then piled out the front door. Luckily, it was a mildly warm, sunny day so most of the crowd felt rather patient. However, I do NOT recommend coming here at peak brunch hours or evening dinner hours near weekends. My friends and I arrived at 12:30pm Saturday prime time lunch. We were able to sit with a lovely individual, but our food took app. 45 minutes before it came out. When I asked a staff member, she told me they were backed up from the whole lunch crowd. So, definitely order an appetizer (I recommend the queso) if you're waiting.  The outdoor seating is quite sidewalk-chic, if you know what I mean. Lots of large picnic tables and plenty of room for pets. They even provide dog bowls if Fido needs a drink of water. The picnic tables, I would say, are also their greatest downfall. Unless you're ok with sharing room family style (which we were), you might prefer indoor seating or taking it to-go.  Food was...less stellar than expected. I had the Korean BBQ and Brisket tacos.The meat was quite tender for the brisket, and I found the pickled red onions added just the right amount of sourness. I was disappointed by the Korean BBQ, a decent but lackluster mixture of chopped cabbage, carrots, and a slightly marinated bulgogi-ish meat. The star of my meal was the fried green chile mac n' cheese. Absolute cholesterol-inducing perfection. This made the meal worth the crazy line.  I would say, if you haven't had Tex-Mex in Texas, give it a shot. If you have, this might not be the best wait.

My husband and I stopped in today for drinks while waiting for his oil change. I had rose and he had the "can't hold water" which was a blackberry lemonade whiskey drink followed by a vodka sprite. Our bartend was very friendly and attentive even with other larger parties there! Although we didn't order food, all of the food that came put looked and smelled amazing! The burger/ fries, cheese platter, and buffalo califlour all looked amazing! We will definitely be back!

Delicious. There was a huge crowd when I went and it's the middle of December, but the lines always move quickly. Plus you always get to see dogs there. Chocolate ice cream with hot fudge and cookie dough  never disappoints.
Food was so amazing! Andy made the best Guacamole... his food and drink  recommendations were spot on! We will definitely be back... hopefully we will be lucky enough to have Andy make our guac!

Very cute and lovely restaurant with a wonderful vibe! We celebrated our anniversary here the other day. We tried the yucca fries, fried plantains, stuffed piquillo peppers and the tres leches cake with peach icecream! My faves were the fried plantains and dessert! I thought the fries could use more seasoning!

Vibes: quaint country home  Tasting notes for brunch: -Shrimp and grits: well-salted shrimp in cheesy grits with cubes of ham and pepper in a red, savory broth. Egg on top is fried with crispy edges. Comes with two slices of either potato or whole grain toast. Potato toast was browned on both sides, a little on the dry side, but not bad for mopping up grits and broth.   Impression: food is alright, nothing spectacular, a bit disappointing. On top of a 50-minute wait, I can only say this place is okay. It was slightly redeeming that the staff was aware of our long wait and gave us a dish for free.

The place is a smaller facility but I really get my workout in.  Depending on which instructor it is, the circuit training may vary but the music always keeps me going as well as the variety in circuits.   They take walk-ins but I just sign up via Classpass.  Make sure to bring $1 since towels are not complimentary.  I try to work a RowZone class at least once a week.   I highly suggest trying it out if you want to add variety to your workout regimen.  Plus the music is a great variety that will keep you pumped especially for those who enjoy hip hop.
I'm now giving district donut a four star because their donut lacks. The donut itself is too dense. It has good flavor but if they were to make the donut dough softer I would give them a five star.
Good specialty tacos. If you're looking to fill up, I would recommend the quesadilla. Fun soda selection. Inside is nice for a fast casual restaurant.

This is a frequent meet up for my coworkers and I. It's one of the best Happy Hours in this area of the city, especially if you're broke like me. If you "win" one of their happy hours, you (and anyone who gives your name at the door) gets free entry, free appetizer buffet, and 2 $1 drink tickets. How do you win? Attend a happy hour and insert a contact card at the door. I've been twice, and "won" twice. Maybe it's a coincidence. Or maybe everyone wins. No complaints either way.  It's a perfectly decent spot to get some free food and start your night, but it can get pretty busy post-happy hour and their drink prices are not the best if you're not benefiting from the $1 tickets. Seating is kind of limited, and if you're not lucky enough to grab a table before it gets crowded, you'll be stuck standing all night. The crowd is typically a mixture of mid-30's bachelorette parties and 21 year olds, and it can get really crowded and rowdy on weekends. It's a fun time if you're with the right people, but if you're looking for a place to kick back or have a conversation, I would look elsewhere.

My husband and I went here on a whim after brunch for Valentine's Day. We had the beer and chocolate pairing flight- it was oh so good! My husband LOVED their IPA and Extra Easy, I enjoyed their Southern Witt. The people working their were very nice and helpful. I would recommend this place for a first date or date night out, regardless of age!
They make a good spicy Bloody Mary, but I wasn't super impressed by the food. It's just okay.

My new favorite place!!! Yelp says they open at 8am... sign says 9am... we arrived about 8:45am... the owner came out and waved us in. Very nice! Beautiful restaurant..simple, clean,  kind of a cottage feel to it. Fresh flowers on each table with small candles. A romantic breakfast. We both ordered the same.  Biscuits and Gravy with Eggs. The biscuits are large,  crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside..oh...and the gravy was divine, lots of sausage, very tasty! Eggs were cooked to perfection and the potatoes were tender and seasoned with paprika..yummmm! We ordered an Peach Fritter to share.  OMG! Four small crispy balls covered in cinnamon sugar. Perfectly decadent, they arrived in a martini glass with a strawberry. Beautiful presentation! Not too sweet, just a nice blend of sugar and cinnamon and peach. The coffee is a local blend.  Very very delicious! I would have drank a third cup but all that food....I was stuffed! The chef, Michael came over and introduced himself and let us know that it would be just a few minutes before the biscuits were done. Everything is fresh made by hand, from scratch. No mixes, or pre-prepared foods. Our service was spot on! I was comfortable,  and pampered! I'll be back to try some of other items!!! I can't wait!!

There's a lot of Casinos/Hotels you can choose from in Reno, but this place takes it all. Sportsbook is huge and has a deli right next to it. Food in every restaurant here is very good except Bimini Steakhouse is a bit overprice for the quality although service is wonderful. Best Spa I've ever been too. Has a nightclub that is gaining it's steam back considering how bad Lex's customer service is at GSR. Yeah I don't have any real negative to talk about this place besides the one time management towed my car because it hadn't move in a week... So if you're staying as a guest, make sure you move your vehicle or else bored butt security will notice your car hasn't moved. Actually for that... I'm taking a star away. Still best place to gamble and stay in Reno.

My family loves to rant and rave about Dairy Joy.  I guess it's been a staple ice cream stand in South Tampa / Inter Bay for decades.  Anyways, this is really an old school ice cream stand. They have soft serve, hand scooped, shakes, floats, sundaes, cones, etc.. All hand made. It's a classic ice cream experience but nothing fluffed up about it.  This place is cash-only however everything is really cheap. A normal ice cream cup is about 2$, you can get a mini cone for a dollar and other things for between 1-3$. It's way worth the cash only price.  Overall if you don't mind eating outside in the heat it's a good ice cream.

I recently brought my dog here for an event with the AWA, Bark in The Park. After my dog was finished with her Yap-atizers and Bark-tinis at the entrance of the park we headed in.   We followed a trail which lead us to the gated area of the park where dogs can explore leash free. The gated area is huge and offers a few different trails. I was a bit nervous to let my dog off the leash because the gated area has a few bushy. But the gated area is small enough that your dog would be able to hear you call.   All the people and dogs I ran into were very nice. There is ever a dog water fountain located right outside the one gated entrance- pretty cool.   Timber Creek Park was a great place to take my dog, but I prefer Freedom Park. Timber Creek Park offers a safe place to start training your dog with off-leash walking, but Freedom Park offers a much more social atmosphere for dogs.

We went here for lunch on Saturday when we were running some errands in the area. I love the outdoor seating area - it is covered, so it wasn't too hot and we still got to enjoy the nice weather. Our server was very friendly and service was fast!  The wings are good - the skin is super crispy and they have a variety of yummy sauce flavors. I still prefer buffalo! Unfortunately, the wings were the only part of this dining experience that was enjoyable.   Fish tacos which were bleak. I would describe them as "three flour tortillas with a tiny bit of fish, a little bit of cabbage, topped with three kernels of corn." They didn't come with any sides either - if I hadn't gotten fries as well I would have left hungry. The fries are okay - just standard, straight cut fries. Can't complain.  My husband got a burger which was a little overcooked and overall, pretty standard. Two lunches, 5 wings, and 3 beers ran us $40. Would only come back if it were for a happy hour or meeting up with friends - and I definitely wouldn't get any of the disappointing food! Sorry PJ's.

Ordered lunch for my coworkers last week. We all had the chicken BLT. Calling the order in was extremely easy, they have a nice pickup counter, and it was ready when I arrived. The sandwiches were great and tasted fresh. Will definitely be back.

0 stars for tonight but had to do one.  We left our card and had to go back and get it a couple of months later since the drive and parking is inconvenient. For security purposes, they wouldn't allow me, as the wife to come get it with permission and ID. We asked the bartender who was clearly annoyed with us not being customers on this night and asking for our card as previous paying customers. She was very busy and after we told her why we were there, she hastily asked, "like what's your NAME DUDE??"- complete with an eye roll. Then she proceeded to serve customers at the bar after he told her.  Excuse me, but we were paying customers then and we still deserve great customer service instead of being treated as less than. She was very ugly about it. The bartender with a messy lump of curly brown hair working on 5-17-17 at 7:45pm was very rude! We are still customers and will be customers in the future. I will have a hard time tipping you bc of your crappy attitude. Take a breath and be nice to customers whether past present or future. Management, get her some help so she doesn't bark! She could not handle that bar alone with bar patrons and fixing drinks for servers tables. It was causing her to be a major B.

Loved this new place! Definitely a must-try.   My fiancée & I saw this new restaurant & had to give it a shot. We ordered A LOT - lamb chops, duck, short ribs, bacon-wrapped sweet potatoes, tempura shrimp and squid.  Be aware that the food is meant to be shared, so the servings are smaller.  Plus, the waitress hooked us up with a side of free rice tots! That's what you get when you're friendly to those serving you ;) We were told the owner of this restaurant is the owner of LouLou's, right down the street on Virginia - so you KNOW the food is bomb.  Everything was great - tasty food, great service, great vibes...you gotta give this place a shot!

Went Monday for lunch with the family. We went to the Village Whiskey when it was in AC and enjoyed it. The Philly location is very small, just a few tables but just as good. Great burgers and awesome fries. Worth the extra money, though the lunch special was fairly reasonable. Village Whiskey does not disappoint!

They are OPEN 7 days a week, as opposed to traditional salons that close on Mondays.  They are prompt and on time. As soon as I showed up for my APPOINTMENT I was greeted, checked in and immediately taken for my service.  My beautician was very nice, informative and provided me with a fantastic SERVICE. I was very happy about the way it felt and looked, as they're using the HARD WAX technique. Never tried it before, but now I'm hooked on it. It guest irritate my skin as much and it first pull my skin, only the hair. Way less painful too!  I was offered FREE underarm service as the first time client, which was a wonderful surprise. They offer services that you can pay a la carte as you go or you can purchase one of their packages (most valuable one: buy 9 sessions, get 3 for free).

Wow, first time stopping in and definitely impressed. The restaurant itself is very nicely set up and decorated. We were seated right away at noon for the lunch buffet.  The buffet was amazing. I've only been to another Indian lunch buffet before and this one definitely surpassed it. The amount of vegetarian options was awesome- I went back for seconds! Make sure to come hungry because you will leave stuffed!

GORGEOUS interior and upbeat atmosphere. Since I'm new to Nashville, I enjoy checking out some of the more popular places in town and Caviar & Bananas did not disappoint!   Ample amounts of seating, large lattes that are actually large and everything you could imagine for satisfy your appetite. Wine? Check. Sweets? Check? Savory lunch sandwiches? CHECK!  I ordered a large iced caramel latte with jalapeño cheddar grits and OH MY! Those grits soothes my soul.  I love everything C&B has to offer and I cannot wait to become a regular!

In trying to find a place downtown to meet some friends Maynard's popped up and I am so glad it did.  This restaurant is located in what appears to be a converted train station also attached to the building is the museum.  They have valet, but they also have a ton of convenient parking nearby.  At first we were confused between the bistro/market and the actual more upscale looking restaurant... mostly because the bistro looks like it has AMAZING food in a casual environment.  However, the restaurant was fantastic and has ambiance in comparison.  We were a table of four and each tried something different - burger, filet, bistro steak and house special pasta.  ALL of us were thrilled with our meals.  I had also ordered the mixed green salad which was truly delicious - and I don't normally say that about salads.  I also tried their house Sangria which is worth going back for.  (they apparently switch from white wine to red wine on their Sangria at the start of fall)  My favorite thing about the meal was the dessert.  I went with the creme brulee which had this homemade cookie and berries with it.  Not all creme brulee's are created equal and this one is way ahead of the pack in terms of what I have tasted over my lifetime.  We would return for sure.  The location was really cool, as well as the atmosphere, prices were reasonable, the food outstanding.  While I can't get as excited about the service we had - I would say it was 'good'.  Our server was very nice but he just, well, was quite inattentive and we had to continue asking for refills on our water and those that ordered iced-tea.  For a restaurant this size service should be excellent, not good because the server was nice.

I had the Sunday 3 course meal and it was great. I started with the butternut squash custard, had the mushroom agnolotti entree, and olive oil cake for dessert (all vegetarian). I enjoyed the flavors of all of them, rich and interesting textures. My companion had a vegan 3 course with roasted beets, roasted sunchokes and crispy potatoes, and a dark chocolate pear dessert. There is a nice selection on the drink list, and the cocktail and wine we ordered were nice accompaniments. The service was great and accommodating and the ambiance was very nice.
