Wonderful little market. If you want great Italian products and good food, this is the place

Wow, we tried this place again & thoroughly enjoyed it! The flavors were balanced & bold. The food was very fresh & full of flavor. I also love the little packet of churros they give you while you wait for the check - very light & crispy! I would certainly go back, glad we gave it another try!

The service was good.  The beer was warm.  The food was not good.  Given the other reviews, I'm hopeful that my experience was unusual, but I had trouble finding anything I was excited about on their menu, so if I return it'll be to give their beers another chance.  Their growler selection is the best I've seen, so I'm hopeful the beer redeems itself.
I'm eating a warm Carmel as I type --- omg the best ever!!! Also try breakfast sandwiches on bagels so fresh and filling!!

John Besh restaurants never seem to disappoint. Luke is one of his best because it is casual with great food at a great price. Ate here with some friends last night, we had the roasted chicken, the fried oyster and avocado salad, bread pudding and creme brûlée for dessert. All was top notch with flavor that is New Orleans own. Next time I go though I must try the seafood courtboullion which is Besh's version of a local classic. You just can't go wrong with John Besh.

The drink menu is everything honey!! Even if you don't order food, try something off of the drink menu. My friend was raving about the spicy rim margarita while I was loving the Kahiki Kai!   The chips and guac were very good. I didn't care for the potato tacos but I will definitely be back to try something else!  Tips:  Seat yourself.  Walk back to the Tiki Bar area because the vibes are much more laid back. Faster service at the bar!

Delicious food and drinks!! The restaurant is colorfully decorated with sugar skulls on the walls and festive chalkboards with special drinks and tacos written on them.  The grapefruit margarita was sooo yummy and my husband had a Mexican mule. It was very spicy but delicious.  We had the appetizer of blt guacamole which was pretty good. Basic guacamole topped with bacon, but very good basic guacamole.  I had the poblanos enchiladas which were really flavorful, but filling! Come hungry.  My husband has the carnitas tortas and dirty fries. Those fries will be in my dreams for weeks!! Luckily it was a cheat meal so we didn't care too much about calories, these things have to be loaded in calories and fat haha. They're covered in cheese and bacon and jalapeños. So good.  Overall we liked this place for a first visit and I'm sure we will be back!
The calamari was good, and I enjoyed their selection of local brews. Initially, there was some confusion as we had a large group. Overall, good place to get a beer and wind down for the night.

Good wine; wonderful service; shockingly good pizza (I.e. flatbread). One of the most beautiful gardens of any place in town, w a towering tulip tree providing a canopy over a cozy space with/ a few tables and music piped in.   Prices VERY fair. This place is such a surprising find. I feel lucky to have happened upon it.

Note: the location of this food truck is next to the Thornton's gas station parking lot. Just drive up along Armenia and you should see the red truck tucked behind a small make shift white fence. I ordered the 4 tacos for $8 special, all dressed with raw onions and cilantro. The tacos were al pastor, steak, chorizo and fish. They were all tasty and freshly made (the fish tasted especially fresh; it didn't have the typical bland, watery taste of defrosted frozen fish). My wife, though, said all the tacos were too salty, except for the al pastor, which is normally sweeter than most fillings due to the pineapple. Our table mates, who were Mexican, said the red ceviche was most delicious. The lemonade was not "freshly made/squeezed" as the sign suggested. All in all, a solid place to try. The service was quick and friendly.

This place was ok. I like how they offer you something to drink and the music is spa like. Prices seem a bit extreme for the establishment. $2 charge to use a credit card is ridiculous. They have a variety of colors. It can get a little hectic since they are busy. I was not overly impressed as some of the reviews say.

As breweries go, this place is well set up. Great porch and a nice interior with views of the brewing area.  The music is good to listen to in some spots, but in others (like parts of the upstairs) the sound can get a little distorted.   Beers are passable and there's generally a decent selection.  They're not going to win any major awards but by Nashville standards they at least brew clean beers.  Thankfully they don't offer a bakers dozen of "IPAs" and have some imagination when it comes to their recipes.  That said, I'd stick to their more hoppy beers and maybe their dark beers. Their more lightly hopped beers are their most drab, and I suspect the pressurized mash filter system they use is primarily to blame--it literally wrings out all the sugars from the grain thereby allowing them to use less grain per batch of beer (so malt *flavor* is lacking).   So 4.0 stars for the brewery venue, 3.0 stars for their beers..

My boyfriend brought me here for brunch on Easter Sunday. It was a bit difficult to find since it's not view-able from the main street. Atmosphere is quiet and nice with a small town neighborly feel even though the view is of a parking lot. They have indoor and outdoor seating.            Our food was delicious, I ordered the breakfast burger(done to your liking) which was on a thick slice of French toast topped w cheese, their caramelized peppered bacon, a fried egg(or however you want it) with maple syrup!!!  My bf got the Kennet mushroom omelette and it came w house potatoes, which were red skin potatoes (my fave!) and was so delicious didn't need salt or pepper as if it was seasoned w/ the ingredients in the omelet already. And we split a fruit platter which I was impressed the fruit was all fresh not a bruise and presented very nicely: it had cut up strawberries, blueberries and raspberries, sliced bananas, apple, pear & a beautifully sliced orange.           Everything was great including police service who refilled our drinks many times. The best part about this place for me was the BACON: It was AMAZINGGGGGGGGGGGGGG!!! I can't wait to come back I think I will just order 2 orders of bacon just for myself.  We also received a little card advertising their dinner so we can't wait to try that! Oh....its BYOB!!!!

We made the biggest Valentine's faux pas which is, deciding last minute to go out for Valentine's Day. Luckily thanks to the Open Table app, we were able to secure a reservation Saturday night for a peak hour seating. The restaurant doesn't look like much from the outside as it is housed in a large, commercial style building, but the inside is fresh, brightly lit and minimally decorated.  I award three stars for the experience, as service was friendly, accommodating, timely and food was executed to the best of their ability. Where I found it lacking, was taste and temperature, general atmosphere. Too picky? We ordered the Blue Crab Dip with Parmesan Cream for our first course, second the Cream of Mushroom Soup and Complimentary Salad for the Special Entree, and Entrees were Eggplant Vincent and the Fish of the Day, Redfish, served whole with potatoes and vegetable of the day.  The soup was served first, and was under seasoned and not very hot. Crab Dip came next and though gooey, bubbly and tasty, was slightly overshadowed by the pools of oil on top.  The entrees were also good, my boyfriend loved the simplicity of his fish and it was cooked correctly. My Eggplant Vincent was  a cup of eggplant breaded and fried and stuffed with crab and shrimp, served over a butter cream sauce and angel hair. Again, slightly under seasoned, but over all a good, tasty dish. Overall, not my pick for a romantic evening or food to write home about, but suitable for an affordable, seafood orientated craving.

From scratch slow roasted corn beef is best I've ever had (and I don't even like it, but looked so good on my husbands plate)!  Great breakfasts. Always crowded on weekend. That's a good thing.....a sign the food is good and fresh!

Let's be real, who doesn't love cemeteries?! Just me? Can't be! I'm a sucker for spooky places like this, and was determined to set foot inside its walls.  Itching to see the famous Voodoo Queen, Marie Laveau's tomb, marked with xxx's, and adorned with gifts brought by fans and admirers. COME HERE!  (As she was a New Orleans hero - as she didn't practice "hoodoo", but good voodoo and helped to free lots of slaves! The most commonly left item left at her tomb - hair ties -which goes back to her days as a hair dresser.)  Interested in brushing up on your New Orleans history, learning of former plantation and slave days, and all about the archdiocese? COME HERE! (Take a tour, which will fill your head with all the knowledge you need! Also stop by the visitor center, as it's filled to the brim with maps, pictures and artifacts!)  Wanting to feel like a VIP and have access not everyone has? COME HERE! (The cemetery used to allow all visitors, but due to the amount of the vandalism, all visitors must be accompanied by a tour guide - unless you have family members inside the cemetery!)  Dying to see where Nicolas Cage will be buried? COME HERE! (He purchased a plot of land for $250, featuring a HUGE white pyramid structure for his body to be laid to rest!)  Looking for a fun way to spend an afternoon and engross yourself with all things  tombs/cemeteries and voodoo. COME HERE! (no really! people are dying to get in!)   Let's face it, there is no better way to spend your afternoon!

Sunday brunch is where it's at!!! I came in wanting nachos and was a bit disappointed that I had to order from a pre-fixe brunch menu, that did not include nachos. But I was hungry, and open minded. $18 for a two course brunch, including a great coffee (which could be served hotter).  I ordered the Cobb salad and breakfast burrito. Excellent portions, and of course always tasty, fresh food. These vegetarian meals never disappoint. I highly recommend for a non-typical brunch!

I have been going to Bella Prana for 6 months and to hot yoga classes all over the world since 2010.  The instructors are excellent. I also miss Mallory because she brought a lot of energy to the room and also because of her upbeat music.

I don't give 5 stars often, but this was an EASY call. The absolute best hibachi meal I've ever had. Anyplace. Anywhere.  I ordered the chicken and filet combo to go because I just didn't want to make dinner.   The salad and ginger dressing were fresh and flavorful, soup was also full of flavor without being too salty, and the chicken / filet & vegetables were perfectly cooked.  I'll be back & can't recommend this place enough!!

It was Friday night and we were feeling adventurous! Adventurous enough to try Indian but not enough to go ALL the way to our usual hangouts in DTSP. Enter: Deccan Spice in Feather Sound.   First things first, I heard this place used to be a nightclub, but they did such a good job re-facing it, you'd never know. I was a little shocked to see white tablecloths and such a "fancy" feeling setting considering this is a really affordable lunch or dinner (and takeout) option. But, I appreciate the effort and slightly strange branding of everything (I'm talking from the napkins to the glasses). But you know what? Go ahead and rep yourself!   Our group hardly ever eats Indian, so this was a gamble for sure, but we played our cards right! They've got EVERY possible Indian dish you could want. After taking nearly half an hour to decide what we wanted, we went for some basics like Tikka Masala and Chicken Korma (de-lish!) but tried some new things as well. Our takeaway? Next time, more spice please. They were probably too nervous to really take it to a medium, so we all felt like our food was more of a mild. I think they are very mindful of their customers, which is nice.   The staff and manager were incredibly helpful, especially considering my bf's dairy and nut allergies. We didn't realize it, but Indian food has A LOT of dairy and nuts! (who knew?) They did ultimately cook him up a special order of chicken curry that he really liked.   The consensus: We WILL come back to try the lunch buffet so we can sample everything and educate ourselves on the different varieties and flavors of Indian food.   Tip for you: They seem to have a big carryout/takeout business and the joint didn't really start getting busy until around 9pm.   Tip for the biz: Yes, we loved guessing the piano versions of top 40 songs (super jazzy) but we really preferred it when some Indian music came on! Play more of that, please.

Let me start with a warning. If you are squeamish about dirty, rundown and outdated restaurants please get over it. You can thank me later.  I accidentally stumbled upon this place and nearly walked out. But it was filled with Korean customers! So glad I stayed.  I will only delve into the uncleanliness and rundown conditions briefly and because it cost them a Yelp star.  The tile floor was sticky, the ceiling tiles dusty and dirty, the restroom gross and the decor unchanged probably since the early 80s.  Ok, with that out of the way, let's talk about the remaining 4 stars.  My waitress was Korean and extremely helpful. Being a total Korean couture amateur I needed help. She guided me through replacing a house salad with a "BBQ dressing" salad.  It came out with six small dishes of unexpected sides. Kimchi, sprouts, radishes, eggplant and two others I forget. All were amazingly fresh and uniquely tasty.  Then came an unexpected Miso Soup which was also great.  When the large cast iron bowl arrived with my Bolgogi I was gleeful about this potpourri of tastes and healthy food.  The Bolgogi came with rice and noodles and an egg on top.  Absolutely and utterly delicious.  It continued to cook while I ate it due to the high heat of the cast. To my ending surprise was crispy white rice at the bottom of the bowl.  So are you reading this while eating here? If so, you are so welcome!

This is another time I wish Yelp had the 1/2 star option. If so, Marrakesh would be 3.5 stars.    I have seen this place quite a few times off of 5th and South but this was my first visit. We were a quasi, unofficial bachelor party for my wife's nephew and probably the strangest and most unlikely grouping for a "wolf pack" ever, but that is another story. The inside is pretty cool and with the tiles, pillows, decor it seemed almost as if we had walked into someone's house in Morocco. The staff was super friendly and very polite and explained Marrakesh has been open for 30 years. Middle Eastern music and Moroccan pop songs played softy in the back ground.  The food on the whole was  good. As others has explained the menu is more or less prix fixed and the choices are limited. The staff gives you some modification options but not much. The only down point food wise was our beef kebab was tough and over cooked. Everything else, especially for a bulk serving style was very good. To check out Marrakesh's menu on their webpage http://www.marrakesheastcoast.com/menu.pdf  Marrakesh has a belly dancer on Friday and Saturday nights and that was interesting. One of our group was Egyptian and he said she was quite outstanding. I wouldn't know good belly dancing from bad, so I can't really confirm. The dancing was tasteful and not raunchy.  One other thing of note is that with some Mid Eastern and North African cuisines, silver ware is in absentia. Guests are encouraged to eat with their hands as the custom in Morocco. All adding to a unique and sometimes a slightly messy experience.  The pricing was pretty reasonable, we were a little under $50.00  per person with 2-3  beers each and gratuity included. Marrakesh does have a small beer and limited drink menu. Marrakesh was doing a brisk business with groups like ours, well no group was quite like ours, but with large parties anyway. Marrakesh is cash only.  To end the night, we were served fruits, baklava and mint tea. It was really a cool place and we all had a good time. I am not in a hurry to return to Marrakesh but for a unique or special occasion it is pretty cool.

Not too crowded on Father's Day Sunday 1pm, maybe because of the new menu (though still standard diner choices.)  The iced tea was watery, my gyro was OK but drippy, others had a decent (but drippy) Reuben, Big Breakfast pancakes and sunn-side up platter.  The only exciting food I saw was the home fries and the pancakes...But the price is right and Dad likes it here, so OK for his day.

Cold.  and I'm not talking about the iced coffee or the A/C, but the customer service.  Listen, listen, not alot gets under my skin.  When I first detected indifferent, almost hostile,  body language and mannerisms I was amused.  Girl,  I don't know what got your undies in a bunch, but maybe I stabbed you in a previous life.  Sweetie, I've thrown so much shade that I might as well live in it.  So, we can exchange business cards.  Also I was in a good mood from pokemon hunting.  As long as the coffee tastes good I thought. It was.  We had ordered eiskaffe and dirty thai ger.  I was so impressed that I was quickly formulating a stellar yelp review for what was about to be my favorite, I repeat, my **favorite** coffee bar in Tampa.  So so close, but then I sent my friend for another round.  Shortly thereafter he asks if we could move onto somewhere where it was, in his words, "more welcoming."  That's where I draw the line.  When my friends or the company I keep feel unwelcomed.  He's an innocent! Don't drag him into our dark world.  Take a cue from a more successful business model from Sips on gunn highway.  I will always choose grandma's love over your inexperienced broody millenials.  That's where you'll find me, pokemon or not.  TLDR:   My experience started with Mmmmmmmmmm, but ended with -isery.  Say it with me,  Mmmmisery.

What a fabulous, cool place!!  Yelp reviews were spot on!  If you are in the mood for brunch type food, you have to try this place.  It has an eclectic vibe to decor and menu.    I highly recommend the Thai Fighter cocktail...if you like the combo of sweet & a hint of spice.  The table next to us had the make it yourself mimosas and they looked good enough to try next time!  The grilled cheese looked fab, as did the Monte Christo but I opted for the pork belly breakfast sandwich.  It was beyond fantastic.  Their version of hash browns is small, chopped potatoes with onion and red peppers sautéed.    They have a great outside patio, which is dog friendly!  There service is attentive and the wait staff was helpful in answering questions about the menu.  This will be a repeat place for food, for sure!!!
Came here last weekend for my bday and the vibe was chill. Definitely recommend getting the IPA here! The food was solid too!

My first time was confusing because I wasn't quite sure what the meals came with and there was a bad language barrier with the servers. But once I realized that any sushi dish came with soup and salad, I was sold. I think I've had about every traditional roll on the menu and everything's amazing. Plus it's quick and takeout is easy. There's almost always a Groupon. And if you don't want sushi there's a good amount of other options.

This review is from February 2017  I heard about this location during my tour guide while i was in NOLA last month, however when i went into it not what i was expecting. This is located in the French Quarter and a lot of tours take place around there I didnt do a reading however i did look around. There was a lot of people walking around and looking at stuff which made it hard to take your time and browse. Someone was having a reading done because the curtains were pulled and they had a sign up about it. This was a fun experience, not much help from the lady that was there she just stayed behind the desk watching.

I've been here a couple times now, and let me tell you, they know how to cook a steak.  I usually just sit at the bar because most times there's a wait.  I was impressed with this place, and I will keep coming back for the steak.  They also have meats available for sale, but i haven't purchased any.  I'll have to try that out and see how it works.

Have fun every time I come.  The food is great I've had the bacon blue burger and the Cuban, fries are really good too and so is the fish dip. The outside downstairs patio often has live music and is dog friendly with a nice view. You can also pull up on a boat.  I recommend their famous $5 rum runners .

Cool little spot where you pic what you want God quality ingredients and cool concept  Only thing they are missing is a real pizza oven , they have good ingredients but the conveyor belt operated oven doesn't do what  a real Brick  or clay oven can do

This place is small and intimate, but overall I enjoyed it. I had the brisket ramen and it was really good. The brisket itself was pretty tender and flavorful. The broth was also full of flavor. I wasn't a big fan of the Matzo ball in the brisket ramen, just because it is rather large and overwhelming. It kind of tastes over the rest of the bowl. I preferred putting the Matzo ball on a side plate and eating some of it with my ramen. The ramen noodles itself was pretty good also but probably not my favorite. Overall, this is a good place for a quick Ramen fix!

Great atmosphere. Very chill tapas place with intimate live music. The drinks were well balanced though I think they cheaped out on the tequila in their margarita.  Duck Crostini was so good that we ordered a second portion when we were done. Kale salad was decent, pork belly was not too good. Saw another table send back gumbo... Surprising amount of negative in this review, but it was a solid experience.

Love Ethiopian food especially the injera bread!   My favorite vegetarian dishes so far are the mushroom dish and cabbage dish.  They give you plenty of injera and if you have leftovers. It lasts about a day more and can be heated slightly in the microwave.

Amazing service and the food is sooooooo delicious and high quality. Dining experience was on par with Flemings and other high-end steak places, but minus the pretentiousness.  It is a very nice but casual place on the inside. Would highly recommend the lamb and lobster mac n cheese! The wedge salad was great too--enough to share between two people. My mom enjoyed her lobster as well. We will be back!!!

Classic throwback diner and one of our favorites. We've only tried breakfast but it's always fresh tasty and full of comfort food goodness.   The food arrives quick and hot. Love the choice of hash browns or home fries. Ham steak is HUGE! Fresh biscuits are a perfect vehicle for the spiced country gravy.   Service is friendly and very down home.

It's good pizza, no question. One of the better options in NOLA. I can't say I've eaten in the restaurant much, but they deliver across the city from a couple different locations so that's a very good thing. Quality can absolutely be inconsistent - don't expect the "A" team during Mardi Gras for instance - but most of the time we've been happy with a good, tasty product. Delivery is usually in a reasonable timeframe and the only thing I would worry about is using their app for a "half & half" pizza order which it turns out overcharges you significantly. The website doesn't, just the phone app.  Lesson learned.  It's good, it's local, and I'll order from here again but with other good options such as That's Amore now in the area it looks like Reginelli's won't likely be our go-to pizza delivery place anymore.

Shhhhh, don't tell anyone but the curry here is outrageously good.    We make the drive to enjoy this spot once a week.  Prices very reasonable, parking no problem - don't be fooled by the icky looking buffet, we have never seen anyone eating from it and suspect that it is merely a clever facade installed to confound the noobs.  The dining room is "behind' the buffet the left, that is where you want to be.    The noodle dishes are above average, but the curry dishes are what you are here for.  They are all very good - my personal fave is the ginger and cashew curry.  Ask for lime and the spice rack.  Beer and wine are reasonable priced.  Ambiance is cozy - not too loud, not too quiet...just right.  The service is excellent as well - our waitress remembered us after our first trip, and remembered our order as well.  Dinner for two under $30 with tip.  I don't give 5 stars very often (as of this writing, only 1 out of 17 reviews), but this place is truly deserving.

Rolling in from Westmoreland County on the Pa Pike, my stomach started growling after almost four hours on the road.  I was near exit 312.  I pulled out my trusty sidekick Mobile Yelp and dialed in "Thai Restaurant."  It came back with two choices in the vicinity, so I opted for the one nearer my location.  Thai Place has an unassuming name, but under its self deprecating identity lurks a very fine Thai kitchen and comfortable dining space turning out very good authentic grub. Where many other restos of this genre approach the atmospherics with dark wood and musty scents, this shop is bright, light, and airy.  The owner works the front of the house enthusiastically, while the single server on the floor managed a warm smile and genuine concern for all the patrons.   I ordered the Tom Yum Soup (Vegetables) and Drunken Noodles (Chicken).  Both were excellent.  They asked my preferred spice level between 1 and 3, and I asked for max spice.  That's the way I got it, right at the point where my sinuses start to run. Love it!  There were plenty of Veggies and Tofu in the soup, and even more in the Drunken Noodles.  There was enough for dinner the next night.    The only reason Thai Place did not reach the apex was that my Jasmine Tea arrived in a lovely lime green mug with a teabag, rather than a pot of brewing boiling water and loose tea leaves.  That is the best way to achieve full bodied flavor, and requires fewer return visits to refill the mug with hot water.  I get that good tea is expensive and often unappreciated, but for me it sets apart the very good from the superlative.

I LOVE The Villagio! This place really spoils you... AMC and the other movie theaters will not be my first choice of venue when seeing a movie from now on. The reclining, puffy, comfortable chairs is what does it for me. The food and beverage service during the movie adds a nice touch to the movie experience. I do like the fact that The Villagio has a time cut off for children under a certain age to be in the theater. This probably has a lot to do with the fact that there's a full service bar, but it's nice to not have to worry about rowdy kids when seeing a movie. I almost forgot to mention that upon purchasing your movie tickets you are able to choose the exact seats you would like to sit in during the movie.

Lots of different burgers and sandwiches to choose from, a few vegetarian options. Mac n cheese was super bland:( my husband had the Napoleon: wish we knew it was a fork & knife  monstrosity, as the server called it. Good comfortable atmosphere, would recommend.

The IMA is one of my favorite places to take Indianapolis visitors. The art collection is wide and varied, providing a good intro to different artists and eras.   However, the real gem here is the grounds. 100 Acres is beautiful in any season and it's a great place to walk around and experience art with friends and family. Also, if you're a John Green fan you'll be interested to learn that you can see the "Funky Bones" sculpture from "The Fault in Our Stars" in the park.   I also recently had the pleasure of attending a wedding reception in the museum's events pavilion. Small dance floor, fun space.
If you like slow service, and anyplace that can ruin a salad then this is the place for you! Can't say much else. Good luck.

Hearing that frozen custard is a STL staple, I knew I needed to try it. I ordered a small of the oreo and it was the smoothest custard I've ever had. Though, I wish it had a stronger Oreo flavor. I think Ted Drewes has a lot of history with people in STL, which is why this place is popular amongst locals. As a tourist, it was nice to share the experience with a few friends, but I would not consider this the best froxen dessert I've had.

I noticed as we entered the restaurant wasn't busy. I expected it to be cleaner. After we ordered and got our drinks we made our way to a table. Had to wipe salt and a few fry's from the table. The food was ready quickly.  I  love the fry's. However, these were so over salted and I mean over salted we were unable to finish. The fish was way too greasy. Once again I was let down. May have to start going to a different location...

We visited here for the first time today for lunch.  I can't say the food was horrible; it was mediocre at best.  Our waiter was great.....very attentive, very helpful.  The place wasn't so busy but we got stuck sitting elbow to elbow with a really obnoxious, crude old drunk who kept dropping the F bomb throughout our meal.  The worst part for me was looking up the ceiling and seeing all this dirt and disgusting black dust just hovering above our heads.  What would it take to clean that up?  You don't let customers see that....at least I wouldn't.  We were going to order a pizza for takeout but the same thing, only worse, was hovering in that area, too.  Sorry, Lemonicello  but we won't be returning.  Clean up your ceilings, light fixtures and wipe your walls down.

Came in for drinks and was pleasantly surprised with great grub. Drinks were reasonably priced, I had a mixed drink and my friend had wine (she said it was really good).   Staff was friendly and service was great.   Food was amazing all around. I had blackened wings, my friend had the burger and we had tots on the side. Everything was cooked perfect and seasoned nice.   Hats off to the local bar that throws down in the kitchen

I loved my stay here. The room service was top notch the view was grade A and the mini bar was stocked with the best stuff. Too bad the girl I was dating at the time was a total B but that's neither here nor there. Beautiful atmosphere great location would stay again!

For a hotel/resort restaurant, it's actually pretty good.  The giant fishtank is obviously the cool talking point here, but don't be one of those jags who goes right up to it if you're not seated there to take pictures with the flash on using your dumb iPhone.  I promise nobody on your snapchat cares about you putting the caption "dinner!" over a picture of a tarpon.   Anyway, they've got a decent local beer selection and the grouper sandwich was legit, so I guess there are worse places to drop $70 on a lunch.  Bonus points for being a Guy Harvey property, so you can go in wearing your GH shirt with a painting of fish flying around a lighthouse on the back and play "match the apex predator"

Casa video has been a Tucson icon and in this time of Netflix video shops are not long for this world. Casa Video managed to change their business model to include a trendy craft beer, wine and cider bar. They too have big screens playing awesome movies, wonderful alternative to sports crazed other bars. I love the theme nights, Star Trek, Harry Potter Trivia, game nights. Free popcorn is always a big hit. And you can still rent movies. With a selection that doesn't come close to Netflix, it's totally worth checking out. It's one of my favorite places for good libations and great energy. So glad Casa Video survives!
The Pho and Vermicelli are spot on! Try the lemonade soda for something fabulous to have with your meal. Great prices and amazing food!

The $1 kids book section is where its at.  The staff are friendly but not overbearing.   The layout and decor makes you want to cozy up in a chair and chill for a few minutes -- and I'm sure many do -- but not me because every time in there I've got my toddler in tow and I'm trying my best to make sure he doesn't eat all of the complimentary cookies that are often set out or start chucking chess pieces from the vintage set that sits near the back of the store!

After driving by and seeing the line out the door the last week and a half or so, I figured it was time to try Raijin Ramen out. Finding a solid ramen option in town has been a bit of a challenge (aka I didn't previously have my spot yet). I ordered the red chile spicy ramen and it hit the spot (if I ever come back, I will surely order this). The egg was half-boiled (a good sign), and the broth was really flavorful.  Our particular server was friendly and accommodating, although when I initially called with a question, the manager (presumably) literally told me "I'm kind of busy right now, soooo..." Yikes. Not the best customer service on that end, but the server made up for honestly a bit of a rude exchange. The quality of the ramen itself is what earns this review--glad this place is here in Tucson.

A great home feel with friendly service and great pancakes. The sweet potato pancakes are packed with sweet potato and covered in cinnamon and powdered sugar. They are light, fluffy and cooked perfectly -- nothing worse than dense, over cooked pancakes. However, I am glad I went on a Friday morning with no line. I'm not sure I would have been as happy if I waited in the notorious long line on the weekends.

I went here a few weeks ago so my memory is not perfect but I did want to register my overall very positive impression. Their raw P&J oysters put paid to my former assumption that large warm water gulf oysters are not good on the half shell. These were excellent and nary a bit of grit in sight. Drinks were great, or at least I recall that the French 75 was. Our waiter was terrific too. The Alsatian tart was a little unusual, kind of sweet, but nevertheless tasty and we did eat all of it. We ended up splitting drum for the fish and while it was indeed tasty, it was you typical NO fare with the dirty butter sauce. I would go back in order to try the other menu items and get more oysters and drinks.

For a cidery, this place has an astonishing lack of ... ciders. We popped in after hyping ourselves up for a delicious cider on our usual Thursday date night. We got a flight of all of their ciders (only 4) and decided there was only one of those we half liked and the rest we did not like at all and had to force down. I think we like dry ciders, not a lot of hops, with a hint of sweetness, but the ciders here were all too sweet, too hoppy, or just plain gross. Recommend going to Cinder if you want to try a bunch of ciders (though not all of theirs are locally brewed obviously since it's a cider/pizza bar, not a cidery).

To be honest I really struggle with this brewery. I love multiple levels of their establishment, but the beer is not one of them. And let's face it, it's a brewery. Isn't that kind of important?   Let's start with the heart eyes emoji provoking assets. Their food is great. Their fried chicken sandwich melts in your mouth (I didn't order this but I always get a taste of my buddies palate ). Their bao buns were not the best I've ever had, but still tasty. I actually prefer mine more on the airy, fluffy side, but they choose to sear theirs so it's a little crispy on the outside. I'd also prefer. More crisp than fatty piece of belly inside but hey look who's being contradictory now (make up ya mind, Tone). Anyways, I was surprised at how much I loved the fried rice in my salmon taco. I actually enjoyed a few forkfuls on its own throughout the course. But even still, almost all of the portions are a little small for the price. Quality is important though here, and I feel they nail it.   The atmosphere is definitely another pro. If you don't like noise, go away. People are having fun and drinking merrily. It's gonna be loud. So talk a little louder, look at your guests when they speak, and laugh at the kids next to you trying to steal their dad's wings. I'm Italian. I'm loud. I love noise. It makes me feel at home. So the tremendously open layout of this building is much preferred over their cafe location.   So the con here. Guys. Whyyy are your saisons sour? No where else do I experience this, and saisons are my favorites. And guess what.. I hate sours. Don't get me wrong, I've had a few of their beers that I have really enjoyed, but overall I think this place is over hyped. My thought is they just try to get too eccentric, and just get a little nutty with their concoctions. Like I said, I don't hate it, but I rarely love it. I'll still keep going though. The atmosphere, food, and occasional slam dunk brew still reels me in.

Yummy tacos. Not sure if they got my order wrong, but my pork and chicken tacos looked and tasted the same. They tasted very good! The Sonaran dog was decent, kinda soggy by the time we got it. The food came out at different times and it was hard to hear them calling names. But it was a good find.

I'm too old to give you five stars......   hahhhahha as we approached the upstairs pool area we waited for some other older folks to exit so we didn't upset the balance of youth and tres chic.  and I'm not trying to be snarky, I just have a sense of humor.   however spending ten dollars a drink on a teeny drank on the roof wasn't that impressive.    this place will take a bite out of your wallet.    while I like the overall idea, I feel like I'm back home in the city I left for a reason.   yes, I'm from portland.   but this is new orleans.   while stumptown coffee is good, I have a hard time not seeing this as a form of gentrification that only those NOT from here can really savor...  I did see a recent show here / female drag, and that was spectacular.   I didn't have any drinks tho, and I didn't see anyone serving any.   I didn't feel like stepping out of this venue to find the bar...  I was mesmerized by the performer.   this place is beautiful, and I can appreciate the open mindedness of freedom of expression in your hiring practices.    I haven't eaten here yet, but I will.   I look forward to seeing how you handle mardigras next year as friends of ours will be staying with you.      a suite / with guitar / 30 year wedding anniversary.  other than my self expression of my age, I congratulate you on your opening.  please know that setting drinks on a drum in the bar area?  is considered really bad luck.    can you find a place for the drum where she can be more appreciated?   thanks.

I love idea of this place. So nicely decorated and themed, but it is sooo over priced in my opinion. 12.50 for a slice of cake and most of the drinks start at 11+ dollars. The cake is pretty good, some better than others. Overall taste from the ones I've tried I'd would give 7/10, but maybe I just had higher expectations due to price.   However there is nothing else quite like It when you are celebrating something special. When we came it was very hot outside so we sat inside, it was just as hot if not hotter. Not sure if they have a/c there or not but to spend that kind of $$ and be uncomfortable was a bit disappointing, and had to cut my date with my friend short due to it.  The drinks are as tasty as they look/sound but again with what you get for for the price? 5/10

What a stunning restaurant, in every aspect - interior, food, staff. The hubby and I found ourselves with a spur of the moment kid-free date night and, upon the recommendation of a friend, decided to make a Coolinary reservation for Carrollton Market.   It didn't disappoint, and I'm still dreaming of the calamari appetizer. Dinner was great. Dessert was amazing too, which was surprising only because I find that dessert is such an after-thought, especially on the limited Coolinary menus at most other participating restaurants. The pot au chocolate with the mini beignets? Fantastic.  Drink menu was on point. I need to return for happy hour to try some cocktails, but we did enjoy some wine and a house-made liquor with our meal.   This is definitely worth a repeat visit.

As you enter Petra, you immediately know you aren't in Kansas anymore.  The Arabic music, the workers in head coverings, the aromatic foods, all whisk you into another culture.  I ordered the sampler plate off the appetizer menu.  It was a full meal.  The stuffed grape leaves were delicious with the right balance of lemon juice & rice, but they were very oily.  The kibbe was the star of the plate with its cinnamon & perhaps nutmeg spiced beef.  The falafels were probably the low point of the plate.  The falafels were heavily fried & had little taste.  I've had superior falafel almost anywhere else.  The plate came with large scoops of labneh, baba ganoush, & hummus.  All three tasted very good.  In fact the baba ganoush had a nice subtle smoky flavor  that was very appealing.  My wife ordered the chicken schwaarma which I helped her eat.  We thought it was very tasty.  The waitress we had was excellent.  The music however was not so good.  It was blaring out of ancient ceiling speakers which were low quality & seemed to be torn.  The music was tinny & would get full of static & the volume would fade then increase.  So I give this place a mixed review.  It was authentic but the food was above average to below average..

Good, fast, consistent and fairly priced. That's all I ask! I usually get one of the burgers, which are good. On the last visit I got a chicken sandwich, made Chik-Fil-A style with just pickles. It was delicious and rivaled Chick-Fil-A. Smash sweet potato fries were yummy. I also tried the Nutty Butty shake. It was too sweet. I've had their other flavors of shakes and it was better. Great place to come for a fast lunch.

Pancake Pantry is one of those restaurants that your family considers its own. The one you know you'll be eating at on vacation, every time you visit that city/town. The kind of homey, nostalgic place that you elevate to greatness in your mind and speak of with reverence.  The fluffy pancakes, in what seem to be a gazillion forms, are the main attraction. I love the blueberry variety with its warm, fruit compote. I'm also a fan of a Frankenstein creation which involves breakfast potatoes (not hashed browns), veggies, multiple cheeses, ham (which I sub for mushrooms), and two eggs. A meal that lasts most of the day into early evening. My mom's new obsession is the banana walnut cakes which she says taste like banana bread in pancake shape.  Prices are reasonable for the large portion size and service is very prompt. They want to get you in and out with a smile so the line can keep moving right up until their afternoon closing time. I've never left hungry and never needed to have to much after a breakfast here.  Having said that, the Pantry (we use one name after all these years) isn't perfect. Parking in Hillsboro Village is challenging - it's right in the middle of a busy hub of shops, restaurants, hospitals, and Vanderbilt. The wait can be obnoxious - on this last trip we stood (yes, stood, they do it old school here) in line for over an hour on a Friday at 11 am. However, neither of these things has ever deterred us from going. It must be love!

August 17, 2013 Saturday - dinner 05:45 PM 2 adults   Atmosphere - 5 star Food - 5 stars Service - 5 stars  Background - Finished herding the cats, okay my friends after the afternoon wine walk.  Atmosphere - Sat on the patio, old sidewalk around their restaurant.  It was fun watching the people come from the parking garage elevator.  Food   Appetizer - none.    Drinks - Strange but I only ordered water.  What a shame, they have great beer.  But after walking around the afternoon I was parched.  Our waitress was good at bringing the water every ten or so, I was jugging it.    My Main Course - The Sante Fa, a chicken sandwich.  It had avocado so I had to go for it.  Loved the pasilla pepper on top of the chicken, it was grilled to add the smoke taste.  They also added smoked mozzarella for more smoke.  Great sandwich    Dessert - Skipped it.  Service - She was there with the water, what I needed.  Great service and she was very helpful when one of the ladies in our party did not like their meal.  Future - Been here before, doubt I will stop now.

A nice little restaurant in Jenkintown right off Old York Road on West Avenue, hence its name...owned by a family from my synagogue who also owns Max and David's in Elkins Park.  I've only ever been to West  Avenue  Grill once, but I will always remember how amazing their mile high french toast (made with challah bread and garnished with caramelized bananas) was.  It happens to be one of their signature entrees, and  I can see why.  Their hours are interesting though; they are open for breakfast/lunch/brunch,etc. and close around 2pm (however, they allow customers to stay and eat if they are seated prior to that time) and then reopen in the evening around 6 or 7pm for dinner.  Though for anyone who is looking for a nice place to have brunch on a Saturday or Sunday morning, provided it's not too crowded, West Avenue is the place to go, and a  nice plus is that it's surrounded by some nice little shops you can check out before or afterwards.

My girlfriend and I just had a delightful lunch.  Lunch specials are $9.95, which includes an appetizer, an entrée and a beverage.  Our server was friendly and knowledgeable, and he gave us plenty of time to decide. There were many choices.  She got arancini, spinach ravioli and iced tea. And she ordered a dinner-sized portion of baked cannelloni to take home for dinner.  I got phyllo cigars, chicken madeira and Diet Coke.  Beverage refills were spot-on throughout our meal.  When the appetizers came out, they were fresh out of the fryer -- hot, but not killer hot. The arancini were sitting on a tomato-cheese sauce. The phyllo cigars were served with a tomato-based sauce.  We shared, so I got an arancini, and she got 1-1/2 phyllo cigars. They were both tremendously flavorful and full-bodied. The phyllo cigars were stuffed with dill feta. Besides risotto, the arancini were filled with gorgonzola and steak.  Our entrees were equally impressive, but less eaten. We were both kinda full -- not stuffed, but comfortable.  I took two bites of my tender chicken, which had a tomato and mushroom sauce, and two bites of zucchini (cut on the bias rather thickly to maintain its integrity) beautifully sautéed to the point of lovely browned crispness.  She took two bites of one ravioli, proclaimed it excellent, and we both took home a bunch o' food.  By the time we left, place was pretty filled up . . . just like we were.

Food was pretty good and the atmosphere was nice as well. Just enough lighting to create a very cool ambiance. There are big windows which overlook a nice pond with koi fish swimming around and a pier to walk out on. Every hour or so, there are fire displays that shoot out from the pond which add to the unique vibe this place gives off. The servers here are also top notch and are so friendly. Aja and Christine are great employees; very friendly and hard working. I can't wait until Union Station gets its well deserved makeover and this place will definitely be a hot destination spot once again.

This is a great place to get a little work done, meet a friend, and sip a nice coffee! Note: you can get free refills on the coffee, which makes it great for a morning study session. Their sandwiches are quite yummy (my fav is the prosciutto one). I only wish I could get a few more chips on the side (it's a healthy little portion of chips). The staff is always friendly and I love the upstairs workspace! Definitely check it out!!

Start with the food.  It is good, not great and they offer small portions for a high dollar price.  Service. Horrendous. They actually have a waiter here that several different people that I know have had wait on them and ruin their meal.   Not bad just not great or worth cash. Service really bad. Or at least the same person who is waiting on all the gulch locals.
Snowballs stuffed with cheesecake? Really? Okay. The kid loved it and wanted it again. My traditional spearmint was the way to go for me!! Delicious and plenty of choices!

Here's the thing...I want so badly to give this place 5 stars. I would give it 4.5 if I could but you know how that is. I love the food, I loved the drinks when I drank and I love the staff. The thing is the first time I came here I was an out of towner. I was an out of stater to be honest. I think maybe it showed and the girl working there didn't seem so friendly. We talked a little bit but something seemed off. I had the bacon Mac and cheese and some good beers so the food and drink were 5 stars I was just a little annoyed by the service. Well a month later I moved to philly and came back. I got the same girl as a bartender and put my best foot forward to be friendly and it paid off. Probably also helped that I was now a "local." Every time I've gone since no matter who's working I have a great time! The draft selection is pretty nice and the can/bottle selection is pretty extensive. I had the shrimp etouffe and it was AMAZING. I will admit the menu is sort of small but it works for me. I love this place and my stomach is growing just thinking about that etouffe!

Love this place. The price is right, the food is good and the tables are all set up for saki bombs! The waitresses are on point and chefs talented in showmanship and presentation. I recommend you make reservations and make a night of it.

Went to Em J's with some colleagues recently and we really enjoyed it.  The owners are super nice and the food was wonderful... I had the most delicious veggie quesadillas - my mouth is watering just thinking about them.  It's a little far from where I work.  If it were closer, I'm pretty sure I would become a regular!

Went to El Camino for brunch this weekend. It was not as good as my dinner was previously.  The huevos rancheros was served in a cast iron skillet, which is cool, but it really wasn't doing it for me. It kind of tasted bland and the refried beans were more like a soup than they were like a refried bean. Also, the breakfast burrito doesn't come with any type of sauce like guac or sour cream, and I think that's just weird.  Our waitress, while very pretty (Megan Fox look-alike), was pretty checked out. She didn't come over to check on us and only refilled our water glasses after my friend had to start drinking out of my glass. She also didn't ask if I wanted a refill of my coffee. When my friend asked for ketchup she brought the bottle to our table and slid it across the table. I don't know if it was intentional or not, but it was pretty rude.  I'll stick with Green Eggs or Cantina Dos Segundos for breakfast now on.

This would have been a one star review had the manger not had the foresight to comp us a round of drinks.  We went here for center city sips last night and the place was busy but by no means filled to capacity so it was baffling that it took more than 30 minutes to get our first round of drinks.  Food came out a bit quicker but not much.  Then when we were ready for our second round of drinks they had run out of the ingredients to make both (yes both) of their special priced drinks.  So we were not able to get them at any price.  The drinks were not consistent. My friend and I had the same whiskey based drink and hers was three times as strong as mine.  The food was inventive and tasty but the portion size very very small. The zucchini that we ordered looked more like a garnish than tapas. With that said to be fair this was a $5 portion for Sips.  I don't think that I would go back here - despite a lot of hand holding from the manager and apologies which were appreciated I think there are enough restaurants bars in center city that have already worked out most of their kinks.

Wow! So far my favorite place I've discovered since moving to New Orleans! The atmosphere was outstanding. I went one night with friends and we enjoyed a bottle of wine and delicious cheese plate sitting at night under the heat lamps while listening to an amazing jazz band. I needed the heat lamps as I'm Florida born and raised!
Great rehab. Friendly barkeeps, cheap drink prices, good food, and lots of places to sit. Highly recommend.

I had a sushi burrito and I loved it! I was a little nervous. Would the seaweed wrap be chewy? Would it stay together? Would the flavors work? The sushi burrito anxiety was real and it took me awhile to try it. If you feel like this, don't worry. It is not that scary.   The Spot is combined with Ha Long Bay. You can actually order Pho with your sushi burrito! Anyway, if you walk into "The Spot" side of the restaurant, you will see that you order your burrito like you would a certain fast food Mexican burrito.  First you pick burrito, bowl or salad. Then you pick your protein. You can pick two or three for a higher price. Then you pick sauce, veggies, and toppings. Then you watch your beauty be wrapped into a burrito.   I ordered a burrito with salmon and tempura shrimp (I highly recommend both). I then asked about the sauce and was recommended the spicy sauce since I am a heat fiend. I added cucumber, carrots, and jalapenos. I then added sesame seeds and little crunch wontons for texture.   Some of the things that you could also add that I remember were tuna, corn, cilantro, red onion, avocado (upcharge), asparagus (upcharge). There were a lot of other choices too, but that is what I can recall.    I grabbed a drink, a container of soy and lots of napkins (note: grab extra! Burritos are a little messy if you are like me and dip in soy).  The first bite was magical. It was super fresh, delicious, salty and crunchy all at once. Each subsequent bite just reinforced my joy. I polished it off and left very happy. I will definitely return soon. It is a little pricey, but I think that it is worth it. You have to try a sushi burrito if you are a sushi fan. You will love it!

Came with my sister after a day spent at San Xavier Mission. We were greeted as we walked in the door, our waitress was very sweet and friendly. My sister says "the soup was so good and I like how they put the water pitcher on your table". I had two tacos and I would get them again. Very good.
Pick your flavor, pick your toppings and watch your ice cream be made in front of you. $6.50 one size, unlimited toppings, great taste! We will definitely be back to try more combinations.

I enjoy Ava for brunch and a large party/reservation. The food is prepared for softer palettes with not as much seasoning, but overall a fresh taste. The staff is friendly, but can be easily overwhelmed when there is a lot of guests. The drinks are my favorite.

I know there are so many fancy local places to get a good burger and I'm cool with all that, but my favorite burger in the city is still at Five Guys. I get my junior cheeseburger "all the way" and share a medium fry with my husband and feel like I've eaten enough food for days. Love the flavor, love the casual atmosphere, and love having such a basic, non-boring amazing burger. Sometimes simple is the way to go!
Dips are AMAZING as are the beef fajitas. I've tried the Mexican pizza, chicken flautas, and chicken chimichanga to my liking, as well. Service can range from average to superb - never bad.
Food is great and service is better. Good daily specials and plenty of tv's to watch any sporting event.

This is a gorgeous place to drive through, hike or picnic BUT shame on you park service for charging $15 for a day (it's actually a week) pass but really. We live local and like to take visitors but paying $15 for a day is ridiculous. I mean come on-- most visitors drive through and even if you offered a $10 day pass and $15 wrk pass but $15 for a day is too much!  Recently I was at a charity luncheon and a group of people (locals) started talking about this-- at LEAST offer a day pass for locals with a Tucson drivers license!!!

Literally the worst excuse for sushi I've ever eaten in my life.  First, the owners are not Japanese, and know next to nothing about Japanese food. If you are going to call yourself a Japanese restaurant, at least take cooking lessons or something.  Second, the rice in the numerous rolls we ordered was overcooked goo, and not vinegared. The low quality soy sauce that came with the sushi was mixed with water - utterly vile.  We also ordered tempura, which was basically an oily, greasy mess - basically deep fried assorted vegetables.   We ended up throwing everything away and cooking our own dinner. It really was that disgusting.

What a bargain! Just about 10 bucks a person for a three course lunch. Food was good and filling. Service was great. If you're ever in Phoenixville looking for a cheap meal, Thai Place is definitely worth a stop!

I have gone here twice with friends and sat by the var. Their fries and crab cakes are wonderful!! Great place to stop for cocktails. The bartenders are engaging. A must do if you are in the city. Parking garage suggested. Enjoy;;;

Love it!  Really, really loved it!!  Eclectic craft beer, coffee and eats in a converted gas station located in Seminole Heights.  The inside is rustic with warm wood tones. A garage door is rolled up to make inside connected and open to the outside.  Outside patio seating is nice and a portion is covered.    It should feel busy right up against Florida Avenue, but somehow the layout makes you feel apart from the traffic and a part of an experience.   It does get busy after work and tables fill up but there is turn over and everyone is having a great, relaxed time. Order drinks and food at the bar and they will bring food out to your table when it is ready.  Really enjoyed everything about it!!!
Awesome food. Note, a roast beef a combo means w cheese not w fries. Mmmm!

Granted we know it's on E Washington, I thought it was going to be something akin to flapjacks, however flapjacks is actually ok.   Wife and I ordered the skillets, I also got a side of biscuits and gravy, came with toast and pancakes. Hasbrowns underneath were not cooked through, corn beef hash was hardly noticeable, biscuits and gravy were already solidified. They didn't even split them. Waitress asked "sausage gravy?" I said yes and found no sausage. Took a few bites, biscuits were dry. Pancakes were flat, tasteless, took a few bites of those as well.   If you want subpar industrial food or have no taste buds knock yourself out. Waitress sensed something was wrong, you just smile pay the check then yelp. Service was fine. Not recommended.

We ate at Jones again today, and I have to say,"No, No, NO, Jones!!!!!!!!!!  You do NOT serve Chicken and Waffles with a bland CHICKEN GRAVY!!!" IT does NOTHING to bring out the flavor of the chicken OR waffles. Terrible gravy. Luckily, the chicken didn't need it, and we ate the waffles with syrup.  While we're on the subject of waffles, what was up with the HALF of Belgian waffle you guys served us?  I have never been given a HALF of waffle at a restaurant before. Surely you guys can splurge a little and give us a WHOLE waffle.

I found this place on Yelp when I searched for "brunch on the beach" and it was a decent find. We walked into a completely empty restaurant around noon on a Sunday and that can be alarming. On a Sunday by the beach, it was actually nice that we were able to sit down right away given most of these brunch restaurants are packed around this time. With an open mind, we sat down and found that the breakfast menu was pretty large and it all sounded appetizing. The restaurant itself is brightly colored, clean, and has some coastal decor scattered about the place. The restaurant must be owned by people from England because their was an "English specialties" section which I thought was pretty cool and something out of the ordinary. I ordered the corned beef hash with extra crispy home fries and it was definitely a good choice. The corned beef was fresh and not out of a can and the home fries were cooked to a perfect crisp. My only complaint would be that in a restaurant as slow as this the food should come out much faster. We were kept waiting for about 45-50 minutes for 4 seemingly quick meals. Luckily my "double" mimosa kept me content while we waited. I would have been a little annoyed if I was kept waiting for bad food but it honestly was delicious and definitely hit the spot. Our bill was only $26 too! I'm glad this place is an option when everywhere else is crowed!
Oh my God I love this place!!!!!!!!! I just hate that its soooo far away from home..Great place to people watch and have great ice cream..
Super cute southern vibes. The crystal chandeliers are my favorite! Try the smoked pork sandwich and an Italian mule. Cheers!

We live so close by its incredible. For 2 years we have driven by this place sometimes several times in a day and never went in till today. We had the carnival and a burrito. Food was fast, service friendly and the bill was 20 with tax. Salsa bar nice .  We will be walking over from now on. Nice little neighborhood cafe

Sooo... this place is awesome. You order at counter from limited yet exquisite comfort food. Chicken, fried catfish, ribs, roast beef and a special ofnthe day. Great sides for you meat n 3. I had the catfish with some corn bread, special (Adam Wainright) heirloom tomatoes and I got the soup of the day (smoky tomato). Everything was to die for awesome. Sometimes there is a line but it moves fast or you can eat at the bar. Owner is on sight and responsive. Eat Here Now!

Ate here for a friend's birthday and was not disappointed. I'm half Italian so I have pretty high standards when it comes to Italian food, but Ava was up to the challenge. I ordered a penne dish with sausage which was quite good, though I wish it came with a bit more sausage.  The service was very good as well. We were constantly attended to and both our appetizers and main courses came out fairly quickly. Our server didn't let me go thirsty either as she was there to get me another Maduro as I finished my previous one.  I've also been here for happy hour and that is definitely something I need to do again. They have drink specials though they admittedly aren't as good as some other happy hours. However, the star here are there $5 pizzas. They aren't huge or anything, but a decent size for the price. We tried 3 of them and they were all fantastic!
5.40 for 4oz bean dip. (few tablespoons) I think not. Won't ever go back!!!!I  Friends and family were right.

Excellent ambience and hang out spot!! Great place to catch some college football! Really enjoyed they're cheese platter. Excellent selections and for a no pork eater, I was super pleased they include only cheeses!! Didn't have to miss out. The toast was on point, and don't get me started on the candied nuts. Yum! Had a perfect Fall flair. Have it 4 stars because they beer was good, but nothing really stood out. Our favorite, by far, was the 1927 IPA. Really delicious, citrusy, dank flavor. I'm normally the imperial fan, but wasn't too impressed. Too boozy over malty. Live music was just a plus. Great place to hang for a little 30th celebration. Would come back, and just might, just wishing we were more in love with the brews!

We just recently had our Company Holiday party at the Peppermill in the Tuscany Ballroom, and I am quite impressed with the accommodations this fine establishment has to offer. Excellent venue for parties and events. The Food was very tasty, especially the cheeseballs. The chocolate fountain was a magical touch as well. The only thing I can say about the Tuscany Ballroom is that it was HUGE. Lots of space in there, and for the amount of people we had, it was hard to walk around and see everyone. This was definitely not the Peppermill's fault though. On the way up to the Ballroom, I got to check out the casino floor, and everything seemed very well run and well kept. Beautiful Hotel, and I can't wait to go back to check out the rooms next time.
Amazing bar food!! Everything I have tried here is delicious!! The meatloaf is a must have, the mussels and the ahi tuna salad also!!

Bueno Onda tacos are hands down my favorite tacos in Philly. I always get the glazed shrimp and glazed fish tacos with a side of chips and guac. The tacos are so fresh and flavorful, you can't go wrong with any from their wide selection. The guacamole has cheese on top, which is not necessarily my favorite, but it still tastes delicious. The nachos are topped with cheese, tomatoes, black beans, and all other goodies so I would highly recommend those as well. Although I haven't been here for happy hour, I have heard from friends that they have $3 sangria and margaritas. I look forward to coming here for happy hour in the future and will keep on raving about these tacos!

Ordered a breakfast smoothie here.  Got the Amazonian whey(?) -  came with banana, agave, cocoa, and whey.  For 7 bucks a smoothie, it's pretty damn pricey.  I think the other breakfast options (an actual meal) are a better value.
Great decor, great service, hot hot fries and hot sandwich.  I highly recommend this place . Bring your appetite though, the portions are huge!!!

Jasmine Thai has portion control sized dishes. In other words, they serve you how much you should really eat in a meal.Most Thai restaurants usually serve bigger sized dishes.This is not a bad thing, just wished I had a little more.It took awhile for someone to get our order but once our order was in, the food came out in 5 minutes.  Their menu is not very big.Unfortunately they did not have any "kid friendly" dishes so our little one did not eat until after we left.I don't expect them to have mac 'n' cheese obviously, but you could easily fry some chicken pieces/strips in tempura or something.  The bathrooms are clean and the hand carved table tops at the booths were beautiful. Our waitress was super nice.

Spent a nice evening there with friends. The white sangria was goos and so was the (very small) portion of pea tortellini pasta. The appetizers we had, the crispy artichoke and the charcoal octopus were good but way tooooo salty. Same with the pizzas. The margarita was better than the burrata, but both we too salty.

This restaurant is small, quaint and has a calm indoor outdoor dining experience. Great for lunch, and we order delivery at least once a week. They have NEVER disappointed us. :).   Hey have an extensive menu. The staff are genuine and down to earth. This place is great for those who are looking for no frills. Easy going, quick bites.   This restaurant works like a well oiled machine.  Autonomous and natural felling to the place.

Chipotle gets a big "meh" from me.  I admit I might be hard on them because of their reputation for being amazing, but I just find it to be okay.  And yes, I do get to pick all of my ingredients, but I've been 3-4 times and ordered different items and combinations each time.  I just don't find the flavors to be very inspired.  There are other fast Mexican/build your own burrito/bowl places in town that I find to be much better, and will way more variety to choose from.  The one thing I really appreciate at Chipotle is that they offer "sofritas" which is a vegetarian option.  I'm not a vegetarian, but I appreciate having the option to go meat-free!

I heard rave reviews of this place being the "best Thai in Nashville" so I had to try it out. It is a cool vibe- an old house that has been turned into a restaurant- thus, the parking is very limited, but there is a driveway that wraps around which helps. They don't serve beer, so I was slightly disappointed, but the food itself is standard and the service was good. They are always busy, so we had to wait in the crowded entry-way, and then were seated right next to the kitchen door so we were "in the action" as you could say. It was a typical Thai place, but not the best I've had.   Note: my standards are high coming from LA, so please take this review with a grain of salt.
Cool little restaurant with a lot of healthy options!  I tried the Recoverii smoothie,it was good but not worth the price.. $6.50 for a small cup

OMG!!!! I hate to leave such a good review for such a small restaurant  (12 tables), but they deserve  all the credit! First up,  the Caesar  Salad....good, but just ok. Next course, the canneloni. ..I was used to the one at Vincent's with the cream sauce....this one was with red sauce, but the sauce  was so good....it was delicious!. Next course was a pizza quite possibly one of the best we have ever had. The people at the next table had pasta dishes that looked absolutely  delicious. This will garner a revisit for sure. The server was very nice and efficient.

Mm....yummy food. The Shrimp Fundido omelette is delicious.  Nothing needs to be added and it's still flavorful.  My husband had the Maggie's Special and the pancakes were pretty good, too.   I love Red Mesa and have been there for lunch and dinner a bazillion times but this is my first time there for brunch and I enjoyed it. Definitely will coming back and more often.
Favorite greek place in Tarpon Springs.  Love the fried cheese and the Gyro's.

First: This place is a solid place to get some work done. Big tables allow you to spread out and the internet connection is good despite the place being full. I also like how spacious it is.   Second: The service is good...when you're there. There was one Saturday morning when I called hoping to just order a grab and go before a morning tee time and the girl who picked up said they don't do takeout orders on the weekends. Before I could ask if she meant over the phone or at all (walking there and ordering was still an option for me), she had already hung up. Didn't even say bye so I kind of just kept talking until the line went dead (oops).   Third: The food is serviceable, I guess. I don't think most people go here for dinner plans but eat when they're hungry in the middle of getting some work done. For that reason, it's a solid food place. I typically have the bagel and lox, though, hard to screw that up. The fiance had a salad with chicken and it was below average, if not poor and the food did take awhile. Two or three parties who ordered after us got their food before us.  Points for: Good place to get work done: big tables, a lot of room to work. Coffee No points: Salads

Car shopping is supposed to be fun & exciting. Car dealerships are getting increasingly hard to write. Why does every car salesman think & act like women in general are stupid. They tried to lead me around by the hand & show me practical cars. I am not a soccer mom ( not that there is anything wrong with soccer moms). I want a fun exciting car & buying it needs to be the same, not gonna happen here. After 3 dealership bad experiences, I am taking a break. I love my car, maybe I need to just keep it. What's a girl to do? maybe stay away from car dealerships?

I remember when I first discovered Flying Cupcake.  It was the highlight of my day if I checked facebook and saw that cookie dough was a flavor of the day.  That is until the day I met the chocolate ganache cupcake.  Sadly, that was about 6 years ago and things have changed.  Prices have gone up (inflation, I get it), but quality has gone down.  My most recent encounter was with the salted caramel chocolate, which sounded like a match made in heaven. Sadly, it was not.  The frosting had a weird sticky consistency, and the cake was dry, until you got to the middle which was filled with caramel.  This experience isn't very different from others I've had, but the number of locations and their food truck is why I keep trying... They're everywhere!

I'm going to start with OMG! The food was amazing. I gave the 5 stars but the service is about 3.5. They are a little slow coming to the table to check you out but Elias really good about getting the orders in and getting the food out. I will be visiting this spot in the future. I really don't know what else to add.

This is a very nice place to get breakfast or lunch. The atmosphere is great, very relaxed. The food is good too. I usually get the tuna salad sandwich and chips and I'm always very happy with my choice. My boyfriend has gotten the breakfast before, which I tried, and it is also very good. I would recommend stoping by for lunch!

Sushi Bar never disappoints! The flavor of the food here is consistent. The Jalapeño Roll is my all time favorite. Not many rolls have a good combo of flavor and are actually spicy. The jalapeño sauce on top makes it. Other rolls here are great too just have my favorite there.

Have you tried Chick-Fil-A breakfast?  If you haven't you should go today!  I love this place for breakfast.  A chicken biscuit sounds a little odd at first but trust me they are great!  The employees are always very nice a chick Fill A too.

Good burgers, great fries.  You cant go wrong with a burger here and Ive had several types.  No, its not a gourmet restaraunt burger but it is very tasty.  The only issue, at times, is the cashier service.  They can be standoffish, not very warm.  They need to do some customer service training, scripting, etc.  Other than that, everything is great.

I've been here on multiple occasions and each time my experience has been a hit or miss. I've had the seafood pan fried noodles which were drowning in watery sauce and the seafood was not that fresh. The shrimp are soo small and seems like they are days old. I've had the Prawn Mee noodle soup again the shrimp seemed old and there was nothing Prawnish about the shrimp at all as they were soo small. The slices of pork were extremely tough and the broth was very very fishy. Even the bean sprouts and vegetables seemed old. The stir fried rice noodle with salted fish was just ok. The fried rice with salted fish was pretty good. The Roti Cani is very good as well. I've also tried the fried intestine and oyster pancake both of which were good. The service was always great as the waiter always makes sure to tell us what to expect in the dish so that part I love. So all in all I feel like this place can be so much better if only the seafood and veggies were fresh.

Love love this place! Food is exactly prepared as the Original location on Christian Street in S Phila. The Mel's Chicken Cutlet is my favorite and the Greek Salad is next!!! We always introduce our friends to this place bc we know it will b delicious!!!

This place is OK- 3.5 stars. We had a large group for brunch and the staff seemed really confused about seating us. We eventually ended up just taking over the restaurant (it was like 12 of us). We actually had a good 'ol time but that was partly because we had such a large group.   The food was just OK- maybe 3 stars. It might have been better if the place wasn't so crowded. If we were in the neighborhood we might come back.

It's a small, very casual restaurant. The staff is friendly. The food is decent. Everyone works together to make sure your cup never goes empty. The seafood omelette is really good. The grits weren't creamy and the hash browns didn't have flavor. I loved how the biscuits are split in half, and the inside is toasted for that extra crunch in every bite. Yet, the biscuit still melts in your mouth.
Always great service when we come! Never let's us down! The food is great! We love the Pizzokie!

This bakery makes the best cookies I've ever had.  My favorite, which I highly recommend, are the chocolate fudge dipped in white chocolate - but they sell out quickly, so go early.  They also make a great Mexican wedding cookie.  The staff is super nice and makes everything on the premises.  They also make a lot of various cakes, but I've never tried any of them - although I would not hesitate to.  Great spot!

Fresh food, artfully prepared . Really good for what amounts to an upscale fast food establishment. You order at the counter and it is delivered to your table or booth. Really hip decor and vibe. The LImeade was good as a beverage. Friendly Service.
$25 medium pizza that took 35 minutes to get to the table? Sure why not. It was fucking delicious. Piping hot and garlicky!
Nice little bar with good food. The bartenders are friendly and the atmosphere is great.

Fantastic, great tasting dishes! The stewed goat and roast pork were better than delicious. The chicken soup was exceptional, except it was served luke warm - once they made it hot it was great. Rice and beans, plantains great too. Look forward to going back. Nice and pleasant ownership and servers too.

Went here the other night with my buddies from Guys Night Out.  Everyone agreed the service, food, and ambiance were excellent.  Happy Hour, and I've been here several times for that, does get a bit loud as the evening moves along.  Our supper group dined at 6:00 on a Wednesday and noise was not a problem.    Another Excellent Happy Hour here this past Friday.  Great prices on delicious food and drinks.  What more could you ask for?

The sandwiches are minuscule and have a ton of bread compared to sandwich ingredients! I had a chicken, zucchini (who puts zucchini on a sandwich anyhow). Anyhow, I ordered ir and this was a major disappointment. There was probably a couple tablespoons of chicken on 1/2 of what I ate. My husband had procuitto and hi was pretty much the same.    So I'd recommend ordering cafe and croissants  here.

Meun Fan is nice because of its Goleta location. The food is average, but their major downside is their pricing. Highly overpriced for the portions and the Thai food market in general. Facility is clean and the staff is friendly.

The BEST! Great after Cards & Blues games. I've been here many times. Always friendly fast service no matter how long the lines. I recommend the Dutchman concrete! A MUST visit if you're in St. Louis.

Really cute, cozy place. The chocolate gelato was good and it was nice enough to sit on the back patio. The gal at the counter wasn't exactly friendly, which was awkward. I appreciate the bin for the plastic cups and spoons to recycle them!

Tucked in a cozy spot on Watson, Yolklore is the type of restaurant I wouldn't expect in Crestwood. Normally a place like this would be downtown or in Clayton. Curious to find a place like this close by my husband and I was excited to try it out.  The space and decor is like most breakfast places: tight and cozy. The kitchen is open faced and there are coffee mugs, the menu, and a listing of the specials to greet you right at the door.   I ordered the Bread Pudding French Toast and my husband ordered a basic breakfast plate. The french toast was good, the eggs my husband had were extraordinary and I really liked the direction they took with the potatoes, using whole yukon gold potatoes instead of cubed potatoes.   I had a smoothie and he had the orange juice. I liked the smoothie but felt it was missing something.   I noticed the manager touching tables as I ate, checking to see how everyone was enjoying their meal.  I enjoyed eating here, however I felt it was on the pricey side ($28 for two people) for breakfast.

Maybe great for breakfast, but lunch was a disappointment for me. Service was very nice. Friendly professionals working well together. My chicken gyro was almost completely unseasoned breast with cartilage included. Tzatzki was also bland. Other's in the party had soups, both of them enjoyed it. Price was okay. I'm sure there are better items on the menu.
