Don't get me wrong, the tacos tastes great, but so does a double cheese burger from Mcdonalds. It's definitely a great place to come for a quick meal. They are always packed but everything seems to move pretty fast which is a plus. Friendly staff and okay atmosphere, I'd stop by again.

We're in town visiting and found The Silverspoon through Yelp! It's a very charming restaurant. We came for brunch and we were hesitant because of the limited menu choices. We ordered the chicken and waffles, the banana bread French toast, waffles (with chocolate and banana special request), a salad and fruit platter. All the food was delicious. The French toast and the waffles were truly outstanding. If you are a local, there's no excuse of not trying it!

I don't really have great things to say about Ants Pants, but I also don't have many bad things to say either.  It's just...forgettable.    First, it's cash only, as are most places in this area.  The wait for brunch on the weekends is insane and in my opinion, not worth it.  You can probably make many of their menu items at home, and save yourself some money too.  While the decor is cute and my mom loved the granola and yogurt (which is near $7...highway robbery!), I'd suggest walking down the street to Honey's or Resurrection.  You might have to wait a bit longer, but it will be worth it.

Midtown is the nicest liquor store in the area, in my opinion. When you walk in, classical music is playing and they just have that high end feel while browsing. They also know what the hell they are talking about people!  They have a fabulous liquor and wine selection, and are eager to offer a suggestion or opinion on what they carry. Complaints... they aren't the most cost efficient place in town (though not crazily so, just noticeable) and since times have been a bit tighter financially for me lately, I feel like a bit of a bum shopping here for my box o' wine or cheap selections.   You know what I mean. I actually wanted a boxed wine recommendation (I prefer bottles but you've gotta do what you gotta do) but I noticed I wasn't approached once when I was in that area, whereas other sections will get you some customer service. Oh well, I guess it was less embarrassing to just grab my box and get the heck out of there before I was judged too harshly.   But I do love shopping here, especially if I can afford something of the nicer variety!

Holy cow. This place is off the hook.   Everything, and I mean everything, that I tried here is delicious. I knew that it would be good food based on other Yelp reviews as well as local word of mouth, but frankly I was amazed by the quality.   I had the unsweetened mixed berry iced tea and could not ask for a more perfect house brewed tea. It was slightly sweet due to being infused with berries, but this was perfectly fine, and the flavors were on point. My group started with the tostones, fried mashed plantains, and they were very tasty and plentiful. One plate was enough to feed a table of four, and it came with their house made guacamole. Their guacamole has a little bit of spice to it, but nothing unbearable.   Feeling drawn to the Buddha tacos I ordered them and wondered if they would be any good. Now I find it unbelievable that some of the best tacos I've ever had come from a vegetarian joint. I got them served on the blue corn Johnny cakes, and liked that they had crisp edges with a more cake-like interior. The filing my consisted primarily of sweet potatoes, avocado and peanuts. Don't let that deter you, it was a combination that was beyond delicious. I would never think to mix them but it worked! Add a little bit of lime on there and it's even better. These also come with the signature guacamole.   I also got to sample some of my dining partners' foods, all of which were equally tasty. The ginger ramen is done well, with a nice little kick to the broth. It's true to typical Japanese style ramen while being its own unique recipe. The falafel has an interesting grainy texture (as opposed to a smooth, doughy one) but it works. Last but not least is the Velvet Verde, comprised mainly of spaghetti squash. It had a lot of particular flavors going on but it all pulled together to make a tasty dish.   I can't wait to come back here! Now I feel like I should try everything on the menu since every dish we got was outstanding. Even if you are not a vegetarian I would still suggest trying this place just once. The food is so good you will not miss the meat.

This is quite a niche place. Lots of vegan and gluten free stuff. I've been here multiple times but it seems like they don't change their menu often. I tend to go for vegetarian options so I've had their curried cauliflower a lot. We always tend to order the same options - chicken satay, grilled peppers, and the wings. They give so many peppers it's insane. I don't think we have ever finished a whole bowl between 3 folks. I've gotten their lettuce wraps twice but the second time felt like I ordered a completely different dish. It was like a waldorf chicken salad, when I expected it to be savory. That's the only reason I'm giving it 3 stars.   Also, one time our waitress said she was going to bring some popcorn for us but she never came back with popcorn :(

One of my favorite stores for home goods. Love this location the most as it used to be the iconic Urban Outfitters years ago. Got to pick up a few gifts for some friends and some stuff for myself. Love the product and the service. They wrapped up all my gifts which gives me extra time to enjoy the holidays and less time wrapping.

My First spot and favorite place to go for drinks before I start my night life.  Must try their St. Germaine drink! Its the best!.  If you plan to go for dinner, try to go early before 8pm maybe. Their food are great!!.  But after 8, Tables are empty with many 'reserve' signs on it nor can they take any orders because their kitchen will be closed. After 8pm I usually sat at their bar or their open window for my drinks so its not so bad.

So I am not a real fan of Japanese Steakhouses such as these because I am not a fan of pasta and rice; however, I went to try this one. Now I haven't been to a restaurant like this in over 10 years, so I don't quite remember the set up etc. This was my first time going to a Kobe so I thought this would be different than what I did remember from my last visit to Benihana's. Ok, let's get into the review.   First Impressions/Appearance:  So when you walk in, the restaurant is very dry and doesn't give you anything to awe at. There is no real pop of red or anything to make this restaurant stand out from any other typical restaurant. The tables are very dark in color, which I typically don't mind but again it was very dry and dull. They seat you at 10 to a table which again I didn't mind, but it is very close and a bit uncomfortable when you're trying to enjoy dinner.   Customer Service: I am big on customer service and theirs was on point. Their customer service was great. We were seated immediately and the wait staff was great and attentive. Our drinks were filled and they cleared our soup and salad bowls in time so we had more space at the small tables. At the end of our meal, there was another lady that came out to box up our left overs which she did in front of us so that gets an extra star from me.   The Dinner Show:  our chef was very entertaining. He did all of the tricks, the 'egg roll', flipping the egg in his hat and flipping the shrimp tail in his shirt pocket. That was cool and so was the volcano and making the fire shoot out and up. He cracked jokes and made fun of all the latest commercials playing on the radio. He cooked the vegetables with such precision and made the noodles and rice with so much action. I don't remember noodles and rice being that entertaining, but he made them a real show. His knife techniques were astonishing and the cut of the meat and chicken all the same size.   The Food:  the soup was ok and full of flavor. It was served hot which was good on a cold night. The salad was fresh and the ginger dressing was delicious, I really could have used more salad. The vegetables were cooked first. We had onions, zucchini, carrots, broccoli, and green beans. I think they were undercooked but I think most people like their veggies a bit more crispy. The onions were very pungent and could have been cooked a little longer along with the carrots and beans. Next came the noodles. Now as o stated earlier, I am not a pasta fan but I do cook it for my family. These noodles were under seasoned and overlooked. They could have used some salt and pepper and should have been a little less than al dente. That way, it can finish cooking on the grill. The rice was next, and it was not cooked, so when the chef went to fry it, it didn't steam enough, so we ended up with hard rice that was greasy and had no flavor. Lastly were the meats. The meat had good flavor and it was cooked well. I had the chicken and steak and both were prepared how I liked. The steak also came with potatoes, which surprisingly were pretty good.   Rating: So why 3 stars? Simply put, the food could have been better and the establishment could have been nicer. I can spend the same amount and maybe a few dollars more on more food and much better food. I know the idea is a dinner and a show, but I think they need to work on their food more since they have the show down. I don't think I will be going back but glad I decided to give it a try since I have never been and now I have a platform to let others know what to expect.

This place is two for two with me!  I went once for lunch and had their specialty Farmhouse Burger - a burger with an egg on top, and their specialty mustard on the side. It was a surprisingly excellent burger. Very juicy and full of flavor. Service on this particular visit was extremely slow, and with all the televisions showing college football plus the music blaring, the place was pretty loud.  My second visit I met a friend for happy hour.  Much lower volume level this visit, and the server was much more efficient.  Great deals for happy hour - I went with the lemon drop martini, on special for $6.  All the appetizers are discounted for happy hour also, so my friend and I ordered a smorgasbord of items.    Duck wings are mentioned in every review, and they were great - nice and crispy, with a bit of a bite from the sauce. I also loved the drunken meatballs - they too were in a spicy sauce, and cooked perfectly. The big surprise of the night was the Kobe Brick Pizza - thin crust, loaded with wagyu beef, cheese, caramelized onions, roasted tomatoes, bacon and arugula, it had a fabulous flavor - I would definitely order this again.   The only appetizer I didn't love was their version of the Seven Layer Dip: white bean purée, harissa, yogurt, oven roasted tomatoes, smashed avocados, smoked cheddar & chopped olives served with grilled naan.  Even with all those ingredients, it just did not have much flavor.  It was still good, but not great.  I will definitely be back to explore their drink and food menus further, and to take advantage of their excellent happy hour.

Stopped by for dinner.  Great place, good vibes, clean with great service with reasonable prices (for Santa Barbara).  Tried the Cobb salad and the rainbow trout.  Both were excellent!  The cob salad was chopped, so I had all the delicious fresh ingredients in every bite.  Nice crispy bacon!! Yum!!  Even if your not a salad water, this dish will not disappoint.  Trout was very tasty.  Grilled, perfectly deboned, moist and flavorful.

It's been family run since 1989.  That's a lot of time to perfect breakfast food.  This is no frills or fluffyness breakfasts.  It's down home pancakes, waffles, omelets and the almighty... Dutch Baby!  This thing alone is what makes this spot where I go for breakfasts.  It takes some time to cook; so grab an appetizer of breakfast potatoes or an english muffin.  Both are great.

We have been there twice and will not return. The Good: both times service was great. Surprising their sides are really good. The Bad: tiny portions, very dry meat, over salty ribs and all of their sauces are super watery. Not a good sign for BBQ place without great meat and sauces. If they guys want to survive they need to change their portions, address the super dry meat, over salted ribs and come up with better sauces.

First time here in a long time. Thought we'd give it a shot. Server was pleasant but service was slow. I had the meatball parmigiana with pasta in white sauce. My plate was by far the best thing at the table. My wife had the roast beef sandwich with mash potatoes. It was ok but a bland and not much to talk about. My daughters kids cheeseburger meal with fries was good she said. Overall with the service and food we got gave us the agreed upon rating above.
The capirinhas are soooo good. My fave cocktail! Especially passion fruit. :) The chicharrones rock, and I've never had bad food here.
the food and the service are outstanding, the best smoked fish ive ever eaten and huge portions

This is one of my favorites in Armature Works! The sangria, the Cuban, and the guava pastry empanada is AMAZING! Probably my favorite in all of Tampa! Not too mention, they just won best Cuban Sandwich at the Cuban Sandwich Festival! Hemingway's is a fantastic addition to Armature! It won't disappoint guys! This place is delicious! #byefelicia
Second time I've been here and they're closed during business their stated business hours.  Going to have to find a new place for consistency.

Had a great experience here in this lovely and clean salon today. Someone recommended it to me and I'm so glad they did. I had a great pedicure with a massage and the person who did it was excellent and also a great resource for restaurants in NOLA.  The owners couldn't have been nicer and I enjoyed talking with them about us both having a French Bulldog as a pet!  I would highly recommend this place!

My husband and I have wanted to try this place since we heard about it opening! We LOVE St. Elmo's and Harry & Izzy's. And I would say I was underwhelmed by Burger Study. Don't get me wrong, the food was good. But it wasn't outstanding like I find the food at their other restaurants. We started with the beer cheese tot appetizer which was my favorite part of the meal. Our friend got the BLT salad which was also very good. We all ordered the homecoming burger which was okay. Also, the fries are not very good. I would definitely get the tater tots. We will be back but I don't think this will be a regular for us!

I recently got to try this place out for their weekend Brunch.  The day we were here was their inaugural Stereo Sunday Brunch, which had a dj spinning live music as everyone partook in their usual weekend brunch activities.  The overall ambiance and crowd was pretty decent and the food offerings were not bad either.  I indulged in a Northern Bird sandwich, which was a grilled chicken breast topped with a pesto type sauce and cheese on a bun with lettuce, tomato, and onion.  I added bacon to mine to make it one of a kind.  The fries that accompanied my sandwich were cooked and seasoned perfectly.  My only gripe with the whole experience was that our server was a bit sparse and I'm not sure if it was due to the crowd of people filling the place or if she was just slow in her duties.  She asked if I needed a refill on my drink at the end of the meal after she had already cleared our groups plates.  One more thing is that the parking situation should be figured out beforehand, the owner/manager asked me to wait as we were leaving when I asked if they validate parking as they had run out of validation slips and had to send someone to Skyhouse to get more.  Overall, a great experience and I'll be back when back down in the Channelside area.

I'll cut to the chase: In Riva may be one of the best restaurants in Philly that you've never heard of. I must have gone past it tens of times while driving down Kelly Drive, but I never thought to look up at the kind of nondescript, set-back building and realize it was actually a restaurant. We only tried In Riva after my brother-in-law raved about it, so we thought we'd give it a try with another couple. It was one of the best dinners out we had a long time.  In Riva's website says it specializes in Neopolitan-style wood-fired pizza and antipasti, which isn't entirely accurate because all their courses are equally delicious. I can't remember exactly what we ordered but it included some salumi and formaggi, pea soup, pork ribs, charred octopus, a trio of pastas and a mushroom and zucchini pizza. Everything was delectable. My favorite was the agnolotti with pea, shallot and truffle. It was the best pasta I've had in Philly (my favorite pasta is at L'Artusi in NY), though I hear Vetri's pasta is amazing.   What makes In Riva so wonderful is that it is so unassuming. It has a casual, dark wood, industrial-feel interior that doesn't ring of pretense, yet the food rivals any of the best-known, heralded nouveau cuisine restaurants in Philly. In fact, the two meals I've had at In Riva have knocked the socks off the past two meals I had at Amada, which used to be my favorite Philly restaurant.  I just noticed Andrew B's negative review and understand that he thinks In Riva may be overpriced. I think because In Riva looks so casual, one would expect the price point to also be casual. I agree the pasta serving size could be bigger. I paid $11 for the agnolotti dish that I ate in a few bites and could have easily downed another order. But, truth be told, we shouldn't eat that much. The price reflects the quality, not quantity, of food.  Philadelphia Magazine recently published the top 50 restaurants in Philly issue, and I eagerly looked for In Riva's name to be listed. Not this year, but hopefully next. It's not to be missed.

Their lunch special really is a good value. For $10 today I was served a very nice salad, fried fish, rice & beans, hush puppies and and ice tea. I've easily spent more than that any other day of the week for a worse lunch.   I'm not sure... but I think if you come at lunch, you eat the lunch plate... you don't get a choice of anything else. Or at least the server didn't offer anything else.   The space has been redone but is still a little dilapidated. The ceiling was all warped. I don't know why this bothered me but I couldn't stop looking at it. And our table was wobbly. I hate that. Freaking annoying.   The food was good... I really liked the salad. The fish was average. Nothing special. The hush puppies tasted homemade... like something grandma would have cooked. Beans and rice were good. I liked that the beans had big chunks of ham in it. The tea was weird. It tasted like it had tobacco in it. Tea shouldn't make me feel like I need to brush my teeth.   I'm glad I visited. Huge props for good, well prepared food at $10. And I was in and out at lunchtime within 40 minutes.  I'll be back to try other items.

We discovered this Laotian restaurant through yelp while visiting our family so we decided to give it a try. The look of the restaurant from outside was deceiving where we almost left after we arrived because of the location, parking situation, and my fear of not being used to the type of surroundings. When we walked in we were surprised by how warm, cozy, clean, filled with customers, and we were greeted and seated promptly. It didn't take long for us to get our food and the food was very $reasonable. We ordered the sticky rice, chicken larb, papaya salad, beef jerky, stick rice mango dessert and all we delicious. We enjoyed all but probably will not order the sticky rice mango dessert again for $8 although we didn't mind paying for it considering the cost of how much it would have taken for them to make it but it was just a little too watering for us. We would definitely do it again next time we visit Philadelphia since we don't have any Laotian restaurants in Tampa area. I would highly recommend this place for anyone who likes to try authentic Laotian food that cannot be found from Thai restaurants. This was the first Laotian restaurant we have ever been to and we truly enjoyed the Laotian unique food experience!  p.s BYOB is a plus..

I actually thought the food was good. The grouper sandwich came on a pretzel roll. It was a really cool combination of flavors.  The portions were small - which I like so I don't over eat. But I did think it was over priced for the amount of food you got.  The service was good - and they have a great drink selection.

All I can say is.......wow! I came here because my friend told me how good the tacos were and I finally dived in and gave it a try. We pulled up and I was shocked of how small the building was. I am always looking for some type of seating or outdoor seating but this is a grab a taco and leave type of establishment.   We walked in and immediately I knew 2 people in there lol. I was thinking oh this food must be good!!! I ordered the chicken, shrimp, and steak taco and when I say it was good... honey..... it was good lol! The tacos were seasoned well and the meat was tender. It was just perfect!   Be careful with the sauces they throw in the bag. It is super spicy and I am not a fan of that. I love the limes and cucumbers that they give us with the tacos too. Very fresh and yummy!   The quesadilla was EXCELLENT! Def will be back.

Taco Tuesdays is my jam! The line type serving is a lot o like chipotle, but the tacos are better. I believe I got the tacos with all of the dressings on top. Highly recommend if you want to get a quick bite to eat downtown. There isn't a lot of seating when it gets super busy, however if you come at a non peak time you won't have any issues.   Unlike a few other taco shops you can take a handful of chips from a heated machine, which is a nice touch. Choose your own salsa and refill your own drinks. You can also get beer here which is a nice touch when you're having a quick taco any day of the week.   The atmosphere is pretty much expected for a taco place, and the price is great for what you get. I've only been here once, but recommend if you're in the area and need a quick bite to eat.

I stopped in here after reading multiple reviews about how great it was. My dad was in the mood for chicken and I remembered this place, so I headed over.  I was here on a Sunday afternoon and the place was packed. I'm talking line out the door, people inside waiting on their order packed. I took this as a good sign, so I hopped in line and got ready to order at the window. I got the 14 piece chicken combo which comes with fries and a salad. It was a little over $10 which was perfect because there's a $10 minimum on cards. After you order they give you a number and you sit and wait. On this day the wait was about 15 minutes, because of the crowd, but that wasn't a problem. When my number was called I got my food and I was on my way.  The chicken itself is tasty and really generous. There was an even mix of wings and legs and I enjoyed it. The fries weren't anything spectacular and the salad was just lettuce, so next time I might skip the combo and get the 14 piece for $8 deal. While Golden Wings isn't on the level of Popeyes, you can't beat the price and the food is enjoyable. I'll be back.
Their happy hour is the best!! Til 7 which is great for someone who works a little later. They have the best salsa.

Quirky, cute, homey friendly and good coffee / tea!  What more could you want in a local place.  They aim to please and have many regulars.  They have a limited selection of muffins and quiche which I did not try.  The tea is loose leaf and brewed when ordered.  They have a "bar" set up with stools, a sitting area with couches and chairs and outdoor seating.  If you drink in they give you a mug, no mugs match they are eclectic and vary in size - it is part of what makes the place fun.  They have art work all over the walls and quotes that will have you inspired or laughing.  Some of my colleagues had the Elvis which was a coffee roasted with banana and peanut flavorings.  They have every milk/non-milk option.  The prices are decent too.  They are also pet friendly.  I have already told 3 friends they should check it out and now all of you.  Enjoy!

If you haven't had gelato, I first have to wonder where you've been for the past 6 years, and then I have to tell you about Frost.  If you have jumped on the Italian answer to ice cream bus, welcome and let's go to Frost.  Lots of flavors--around 25--are just the first option available. Then, one flavor, 2, or 3? What size? Chocolate lover, it's here. Fruit-a-holic? No problem. Wanna go to Italy with the flavor, too? Easy.  I don't think it's possible to leave Frost without a smile on your face and a happy belly...if you do, well, you're not like anyone I know.   Lemoncello, Strawberries and Champagne, Mocha, White Chocolate Cream, Margarita...these are just some of the flavors avaiable in May 2010. A mixture or a single selection, either way, you're set for about $4 for a medium size. And once you get the hang of your favorites, try a shake or freeze!

I got a yelp email and it said that this place has some of the best wings in town. So we ventured over and it was true! We loved their wings so much. Pizza was just ok but it's worth going just to get the wings! Their sauce is soooo yummy! The wings are smaller but I like that. It weirds me out when wings are these huge, unnatural giant things. These are the perfect size and flavor. Go get the three pound portion and share with a friend!

Hands down the best sushi around! The rolls are creative and fun, the fish is fresh, and there are so many different options. There are many other menu options, but I can't stray from the delicious sushi!

I've been exploring han's dynasty and here are my preliminary rankings (1 is best)  1. Exton 2. Old City 3. Manayunk 4. University City  Coming in at the bottom of the list, this location has a hip ambiance with a nice bar that saves it from 1 star. The dry pot was swimming in soup (more like a wet pot) and other dishes had execution issues as well.
Fabulous food, professional and friendly staff, fun music, hip ambiance.  I keep coming back for more!

This chain is normally one of our favorites. Last night for our anniversary dinner- not so much. Service (Benjamin) was totally lacking. Almost awkward to be honest, Cheese steak egg rolls (our favorites) were overcooked and extra greasy,.   Waited for drink refills for a long time.   Chicken was overcooked and it tasted like they needed to change the shortening in the fryers, (old taste). Potatoes were cold ( they did get me new ones). My wife said her food was just OK. Dessert was lackluster.  $100 later- we left feeling disappointed. We love the Iron Hill. Hope tonight was just an off night.

This is the best venue I have ever been to.  I've been here for countless Phillies games, ballpark tours and for the Winter Classic series. I've sat just about anywhere you can sit and can't find a bad seat in this place. The view of the game is always great.  I always recommend CBP to my friends who are visiting from out of town because it's kind of a Cliffs Notes version of Philly. You get a great sampling of all the local cuisine- cheesesteaks from Tony Luke's, hoagies from Planet Hoagie, crab fries from Chickie's & Petes, that heavenly chicken from Federal Donuts, Philadelphia Water Ice, and so many more. There's also a number of gluten free and vegetarian options as well.  As far as beer, CBP sets the standard for all other venues. There is no shortage of locally brewed beer from breweries like Victory, Prism, Dogfish Head, and of course Yuengling. Of course there's also McFadden's and the High & Inside pub if you're looking for more of a bar atmosphere, and Harry the K's if you want a sit-down meal.  The views of the city are beautiful, and the liberty bell that flashes and rings for home runs is a great nod to our local history. Citizens Bank Park really captures the heart and soul of Philadelphia and rolls it all into one package.

Went here to shoot for the first time. Recommend getting ear muffs over ear plugs vecuase it was busy and loud inside the range. Staff was very friendly and helpful. Pricey to rent everything for an hour though
Service at the bar was so, so.  The Italian beef deep dish was fantastic.  Their pizzas look to be good,  but expect at least 30 min for your food.  At least it's fresh...

We really enjoyed our experience here. The food was really good and so was the beer. The service was friendly.   We really liked the cheesesteak egg rolls for the appetizer. I ordered the burger of the day from their promotional May burger menu and it was great. My wife ordered the chicken ceasar salad which she loved. The only complaint we have was the strawberry mojito was awful.   We will definitely go back.

I visited Kei twice this week! This is my new favorite spot in town!  The menu has an awesome mix of traditional rolls you know and love and some new and interesting rolls I haven't seen on any other menu in town.   My favorite roll is probably the Tiburon- salmon, spicy tuna, pickled jalapeño, daikon and sesame seeds.   The service was awesome too! I sat at the bar and my plates were cleared quickly and my drink was always full. I can't wait to come back for more to try the specialty nigiri!
Nasty flavor. Very sloppy presentation. Empty on Thursday night. Never again. Best part was the serve staff.
Fantastic cinnamon rolls according to my kid. I had the Denver omelet which was great. The coffee was good. I liked the patio seating.

Tried this place out for dinner the other night and was pleasantly surprised. They have such a fun and creative menu. I can't wait to try something else they offer. I also love that their menu has a really fun sense of humor to it as well (check out how they can slice your pizza) we got the mooseknuckle which was just pepperoni and pineapple and an order of wings.   The pizza crust was cooked perfectly and the sauce was flavorful. Everything worked well together... and the pizza was not overly greasy which is always a plus. The wings were delish... I wish they came in weight and not number (you can get an order of 6 or 12) we got the Buffalo flavor and it was super tasty.   This is a smaller place kind of tucked away in the back, they do not deliver so you'll have to pick up. There isn't much room to dine in but there are seats inside and outside. Will definitely eat here again.

I always liked cracked barrel  but today was the worst!  wait time to give your name so they can get a table  ready 15minutes. We get seated and wait 20 to 30 just to get our food we ordered. took our waitress 15 minutes just to come to our table to apologize for the wait time. We finally get our plates and one was warm and the other was cold,they take the plate back to kitchen and replace the pancakes and rest were put in a microwave,let's just say the eggs,sausage and bacon was rubber

The fish tacos are my go to item here. There is a self serve salsa bar. The quality of some of the stuff in the salsa could definitely be better but it's not too big of a deal. I have also tried the carne asada and the chicken tacos. They are also pretty good.

So a vegetarian walks into a meat-centric restaurant... and a four star review ensues? Yep, that's right. I like The Block a lot! Everything I've had here has been good. I've been a handful of times. The only thing I wish they had was one more vegetarian entree on the menu for variety.   I love the happy hour here. Great drinks and food specials. My favorites are the fries and the soft pretzels with cheese sauce. Both delicious!!   For lunch or dinner, their salads are great. They always have a special salad and it doesn't always include meat, but if so, they can modify it. Their salad dressings are great and ingredients really fresh. Half or whole portions are available for all appetites. Their mac n cheese is a good option but it was missing something to make it a little more flavorful. I loved the cheese on top.   They have house made desserts, which are amazing!! Their homemade ice cream is to die for. Really. Don't miss it.   Service is always good.   I'll be back!

Was really looking forward to coming here, but just wasn't that impressed with the food.  It was okay, but I think there are much better options for ramen just a 10 minute walk away on Washington Ave.  The waitstaff were all very friendly, and the place has potential.

This strip mall restaurant really confuses me. The inside is relatively small but clean and well kept. There was also outside seating available which is nice. On a Sunday afternoon there were about 6 tables occupied with 2 tables that were occupied by Hispanic people which is usually a good sign. My son and I were seated quickly and chips and salsa brought to the table. Tri-color chips? Really? The salsa was smooth and tomatoey. Drinks were brought out timely. On a Sunday early afternoon, 1 TV was on American football, 1 on European football (soccer), 1 on CNN and 2 on a camping show. The menu is huge. Too many choices.  I ordered their signature cilantro cream enchiladas and my son order the Monterey Quesadilla. The food came out fast. I mean unexpectedly fast. The quesadilla was rolled which I hadn't before and appeared to be heated on a flattop but it came out way to quick. My son said it was good. The cilantro cream enchiladas were just 2 small tortillas rolled with only precooked chicken chunks, no binders. Every time I cut the tortilla the chicken chunks pour out. The cream sauce was good but needed more cilantro. The enchiladas came with rice and black beans. The rice was pretty standard overcooked and dry. The black beans came in a tortilla cup and they had no flavor. They didn't even taste like beans. They had NO flavor. They were very confusing and I left them behind.  All-in-all, there must be something tasty on the menu with so many 4 and 5 stars but I didn't find it and I am not planning to go back and try to find out.

I came with a friend a few weeks ago. There was one other party in the entire restaurant, but they did have a lot of Togo orders. It was frustrating because we ordered miso soup and chicken and rice soup and mine came out good but my friends was like warm and the chicken was not good, very flavorless, not to mention it literally took about 30 minutes for just our soup to come out. While they had time to eat and talk and take care of Togo orders, while we wait waterless and impatient for soup that came out 30 minutes later and not even warm. Then we waiting another 15 for our rolls. They were fresh and we enjoyed them. Just annoying to feel like we weren't taken care of when they easily could have with how slow they were and with the three people that were on. Probably won't eat in again, obviously the Togo orders take priority over the guests in your restaurant.

It's Sunday night and the Cards are in the playoffs, we're not cooking or going out for dinner. We didn't want pizza, Chinese or any type of fast food. We were at a a loss for ideas. I was searching Yelp for ideas and checked to see if Joey B's did carry- out and to my surprise they did.  We checked the menu and made the call.   We ordered two of their Blue Plate Specials; Country Fried Chicken and Homemade Meatloaf. Both came with mashed potatoes and gravy and corn on the cob. The meatloaf also came with a side salad that was humongous, it was big enough for both of us to share. My Homemade Meatloaf  with a brown gravy truly had to down home taste. It is way better then other "Blue Plate Specials" near and on "The Hill". My SO enjoyed his Country Fried Chicken and its white gravy topping. Their great taste surprised us coming from a sports bar which is known for burgers and pizza.   I think Joey B's will be getting some more carryout orders from us.  They're close, the prices are right and the food is good.

We found this place on Yelp and went for dinner one evening. We ordered crab rangoon, drunken noodles, hawaiian fried rice and panang curry. Food came pretty fast and was just okay. The staff was quite friendly and responsive. I think I would do take out next time, but not worthy of dine-in.

My go-to place for authentic Vietnamese food! Usually I eat in but this time I got carry out. I've been craving for some Banh mi so I ordered BBQ Banh mi. I love this toasted Vietnamese sandwich. It's full of flavor and they don't skimp on the meat either. When eating in I usually get the Special pho with all the tendons, tripe, flank meat, and meatball. The broth is perfect and requires little seasoning. The Spring Rolls with the peanut hoisin sauce is a great appetizer to start with.    While the food is good and cheap the service is another thing. Expect to be greeted and waited on with an attitude. Also expect to pay with cash only. No cards!

The location of the place was a little hard to find since we were dining for the first time. Arriving there, the staff was friendly and inviting. We made a note on the reservation that there was a birthday and we were seated in a window booth with an outstanding view of the city and sunset. Our server was very informing, courteous, and made our first experience delightful. It is a higher end restaurant so be prepared to spend some cash, but it is perfect for special occasion.

Eclipse used to be my favorite California style pizza place in town. But I've been very disappointed my last two visits-  the margherita was a particularly poor example of the classic, and leftovers got tossed. For the price, I expect the fresh, high quality ingredients and careful oven temperature management I remember from several yrs ago.
I love the kuchi for two deal!! Only $49! Ask for chef Andy he's funny and makes the best hibachi!

Italian Affair has been around for a long time now. It was a favorite of my parents' when I was in high school and they've continued to visit together ever since. I couldn't remember the last time I tried it since I was never the biggest fan back then so I figured I'd go in with an open mind and see how they've evolved.  It's safe to say the space itself has not evolved. It looks exactly the same as it did years ago. But the food seemed to get better or perhaps my taste buds were just more refined..? I ordered a lasagna dish and it was pretty good, but my mom's bowtie something pasta (sorry I forget!) was the star of the table. It had spices and olive oil and it was just really nicely prepared all around. I ordered tiramisu to go, which was also a quality item.  In terms of Gloucester County's Italian restaurants, I'd say this is a good met for a nice meal. It's not outrageously good but it's satiating nonetheless.

As a non-vegan I knew it would not be my new favorite place but I was eager to give it a try as I always enjoy trying new places and I try to eat pretty healthy.   I can't say I was disappointed but i also was not blown away.   It was also pretty expensive. Which granted is expected in montecito.  But to serve chick pea "crab cakes" at a comparable to reall crab cakes is a little absurd.  Everything was tasty though.  I did order the crab cakes and I really like them!  I had the lush bowl and tuna salad as well.  The only thing I would not order again would be the potatoes with truffle.   Very very bland. Even after adding lots of salt they were not very flavorful.  Great service! Our waitress was very delightful!  And the guy who was setting the tables around us was very friendly as well.  The cocktails were good.  They are basically pressed juicery juices with alcohol. A little expensive for being so simple. The bartender was nice but not super talkative.- which I thought was a requirement for all bartenders to interact with the customers well.   I would go back with my vegan roommate but probably won't take my sister when she comes to visit.

This place is a bit overrated. While the service was great, I think you can find better sushi almost anywhere you go. I didn't dislike my yellowtail canopy roll....but I also wasn't impressed by it. The yellowtail canopy was finely chopped rather than the normal whole pieces of fish, which I worried was hiding bad quality fish. I ended up removing it and basically just eating a spicy salmon roll. We also had the sashimi sampler (meh) and the pork dumplings which were actually very good.   I probably wouldn't come back if not for our very attentive waiter.

Short:   Good food, Good service, small parking lot.  Long:    This place is smaller than the one located at Carrollwood, but is much closer for me.  The food here is just as good as the other.  This time I got a brisket sandwich.  It was cook perfectly, wasn't dry at all.    The style is still the same as most BBQ places, cafeteria style.  You got up order the food, and they load up your tray.  The food is prepared and ready to go on their hot table, so it definitely a great place to grab some good food quick.    All their sides are delicious, I haven't been disappointed yet by any of the sides I've gotten.  Its hard to choose what sides to get.  They even offer to let you get a sample of a side if you can't decide.  As for meat, I recommend trying the brisket, it is my favorite meat choice over there.

Try this place once, they definitely have some items that you won't forget: like the roti canai, amazingly crunchy and warm, a little bit sweet and creamy, perfect! I love their satay skewers, and even though my husband is not that into those. However, their Hainan chicken and chicken broth rice is just off, I can tell not too much effort has been put into this dish. The cut of the chicken pieces is a mess and they probably marinated these just briefly. The chicken broth rice is not too much difference compared to regular rice, where is the heavenly chicken flavor? Go eat Hainan chicken in Hongkong or any Cantonese restaurants in China! Anyway, a rare and pretty authentic Malaysian restaurant here, a nice addition to the Chinatown.

Cool spot! Reminds me of a BBQ joint in rural North Carolina. In the wood. Cold cabin without heat. Good brisket. Cold beer. If you can survive the freezing cold venue (in the winter), you should check it out. Oh, you might have to wait in line, but if you're not in a hurry, no worries.

Great solid sushi spot. Got the sashimi and some rolls and was pleased. There seems to be a great bar and they also do Thai food. selection of specialty rolls was large and seemed pretty creative. I will have to try some out on my next visit. Also, there's a half off deal on some menu items due to their grand opening.

I really like this place. It's one of the best places to go with a group in Philly, I think. The food that they serve here is really so delicious.and it is all at a most ridiculously good price. I've been here twice - the first time they ran out of the naan-like bread (!!what!!) and the second time was thankfully perfect. Unfortunately, little did I realize that when you come to Marrakesh multiple times in a row, you get the exact same food each time. And for me, that is enough for me to not come back again. BUT, that should not deter you from coming to this place. If it's your first time, I'm so excited for you. But if it's your second time, hopefully you're here for a special event; otherwise I'd tell you to go somewhere else.

Really enjoyed this place. We all did the fixed menu, and it was a lot of food, but tasty. The fish and steaks were good, as were the sides. Waiter was knowledgeable, and attentive. Very enjoyable evening.

After a disastrous first experience (which I won't go into), I vowed to never return. This review, however, is a story of second chances. After my 2+ year boycott, I decided to try Burger Up again, and again, and again. Burger Up is now one of my favorite burger spots in Nashville. My, how far we've come.  My favorite thing on the menu is their bison burger, topped with rich white cheddar, cut by fried onions and jalapeños, romaine, tomato, and honey Dijon aioli. It's full of well-balanced flavor. Their hand cut fries are good, but their truffle fries are even better. Be sure to try their house ketchup and aioli.  To start, I recommend the fried pickle chips, pimento cheese plate, or fried oysters.  Looking for something lighter? The chopped kale salad rocks, and the Marathon (black bean and quinoa burger) is one of the better veggie patties I've had.  They offer a suitable beer selection and have, more recently, introduced a cocktail list with delicious seasonal offerings. The ambience is contemporary and cozy, with high ceilings, wood finishes, and plenty of natural light. Expect to wait for a table, especially at peak hours and on the weekends. Service is generally very good, intermittently scattered with space cadets.

Went to celebrate a birthday with my family and had a great time.. we went in time for the early bird specials which are a very good deal especially for the large amount of food served us. I ordered the Steak & Shrimp . The steak was flavorful and the shrimp were fresh and tasty. Our server was very good, promptly refilled drinks and pre-bussed all plates before next entree. I was surprised the entire restaurant was full on a Tuesday early evening, just another sign they are doing a great job .

I visited the IRT with the little Yelper's club to see Peter Rabbit! I wasn't sure how a toddler would do with a 55 minute performance, but he loved it!   We had floor seats which meant we literally sat on the floor. (Dress accordingly!) We participated in some interactive singing and heard about guest expectations -- all using kiddie terminology!  The show kept my sons attention for all 55 minutes, I was impressed.   The interior of the IRT is stunning! I highly encourage all Indy residents to see a show there. I know we'll be heading back again soon - though it may be once my son is part to 3. He did great, but I was definitely restraining him (lovingly, of course) for the duration.    Also at this event were treats for little Yelper's & their grown ups provided by Market District in Carmel, IN. The oj was FRESH squeezed that morning!   Thanks again Britt for a fun outing w/the Yelp crew! Always an absolute blast! I've already raved about the play to a few mommy & daddy friends!

Food is ok, probably 3 stars out of 5. However, service is horrible. They have no basic manner. We came in during lunch hour when more than half of the restaurant was empty. When we asked how long is the wait (there were about 10 people waiting in line), the waiter just turned away without even responding.

My wife and I found this place while traveling in St. Pete.  The first night we tried going we were too late and ended up getting there 20 min before closing and weren't able to eat.  We were glad that we went back for a second try and found it was worth the wait.  We split the tomahawk steak and it was more than plenty and the split plate charge for the side and salad is more than reasonable.

I ordered the Hip City Ranch, supposedly their best seller, and it was not good at all. The sauce was off-putting and did not taste like ranch. The fake chicken wasn't flavored well either. I tried this place a few years back and didn't like what I had at the time but thought I'd give the restaurant another shot.  Not worth it.

I've rarely have had a fun time at the bar here but there have been a few moments that were enjoyable.  The food here used to be much better and they once had a great turkey burger BUT the food has gone WAY downhill and now I just refuse to eat here.  The last terrible meal I ate here was around Mardi Gras and two of the cooks got into a huge fight that spilled out into the street and there was all sorts of great blood and gore everywhere (this is probably an extra star for sheer entertainment).  Of course due to the fight it took three hours for us to get our food.

Fun vibe.  Good service.  Decent drink prices. Good food....BUT it didn't stand out as anything special considering there are a few other restaurants that are similar in the area.  A $12.00 burrito that is average does not make me want to go back any time soon.

I will admit that I've left here a few times just feeling ehh...due to one particular unfriendly bartender. But I've not seen her lately so I've been really enjoying the place. It's the perfect spot to grab a drink and watch the big screen or one of the many other tvs they have throughout. This is obviously a soccer bar so when those game are going on it's definitely a popular spot - expect it to be busy.  They have some great menu items but my favorite are their wings. Their special sauce is great but I was told by a gentleman at the bar last night that their hot is even better. I'll definitely be trying those next! Also I'm not a huge fan of bone in wings so for me to really love these it's saying something.  They also have a small back patio area. If you are looking for a chill place to enjoy a beer then this is your spot! Check it out.

Well, as for me, there are no good Chinese restaurants on the east side of town. Once again, a high review at an Asian restaurant disappoints this Asian.  We ordered egg rolls, Kung Pao chicken and BBQ pork lo mein. The egg rolls were decently sized and were not bad. Not great, mind you, but not bad. The Kung Pao chicken was disappointing. The sauce was soy sauce based and the flavor was not wonderful. The chicken was a bit dry and fibrous. The lo mein had more bean sprouts than noodles. I really didn't taste any noodles nor did I have any noodle texture. Too much bean sprouts. The pork was decent, though.  The service was nice. She was polite, concerned and attentive. It is a nice restaurant.  Will not ever return.

DUNEDIN BAGELS & DELI I picked up bagels here EVERY week & loved them! My very first job, at 15, was at a bagel shop in NJ with New York owners, Norman & Miriam Fleischman. I know a great water bagel when I taste one. However, I bought a half dozen cinnamon raisin bagels that tasted like onion. The first one tasted like onion. I thought that it, perhaps, came in contact with an onion bagel; no problem. The 2nd one tasted like onion & I thought then that they didn't properly clean their oven. After tasting the 3rd bagel, I called them to ask for 6 more. Well, the owner was angry & inappropriate. He sniffed each of the 3 bagels that I returned & said, "they don't smell like onion!" (Accusing me of lying?! Really?!?!) I would have simply apologized & replaced the 6 bagels...that simple. He said, "you take bite, you taste onion, you bring back!" I tried to explain that I thought it might have just been the one bagel. He got angrier & forcefully threw the remaining bagels in the trash before handing me a new bag of 6. Well, he lost a loyal customer for nothing!!!!!!! BEWARE; do not cross the boss!!!!!

I love their fritters and craft cocktails and brews. I'm disappointed that I can't get a burger past 3 pm, but that doesn't really matter when all of their food is fantastic. They have fried Mac n cheese with crab on top! Yum

I've been to Crazy Sushi quite a few times (most recently about a month ago with my SO), and I have to say that the quality has gone down.   They specialize in novel rolls (hence the name), and while they might be good (I never get them so I can't say) as a sushi spot you have to do traditional rolls right as well. Unfortunately, crazy sushi does not do that. The fish isn't super fresh and the rice-fish-seaweed ratio is off. It's not worse than most other sushi spots in the city, but it's not better either.

Myself and a small group of friends ended up going here over the weekend actually by accident. We needed to find a place nearby where we were and the Underground Tap & Grill was the first sign we saw. Their menu was posted outside beside the door, which I appreciated because I like to know what I'm getting myself into! We were seated right away, and the hostess, wait staff and manager (as I assumed he was) were all extremely nice. They clearly made an effort to be as friendly as possible which made the atmosphere of the place very enjoyable.  All of us started with drinks - I ordered the Coconut White Russian which was stronger than anticipated but still very good! I just wish they had ounces on the list before I had ordered it! We then ordered the nachos which were deceivingly smaller than they appeared, it was actually pretty comical and no one found it to be too big of a deal. It was a pretty generic bowl of nachos - nothing amazing , nothing bad - just your good old fashion kraft cheese nachos. All of us ended up ordering the Bison Burger for our main meals. Some people asked for cheddar cheese instead of blue cheese which is probably what I should of done as I found the blue cheese a bit too overpowering on the burger. But honestly not horrible - just a little funky.  The highlight however ... and the sole reason I would come back ... is for those sweet potato fries. Oh my. You haven't lived until you've had those sweet potato fries. Those alone get a 100/10 - delicious!

I really liked the deep dish hot chicken pizza. The ranch is amazing. But the thin crust pizza is not good. The wait for food is ridiculous and carry out always seems to be cold. I gave this place a chance, but I am extremely underwhelmed.

The lobster roll has almost too much lobster. Delicious as was the lobster bisque and clam chowder. If you're looking for great seafood this is the place. Not the cleanest place I'd have to say, the open kitchen looks clean and staff are all friendly. Many regulars there when we were.

Nice coffee shop on Central Ave. Plenty of communal working space and some smaller tables on the side. The coffee is ok, could be a bit stronger. The chocolate almond ball is a good treat to curb a craving, not too big or too sweet.
This was one of the best lunches I have ever had. I am vegan and a picky one and the food and service was excellent.

This place gets a 5-star simply for the no-kid rule after certain hours. There's nothing I hate worse than paying top dollar to see a movie, and having to listen to kids screaming, and mom just tuning them out. This no-kid rule remedies that. This place is a bit on the pricey side, but I honestly think it's worth it, because the food is amazing, you can get alcohol/buckets of beer, and the seats are so comfy that you can sink right into them! Also, you can pick your seats when you purchase your tickets
Local favorite. Cannot go back to Dunkin' Donuts any more!  I like simple grazed donuts or cinnamon sugar.

I ordered a Shrimp Cauli Bowl via uber eats. My order came quickly within a reasonable period of time. My food was cold, I'm thinking it's the clear plastic clamshell container used, it doesn't keep the heat in. Not a big deal for me. My total without the uber eats surcharge was $19.90. For close to $20 I would expect more than a container with 80% brown rice, some diced cauliflower, 6 florets if broccoli (literally, I counted), 3 paper thin slices of carrot and 8 measly shrimp. It did taste good but it is waaayy overpriced for what you get. I will give it another shot on a day when I can actually go in, in person but initial reaction is not that SoFresh is not SoImpressive

This is the very worst!!!! With our order, we picked to have fries as our side. They just handed our food with no side.... we said we ordered fries as our side. She looked at us like we had 3 heads and said well we have none made. So we said is there anything else and she said yeah it won't be an extra charge. Really????? So we ordered mash potatoes and she hand us a tiny Dixie cup size. I will never ever recommend this place to anyone!

This is a solid reliable taco and steak place which also has Sonoran style hot dogs. They have a nice fixings bar with all the guacamole you like salsas excetera. I find the quality is good the meats are well done and chopped properly. The tortillas are a fairly good quality not the best but good and they have states which are good but for the money. It's a bustling place about medium in price but I have always found them to be very reliable and very consistent.

This café certainly is cozy. Great ambiance! Perfect for a quick pastry or have coffee with friends. You can choose to sit at a table, at the bar or in the nice couches area.   They offer a great selection of coffee. I had a latté and it was delicious. However I went there at 9am and had to realize on the spot that this is not a breakfast café. They do offer a choice of 2 breakfast sandwiches but it was very disappointing: small, tasteless and soggy. Makes me wonder why they bother serving that. It gives the wrong idea of their potential.   Still I really enjoy the place and will definitely come back and try more.

This newest addition to Reno's galaxy of Mexican cuisine opened just as 2013 was making its last bows, and I think they're off to a pretty good start. I've watched this space during its remodel over the past year, and for a while wondered whether Bistro Habanero was coming or going. I actually had to create this Yelp page in order to write a review ... it's that new!  As it happened, I was putting gas in my truck (it's next-door to a gas station I frequent) and noticed the lighted OPEN sign in the window. Even though I wasn't really hungry, I wanted to check the place out and purchase a nibble to take home. Although there is plenty of Mexican fare on the menu, they also have some Salvadoran cuisine. I ordered two of the most common Salvadoran favorites, pupusa rebuelta and pupusa queso y loroco, and hurried home to share them with my wife.  In a word, OUTSTANDING! In particular, I really enjoyed the pupusa rebuelta and will definitely be back for more. The service was very friendly, the place is clean & nicely decorated, and in general was a far cry from what I had expected during their lengthy build-out.   On a repeat visit I tried the ceviche tostada special and a stacked quesadilla. The ceviche gave me pause because it's the first time I can recall having this item with refried beans in the mix. However, I was pleasantly surprised by how well these flavors combined, and the half-moon fried tortilla was light and crispy underneath all the fish, veggies, and sauce. The stacked quesadilla (meat & veggies & cheese, oh my!) was totally tasty, and my wife enjoyed the mix of tacos she ordered. Everything tasted very fresh, including the tortilla chips.  P.S. they're open for breakfast Mon-Sat at 6:30am (10am Sun). Might have to make a detour on my way to work!
We order from here on Saturdays for all of the sales staff at our dealership.  It is AWESOME!!! Never had a bad meal from here.

After walking around last night we decided to stop in and have a late meal.   First thing that stood out was the friendly and attentive service. I wish I could remember the young lady's name but she was great.   The beer was ice cold which hit the spot on the a hot night in New Orleans.   Gumbo and shrimp poboy were very good.   Not really a fan of the deserts but other than that will definitely be back.
Hated it.  Messy, overpriced, noisy, obnoxious chain.  I could kick myself for agreeing to come here.  I couldn't find a damn thing to eat either.  Meh.

I love this place, especially their crab wontons, lettuce wraps, Ginger Salmon and Mongolian Chicken, Beef, or Shrimp. I love the atmosphere too. Even though it's a chain, it's one of my favorite places to eat.

Good sushi. Everything tasted fresh! The fried rice and seaweed salad were delicious too. Service was great. We went on a Monday night around 7:30 and there were only a few other people eating. Very reasonable prices too! Couldn't believe the glasses of wine were only $4! I will definitely be returning or getting take out from here sometime soon.
Such a cute little fun place! Bonus points for dog friendly bar and good beer. It gets pretty crowded though. Pretzel was okay

Had a great time at Momo's!  We made a reservation for Friday evening in case they were busy.  There was a belly dancer for part of our meal, which was super fun and kept the atmosphere alive.  We ordered the roasted red pepper hummus which was delicious.   We also order the lamb gyro....HUGE!  There is so much to this that you can't even pick it up to eat it.  Very delicious!  The restaurant was kind of musty and warm though.  Could have been that it was starting to rain and they had people coming in/out from the outside patio.  Had a great time overall!

I have been there three times now. Service is not good. Portions are as small as I have ever seen. The tacos are so small you would think they came off a check Loren's menu. Same with the burritos. Salsa was ok, I prefer something with a little more spice but I realize you have to please the masses. But will not go back due to portion sizes. The only thing I have had there that was tasty and a good portion size was the Nachos.

I've come here for happy hour and quizzo a few times, and it's always been pretty darn good.   The margaritas are tasty and strong, and for the happy hour price, you can't beat it. I also love the $1 PP endless chips & salsa. It's a great yummy, cheap snack to share with friends.  I have had a few of the menu selections including the mac & cheese (awesome), pulled pork (tasty) and pork belly. The pork belly was not my favorite. I'm hoping maybe it was just an off day, but the pork belly was very chewy and flavorless. I probably would not order that again.  This is definitely one of my favorite happy hours in the city. There's plenty of seating indoors & out, good service, strong drinks and great prices.

A solid 4.5 star in my opinion, which is great for a restaurant in it's first week of operation.  I'd be anxiously awaiting this location as the original in St. Pete has always been a favorite of mine. Getting a reservation on Open Table was easy and we were seated quite quickly despite the restaurant being at capacity and having a wait once we arrived.  Our server seemed to be pretty scarce throughout our time here and it took a while to place and even receive our drink orders.  We ordered the Meatballs as an appetizer and requested it come out before the entrees, which was verified by our server as she took our food orders at the same time.    Nevertheless, the appetizer arrived no less than a minute before our entrees came out.  Not sure if this was a kitchen issue or if our server had just forgotten.  For my entrée I had the Chicken Fried Chicken Parm, which turned out to be a generous portion and cooked very well.  The chicken was crisp and not soggy like some places and it was overall a great dish.  I can't wait to try this place out again and for brunch sometime soon as well.

One of my favorite places to get a pie, clean and friendly staff helps make your dining experience awesome. Be sure to get cheesy bread, which isn't on the menu but really should be because it's a great way to start your meal. Wash it down with one of their great beers on tap and you'll be a happy camper, go!

I'm a big fan of Rostizado! Eating there multiple times during lunch and dinner I've refined my selections and found my favorites on the menu.   #1 is the rotisserie pork platter. Although the chicken is their staple I'd say skip it for the more flavourful and fatty pork. The platter comes with some veg, slow roasted potatoes, and dipping sauces so it's a meal in itself and big enough to feed 2 adults.   I'll often go with a starter but I find they are slightly overpriced so instead I get the house fried tortilla chips with multiple salsas. There's also deep fried pork skin in the chips order and they are the gems in the basket.   Finally I always have the churros. They are a great balance between chewy, crispy, and cinnamony and the chocolate sauce adds the right amount of sweetness.

I was driving by, and decided to give it a try.  The dining room is kind of noisy.  But I like the homey feel.  The server was attentive, friendly, and knowledgeable of the menu, which has a lot of good choices.  I ordered the cheeseburger with bacon.  It came out exactly as I ordered it, medium, hot and good.  The burger was served on toasted thick bread, different, but it works well here.  The fresh dill pickle reminded me of El Cap, and I loved it, I only wished they would of served two slices to complete the burger, as one did not seem to cover the whole burger.  After I was served, the owner/chef, came out and asked me if it was o.k., I appreciate the genuine concern by the owner, to find out if I was satisfied, something you do not get often these days.    The choice of french fries or tater tots was a good change of pace too.  I opted for the tater tots.  They were hot, a little over cooked for my taste, but some may like crispy, good never the less.  A surprise here, and Ill be back to try the Cuban!

I have been to this store several times over the years.   It strikes me as light and bright with good space between the isles.   it often has what seems to me a theme of merchandise through out the store, so if you are not in that mode you are out of luck. but for every day stuff they seem to have a good mix and prices.  today our visit was precipitated by Gus as he was looking for three things all of which he figured he could find at target, he was wrong. not one item of want was found so off we went.
Pretty fun place! We had a good time. And the staff was nice, which is always a plus!

A gem in manayunk! Stopped in today for some breakfast and left satisfied. The sandwiches looked delicious, but I ordered the double down: 2 eggs, 2 french toast w/ sausage. The staff was very kind. It's small, so if you think of going with a large group, call first.

Hands down the best restaurant in New Orleans!  Crispy brussel sprouts appetizer was good, very saucy, not bitter.  Lump crab appetizer was pretty good.   Jumbo Louisiana Shrimp "En Cocotte" (roasted jalapeño cheese grits, andouille & green onion sausage) is my absolute favorite meal in New Orleans. Also their jalapeno cheesy grits is the best in town. No one makes it like them.

Some times things just work. The wildflower is one of those places in time that just works. Leslie one of the two owners met me like an old friend at the door Cathy my waitress made a wonderful suggestion for lunch that was on the regular menu bit not the days special.    I had the roast beef sandwich with tomato jam Lettuce and a special dressing on home made bread.   The roast beef was perfect and tender. I am talking butter tender.  One look at it told the story. Only premium cut cow here.   I had an ice tea and an oatmeal cookie (also home made.   Oh they have a little market if you need things to go.  Clean tastefully decorated with a tree shaded porch.  Parking is good.   Hey I had never been there before today.   I will be back.    Sandwich with chips $11.00. A great deal with the heap of roast beef an attentive service

My boyfriend and I were looking for a froyo place close to his place and found this. I am so glad that we did. They don't have a huge variety of flavors, but the ones they have are good. Plus, they rotate them. Then there are the usual froyo fixin's. The Reese's cups, cookie dough, and cheesecake are all HUGE chunks, which I appreciated. Though, I'm not sure I recommend the cookie dough from there...  The main talking point here is their ROLLED FROYO. I don't know why no one's mentioned it yet, but YES. THEY HAVE IT. I'd been searching for a rolled ice cream place in Indy since the trend hit the internet. You can create your own (froyo base + 2 mix-ins) or choose one from their menu. I made my own with strawberry and chocolate. She even topped it off with strawberries and some whipped cream. Even though this isn't exactly rolled "ice cream", it's so so good.

Extraordinarily average. Food is just "OK" and portions are small and pricey. Service is meh. Restaurant itself is beautiful, and it's not a bad place to grab a happy hour drink, but I wouldn't eat there again. Definitely not one of Jose Garces' better spots.

Bananas Foster French Toast. Best french toast I've ever had. Not too sweet. I don't normally like ordering sweet dishes, but this was amazing and worth it!   Shrimp & Grits. Super creamy grits. Delicious.

Great experience.  Aly was our server and was so nice and courteous.  We had Ceviche Del Día and the Empanadas de Hongos...DELICIOUS!!  The Ceviche is on the spicy side but lots of shrimp.  Also, the Pollo IRON SKILLET TACOS were awesome as was the Rancheros style ENCHILADAS Costilla(Braised Short Rib).  Will definitely be back.

They accept credit card. Definitely not just a juice bar, let alone the first in New Orleans. Given the hype of all these other reviewers with Surrey's shrimp and grits, we had to indulge. You absolutely have to try it. The shrimp is cooked so well, many places tend to overcook, but they are so tender. The grits are so buttery and delicious, and were totally perfect with a few drops of hot sauce.   Wonderful service, everyone was so friendly and accommodating.

Awesome place! The staff is top-notch! Excellent customer service. Friendly folks and fair prices. They also have everything you're looking for. If you live in center city, Rittenhouse this should be your go-to for hardware needs.

The tour was a little on the long side, but the tasting was absolutely wonderful. The girl who facilitated the tasting seemed more personable. You could really tell both the tour guide and the lady who gave the tasting really loved their jobs.

The Kenner location is just as good as the New Orleans location!! I'm glad that the parking is a bit bigger than the New Orleans location, but it is still a strange parking lot.  We went during the week, and it was surprisingly not that busy, yet my food ticket got lost.  My coworker and I split a salad and two sandwiches (nom nom nom) because that is how we roll at The Big Cheezy. I'm glad that we did split the two sandwiches because I was the second one to order, but my food never came out until I asked about it. They had to reprint my ticket so the kitchen could make my food. Even though everyone was already done with their meal, I gobbled up my food and left.  I really wish they would make Bacon Mac n Cheese fries a thing.
Very good food and great service. This is a great place to grab a very unique burger and milkshake in downtown St. Louis. It's a bit overpriced for what you receive but definitely worth trying.

I think the Black Sheep is my favorite in the city. They have yummy food (especially the fries), the first floor is lovely and bright when it's nice weather because of the big windows, and there are 3 floors so usually you can find a spot somewhere to have a drink.

Bartender Logan was super helpful. They had a gluten free crust that did not make me sick. (I'm celiac / super sensitive). Only issue was how long it took to get our charcuterie board. We got our pizza and finished it before the board came out. Was hoping to eat the cheese / meat first but everything tasted wonderful.

I've been here two times so far, about a year and  1/2 apart, and both times I've been very satisfied! They're definitely a popular Central West End spot on weekends, so I HIGHLY recommend making a reservation ahead of time via Yelp or Waitlist app.  When we arrived and checked in with the hostess, she said it would still be about a 30 minute wait for our party of 4. A little surprising as we had placed ourselves in line via the Yelp app 30 mins prior on our drive there, but nonetheless we grabbed drinks at the bar after a few minutes of waiting. Our table was ready by the time we received our drinks (didn't take the whole 30 mins).  The overall atmosphere is a very unique one for the STL area. They have a very long bar downstairs with an equally long set of high top tables to eat at and a gigantic wall of antique glass windows. We were seated upstairs which consists mainly of 4-6 person tables that either overlook the main floor or the side streets of the restaurant.  With them being so busy on weekends and usually having a full house, it can get pretty noisy in the restaurant as they have concrete walls with little insulation. Don't expect to have a intimate conversation at your table.  Burgers are excellent quality overall and the fries are always crispy and perfectly cooked. I had the Whiskey BBQ Burger and was very satisfied with my choice. They have a wide selection of sauces to try too, I highly recommend the Roster Sauce. My only pet peeve is that the burgers are pretty pricey ($10+) and a little on the smaller size and they don't come with a side.  Our table split a pitcher of the Peach Ginger Spiked Lemonade and it was delicious! The picture was enough for all 4 of us to have at least 2 servings each. They're famous for their boozy shakes, but we didn't partake in any of those.  Overall great place to enjoy at evening at downtown. Good vibes, friendly staff, delicious food.

I loveeeeeed this place!  I always go to lunch on Friday's with co-workers and we try a new local place each week...preferably a non-chain!  The food was sooooooo good, so fresh and had amazing flavor.  I really forgot I was in Florida when sitting in the room eating...almost made me feel like I was in Georgia or the Carolina's by how they have it staged.  I had the Turkey Cinnamon Tea Sandwich with the homemade and amazing Tomato Basil Bisque....was outstanding and just the right size.  I am a sucker for scones and had to try one to go...was soooo good!  They have a small store/bakery just outside of the restaurant that was perfect.  You can go in and get some items to go and every single item in the case and on the shelves looked amazing.  I highly recommend going here as I will be going back!

Our extended family brought us here for some hangover therapy.   ATMOSPHERE: Its a huge sports bar with big windows, wood everywhere and more TV's than a Vegas kino parlor.  And they have THE coolest restroom decor I've ever seen!  FOOD: Crab Fries - Apparently their specialty.  The fries were average but the dip is to die for!  Not sure why its named that way as there's no taste of crab.   Cheesesteak Nachos - Huge portion with a good amount of toppings.  Very good.   Lobster Cheesesteak - Meat, fresh lobster & cheese sauce.  This isn't the best cheesesteak I've had, but maybe if it came with real cheese it would've complimented the flavors better.   Mussels Marinara - A family member's dish, so I just tried one.  Tender & lots of flavor but I'm not a big mussel fan.   Dungeness Crab - Tried a portion from my hubby.  It's good but mainstream.  Nothing unique.   SERVICE: We walked in at lunch time and got seated in about 15 mins.  Our female server wasn't the friendliest of people but she did check on us throughout our meal.   OVERALL: This is the place to be if you're a sports fan or if you just want some great bar food.

Stopped by for the world famous Greek salad only in tarpon Springs fla.  We had there fried grouper sandwich.  Taste out of this world. One of the best ever. Service here is always a 10. Top notch. And if you have room. There Greek deserts are to die for.
Wish wish wish they would open the balcony on days they don't have upstairs reservations.  The view is wonderful, service impeccable. ..food very good.

Went here in January and it was great . The meal included a salad . Went back in Feb. and got charged about $20 EXTRA for our 2 salads ! That is a huge price increase . You are better off to go to Sperry,s who gives you a huge salad bar with the meal for free . Don't ever order the 3 filet meal here . The 3 filets are about the size of 3 pieces of cut up stew beef cubes . Apparently , this is now a very price driven over-priced eating spot. Too bad .

It's been 7 years since I last reviewed Pal's and the bar has certainly changed a lot.  David and I stopped in here on NYE for a cocktail before a party. You have to get buzzed in, which always makes me nervous, but I just deal with that. Inside is so much better than it used to be: it's no longer dark, there's no smoking (of course) and the place just has an overall cleaner feel.  The bartender was extremely nice, there was good music playing the night we were here and they have a pretty good beer selection. I no longer feel "unsafe" when I hang out here, so that's a huge plus. This is a quintessential neighborhood bar and the patrons are kind and welcoming.

Ok....I'd been dreaming of this place and my experience turned out kinda "blah"...our order was messed up, added some of my sides to my cousins bag of food, then told me basically I had to get mine out of hers since they accidentally gave her my extras, leaving me with the type of sauce she ordered on my potatoes and corn. Not a big deal but I wanted what I wanted. My food came 15 mins after hers. I did enjoy my dish and would have been pleased if it was how I wanted it, but unfortunately it wasn't. The sauce on my cousins food kinda looked like mud so for presentation it was a "No" also. (What's pictured is my cousins meal because it looked much better than my little bag)

My boyfriend and I finally ate here last night after wanting to visit for awhile.   FOOD All of their dinner entrees range from about $16-$28, making this an upscale Mexican restaurant. They aim to be very authentic with details such as: making all of their own sauces, having only Mexican beers, etc. They have delicious salsa here and give you endless chips before your meal - yay! I had the mixto flat iron tacos and my boyfriend had chicken enchiladas. My tacos were delicious! They come fajita-style, meaning you make your own tacos. The cheese that the meat came in was delicious. It would be cool if they found a way to keep the cheese soft, as it sort of seized up after a bit. That being said, I eat relatively slowly. My boyfriend's enchiladas looked amazing and he loved them! Everything was wonderful.   SERVICE Service was awesome! Our server was kind, knowledgeable, and checked on us frequently. He made the restaurant that much better! We haven't had that good of service in a long time. They end every meal with churros (so good) and worry dolls. It's such a nice touch.   ATMOSPHERE  This place is beautiful and busy! We ate on the covered patio and it was lovely. They have pretty lights hanging everywhere and it doesn't get too loud. The inside of the restaurant looked beautiful as well. Everything was decorated well.   We will definitely be back and had a great time here!
LOVE LOVE this place! Dill pickle soup - sounds gross - but is UH MA ZING. Martinis are ALL good. I've been there several times and it's awesome every time!

Safety Harbor is lucky to have it's very own local brewery. This place is great! Love the atmosphere - they have a huge outdoor area with lots of picnic tables, a fire pit, corn hole, and many times live music and a food truck. We've been here for a number of different occasions from a birthday party for me to a Halloween party to the baseball playoff games and every time has been a great experience.   The beer selection is really good and they do change them up enough to always give you something new. They also offer a few wines. They're decent (as more of a wine connoisseur) but I've also had a few of the beers. Service is usually good. If there's a crowd, you might have to wait a few minutes to get the bartenders attention. They have games to play if you want to hang out, dogs are welcome and the place just gives off a real friendly vibe.
