Parking can be a bit of a pain - a lot of the local businesses don't want anyone to park by them. All in all a good time for all!

came here with a couple of friends for the first time and had a chance to try their main selling burgers and fries and margaritas.  unfortunately, both the burgers and fries and margaritas were all average in my opinion, compared to other restaurants in university or center city.    for the burger, you choose the type of meat you want and then a type of topping(s) below on the menu.  I opted for the Charleston, which ended up tasting like another average burger with meat quality average enough to not be too distracted from it.    for fries, I tried their spanish fries which is essentially fries mixed with fried onions and peppers - fries overall were on the more moist, potentially soggy side, versus traditional fries that you imagine being more crisp.  you can also ask for their small side of garlic mayo which they will charge you for .59 cents per small cup to dip with your fries or put on your burger.    for the margaritas, I opted to for the orange-mango frozen margarita which was average as well - it tasted more diluted in my opinion and not as well blended with some large enough chunks of ice to disturb the consistency of the drink.  If you get it on the rocks, they will give you the whole shaker to pour the margarita yourself.  I would say if you are looking to simply fill yourself up and eat with a friend or more in a casual environment, whether indoor or outdoor, this place is solid.  If you come for their daily specials, especially on wednesday's, when they have half off burgers for ~$5, this place is more worth it in terms of price and food quality. Enjoy!

We are from Missouri and stopped at this location for lunch. Is this place an epicurean marvel? No. But is very good and basic good food for the price with an added positive decor thrown in. Our food was good with a generous portion at a fair price. I had fish and chips and my wife had a pasta. Both were good. With drinks our bill was in the low $20s. No place in this price range is perfect, but when the service is good, the food is good and the place is clean, a customer can't ask for more.

Our first-ever Yelp supper club event and hopefully not our last!  Great food, great service & great company!  Wish I had ordered the scallops, though - Steven B, I was jealous...  Best baklava I've had locally!  The sampler was pretty good too.

I really wanted to like this place, but after my second visit they did not convince me. The first time I went a few months ago the service was spotty, and their breakfast was bland to say the least (I had the apple oatmeal which needed heaping amounts of brown sugar to make it taste like anything), also their coffee seems to have a funny taste and always seems to come out cold. When I went more recently, I found a HAIR in my oatmeal. GROSS. Needless to say, I was planning on returning to try their pancakes, but after the hair experience, I think I'll make my own at home.

So many good things about Mazzaro's. Starting with the murals on the walls (can't see from 22nd). Once inside the selection of cheeses, coffee, meats and most importantly WINES is AMAZING. Do yourself a favor and stop in-I guarantee you will love it!

Food was basic but really good. I had a broiled grouper sandwich that was perfectly cooked and very good. The best part is that you could really taste the grouper because it was not hidden in some sort of sauce. My daughter had the hilly cheesesteak which was really delicious and not greasy.  The service was very good and friendly.  This place was clearance and not fancy but very good. Right across from the beach.

Consider this a 3.5 - as a loyal and by loyal, I mean dangerously loyal customer of The Flying Cupcake I hate to be leaving anything less than 5 stars. (for the record, the location of my residence makes this loyalty inevitable). As of late, I've been disappointed by the new icing "methods." By this I mean we went from a 40% frosting/60% 'cake to 18% frosting/82% cake. With the icing not covering the entire surface of the top of the cake, how am I supposed to be certain that each bite of my Here Comes The Bride has a dabble of frosting? ...and with that, this review has taken a turn for the "first world problems" category.

Such a yummy restaurant with a fun environment. The Jumbo Shrimp on Jalapeño Grits was delicious and cooked perfectly (we even added crab on top). Definitely recommend the Crab and Shrimp "Etouffee." It is amazing and served on top of roasted rice to add a bit of crunch. The service was great thanks to Jessie and Chi who were both very knowledgeable and very helpful!

Phenomenal food. Freshest sushi, delicious flavors. We got Izumi roll catepillar roll monster roll and yellowtail canopy and all were so flavorful and fresh. The seared scallops were also excellent. One of my new favorite sushi places.

M3 was better than I had expected! They had good wine selection (I got the Malbec) and local beers on the rotating handle. They didn't have an actual cocktail list but we were told the bartender was great!  My friends and I ordered a few different things to try and share. The white mac and beef stroganoff were both so amazing! The pasta was cooked perfectly and the sauce that was in both dishes had a unique delicious flavor.   The dishes also came with a side of bread which I was not impressed with as it was hard and didn't have much flavor which was ok because I didn't know it came with it.   Overall, the service was awesome and the food and drink was was great!
Absolute best in the city. The spa is beautiful and well designed. I relax immediately after entering. Always excited to go back.

My words cannot give this place justice.   SERVICE: Fast, attentive but not overbearing, teamwork among waitstaff, friendly.   ATMOSPHERE: trendy; rustic warehouse vibe; good for date night or large groups; food served family style/to share   FOOD: IN. CRED. I. BLE. We had: -maple glazed carrots - delicious!!!! -kale slaw - tasty, but nothing new -whipped feta- creamy, deliciousness heaven. MUST.  -shaved root vegetable - average and small  -brisket meatballs - moist and excellent; served with habanero hot sauce, pickled onions, and a lime yogurt sauce; literally to die for  -lamb with couscous - perfectly al dente cous cous with tender lamb off of the bone. First time having lamb and I am instantly a fan -chocolate chess pie as a check mate to end the meal. Mmmm  BASICALLY: excellent. Must go.

This place is just "OK" in my book. I'll go if I'm waiting for a table to open up at a neighboring Fountain Square restaurant or if it's one of the only places open at an odd time, but I would never make a special trip here. It's usually crazy busy, especially on weekends, and I've had to wait a while for my order. Margaritas have a strong "from a mix" taste that I don't like. Tacos are fine, but nothing memorable. We have many better tacos in this city, so I'd skip these unless absolutely necessary.

Ok I LOVE VILLAGE WHISKEY!  Three reasons.    1. The best damn old fashioned on the planet, no fruit cocktail here!  Just 100 proof bourbon, lemon ice sugar cubes and their house made bitters!  They stir, add, stir, add till the bourbon expands upwards - takes about 12 min!!!  Worth every penny.  2.  THE BAR STAFF IS FABULOUS!!!  My personal fav is sasha, she has my old fashioned started when i walk in the door.  All of the bartenders are awesome.  They will show you how to make drinks, answer questions about the bourbon at length and take care of you like no one else.  3.  THE BEST HAMBURGER IN TOWN!  No lie.  Free range angus.  Its worth every calorie.  Ok the con - the small space.  With product and service this good it was silly not to have made the place bigger.  They did it intentionally but it was really a bad idea.  Why they couldn't have made it bigger and tinto smaller or even put tinto elsewhere i really do not know.    They loose business more and more because people can't stand the crowds and the wait for a table.  A place that small really needs to take reservations.  I myself always go there first and am frequently disappointed that there is no space.  Its just awful.    That said - if you go at off hours you may have luck.  To this day i have never sat at a table.  So one or two friends going is good. three is pushing it.  A large group - take them somewhere else.  COME ON JOSE - WE LOVE IT - EXPAND INTO TINTO ALEADY!  We have tappas elsewhere in philly no one has what you offer at village whiskey!
Delicious! Very friendly staff, love that the food comes out as it is ready, really adds to the atmosphere. Each dish was very tasty and I love the feel of the space inside! Definitely recommend.

Love sofresh for lunch. I usually go to the downtown one but was in south Tampa today so decided on eating here. Food is just as delicious as the downtown one. The only down side is that its smaller, a lot smaller than the downtown one. Had to wait a few minutes for a table, but one opened up and we were able to enjoy. I had the Super Food Bowl with brown rice and Quinoa. It was delicious. It comes with a vinagrette dressing but I traded it in for their basil pesto dressing. That dressing is so delicious. As always food was delicious, fresh and fast. This place does not disappoint.
Fantastic brewery. Love all of their brews, in can or from a tap.  Top two brewery in Boise, along with Sockeye.

Great mole!  We ordered the mole negro and a HUGE appetizer plate.  Both were delicious.  The mole was thick and flavorful and pretty close to some of the Oaxacan restaurants in LA that I miss so much.  I was so happy to find authentic Mexican food in Philly that tastes good.  Horchata was not on the menu, but something made me ask.  After all they had other aguas frescas.  Our waiter said, "let me check."  He came back with two horchatas for my friend and me.  Wahoo!    Las Cazuelas is a BYOB, so stop off for some Negra Modelo or tequila before you get there.  But do visit.  It's not cheap Mexican food, but it is delicious.  The atmosphere is nice (it could even be a nice date spot), and the staff are great.  UPDATE: horchata is still not on the menu, but it's there if you ask for it.  The mole poblano was good, as were the shrimp and beef dishes we ordered.
a great place to hang outside or inside great coffee, great desserts, very nice staff i think free internet too

The Gryphon is what you've always wanted in a coffee shop: friendly baristas, delicious drinks and food, and regular live music that you can enjoy while sitting at one of the long, worn wooden tables.  It's cozy and relaxed and good for an easy night out. The only complaints I have are the size (too small, and packed when there's a live act) and that, as of late, it's gotten a bit overrun by high school hipsters.

Right down the street from our house, we didn't go here for months after moving in. Not sure why, but I just didn't think it would be good.  Location is strange, it's in our residential area of olde Richmond... But great!!!  Awesome service and mostly importantly great food.  Went 3 other people and we all ate each other's food because everyone wanted to try it all. Chicken parm - awesome. Lobster ravioli - awesome. Steak - awesome. Fish dish - awesome.   And it's BYOB which makes its extra great.   I'd suggest this to anyone in the area!!!   Call ahead though, because it gets packed and you need a reservation to get a table on a busy night!

I've lived in Nashville my entire life, and I've noticed that this place seems to be a staple for a lot of newcomers and tourists, but I'm not entirely sure why. I couldn't have helped but notice the insanely long lines wrapping around the building, so one day I decided to try it myself.  I was not impressed with the food here at all. It's your average breakfast food, and honestly, the quality is about the same as something you'd get at iHop. While there is a wide variety of different pancakes to choose from, I still wouldn't say that this makes the restaurant worth it. If you're looking for a good breakfast restaurant, save yourself the time and choose somewhere else.  However, if you're wanting to try to popular restaurants in Nashville but don't care too much in terms of quality for the price, then this is the place for you.

It took several calls to Jason Martinez, who works in the tire department, to get through and make an appointment for 4 new General tires.  The transaction was set up by my father, so they may not have connected me to the service as I use a different last name.  Hopefully, the service will happen tomorrow at 1:30pm with no glitches.  Their shop is near King's Buffet, and the Savers/JoAnn fabric and Harbor Freight shopping center (as well as being near the Atlantis and Burlington Coat Factory) so I can do some shopping while I wait the approximate 2 hours for the tires to be installed.

One of the most charming and unique hotels I've ever stayed in, a converted train station and historic, but not over the top. The rooms reminded me of a boutique hotel feel. Staff was terrific and close by everything we wanted to see.   Historic atmosphere, modern amenities. Win/win.

Thank you yelp! We came in on for Sunday brunch and requested something we've seen on YELP! They were more than happy to replicate the dish. We also ordered raw oysters and crab benedict. The main star dish was the Roasted jalapeno cheese grits with yard egg topped with a crispy fried soft shell crab. Yes, it was very rich in favor. The egg yolk oozing with perfection & you can taste the jalapeno like you just bit into a fresh one.

If you are in the neighborhood, this is worth checking out. The wait is first come, first serve so expect to wait at peak brunch times. I will say they do move quickly so the wait isn't as bad as you would think. That being said, i only think the food was average. You will likely find better and more unique brunch spots in the city.

Came here to support a local band, Island Avenue, and have some BBQ. The BBQ here is outstanding. Started with the 'smoked bologna' which is off-the-chain,AGT. Then , brisket and spare ribs with garlic red potatoes with cornbread pudding. Mmmmmmm mmmmmmm gooooood! The smoke near perfect with an outstanding rub.  All this listening to some great music. The is a great place and it is only a Sunday night! Will try another night soon.

Overall experience was good. Went here after going to a few other bars. Loved the basketball shooting game they have as well as the outdoor fire pit. The outdoor dining makes me feel like I'm in my own backyard and I absolutely love it.
The duck fries are a must but one hair in my Mac and cheese and well to the " do not go list " it has become

Tonight was our first visit here. Paid $70 each person for VIP tickets plus $10 for parking. Got through all 5 attractions in less than 1 1/2 hrs. It was nice skipping the lines cause I would have been pissed if I waited hours for these attractions.  I'm easily scared but this place didn't do it for me. The interactive ones aren't really interactive. Just light touches on your arms and guiding you different directions than your group. At one point it started down pouring raining and instead of people walking through the attraction they stood there and held up the rest of people. The guests were more scared of the rain. We had to push through people to get through which messed up the scares.  I wanted to get scared and that's not what I got. I guess I'm still on the look out for an actual scary experience.
It's extremely tiny and packed.. and very loud!!! The service was pretty good.. The food was okay, but not anything special! It's BYOB, so that is a plus.
These tacos .. people.. we ordered the shrimp tacos, the chronic sandwich and a side of pork belly. We can't wait to go back.
Very fast, price was a bit high but I guess that'll teach me a lesson on losing my rental keys. The locksmith was a very nice guy.
I have had a couple more meals since writing my initial review and they were definitely improved, but they still need to make the overall experience better.

Went to finally try this after hearing so much about it. First of all parking is very challenging as it is in Hillsboro Village, which in itself is a problem. The line to get in was not bad so ordered pretty quickly, Ordered the Southern Benny, I felt the biscuit had no flavor basically and fell apart the eggs were okay but the gravy was way too salty. Frankly an expensive so called breakfast way too many other authentic places in town for this type of food. Been there done it probably will not go back. A little too touristy and too much hype about nothing spectacular.
Always excellent food and service. Great new location with bar and patio. Same quality as before.

Hopefully they have more to offer that soothes the palate and make you savor the fusion of flavors. But for tonight, it's just ok. The spicy seasoning for the Nashville cauliflower tasted more like a  topping that did not have time to blend with the cheese which saved the dish. The smoked pork and dates were more like mostly pork fat and dates. The shrimp gambas were the best of the three and just almost hit the spot. I'll probably come back and try the others and give them a second chance.

I have lived in St. Louis my whole life, and I haven't figured out why this is such a hot spot. They have okay ice cream, and when I go with my family I often don't even get ice cream. I much prefer to get something like a brownie or slushee over the ice cream.

The best haircut that a guy can get in Indy. You know how at some barber shops, you need to make a reservation with a certain barber because everybody else there sucks? Not here. Just about everybody at 4:59 can give you a top-notch haircut. I've only had one mediocre haircut out of about 10 different trips with a different person cutting my hair each time. 4:59 has cool decor with cherry wood on the walls. Each cutting station has a TV playing ESPN. They make you feel comfortable to be a guy. It's not a frou-frou salon...but if you're a frou-frou fella, they've got what you need (manicures, pedicures, etc.). The staff is friendly and good with customer service (they offer a free soda when you check in). Most of the staff is attractive too...not that it matters regarding a haircut, but it doesn't hurt, am I right? I would give them a 5 if the prices were cheaper. To me, $28 is a lot of money for a haircut. They do a great job though so I suppose they can afford to charge more than the average barber shop.

I've never been to a Village Inn, as we don't have one near us, but have heard about their famous pies.  We were greeted by Chelsea, a very personable waitress. The hostess/other waitress was friendly as well. I enjoyed talking with her and used some of her suggestions in my ordering.  She was really helpful in telling us about other area attractions and restaurants that are good (Michael's grill has halibut, for example). That's nice to have good conversation, especially if you are learning the area.  I think my husband actually likes this place better than our local Denny's (which is saying a lot...he's obsessed with Denny's, for some odd reason).  The food was delectable, reminiscent of my days as a child going to the local HoJo's up north, only the decor is a brown and orange instead of aqua and peach.  Not sure  why the restaurant only had one other patron, maybe because we went at 3 pm on a weekday. It's certainly not for lack of service, because they obviously can cook and serve with a smile!  Will definitely be back.

This place should change thier name to Bo 7 Mon An Hoa...cause damn they make a great 7course of beef. I'm sure the pho is good as well but I have not had a we'll portion, well tasting 7 course meal like this ever in FL. The wife and I had the three portion meal which is WAY too much for two people. They sure did not skimp out.  See you in a few weeks! Try them out people! You will not regret!

This place is the best.  We have been there several times and the staff and service are superior.   The food is always cooked perfectly and the pricing for a good steak house is right on. You get sides with your meal and don't have to pay a la carte.  Great food and gray place.

Huge letdown.  Arrived on a Saturday afternoon and the place was completely dead.  Ordered the okinomiyaki and some appetizers. Server took 15 mins to tend to us (there were only 3 other tables) food came out cold, the okinomiyaki was horrible. Not even remotely like how it should taste. The manager apologized for the service and cold food and compensated us with an extra appetizer, however the damage was already done.   This place won't last. Hire some people and improve your menu.

I have not been to every salon in the city but I think this is the best salon in the city so there!! Go see Tarik ! Thank me later ! When you first walk in your overwhelmed by the nice open feeling and huge windows. The salon is very fashion forward and trendsetting inside and out. They carry an array of great products and they also have the amber line which is one of my favorite fragrances !!!

I'm always on the lookout for a good pizza place and since we were headed to the Eskimos/Riders game, it seemed like the perfect opportunity to check this place out!   When we arrived it was PACKED, but thankfully we didn't have to wait (at all). The restaurant is quite large and there were a lot of Saskatchewan Roughrider fans (myself included) enjoying a meal before the game.  I ordered a coffee and we got a large 3 meat pizza. Unfortunately with it being so busy, my coffee cup sat empty for awhile. The pizza arrive in a reasonable amount of time, fresh from the oven & piping hot.   The pizza was very good, no complaints! I loved the chewy crust with the crispy/cheese edge. There were a decent amount of toppings, tasty sauce and lots of cheese. Perfect.  Our waitress was extremely busy, but took the time to compliment my Rider jersey and apologized for being so busy. She was very sweet and was clearly busting her ass to trying to do a good job.   While we were waiting for our food I saw a lot of delicious food coming from the kitchen. The steak & shrimp looked delish! We definitely plan on returning to try a few other items on the menu!

Visited the orchid show with my 2 year old and it was a great time! wish there were more golf carts to transport us with small children from the museum to the greenhouse. We went on a first Thursday = free admission! some of the Lilly house is being renovated until May, so we didn't get to go upstairs and the pop up shop with the Orchids was double the price of Home Depot ones (which I purchased from Home Depot when I left lol) Can't wait until the Beer Garden opens at the end of this month!

I've bumped up to 5 stars after having a great lunch from there yesterday. The samosas were great and the saag paneer was the best I ever had! Chicken tikka masala- excellent. A lot of times I find chicken in an Indian buffet sub- par but this is good quality, well prepared moist white meat.Cauliflower-potato curry was excellent. I love the food here, always fresh and tasty. Don't forget to ask for hot sauce; it's extremely flavorful without being too hot.Skip the naan. I had a Trader Joes paratha with it instead!

Finally checked this place out on a date and now I'm wondering why it took me so long!  Beautiful lights, a "big city" feel, great view, excellent cocktails, and complimentary popcorn flavored with truffle oil and sea salt? Sign me up.   We visited on a weekend night so it was a bit crowded, but the service was excellent. I was able to successfully order one of my favorite old fashioned cocktails off menu with good results. Great spot for a nice drink downtown!

Ordered 2 coffees and a muffin. Got one coffee and the muffin 20 minutes before the second coffee. Watched people who came in after me get drinks while I continued to wait for the second coffee.  How do you give half an order to someone and then make them wait for the rest while the first one gets cold?  This is not the first time this has happened.

Being kind in the review as it appears management who passed our table more than once totally avoided us.  Yes the view is the only good thing.   Ice tea old and nasty.  Fish so greasy oil could not have been hot enough.  Coating was hard greasy and gross on the fish.   Others who came after us served long before us.   Save your money and eat somewhere else
DONT GO IF MONEY IS AN OBJECT. BUT if it's not thus place is great. Excellent fish tasty veggies and home made ice cream with chocolate sauce. Drinks any size shape or color.  Repeat often.

I tried this gem a couple months ago. Their menu is small but satisfying. There is something for everyone. I am on a diet and the owner actually made me a delicious salad that wasn't on the menu to cater to my needs. The prices are reasonable and I'd definitely recommend this place.

All the positive reviews about this place are spot on.  This isnt a fancy restaurant,  it is more like a fast food restaurant on steroids.  The employees are welcoming and the guy behind the counter is absolutely entertaining.    Despite being across from Busch Gardens, it isn't in the greatest neighborhood.  I say this only because this lends itself to a steady stream of interesting characters stopping in to eat and to pick up take out.  The menu has a lot of food choices and we chose to share the gyro, a greek salad, and chicken schwarma.  Everything we ordered was nothing short of spectacular.  The prices are also very reasonable.   I will defintely be back for the food and the service.   If you haven't been to this place,  do yourself a favor and give it a try.

After hitting all the local haunts in the Soho area for food options, I finally decided to give this healthy "fast food" place a try. I only use the term "fast food" because the food orders come out in a quick and timely manner, I say it devoid of any indication that the food isn't tasty of high quality because I very much so believe it is and like the name says, FRESH. Housed in a strip of other eateries and stores, there is parking lining the retail complex which is in close proximity to the storefront.   The premise at So Fresh is that you choose a salad, a wrap, or a bowl from the menu. The menu descriptions serve as a base and then you add your protein, hormone and antibiotic free chicken and organic tofu are included in the price with hormone free beef and ahi tuna with a small up charge. Any bowl has the potential of zucchini "noodles" to be added on (I believe $1 for half or $2 for full bowl) or avocado for a nominal charge.   My particular favorite here is the superfood bowl which I have gotten with both seared Ahi tuna and organic tofu, both of which are delicious. I enjoy that the restaurant keeps bottles of Sriracha for patrons and I fee or really compliments the meal. There are fountain drinks available and they sell gluten free (I think) oatmeal cookies for a sweet treat. The staff is always friendly and helpful and able to answer any question you may have and works to give you a quality product efficiently. I have never waited for more than 5-10 minutes for my meal so I would advise coming here if you want good, wholesome healthy food and are in a rush, as long as it's convenient to you. There is also another location off Fowler which is their original location. I will be sure to return again and again. I really enjoy their flavorful options and inventive creations.   The interior, although not large is comfortable and has nice decor appropriate for the restaurant. I can attest that the restroom that they have was clean when I used it to wash my hands. Overall, a really great place and I look forward to my next meal from there.
The place is dark and noisy. Service is good but at Montecito pace, i.e. slow. The Greek omelette was totally bland due to lack of any seasoning. Potatoes tasted old but the bacon was good.

This place is FANTASTIC!! Family owned. Local food. Great service and a really cool/modern ambiance. I forgot I was just getting a quick dinner in Tampa. The sides are amazing. The meats are super moist, tender, and flavorful. They also have dessert! The seating is super cool - communal seating but a very family feel.   Hands down one of the best bbq joints I've ever been to, and I've done a cross-country bbq tour. We made the mistake of driving by so many times. Boy did we miss out!

Can't wait to come back here... The menu was fascinating and what an array of mimosas! Service was just superb and very cool artsy vibe atmosphere although a little noisy - not the best acoustics I had the sweet potato hash which was one of the healthier items on the menu ... A friend had the special which look like acfrrnch dip and fries and I really wanted to take some of hers... Another had the French toast with berries and it looked incredible .  I wish  they offered more gluten free pastries and breads and also some cappuccino or lattes. I look forward to going for breakfast or brunch

Stop right there, thank you very much, I need some chicken with big flavor and crunch.   Follow these steps:   Drive to Sassafras. Sit down. Order the fried chicken IMMEDIATELY (cooked to order, takes at least 30 minutes) Order some apps. Wait patiently for amazing fried chicken. Control your salivary glands when it lands on your table. You will want to pick it up with your hands. DON'T...unless you like 3rd degree burns. Finish whatever the hell came to your table first while it cools. It's cool. Snag a piece with your fork. Plate it.  Breathe it in. Sink your teeth into the juiciest, flavorful and crunchiest fried chicken you will ever have the pleasure of eating. Go home and cry because your meal is over.

Yummy bagels, soup, sandwich, coffee, and breakfast place. Very friendly staff. It was pretty similar to panera (bread co) just a bit of a smaller local chain. I'd have to go back several times to figure out all the differences. I think there would be some advantages to not receiving like frozen ingredients from corporate or something.  Not exactly my kind of food, but it would be good to sit and work or meet up with a friend over coffee. Always nice for coffee places to have real food options though.

Pita jungle has some of the tastiest food especially for the healthy conscious! Been here a few times ordered lunch one time and everything went smoothly. Also been here for team lunches. This time for my daughter who has never dined here. Started with the cilantro jalapeño hummus. Why would you not! We ordered the lavish turkey pewrap and a lamb gyro. Can I just say the lamb had a crispiness that added-a ton of flavor. The amount of sauce was just right! And let it be known that you could taste the freshness in every bite. I tasted the wrap and it too was crispy and full of flavor. We sat out on the patio, first time for me, usually I don't like to sit on the patios I feel like I might be missed or forgotten but not here! Service was spot on and everything came out as asked even down the lemon I asked for in my water! Really good and impressed with all.

I love it here. The nachos are by my favorite thing here. The food is always good. The burgers are great. The beer selection is nice too. If you are going during the weekend dinner rush, get a reservation.

Found this gem on Yelp while I was nearby picking up my mom and I will definitely be going back! We ordered the pad Thai and the Umami roll but substituted one of the fish for eel and it was very delicious. My mom and I are both very picky about our food and if it's not to our standard we don't go back. However, this is a spot we will be going to when we take a trip to Nashville! ALSO, the service was great! It was like 9pm and our server was very patient and friendly!!

If your philosoply is the more crap on a pizza the better, then you'll like Black Thorn pizza. And I am not a Chicago style pizza hater, but this stuff has absolutely no balance or finesse. Actually, it in no way resembles any chicago pizza I've ever had. Just loads and loads of gloppy cheese and a heavy, leaden crust that is quite capable of sickening anyone who would dare to eat more than a slice or two.  It just blows my mind that anyone who isn't thoroughly drunk and/or stoned would really like this stuff.

Absolutely loved the food! The decor isn't anything special, and our server was actually kinda weird, but it made it all the more interesting. We ordered two appetizers and entrees, and it was enough food for FOUR people! The food was totally delicious. It's BYOB.

This place is a fun spot off the main Broadripple strip.  It tends to be a mid-30s crowd which is a needed break from the 21 crowd of Broadripple.  This place feels like it belongs on Mass Ave, or Fountain Square.  I come here for the Bread Pudding dessert.  Delicious! :) Although this time they put way too much honey on it which stole the flavor.  Good beer selection as well.
Lots and lots of pies! Fried chicken, roast beef and typical cafeteria style foods. Good place for comfort food. Nice service people. Always crowded!

What a yummy place for handmade icecream! Lots of flavors, 5 or 6 different cone choices, fair pricing, and a very pleasant staff. Be prepared to stand in a line, it's a full house (in a narrow space)!  Only reason I wouldn't say 5 stars is due to the location. There's no easy way to access this location unless you're already downtown. You could get lucky and find a parking spot, but with 2nd street becoming more popular, it's a bit difficult to get to.  I enjoyed a scoop of cake batter with sprinkles. It wasn't really all that, but my boyfriend got the red velvet cake, and that was to die for! ABSOLUTELY delicious!!

Their food tastes very authentic and good. Definitely try their soups because they will warm your soul on a cold rainy day, which is prevalent here in Indy. When it is summer, their bubble teas are delicious and a great way to cool down.

Be on the lookout for this place because you'll probably just drive on by without even knowing it's there. The place was slow when my gf and I were there but that was not a hint in how our experience would be. I had the Special Dinner and she had the Camarones Rancheros. Both were great.  Ever since moving down to the area, we've been trying to find that classic, free chips and salsa, albeit not authentic, Mexican place. Well, we found it here. I love true authentic Mexican food but sometimes you just crave the good old americanized version. Huge portions, quick service, and 2-4-1 happy hour. Great little place, we'll definitely be back. *Five stars if the margaritas were a little stronger.*

I love rescue spa.  The staff is so friendly. The spa/salon is clean and very modern.  It's quite beautiful.  Hardwood floors, chandeliers, calming aroma- very Zen.    I've been here several times for waxing and am ALWAYS pleased.  My most recent appointment for the Lotus wax was with Chrissy.  She is an excellent esthetician and was fast, efficient, friendly, and fun.  All things positive when you're getting waxed.  The Lotus is $60 and is worth every penny.   I've been here before where my esthetician used blue (hard) wax- a bit painful but as they say "the juice is worth the squeeze".  Results are awesome.  The staff at Rescue are well trained and leave you feeling wonderful.  That's the point right?  Rescue Spa is my go-to in the city and no other place compares.

My family and I ate here after my graduation and I am so glad we did. At first glance I was a bit skeptical because the place is so small but we were sat fairly quickly. The service was great staff was very attentive to our large party.  We ordered hibachi, sushi rolls, and edamame which was all delish.  My favorite was the tempura lobster roll. Even my Granny who has never tried sushi enjoyed it. To our surprise, we received a $10 gift card for our future visits! Since this visit, I've ordered Tomo on Grubhub and it was still as good as it was in-house.

This is such a great breakfast spot to eat in the gulch neighborhood area. Parking is located directly across the street, and the first hour is free. If you arrive early, you will more then likely get seated promptly. The place itself has plenty of seating for small and larger groups. If you're looking to order and grab and go, there is that option as well. For starters, I was highly impressed with the service and hospitality. My drink and meal order was taken promptly and brought out even faster. I ordered a vanilla bean latte made with coconut milk and the hot mess. The aroma of the latte alone was mouth watering. Overall, everything was great, and had amazing flavor. This place is definitely a must try if In the Nashville area.

Interesting place with an interesting crowd given that I guess it's a speakeasy-ish kind of place, but that's why I like it. You've got your upholstered furnishings, paintings, and an aesthetic that fits right in with the other speakeasies in Center City. But the crowd is more Center City after-hours casual - that's not to say you can walk in with flip-flops or gymwear, but the general feel is that it's way less of a hassle to step in and enjoy a drink or few over the course of the night without having to endure long lines, cramped and dark waits.

Extremely delicious sandwiches. Great decor, diverse crowd. The mulfaletta is the best!!  Great service, nice owner. What more could you want?? Live Blues every Thursday and Saturday. Definitely coming back here more often.

7:50pm on a Saturday. Terrible service. Been here 30mins so far and no food, our order was just taken. Took almost 20 mins to get a glass of wine. The wine selection is really small and I would not say good. The wine we picked it terrible. It seems like everyone in here is waiting for something. The food was good, however no one ever came to check on us. Like at all. We are still currently waiting for the waitress to come out. It is very awkward in here, considering my table and 1 other table of 2 are left. Watching everyone clean.

This place is a great location with an awesome vibe! I appreciated that they knew we were in a hurry so they got us in and out quickly and worked with us on running late. Heads up! The Asian shrimp appetizer is SPICY!! It was too spicy for most of us!

I hadn't been to this Marathon in about 2 years. Since then the prices have risen but I found the service is better than it used to be. I will not be returning though.  I had forgotten my green card and they were unable to give me points for the two expensive breakfasts I had. It's not worth the price anymore. I will go back to frequenting Le pain quotidien. It has less options but the food is organic, tastes better and about the same price now!

Went for lunch and thought food and portions were really good.  Besides the bread, they seriously gave us 1 slice for each person.  I am like Oprah, "I Loveeee bread!".  Service was slow and the place was not even busy and not too fond of their dirty looking plastic woven placemats that were on all the tables, do those things even get thoroughly cleaned between customers?  With all that being said, If someone twisted my arm, I would probably go again for lunch or breakfast (and discard my placemat).  I don't think I would ever go here for dinner though, seemed just like a Cheesecake Factory, but way more expensive.

Came here because I saw they had a check-in offer for a free slush cooler with boba (bubble tea?) while we were next door. So we ordered a taro w/ boba, lychee w/ boba and rainbow jelly and bought a siopao (steam bun) and ube (purple yam).   Service- was good...I guess...Although we did stand at the wrong end of the counter for about 5 minutes before another customer told us we ordered from the other side. Not sure if employees saw us or not. Lol. The lady at the counter was friendly enough. The guy giving us our slush coolers seemed less than enthusiastic.   This review is going off just the slush coolers (boba tea??). Not as good as other places. I feel like there should have been more boba and my taro flavor was not very distinct. The lychee one was good though. Very refreshing and the rainbow jelly was yum! Unfortunately, I did not get to try the ube or siopao. We left the ube on the kitchen counter for a few hours while preparing dinner, so I think it was my fault- I should have put in the refrigerator. But when I went to open it and try some, it was kind of watery on top and didn't look appetizing. My husband (who is Filipino) ate the siopao when he got home from work and said it was just "ok."  Interior is nice and bright, modern looking and there seemed to be a good constant stream of customers coming in. Will definitely have to try some of their food specials and hope to update this review and add some more stars.

The portions are small, but maybe only by gargantuan American standards. The fries and various sauces are all super tasty. After having it a couple of times, it has become one of my favorite burger joints in St. Louis.

This is our go to Hibachi Restaurant! All of our kids want this as their birthday dinner every year and we go when we're in need of an Asian food fix. Hits the spot when we are missing our fabulous restaurants from Boston. Sushi is always delicious and presented beautifully and the chefs are entertaining.  We've gone a few times at lunch for the Bento boxes too, a great meal at a great price. Definitely worth the stop!

Went here for a quick lunch with a girlfriend, we both got the rosti-pollo cemita, food was so tasty. The side of roasted potatoes were delicious.   The only issue we both had (maybe it just wasn't picked through enough) but we both ended up picking bone-type pieces out of our sandwiches near the end of our meals, so it wasn't just the meat in there, which we didn't appreciate at all.  Everything else was amazing, the service here was much friendly than their other restaurant Tres Carnales. I will be back for dinner, as I feel it was a bit on the pricey side for lunch.

Went last night since we live nearby. Salsa was really good with lots of cilantro, just as I like it. Chips, however, were really greasy. We ordered the chicken fajitas for 2. Really disappointed by it. The chicken and rice lacked flavor. The guacamole should have been called onion slaw because it was mostly onion with some liquified avocado mixed in. The tortillas were maybe 3 inches around and held one piece of chicken and some cheese. They were homemade, but kind of dried out even they were brought to us wrapped in foil. Don't think we'll go back. :(.

Really, really warm in the concert hall, last night. Instead of reading the magazines they give us, when we sit down, most people were using the magazine as a fan... and there was a lot of "fanning" going on..  I sat behind the orchestra for the concert, last night. Good seats, can watch all the musicians do their thing, talk to one another, drink their water... Lots of stuff going on that is fun to watch.  Again, acoustics are good, seating is good, seats are comfy, plenty of legroom, lighting is good... my only gripe was it was so damn warm, last night...  I have tickets for a February concert and believe you me.. I will be wearing something summery, so I don't sweat my ass off.
Service was very slow. It was Labor Day and around 2:30. It is now 3:30 and our food JUST arrived. Had to ask for napkins and silverware. My salad was sent back as it was dead lettuce.

This is a super cool place . Have been wanting to go here for so long and glad I was able to go here today!! Good lunch menu !! I had a big glee to see so many veggie options (obviously it's a veg/vegan place). The food was yumm !!!  The service was prompt . The place is so cool , bright and fresh .. loved the clean design . They also have an outdoor seating which is an aded

Returned today. Had a delightful veggie sandwich. It was really the best I have ever had. On a soft bun, the Panino Fresco will not disappoint. Nestled inside is a slab of fresh mozzarella with their delicious olive salad, tomato, onion and a vinaigrette. Wow, it was quite delightful!!  I think this is my all time favorite sandwich place in St Louis. A great place in Benton Park. Well worth the drive- only about 15 min on 44. The owner quite a nice fellow. Enjoy!!

I love Guillermo, employee 3232218, he is always so nice and helpful. He has a wonderful way of brightening my day.   They keep my car sparkling in the Arizona sun.   Thank you, Mister Carwash, and especially Guillermo.

No surprise at the negative reviews. Came here a few times with some coworkers for a quick lunch.  Never a hostess up front, you have to wait a long time or just seat yourself.  It's never been good but our last visit was definitely our last here. Server was complaining the entire time how tired she was and how she had a work a double.  Moreover, the place was very dirty and quite empty for the time of day.  I could see why! Food was below average, I left the majority of it.  We asked for the manager.  Waited about 10 minutes for his arrival.  Totally arrogant and cocky, a real jerk. Could care a less about anything we said.  Talked down to us, like we were beneath him.   Amazing that type of person can hold a so called management position.  Doesn't say much about Applebee's. Will never return here and neither will my co workers.

I am so happy that this place opened an additional location in Clayton because it fantastic! I had the Fatty B and added spinach.  The meatballs were sliced so it was easier to eat and the calzone was filled to the brim.  It was also big enough for me to have a meal the next day which is always a bonus.  My boyfriend and I split the Smokehouse salad which I would get again today if I could leave work.  The butter lettuce and housemade BBQ ranch is really what made the dish excellent.  We ordered at the counter and had a beer while we were waiting - really great selection of craft beer and friendly staff.

HELP!!!! PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE DO NOT GET RID OF THE COOKIE CUPCAKE!!!!!! I was bummed to show up to get the cookie cupcake for my birthday but they had already sold out for the day!! However the staff member proceeded to tell me that they were planning to take it off the shelves THIS MONTH!!!!!  I don't know if this cupcake is already off the shelves but if so... I might have to take away all the stars ya know?!  Since they were out I decided to try the Tequila one, which also I heard was leaving at the end of May (insert very sad face)!  G-U-Y-S it was so good!  I would recommend that one too, but hey if they discontinued that one too, then let's just board the place up, it's done.  I have tried several of the different flavors, as well as the ice cream, which I do love. I have yet to try a cookie though.   Sprinkles you have my heart, and my pocketbook.  I also must say PLEASE SIGN UP FOR THEIR PERKS if you haven't already. It's worth the free cupcake on your birthday, even if you have to drive a ways to get there for the freebie. It's completely worth it.  *HIGHLY RECOMMEND*

Always a "Must Stop" when I'm in town. Get here early because there'll be a line before you know it. I always sit at the bar because there's usually not a wait. Try the 1/2 grilled 1/2 fried alligator plate. The Shrimp Po Boy is always delicious, think fresh lightly battered oysters surrounded by some of the best bread you've ever tasted. The Shrimp Gumbo  is some of the best around. Tuesday night is $1.50 Abita Amber drafts. Close to the airport and within walking distance of the Fairfield Inn.

Pretty decent banh mi. We loved the fresh baked bread, although would've liked it spicier and more acidic- the slaw was a bit boring. More pate would have been nice as well. This is definitely not the place to go for ambiance, it's a counter tucked inside a convenience store but the food is better than you'd think from the atmosphere.

I came to Sushi Rose to celebrate a friend's birthday (free AYCE on your birthday!) and I was pleasantly surprised. The food was really good. Many rolls come with cream cheese - which I LOVE! Overall, we found that the sushi rolls were good but the fried ones were definitely not exceptional.   The service was okay, nothing spectacular, but there was nothing to complain about. They have a friendly staff who manages to keep up with the busy environment. I'll definitely be going back, especially for the birthday perks.

The best tuna burger outside of Kazakhstan.  Nice wine list.  Cool decor.  Hats hanging from the ceiling!  *Do not stand on the bar to put your head into one.  They frown on this.  Found out the hard way.  Staff is very friendly and welcoming.  Would like to try the inn but would need to find a big reason to do an overnight in Doylestown. (the dusk 'till dawn rave scene has apparently fallen out of favor there)  They have a cool lower floor but it's closed off during the day - the only time I've been there.  Would like to give it a try some night.  Appears dark and brooding.    Seafood tacos rock, as well.  Very clean and tightly run outfit.
Friendly staff. Coffee was really good. Another great addition to the city. Looking forward to returning!

If Harry & Izzy's is a indicator of how could St. Elmo's can be, then I can't wait to eat there this year!!  I have only been for lunch their, but our service has always been spot on.  The Shrimp Cocktail is, of course, worth the hype.  Steak sliders?  Yes Please!  House starter salad!  Oh, gawd!  I do love dining her during one of my many trips downtown for lunch dining.  I thank god everyday for my job downtown because I have the opportunity to dine at great restaurants like Harry & Izzy's.
Great pizza place with a nice outdoor seating area. Thin crust, flavorful toppings and great salads.
Do not eat here. Overpriced and the food is disgusting. $4 for a glass of iced tea. Chicken wings were nasty,  no flavor.  I think they were boiled.

Happy fish, take some notes. The ceviche at Cuenelli's is amazing! The fish is cut into small pieces, little to no membrane on the fish, and the ceviche is flavorful and it is refreshing. Every bite allowed my tastebuds to go on a new adventure, from spicy to zesty to tangy. The onions and the accompaniments to the ceviche were also amazing and fresh. The fried yuca was crispy and although lacking in flavor, it was delicious when it was dunked into the juice of the ceviche. The service was attentive and the ceviche came out in ten minutes. Unlike a lot of Latin American restaurants, the exterior and the interior had a clean and modern look. Overall, the best Peruvian restaurant in Tampa!

Would not return. Grilled pork was like warm. Obviously microwaved. Sent it back. Microwaved it again. Got it back hot. But it was one of the toughest, dryest cuts of pork I have ever had. Terrible. They also gave us chicken instead of beef for our other dish. The only thai iced tea they offer is in a can. It was clean and the furnishings were up to date. That's the reason for two stars. But they seriously need to rethink food and service quality if they are going to stay in business.

Think I found my new spot in town.  Ish is the man if you're looking to get groomed up!  I came here to clean up before an interview for my grad program, and the barbers here proved great at doing just that.

This is the second time we have eaten here. Both times the food was excellent, from the fresh, warm chips w/ both salsa and bean dip to the delicious chimichanga. They would've gotten 5 stars except for they sat us in the bar. Usually that would be fine, but today we felt like we were in the employees break room. The LOUD bartender (Leo) wouldn't shut up and the waitress was only a little better than okay. At times there were as many as 5 or 6 employees standing around-even climbing up onto one of the booths right next to us putting up Christmas decorations. That didn't change the fact that this is the best Mexican food we have found in NOLA. Just wish they would pay attention to details-$34 for lunch leads me to expect a little more professionalism.

Super friendly and helpful staff, and be prepared to get dirty as you're provided a bib, gloves and scissors to tackle the hot delicious bags of seafood that come out. You can choose the type of cooking broth, spice level and order seafood by the pound. We rounded out our seafood boil with corn, sausage (small amount of thinly sliced garlic sausage) and potato, seafood rice (a decent amount of shrimp and scallops in buttery rich rice) and crispy calamari. If you prefer not to have a mountain of seafood carnage on your table, you can ask for a bucket to toss it in.. almost everything is composted, so you can feel good getting messy and filling up on seafood! Only downside is this place is smaaaaaall, so they dont take reservations.. try to pop in during an odd time and you may luck out and get a table without waiting for hours.
My favorite pastry spot in the RTM.  Biscotti is amazing, they serve La Colombe Coffee and they never run out.  Short and sweet.
Very good sushi spot, tucked away in a small strip mall. Gyoza and the Spider roll were top notch.  Enjoyed it thoroughly.  Staff was very friendly.  Fyi, this is a byob place.

We stopped in last night for dinner and really enjoyed this place. The food was great as was the service. We did find their prices were a little high given the quantity of food you get, but we are looking forward to trying their lunch buffet which is a much more attractively priced option.

The gator gumbo is pretty solid - rich with flavor but a little on the salty side. You can definitely tell that it's made from a well-loved recipe. I would highly recommend this dish for anyone who's first coming to Reading Terminal!
I wish there was a time I went here that I didn't have to deal with a hellacious line that doesn't move. I love the salads and the beg cheesesteak is a solid option.

Great doughnuts, bagels, & coffee. We enjoy hitting this place for their breakfast bagel sandwiches and the occasional doughnut. Their coffee is great as well. We enjoy the multitude of different flavored cream cheeses they offer. The maple bacon doughnuts are to die for, but usually sell out quick. Best bet is to reserve them the day before if you would like to try them.

Great deal for happy hour -- fifty cent raw oysters, one dollar fried oysters, 50% off drinks.  Unfortunately, other than the oysters and drinks we were a little disappointed. The Etoufee was moderately tasty but very small for the prince, the complementary bread was frankly inexcusably stale and un-appetizing and the foie gras terrine was on the edge of frozen when it came out.  Come for the happy hour drinks and oysters but skip the rest.
Every coffee I have had from here has been delicious. Can't wait to try the cupping class they offer!

I went to this brewery on a late night on a Friday and the place was still packed. I ordered the space dog IPA. It was great, we tasted a few of there other beers, including their green beer which was interesting. My one complaint about the place is that all of their beers were unfiltered so if that's not your thing then I wouldn't recommend coming here, but if you do like that then definitely check this place out.

I've heard SO many great things about this place and I was finally able to try it today. We arrived around 3:30pm and the place was empty... like no customers, no staff members. We walked further inside, past the hostess stand back towards the bar before someone randomly passed by and noticed us. The female who noticed us was very kind, welcoming and apologized for the wait. She sat us immediately and explained the "all you can eat special" and went to find our server. Well, about 10ish mins later she comes back and apologizes again saying she cannot locate our server but would get us drinks. Finally, our server was located and came back with the girl to deliver our drinks and took our order. We ordered General Tsos chicken, tuna avocado roll, California roll, Philly roll, yummy yummy roll and the Philly roll. Everything was good, except the sushi has a fishy after taste. Tuna shouldn't be fishy. I expect salmon to have a slight fishy taste, but not tuna. So, the all you can eat is cool but there's a catch- YOU HAVE TO EAT IT ALL, or else you'll be charged a surcharge and you cannot take anything to go. Luckily, since it was our first time eating here we were cut some slack and were able to take some food to go and not charged the surcharge. I still can't get over the fishy taste of the sushi so I dont know if I'll be back.. I'll have to think about it.

This place has grown on me over the years. I find myself going here more and more, despite the (fantastic) proliferation of coffee places in the northern Camden County area. It's just a solid place to come back to again and again, and hopefully this'll give you some insight into why.  Let's start with the seating. It's got a lot of different areas to hang out; aside from two large seating areas on either side of the counter, there's an awesome courtyard right on the corner of Haddon and Redman Avenues and plenty of sidewalk seating out front for the warmer months. Not enough places in the suburbs have outside areas for hanging out, so these spots for people - and puppy - watching is much appreciated.  Their coffee's always been pretty good, and I think they keep switching up what beans they get. From what I can remember, I've seen Rival Brothers, Square One. They do pour overs, which I love, and they've got some more exotic things sometimes, like the Gibraltar (a variation on a cortado) and the "Americola", a fascinating mix of Mexican coke and a shot of espresso. To eat, there've got a variety of pastries like muffins and homemade pop tarts and quiches.  Also, it's worth noting that this is one of the only coffee places open on a late Sunday afternoon/early evening worth going. There're a lot of coffee places around here, but they all seem to close between 1 and 3PM. For years now, Jersey Java's open open til 6PM on Sundays, which I really appreciate. I don't know if the other places think people just hide out at home on Sunday afternoons or what, but it's great this place stays open.

My favorite -- the chicken pot pie on Sundays and their herbal iced tea!  Much of the rest of their food is good -- I try to avoid the fried foods as my heart isn't appreciating those these days, but the sweet potatoes, cauliflower (OMG -- yum), and banana pudding are pretty darned good. Definitely give this place a stop if you enjoy good old comfort food.

Friendly staff, great coffee and snacks, wine, new art displays on the walls every month, tables and lots of space. We love this place. Thanks for making it homey and funky. The NOT Starbuck's small, local, human place.

Busy place.  I was disappointed since the reviews were so high I thought it under delivered.    We came in on a Friday morning around 10am.  I had the French toast and it came out cold. The syrup was cold also, but I ate everything.   The fruit was yummy but I was surprised they would cut up and serve unripe cantaloupe. I ordered a cup of drip coffee and the coffee was excellent.  Serve your self water, it was unfiltered and tasted nasty.  After we place our order and paid and tipped....we were told food will take 25 to 30 minutes to receive.  This would have been nice to know prior to ordering.  My son had the build your own potato with gravy and my husband had same with salsa.  They  both seem to enjoy it but both mentioned they didn't have enough potato?  One star for coffee and one star for the good location. Bathroom was dirty.

Amazing sushi! It tasted fresh and yummy! We brought our own wine and ordered a few appetizers to share but when it came to sushi we did not want to share! That's because it was delicious! Will definitely be back!

I almost hate to write too glowing of a review for this place because I worry people will discover it and the lines will become ridiculous! But it's really too good not to share. A friend and I stumbled across this place one day and it's now become our go-to. Whatever you do, GET THE TILAPIA BURRTO. Don't let the captain crunch scare you because somehow it works. The short rib tacos sell out if you arrive too late in the day, but I actually prefer the chicken anyway. And the curry is sweet, savory, and piping hot. The katsu is probably my least favorite thing on the menu, and I even love that. Everything from the tortilla to the rice, and even the garnishes and sauces at this place are perfectly made and balanced. DEFINITELY worth a visit!
Metered parking and busy place. I have only had their coffee but I am looking forward trying their food soon!

Tom and I stopped by for dinner around 5:30 this past Friday, there was only 2 other people seated at the time, the hostess asked us if we had a reservation and checked her incoming reservations then politely told us we could sit anywhere. After that it took 5 minutes before anyone acknowledged us again despite the place being empty and there being 2 other serving staff but I did notice a couple of people coming in to pick up take out. The decor is quaint put the place is small and tables are close together so as it began to fill up it did get a little loud. When we finally did order we started with the Duck Fries and Mac and Cheese. The Duck Fries were good, the portion is definitely enough for 2-3 people. I was disappointed with the Mac & Cheese it was just ok  it reminded me of Kraft Mac and cheese it had a runny cheesy consistency I was looking forward to a baked Mac & cheese. Tom like the Mac & Cheese so it really is about your personal preference. Our server read off a long list of specials & I was impressed that she remembered them all,me on the other hand I forgot them & ended  having to get her to remind me again because she disappeared for 10 minutes. I ended up ordering the blackened ravioli which I found out when they brought them out had hot peppers on them, they kindly took them back so I ordered the Ribeye which was worth the $40.00 price tag. Not only was it cooked to perfection the seasoning was out of this world. Since the specials are recited to you and not written down I was surprised when I got the check at the $40 price but it was a good cut of meet that was accompanied with some potatoes and mushroom. Tom had the Northeast Burger and said he liked the Burgers at Gallo's Burger Barn better, again it is all about preference. In conclusion I had a good enough experience that I would try the place again Tom said he would pass, he felt like it was too loud, wait staff could have been more attentive and specials should be written down with the prices published. We both mentioned that to a gentleman who may have been the manager and he said we can always go to their Twitter page to look them up. Not the response that I expected but this is the age of Social Media & I don't think the gentleman was trying to be rude. Sorry I forgot to take photos we were starving by the time we got our food.

SATCOOOOO  I've been coming here since I can remember, my dad has been coming here since he went to college, etc. Whenever I want bomba$$ tacos I come here. It's cheap, tortillas are made in house, healthy? (depends?), delicious, and fast. And it's open late which is highly convenient for late night taco cravings. Not to mention classic rock songs always playing :) My go to tacos are the grilled chicken and fish. The enchiladas are also great! The sauce they use makes my heart happy. Sometimes I'll get enchiladas and tacos and dip the tacos in the sauce. YUP. I also enjoy putting queso in the tacos. And on the rice. And everything.   Come here if you like tacos and a happy atmosphere!

Solid food with really friendly service. I've tried the sweet potato chicken curry and chicken katsu. Would recommend both but if I had to pick one katsu wins. Usually a big crowd so expect to wait at least 20min

Have been tons of times before - brunch, nights out on the patio for drinks, dinner. Went back last night with my daughter and we had an absolutely fabulous experience. Service was stupendous from the time we were seated by Paige through our server Mahim. He was attentive, friendly, gave a great recommendation on my meal, and was awesome from start to finish. Menu is improved and massive, dining room has been recently updated again, and the beer list is even better then the last time I was there. We started with tortilla chips, which came with salsa, sour cream and guacamole. Yum. Great kids menu, with lots of choices. I almost went with the special, but our wonderful server recommended the triple decker reuben, which was fabulous. Dessert was great as well, and we both had a blast. Wonderful night, looking forward to getting back! Thanks for a great time.

This restaurant is consistent with good food, new kinds of dishes, excellent choices for vegetarians, the best coffee in town and servers who Are always friendly  and efficient. Super nice patio To bring your dog who also get excellent service. One of the best restaurants in town.

Super-convenient location, but be ready for incredibly loud music until about 4 am if you stay on Bourbon Street. Obviously, this isn't the hotel's fault.....but the extremely narrow balcony on Bourbon Street is. Great spot, excellent coffee shop, and no complaints about service or quality......just need to widen and connect all balconies.

Sabrina's is one of my favorite brunch spots. I first went to one of the locations in Philly and really enjoyed it, so I was thrilled when a Jersey location opened up.  I've been here several times, and one of my favorite things is the ever changing and incredibly fun to read specials menu. They have had horror movie specials around Halloween, patriotic specials for 4th of July,  TGIF edition specials, and today when we went it was all St. Paddy's day themed specials.  We actually got there 20 minutes before it opened today (should have checked the website but we were being impulsive!). The door was open so we walked in and were greeted by a very nice woman, possibly a manager. She told us they didn't open til 8 and that the servers weren't in yet. We didn't really want to venture back out into the cold and luckily for us she was very accommodating and offered to seat us anyway. She even brought us water and coffee! So nice.  My boyfriend loves a sweet breakfast, so as usual he ordered the stuffed French toast. Fine by me, since it means I get to have several bites. The portion size is HUGE. I've never seen bread slices so big. It's stuffed with a yummy sweet cream cheese mixture, topped with bananas and syrup. We always end up taking half of it home. He got a side of scrapple and wanted me to include in my review the fact that it was a very large portion size, which he was pleased with.  I prefer a savory breakfast so I almost always go with a special. Today I chose "The Special Breakfast that Drove the Snakes out of Ireland". If nothing else it was fun to order. I enjoyed it very much, though I did ask for some franks red hot to spice it up a bit. It was a polenta base with corn flake crusted fried chicken, two over easy eggs (it was super delicious with the runny yolk pouring out onto the chicken as I cut it open), a fresh tomato salsa, and of course a side of their breakfast potatoes. It was very yummy, and similarly to the French toast I ended up taking half of it home for tomorrow's breakfast.  As always with Sabrina's, I'm looking forward to my next visit!

Whaaaaaaa?  Whaaaaaa?!   How many years has this place been here and I never wobbled my way into the door?  Whaaaaa?!?!!!    I have come here quite a bit since I found it, and let me tell ya.... the buffalo wings are rollin!!  Im not kidding, they are really super good!  The right size, right amount of batter, no feathers, and the hot sauce is actually kinda hot.  Made me sweat , well that isn't saying much, but its a good sweat and I keep coming back for more.  If you are going to someones house for the game or a garage sale or whatever, drop in here real quick like, they don't do phone orders, and order 50 wangs real quick.  You will be kicking yourself like I have as to why you haven't been eating wings here all along.  Oh...and they are ludicrous cheap

I've only been for lunch and sat at the bar area.  They have a good lunch special.  They offer teppanyaki style table cooking for groups or you get paired with other folks.  Menu is varied from sushi and tempura to miso soup, salad and entree's.  Full bar is available.  Enjoy!

Overall it was a great experience.   We had drinks at the bar and then got a table.   The margaritas  we had were very good.  They also served us chips and 2 different salsas and a bean dip.  All three were really good.   Our server was a young sweet girl and this was her first week ever serving.   She tried really hard and in time she will be a great server.  Getting drinks at the table took forever.   The food for the most part was good.  We tried the quail appetizer, the funego dip, taquitos and a green tamale.  The dip was the best.  The tamale could have been good but was very dry.  It needed a sauce. I had the poblano grouper and do not recommend that at all.  It had no taste or any flavor.  Extremely boring.  Hubby had a flank steak with an enchilada and it was very good.   A friend had bean soup and it was watery.   Another friend had the red snapper and really liked it.   Will come back and try it again.  Hopefully, this time my entree will be yum.

The Mr was looking for a voodoo doll to bring back for a buddy at work & the Yelp App came up w/ Voodoo Authentica  We wouldn't have entered had it not been for Yelp - the outside signage states it's a "cultural center" so we thought it was a museum but rest assured it's a shop & they have absolutely everything you'd ever be looking for, when it comes to voodoo  All kinds of dolls to ward off evil, protect health, promote sexual well-being - there are big dolls, small dolls, pencil dolls, doll-magnets, feathered dolls, stuffed dolls & this doesn't even begin to cover all the candles & scents & herbs & books & whatnot you'll find all over the shop  The girl working there was super-nice & allowed us to browse but was totally available & willing to answer whatever questions we had.  For providing education & every single juju supply one could think of, this place has got to be as good as it gets!

Have only been here for lunch a few times...and each time I ordered the Pork Belly Banh Mi which is braised pork belly, Cajun pate, pickled carrots and tomatillos, chipotle aioli, jalapeno and cilantro. Wow! I dream about this sandwich. I think it's the best banh mi I've had anywhere. I haven't tried anything else because I keep coming back for this sandwich. A friend had the pulled pork sandwich and said it was very good. Service was great, prices are okay. I'll update once I've tried other menu items.

Two stars and that is being generous! OVERPRICED AND ALWAYS CROWDED. Oh wait they do have a student discount, but it's only valid I think one or two nights a week! What's that all about? If you do make the mistake of coming, be prepared to spend close to $30 for two people and snacks.

The food was amazing! We are definitely making this place our go to spot for sushi! We ordered the Mexican and shrimp tempura rolls. They were excellent. The miso soup was on point. We ordered a bowl of yakisoba too. That was delicious! Overall, I would definitely recommend this place. We are going back for sure!

Donuts are good, but the real star is the food.  Their donuts and coffee are great- fun flavors that were seriously delicious and worth the diabetic coma HOWEVER their porkbelly breakfast tacos were what had us coming back for seconds. Be prepared to wait if you get there past 9 though! Highly recommend for a quick but super yums breakfast.

Baileys' Range is definitely worth a shot. It has a nice open feel to it (although when we went it was kinda hot upstairs). The drink menu was fun and all of the beers are from local breweries. I don't eat that much red meat so originally I was going to go with a chicken selection. I am glad I went with my choice...the LGBT. SO GOOD! I thought the portion was perfect and I think the prices are good. I shared the fries with someone and got the rooster sauce. Everything was really good and our server was great. I will be coming back and would suggest anyone visiting to check it out as well!

I attended a private event at Harry and Izzy's. The staff was attentive and friendly.I ordered a steak and it was perfection. The atmosphere is very comfortable and relaxed - perfect for big groups and social gatherings. I had a wonderful evening and would definitely come back.

I had wanted to visit Bella Pizzeria for quite a while. Located in a rather large strip center on East 82nd Street just East of Keystone at the Crossing, Bella Pizza is a four-location indiana mini-chain with locations in Keystone, Hamilton Town Center, Clay Terrace, and in Kokomo. Self-dubbed as a "New York Style Eatery," one can only assume it's because the restaurant tends to be dirty as it's certainly not a description of the pizza.   Okay, I'm sorry. That was a tad too snarky.   Bella offers pizza by the slice along with whole pies along with calzones, pasta, salads, breadsticks, a kids menu and some desserts. The location is next to a Mattress Firm  and not too far from Maggie Moo's. It happens to have a couple of accessible parking spots right in front of it, and while they're not quite ADA compliant I'd suspect most people won't have any trouble with them. You enter the front door and head straight toward an ordering counter. They do serve up several specials. I found their slice prices to be pretty workable, though I wasn't as impressed with their whole pie prices. They do offer delivery.   I ordered a slice of sausage pizza, two pepperoni breadsticks and a large fountain drink. They do have Gold Peak iced tea, so that was a definite plus. My entire meal came in at just over $7 - certainly less than you'll pay for mall pizza, though this isn't a mall and they sure don't have the convenience factor going on. You can actually order online.   I will admit that considering their location I'd expect it to be a lot nicer inside Bella. Yes, you have multiple tables where you can sit down and enjoy the meal and they certainly are decked out in the usual New York urban decor, think a grittier Subway, but it all seemed a lot more kitsch than I expected.   I've seen a couple Yelp reviews refer to Bella's pizza as glorified mall pizza and, quite honestly, that's an accurate description. The sausage was chunked, which is fine, but it clearly wasn't fresh and it was barely warm. While I expect this in a mall, when I'm actually in a functioning restaurant I do have higher expectations. The pizza wasn't bad, but there's definitely no way I'd go out of my way for it. The pepperoni breadsticks were warm, though they lacked anything distinct in terms of flavor. I suppose for $7 it wasn't bad, but since I was splurging a bit on my diet I really was hoping for a tastier splurge.   Bella's was semi-clean. It certainly wasn't divey, but I couldn't help but notice with 3-4 employees working and about as many customers that it seemed like socializing was a higher priority than actually cleaning or keeping busy. They weren't unfriendly and I didn't feel neglected, but with a little interaction with customers they could have really created a much more positive dining experience.   With Indy really possessing a pretty good pizza scene these days, it's hard to fathom a good reason to return to Bella's. The employees didn't seem to care that I was there and, quite honestly, if you can't serve me a simple hot slice of pizza then you're really not worth my hard-earned dollars. With Napolese in the mall across the street and Puccini's not that far away, when I want a good meal I'll visit them and when I want mall pizza I'll actually go to the mall.

The Pecks Smapler is a nice choice with boneless wing, mozzarella bites and fried pickles are a good starter or meal depending one your appetite.   The spicy Thai wings are a good side item to go with the sampler

Love this place! The espresso drinks are well crafted. The baked goods are solid. The ambiance is simple and clean, an excellent environment to chat with friends or use the free wifi. The service is good and friendly. The place makes the neighborhood better. On top of this, it's local.

Today I enjoyed lunch with one of my Sweetest Friends/Sisters (she's a foodie too). @hendelscafe was her suggestion and it was perfect! First of all, I love that this historic little building is tucked away in a residential neighborhood and the restaurant is absolutely beautiful inside and out. We shared the Crab Stuffed Mushrooms (not for those that do not Garlic), she had Fish Tacos, and I had the Salmon BLT (salmon and bun were fresh, soft, and flavorful). And of course we couldn't leave without sharing dessert: Salted Carmel Cake... I am not sure who their distributor is but I will seriously be stalking someone about that cake! Everything was delicious and on top of that, our service was great! #stlmetrofoodie

Went early on a Wednesday night between the holidays and it got packed quick. Wait staff was wonderful constantly looking for what you needed refilled.  I asked the waiter for chicken Marsala oddly not on the menu since the veal was. It is a simple dish that I use to compare Italian restaurants. Should have went with the veal, as they did not know how to prepare the chicken. It must have come out of the freezer and they forgot to tenderize it.  Shrimp scampi was good, as was the salad.  We had a group of six celebrating our kids engagement so they sharply provided a large serving of tiramisu which they make in-house and was worth the meal.  The bill was a bit of shock at $45 per, but you pay for the nice ambience.

It was extremely busy when we came on Saturday. It took about an hour to order and receive our donuts with how many people were there. You get an order form and can pick what donuts you want. They have some set donuts or you can do all custom. Despite the fact that they were packed, everyone was moving as fast as they could and they were all super polite. It was worth the wait! We absolutely loved the donuts! They were delicious and we will definitely be back.

This is the go-to place of a friend and I can see why. We ordered the scallop app and the meatball app. Would have preferred one more of each in the serving, but probably because they were SO good. Our Margherita pizza was one of the best I've had. We opted not to drink on a school night (lol) so it kept the bill down. I look forward to going back and sitting on the patio next time.

Classic old school train station that has Amtrak, Septa and NJ Transit trains.   I love it purely because of the architecture, not everyday you walk in a room that large.   I used to be here everyday because of commuting to my old job so now when I visit, I get pure joy out of nostalgia.

I do happy hour here pretty regularly. It's near work and there is outdoor seating, a key element for this time of the year. There's good people watching and most of the wait staff is pretty good. Shout out to Armando--you rock!! He was my server last night and was good enough that I'll be remembering to ask for him next time. That's a first.
