The happy hour prices are very reasonable, as long as you aren't too picky about what you're drinking. It's pretty much Coors Light, Yuengling, House Wine etc. but it's do-able. I prefer something a little more original.  SO, their White Sangria is fabulous if you are picking something off of the regular drink menu. Food-wise the Risotto Balls are a decent option off of the HH menu and I enjoy the Margherita Pizza as well.

One person who reviewed this place and said he got a big whif of stinky strong fish. I will disagree with that. It smells like a typical Chinese Buffet, nothing more, nothing less.  We were in the area, and figured we would go since it just opened last week, and we would be shopping in the area.   They have a hibachi grill, sushi bar, fried items, typical Chinese dishes like chicken and broccoli and veggies, noodles, etc. They also have pizza, macaroni and cheese, fish, potatoes for the kids. They have a salad bar, soups, fruit, and desserts, and ice cream. Same type of place different day.   Not horrible whatsoever, just standard and nothing out of the ordinary. Not too much seafood either.
Delicious vegetarian food and meat lovers go crazy too. My favorite is the Chawla Kadu. It's sautéed pumpkin over long grain rice. So good. And BYOB

I really enjoy this place for lunch.  They have very limited seating, which is kind of annoying since I have to walk a bit of a ways to get here from work, just to turn around and get back to my desk to eat.  Although, I will say that the few tables they have here is better than the center city location, they only have a few stools and a long table against a wall.  I really like to make my own creation, I usually get the salmon with various veggies such as the papaya, jicama, carrots, etc.  I also like either the spicy mayo or the peanut sauce as my sauce.  The only reason I have not trued the other sauces, is because there is no description of them to be found and the restaurant is usually pretty busy when I am there, so I feel like I am holding people up if I ask. The staff usually wants to get you order quickly and get to the next customer.  I also enjoy either the fried shallots or garlic as my crunch.  I do which there were more meat/fish options.  I would love to have more sushi quality fish in my sushirtto.  It is kind of disappointing when the only non-fried fish option and there are no real sushi fish options either since everything is cooked. However, even though I wish there were more options as stated above, I do always enjoy the salmon.
Great breakfast! Service was top notch!  The portions were HUGE! if you want a great breakfast stop in... good coffee too

Big portions. Too thick a batter ( I know some people like that. My grams did when she still had the good dentures.). Hardest onion rings ever. Decent fish Really slow service OOOOLD fashioned place.  When you walk in, you are not sure what's going on because their front portion is really dark and unusual. I thought I was walking into a bar at first. The manager told me that Guy Fieri stuck his head in when he was in town...looked at how dark the front was, and went over to The Joint across the street. I can believe that.  This is not the place to go if you want modern New Orleans fried seafood. This is where you go if you want to pretend you are back in the 50s when McCarthyism was great and breading was more important than what it covered (Just think of deep fried mushrooms or shrimp from drive-ins). Yeah. That old fashioned. Personally, I prefer to taste the seafood now.

The meal took quite a while to get. We ordered a pizza and calzone which were both pretty burnt after they finally came out.   However, without having to ask the manager saw we were unsatisfied with our meal and addressed the situation. He got us a completely new meal, that was delicious. I have been out here quite a few times and I would say that when done right the calzone is the best thing on the menu. The small is easily large enough for 2 people.   To make the expierence even better, the manager also brought us complementary cheese cake. Overall the meal worked out, and the food was fantastic. Very friendly staff.

Try this place...you will LOVE IT!!!!  Such friendly owners and extremely attentive and concerned that the food is to your liking...which is was.  Excellent dining experience.  The Pan Fried Gyoza was delicious.  We also ordered the Yaki Udon Stir Fry with chicken with the side of Miso.  And the Chirashi Zushi Bowl with Salad.  DELICIOUS!!!  All of it...we had never had Yaki Udon...the noodles and stir fried ingredients were flavorful and the chicken was tender.  The extra side of Super White Tuna Sushi was fresh and a generous portion.  A must visit.

Well it was Girl Scout cookie inspired beer day and they did not disappoint. I got the mint chocolate stout and s'mores stout in a bottle, while they lasted. I proceeded to get myself a 12 oz Chocolate peanut butter porter, that I loved. It was $7. The place was densely populated for the unveiling of the aforementioned Girl Scout beer. I stayed at the bar and chatted up a random customer till my porter ran out.  I like the layout of the bar and the beer was amazing. Do yourself a favor and swing on by for a cold one.

Saw all the reviews here so wanted to try them out.   Not to mention they were downstairs from my hotel.   Everything was good. Food had slot of flavor, servers were fast and easy going.   Price is good for the environment.

*This review is for the Sunday brunch only*  Any Brunch snob knows delicious melt in your mouth waffles are a must. These waffles were so dry, they practically disintegrated.  The same for the biscuits. Dry and nasty. Thankfully the sausage gravy was delicious and saved the biscuits from being almost inedible.  While the omelets were delicious along with a few other things, there was not very many options and definitely not worth the $25.  Gratuity was included with my bill beforehand which I didn't like because my server was missing 75% of the time.   The resort is nice and I enjoyed the man on stage playing piano while we dined but the brunch could of been better.

I really, REALLY love this place (for lunch, not for dinner). Not that their dinner service isn't great, it's just that I almost *always* go here for lucnh. The service is always great, as is the food. I always opt for the veggie burger (which is not vegan, FYI) and it never disappoints. Add a glass of sparkling and you have yourself a nice little lunch with great service. Love this neighborhood gem.

I don't understand all the reviews for this place. I've been twice, just to make sure the first time wasn't an off day for them, and because I live nearby and want to support local places, but I've yet to try anything from their menu that didn't have dry, chewy meat in it. You can get much better for the price elsewhere in Nashville. The decor is typical modern discomfort, most notably with tall metal chairs that are hell on earth for a short person to get in and out of. Service was friendly enough, but the food is what's important and so far it's lacking.

The drinks are expensive, but they are unique and delicious. The fact that they give you a little bag of truffle popcorn kind of makes up for the bill. Comfy seating, great bartenders, and nice decor. I could spend an entire evening here if I wouldn't go broke doing so.
A quiet place to have a cup of nitro cold brew while doing work or reading a book. The atmosphere is a lot nicer than the chain coffee spots in Newtown, and at a comparable price.

Cute little shop with unique ice cream flavours. Their cones are huge, so don't fill up in dinner before stopping by.   I swirled the pandan and watermelon together in a charcoal cone. They had enough flavour that the taste was distinguishable, but it was mild enough that someone new to Asian flavours won't be overwhelmed.  It's definitely worth a try. I will come back another time to try different flavours.

Germantown Pub was the sports bar I landed in to watch the Superbowl. They had a limited menu but the full bar. So I chose the wings and fries, which were delivered by a polite and friendly waitress. The hot wings were indeed "hot" but actually had some flavor instead of just tasting like "fire." Meaty and cooked properly, these were quite good. The fries were a little mushy though.  Two-for-one beer specials was a nice touch, so I enjoyed my two Blue Moons each with a big wedge of orange in them.  The sound quality was pretty poor. Too loud, a speaker that sounded blown and they were slightly out of sync. Still, there were eight or more TVs with the game on and lots of cheering.  I'd return to Germantown for a game but maybe not THE game.

I ordered the Pima Melt Sandwich is my SO ordered the Unforgettable sandwich. I thought my food was fine but a bit too salty. I've eaten at the Baggins downtown location plenty of times and never had so much salt in a sandwich. My SO also thought his was extremely salty and he even had stomach problems afterwards that I will not go into details for the sake of grossing other people out.   The service was pretty quick, but it could have been quicker if the drink machine was not located RIGHT next to the cashier in a corner. There was a line when I arrived and it was extremely difficult to access the drink machine with the huge line blocking access and limiting the space around the drink machine.   I highly recommend forming a line to the side by using arrows on the ground or somehow signifying to customers to form a line in the opposite direction instead of a straight line close to the drink machine.   I don't think I'll go back to this Baggins even though it's located so close to me.

Not bad, but not out of this world. City Limits is a popular breakfast spot in Bellevue with a roomy interior, outdoor seating, a large array of coffees, and friendly employees.  The extensive menu offers a number of options and specializes in the banini (various panini-style sandwiches framed by a "b"agel). On paper, the Huevos Rancheros banini seems like the winner, with pepper jack cheese, egg and bacon, and my favorite condiment on the planet, jalapeño cream cheese, all between an everything bagel. But there wasn't that spicy kick I had hoped for, reasonably flavorful but not the heat I anticipated. The bagel was blander than most I've eaten, which really surprised me because it's hard to mess up an everything bagel.  The Southern Banini was better, with egg, bacon, and cheddar cheese, but the most significant difference was the flavorful sausage offsetting the blandness of the sesame seed bagel. Would I eat here again? Sure, if I were meeting someone for breakfast. But I wouldn't go two blocks out of my way to eat here. Quite frankly, when I'm in the Bellevue Station strip mall, I'm heading straight for legendary Pizza Perfect!

This is beautiful little Parisian dessert shop. Very intimate. The charcuterie board was underwhelming and not enough baguette for two. Definitely not worth $25. Also ordered a brownie for $5.25. Again overpriced. You're paying to say you visited. That's all. Friend ordered the quiche and thoroughly enjoyed it. The free champagne with check in was a great addition. Great spot for an after dinner dessert/drink with a friend. Cute vintage cuterly,stemware,furnishings

We had an early dinner rez at 24 and were seated promptly after a friendly greeting! The menu is great with various apps, a great selection of pizza and pastas, and various mains. We loved the scallops entree and pesto pasta! The pizzas were good but nothing exceptional. we greatly appreciated the service and overall had a great dining experience! The drinks selections were great as the wine list wasn't vast, but we loved every drink we ordered! Will be back!

Super quaint little place between downtown and the fairgrounds. Parking is super easy and free. Went today for brunch. Menu is limited. But the bartenders hooked up to amazing drinks based off the things that we told him we liked.Will definitely be back.
I had the mofongo con pollo and it was delicious. Washed it down with tamarindo juice. Liked the atmosphere service and food I hope to go back soon

This is a true 5-star place. Great food, great service, great decor and atmosphere. Their food is so fresh they don't keep any freezers around! And the table-side guacamole is probably the best I've ever had. This was our second time here and they were amazing enough to make the special my husband absolutely loved last time even though it wasn't on the menu that night. Just amazing service and or waiter Andy was especially wonderful. Oh and, the margaritas are great told. Basically if you're thinking about checking this place out, stop thinking and get hungry!

Funny that this place has so many mediocre/bad reviews, yet is fully packed!  We were lucky to get seated right away.  Food quality is pretty standard for a bar - not bad but not amazing (had the grilled cheese & roast pork sandwich).  Our waitress was not particularly attentive and we waited quite a while for drinks and the check.  They have a decent variety of drinks however, the Stella on tap was definitely stale.

Had takeout from Marathon.  Meatloaf was pretty tasty.  Solid.  Turkey plate was hard to eat.  Actually, I didn't want any more after trying a bite.  Turkey was overcooked and dry.  Stuffing was overly salty.  Maybe the turkey didn't travel well for takeout, although overcooked is still overcooked whenever you serve it.  Somewhere between 2 & 3 stars.

Very impressed with this place and surprised I haven't heard more buzz about it. Everything I had was great and my whole party of six loved every single course, from drinks and appetizers to dessert.  My girlfriend and I arrived first so we waited at the bar. The bartender was friendly and accommodating and didn't mind transferring the check to our table when everyone else arrived. The oyster appetizer was delicious; the portion of four is perfect for sharing. They were rich and flavorful. Yum. For my main course I had the steak frites, which I figured was a safe bet but I was jonesing for some red meat. I usually get the fillet but they served a strip and I had no complaints about it at all. The fries were crispy and awesome. Everyone else loved what they got too -- the snapper was apparently very good but had a strong coconut flavor, so watch out if that's not your thing (like me). The only complaint we had was that my uncle said the scallops weren't hot. They were just room temperature so he sent them back to get them warmed up. I'm pretty sure they gave him a whole new order since it took a few minutes to come back to the table.   The service was also excellent as our waiter took care of all of our needs but wasn't overbearing or intrusive. I can't comment on the price, really, as I was treated to this meal. Entrees range from the mid $20s to $34, which was the highest price I saw on the menu. Shockingly, it was for a burger! But it did have foie gras on it, so I guess that's why it was so pricey. This is definitely a special occasion kind of place but I hope to be back again soon.

Whenever I come back to visit the fam, I try to make a stop at Gryphon. It's a great neighborhood cafe with a chill atmosphere, perfect for a meet up with friends or a solo work date.  I usually get their chai lattes, which are a bit sweet but spice forward (mostly cinnamon and something else i can't quite pinpoint). They have both soy and almond milk as non-dairy options. The Tall Man enjoys their cortados, and I've picked up their beans for satisfying coffee at home. One day we'll have to come back for the nitro and kombucha on tap.  They do have lunch options, but I've never tried them. Their pastries are a fresh treat, but often not gluten or dairy free.  Sometimes this place can get crowded and a little cramped, but I've often been able to find a seat and/or outlet.
It's as good as can be expected.  The service was friendly and everyone enjoyed their food in our party.  Not much more to say about this place. ;)  The do have a nice outdoor patio facing the river.

I've lived here in New Orleans since Katrina. It's a city of culinary delights on par with New York and Paris. Restaurants here have to be good to compete and have success.   One of our great seafood delights is char grilled oysters. I've tried them all over town, and the ones here are in a league of their own. Lots of tender loving care goes into all the prep work, and the actual grilling is an art form to behold.   The flavors come together in beautifully plumped up oyster heaven that hits my taste buds in perfect harmony. You have to stop in and treat yourself to half a dozen.  The service is attentive, the prices are competitive, and this local can't get enough.
This place is an absolute Gem. The cheeses, meats, and baked good are excellent! It can be crowded, but that's because it is an authentic market.

Was expecting a lower priced menu but quickly realized the potential of high quality food and service. Food took a while to arrive but when it did it was HOT and fresh. Couple with no alcohol can expect to spend  $45.00 or so.

The flat white was very good as was the pour over.  The hot chocolate we ordered for the girls was very late - in fact didn't even get made.  I'm not sure if the request wasn't heard by the cashier or just not entered.  Fun little place.

The atmosphere is trendy, kinda loud even in the back of the restaurant but it is a beautiful spot. The food was good but not amazing! We had the goat which was probably the best dish, the pig ears,  dirty rice balls, beef and shrimp. The service was slow but the water guy was on top of it. I really expected more. I would only come back to have cocktails and hang in the bar with maybe a snack and/or dessert.

Panera bread is reliably delicious. Use your owners card for discounts and free treats. I find the salads, even half a salad, to be a large portion. Some of the sandwiches are flat and sad, but the pasta is consistently good. I like how this Panera gives out numbers and personally delivers the food to your table. This location is a nice quiet spot for some along time or a business meeting.

Always a fan of Sabrinas in Philly so we decided to check out Collingswood location. Food is still delicious but I forgot portions are huge. We ended up bringing half our meal home. Coffee is still the best I've ever had. My only complaint is the noise level. It's too loud in there. We could barely hear our waitress let alone hear each other and we were sitting at a table for two! It sounded like we were dining at a football stadium during the Superbowl. Not cool!

Tried all the meats by becoming hooked and going here at least 6 times already. Take a few pounds meat home or get a good meal there with a large selection of side dishes to choose from.   There are 3 sauces to choose from all kept hot and serve yourself.  Try the brisket, ribs, ham and the sweet BBQ sauce.  This place is good meaning it will get pretty busy at lunch time and if you're getting the order to go order online.

By far the best sushi in south Tampa! Especially the bronze pebble and I like that they offer all types and forms of sushi, making it a very well rounded menu. Plus, the urban ambiance gives the joint a nice modern twist. Great for lunch or dinner.

Indiana City Brewing is one of my favorites in Indy! I love that they always have a light beer on tap, that is hard to find these days with everyone enjoying dark beers or IPA's. I only wish they had more to the space like games, music or something. But overall love coming here!

A regular staple for evening dining in chain-riddled North county! Warm quaint atmosphere & great service - perfect for a date or even adult family dinner. Cajun carbonara is very tasty, as is the grilled portobello - very generous cuts of meat & fish! All in all tasty fresh food & a great atmosphere in the heart of old town Florissant!

Yo the croissants here are banging. Probably the best in Philly. Great coffee and solid spot to get some work done. They also accept credit card, and it's such a clean and modern looking bakery. Everyone here is so friendly. A real gem right here.
Excellent coffee, great ambience, and friendly staff.  Oh and did I mention... the macaroon was absolutely to-die-for?  Great way to spend a weekend morning.

The atmosphere is nice and casual. The service was friendly. However, I was unimpressed with the food. The dishes we tried had a strong tomato base and it just made the flavors weird. The beef itself was nice and tender. The rice had stale grains mixed throughout. My bf didn't enjoy the fried plantains or his coffee all that much. We skipped dessert.

The Love is a perfect example of what two people can do. Aimee Olexy of Talula's Garden fame and Stephen Starr pair up to bring us this amazing new edition to the regions culinary scene.   The decor is minimal yet cozy highlighted by the arched brickwork in the ceiling. The cocktails are lovingly prepared. I highly recommend the $20.00 Manhattan. A custom made over proofed rye made specifically for The Love is the standout ingredient in this perfect Manhattan.   The food, not surprisingly, is well executed and was absolutely delicious. I enjoyed every bite of my Farmers Salad, Roasted Brussels Sprouts, and my Acorn Squash with red sauce and burrata. We finished off the evening with the Chocolate Slice and the Cookie Plate which paired very well with my glass of vintage port. I loved The Love!

Fantastic little restaurant on the edge of the Quarter near the French Market and the Old U.S. Mint.  It's clearly related to Saint Lawrence (on the other side of the Quarter) but this is more of a casual restaurant than bar and the food & service initially appear to be better (or at least more consistent) here.  On a recent weeknight visit everyone in my group absolutely loved their food - deviled eggs, red beans & rice, char-grilled oysters, fried green tomato & shrimp po-boy, and gnocchi.  Wow, some of the best traditional NOLA food you'll find in town plus they've got some additional items & twists.  Service was great (admittedly on a slow night), and while prices may seem a tad high initially you'll also find that the portions are huge and everything appears to be cooked in-house from scratch.  Plus you can get a real daiquiri including the signature Pimms Cup from Saint Lawrence and they donate $1 from every daiquiri plus some of the restaurant proceeds to The Roots of Music (Saint Cecilia is apparently the patron saint of musicians).  They've also got discounted oysters and drinks during happy hour from 4-7pm and they're apparently open long hours from 9am to Midnight daily.  This will definitely be a go-to place when I'm going to Frenchmen Street (where there's not much exciting in terms of restaurants) or the Old U.S. Mint for festivals.  Or when I have folks in town who want real New Orleans food including some of the best red beans in town.  Bravo!!

These are not your average Taco Bell, Moe's, Qdoba, and dare I say it Chipotle (sheesh I really love Chipotle) tacos.   Took a suggestion from a respected fellow Yelper, Christian S., and tried this place. Well let's just say I only got 3 tacos and boy did I want more. I already knew what I wanted before I got there. Online menus serve a purpose people, if nothing else it helps you narrow down your choices and if you're like me, it pretty much helps you walk into a new place looking like a pro. Back to the matter at hand, I ordered 3 shrimp tacos, fish of the day was perch, no thank you. And Mahi Mahi, is a little too strong for my subtle fish palate. So 2 glazed shrimp tacos and 1 fried shrimp taco it was for me. Took it to go. First I definitely appreciate the care they place in their to go containers. They serve their tacos on these taco triangle thingamajigs in the restaurant which help the tacos to stand up. Well lo and behold they have a cardboard version of it for the take out tacos. Major points for that, they seriously take tacos serious... So I ordered my tacos and a drink and I waited. A girl at the bar who was eating told me to place my number in the holder. That's how they identify you. It was appreciated but probably not needed only because the restaurant wasn't busy and when the lady bought my tacos out she called me by name. But the hospitality was gracious.   Got to my car cause I had somewhere to be, opened the box of tacos and one side of the cardboard taco holder had flattened and my fried shrimp taco was all over the place. No biggie I just put the taco together. First bite, the glazed tequila lime, or whatever shrimp taco. It was good, two nice sized juicy, sweet, perfectly cooked shrimp in the taco. I was in love. I had been searching for a great shrimp taco and I think I found it. I had one at El Camino a few weeks ago and it was abysmal, lacked flavor and totally disappointed my taste buds. Not this taco at Buena, was fabulous! The chipotle crema was really tasty and had a nice zing. Now I love hot sauce and was about to put some on mine but I'm glad I didn't because the crema provides just enough bite. Now onto the fried shrimp taco. Batter beware, really good breading and batter on these shrimp. Kind of tempura style so the shrimp aren't buried in the breading. It was a smidge salty but I am really averse to any salt at this point in my life so don't take that as a cautionary measure from me.   I forgot to mention that they have Boylan's soda on fountain tap. Umm yesss because this is my favorite soda ever, well that and Frank's and Dr. Browns, but these sodas are so yummy it was nice to see it somewhere as a fountain drink as opposed to the big box soda dealers...  My side notes and suggestions: They should increase the size of the soda cup. It's small for the $3 they charge, even if it's Boylan's. If you're hungry order more than 3. If it's your first time order 3, then order more if you like them because you will be hungry. I forgot to order the corn with mine so that may have helped stave off my lingering hunger after I ate my tacos. Finally, they have a rewards program. Really nice to have for a non chain establishment.   Finally, I expected these tacos to be good. I mean come on, it's from Jose Garces who had like the best taco truck, Guapos Tacos, in the city for a while. Plus pretty much everything he, Stephen Starr, and Michael Solomonov seem to touch or open in this city you can pretty much guarantee a good meal.   Went on a Wednesday night and it wasn't crowded. Municipal parking lot right across from the restaurant if you struggle finding street parking.  Very good uptake on fast, casual dining.

If you're in NOLA and want great food outside of the French Quarter, stop by Morrow's. I highly recommend the BiBimBap. The prices are great. Extremely friendly staff. The drinks are !! The location is small, but the food packs BIG flavor! The owner/proprietor, Larry Morrow, is Nawlins native and he's all about serving his community. I suggest getting there early, as they're known to have a line. Just know, it'll be well worth the wait!!

I came here on a Monday afternoon with my friend and we sat at the bar. WOB is your typical chain restaurant, however I was very surprised by how delicious and reasonably priced my lunch was. We both ordered the 8.99 buffalo chicken sandwich which comes with your choice of side, I chose mac & cheese. Wow all I can say is I was blown away by how good the Mac was. It arrived a small dish topped with toasted bread crumb coating. The mac and cheese had a kick to it as well. The buffalo chicken sandwich was delicious and crispy!although I was really hoping for some blue cheese to dip into it.   Unfortunately yards PYNK was tapped, so I ordered both sangrias and a cider. The red sangria was nothing spectacular. However if you like sweeter sangria, I HIGHLY recommend the white peach sangria. Ugh it was so good I wish I ordered that twice instead of the red.  All in all, my friend and I had a great meal. The bartenders were all very attentive and super cool with me sampling out beer.  If you like beer and good bar food I recommend.

Last year, they recently increased the toll from $4 to $5 for Passenger vehicles entering Pennsylvania.  And while there are discounts for frequent commuters ($6 credit to an E-ZPass tag for crossing 18 times in a calendar month) and senior discounts program, it's tough to shallow the increase knowing that there is a toll surplus.  That mismanagement by the Delaware River Port Authority and their fear of a lowered bond rating motivated the increase.  And this is while the DRPA executives and their families and friends were able to cross the bridge for free.  I gave an extra star since this 85 year old steel suspension bridge is still impressive to look at when light up at night.

Seasons is one of the better Vietnamese cafes in the St. Pete area. They have an extensive menu for a small cafe. You can get full rice plates, noodle bowls, banh mi sandwiches, and desserts. You order at the cashier, pay, and choose a seat to where your food will be delivered to you. All their foods are fresh and made to order. I've been here a few times, and the employees are all very friendly and accommodating.  I enjoy their banh mi sandwiches. I usually get the special, which has cold cut meats (pork roll, head cheese, and jambon) with the pickled veggies. I also got Brian's Special, which has grilled pork and egg. I love both sandwiches, and they are always made fresh right at the counter. You can also customize to your taste (for instance, I skip the jalapeño).  Seasons also has a desserts menu with something for everyone! They have shaved ice, bubble tea and slushies, coffee, and regular teas. You can get different toppings on the bing soo, like crushed Oreos, gummies, nuts, and various other candies. They cook their boba just right; they're not too chewy (I'm very picky about my boba). I ordered their Thai iced coffee and liked it. They put a lot of condensed milk in it, which is nice that they don't skimp, but it made it too sweet for my taste. Next time, I will ask for less, but it was a delicious drink regardless.  The inside of the restaurant is somewhat small; the tables get filled easily during peak meal hours because there's only about 10 tables inside. You can also take your food to go.   If you're looking for a quick bite or a full sit-down meal, Seasons Cafe has great choices for Vietnamese street food.

Can't ask for a better cheap breakfast. I go here all the time when I want good and cheap food. Its filling, it taste great, and the service is quick. Definitely worth a shot. (Standard diner food - do not go in expecting any frills)

Such an adorable little gem!  I absolutely loved the intense pink décor and glitter - I felt so girly and fancy being here - no joke!  Ordered wine and cake (the Hummingbird) while celebrating for my sister's birthday, and it did not disappoint!  We had a great time, and I'm already begging to go back!

Just walk up and order then go around and wait for your number. Small menu but some authentic Italian choices. We opted for a small bestia. The spicy sausage went really well with the arugula but overall it was just ok. The bites where I got the sausage, cheese and arugula it was really good but on the small pizza it was not a lot. The bread was pretty tasty and I really liked the oven baked flavors. The whole armature works is very loud but the pizza was pretty good. We sat at their counter and watched them make the pizza.  A cool experience.
HUGE calzones. Amazing salads. Lots of beer options. Plenty of seating. On street parking. Friendly staff. Had the Five-O calzone and it was great. The sauce was sweet and delish!
BEST Fish tacos in the area! I really love this place and come here often.  You cant beat the prices either!

Overall a well done place imo. Food has been good both times. Atmosphere is trendy and has all the usual offerings these types of places thrive on. Craft beers and specialty diet standard options etc.   Basically if you have a millennial with a beard  and love selfies on Instagram this is your heaven on earth.
I had the tuna sandwich seared! Fabulous sandwich to say the least. Friendly staff and very clean cafe. I would recommend this place for lunch!!

WOOT WOOT  This place rocks!! You can sit outside and enjoy a coffee, tea, or any beverage of choice. They also have fresh pastries and breakfast sandwiches made daily.  The best chai tea latte I have ever had. The sausage egg and chees on an English muffin is delicious as well.  GO! you wont be disappointed  cheers,

Overall: Grassfed Missouri raised cow with homemade patties! Can you ask for more?  Ambience: This place is located in a busy corner of downtown STL. Parking is street only, just like most places in downtown. When you come inside, you'll notice, this restaurant is pretty big. You almost never need a reservation but it is good to have one for larger parties. The crowd is here everything from families to college friends to tourists.  Food: The place offers Missouri raised grass fed beef. So you know you're getting good stuff. If you're not into beef, their chicken burgers are also really good. I will say though their vegetarian patties aren't as flavorful. I have been to very few burger places that can do a good veggie patty. After all, this is a burger joint. We hope you didn't come here for veggie patty. We asked for ketchup and they had a house made version. I'll say, I'm not a fan of it. It tastes like puree than ketchup. The sauces on burgers could use some inspiration. They have the basics such as siracha, mayo, mustard, bbq... But if you really want to set your burger apart, step up your game on the sauce!  Drinks: They have a pretty nice bar with TVs, if you're looking for a place to eat by yourself. With that said their spiked shakes are pretty awesome. If you haven't, you should try their normal shakes and ice cream too!  Service: It was pretty busy when we came here, yet the service was fast. Our server cared for what we wanted and checked up on us a few times.  Price: $12-$14 for a burger with no sides is alright. You could throw fries with it and charge extra for other sides such as sweet potato fries or onion rings. Still can't complain.

This place could be seriously addictive and it's more expensive than Starbucks. After seeing a few Yelp buddies check in I bookmarked this place as a must try next visit.   I did not know what to order and I had no idea that placing an order and receiving the order would take as long as it did, but it was worth the wait.   If you are a fan of the the Berry Chantilly Cake at Whole Foods or any other store in the area, the Chantilly cake snoball is a must try! The concept of cheesecake, fresh strawberries, fresh blueberries AND condensed milk in a snoball is simply ingenious. It was very rich but absolutely wonderful.   March 2016----My new favorite is the Strawberry Lemonade. It is a lemonade sno-ball with a lots of freshly cut strawberries throughout.    Nicely done.

Another great dinner.  My wife had the salmon entree --- DELICIOUS.  Really good served over a bed of sauted garlic spinach.  I had the traditional lamb gyro.  The presentation was a little different than I am used to but was fantastic. Different in that usually, I can fold these and eat them kind of like a fajita or taco.  This just had too much stuff on it to do that.  Wonderful mix of flavors and eating with a fork and knife was just fine. :-)  We shared a baklava for dessert and it was excellent.  Presentation is very nice with some sliced strawberry, toasted walnuts, whipped cream and an orange slice.  The additional flavors enhanced the baklava and was a nice touch.

This is my new favorite go to with my friends! They have a wide variety of food choices to choose from besides just pizza, but if you love pizza you're set! I usually start off with spinach and artichoke dip, and it's probably some of the best I've ever had. It's perfectly spicy and just amazing. I could go there just for the dip! They have a huge selection of pizzas and thus far I haven't had the same one twice . I haven't been disappointed yet and can't wait to go back to find something new to try. I recommend trying here at least once and then you'll be hooked!

First of all, I will add the disclaimer that I was going to dinner with my boyfriend's parents for the first time when we went here so I was not doing my usual fine-tooth-comb of the facilities and services offered.... as I was, well, let's just say more than a little nervous!   They had a great selection of red wines, and French reds and Napa reds are pretty much my favorites hands down and they had a lot of good ones. The Bordeaux and their house Cabernet are both delicious.   We all got different appetizers so we could try different things. I had never really had French food.   It was here that I realized I had never had a properly cooked scallop. I had spent my entire life boycotting scallops because they were gross rubbery balls of wasted calorie intake. Blegh! Who wants to eat something with the texture of an inedible gummy bear!? Not this girl. However, Provencal served us four perfectly cooked scallops in a delicious creamy butter sauce. I dipped my bread in the remaining butter sauce (I know, I know the chef was probably like "For real????! Oh I see my bread alone wasn't good enough for her!") but the truth is the butter sauce was just that delicious and renewed appreciation for properly cooked scallops.  I got the roasted red pepper bisque - absolutely delicious - it was on a prixe fixe menu for that night. Amazing.   For the main entree I got the steak and frites. The steak was probably the best steak I have ever had... I couldn't finish it all, and it reheated beautiful and it was excellent "brunch" the next day.   The dessert, that I can't remember the name of, was also delicious. I told you I was distracted... and a little nervous considering the context of the meal... LOOK! Just eat there, OK? I promise it's delicious.

This is one of the coolest hidden gems in Nashville. The cocktails are extraordinary and the bar tenders are so cool. The interior and architecture of this bar makes for an amazing atmosphere. I will absolutely be back, especially when I need to impress friends with a cool spot to get drinks.

Very good food for sure.  Wife's fried oyster banh mi was fantastic.., 5 stars for that.  I had the red beans and rice.  The grilled andouille sausage was excellent as was the cabbage.  The beans and rice was a bit too heavy IMO. Still, good food and excellent outdoor service.

These are some of the BEST cookies I have ever had. I bought a six pack of snickerdoodles and chocolate chips and ate them up faster than I should have. They are gooey and warm and definitely don't skimp on quality. I can't wait to go back and try an ice cream sandwich! I would recommend this place over Insomnia Cookies.
Very nice Tucktaway spot in West chester. Great service outstanding beer. Plenty of parking in an outdoor eating area that is dog friendly.

I went to the Twisted Tail after hitting a few other places earlier in the evening.  I was having a cool evening with friends and we happy to find this cool place to bring our night to a close.  It was super crowded, but we hung out and stood real close to a few people sitting at the bar and they finally got the hint and we grabbed their seats....LOL  Although it was busy, the bar keep and was patient and friendly.  I enjoyed the energy of the patrons and I think it was because it was different age groups here and that was a nice balance.   We ordered some appetizers and drinks and both we pretty good.  I had the lamb meatballs and friends had the cheese plate.    I would go back to really enjoy the atmosphere when it is less crowded.  I also like the neighborhood.

I came across Newk's Eatery at the Lobster Festival that was held at the Kapok Tree venue in Clearwater. I read good reviews about the restaurant so I was glad to see them there. The food was very good especially the Shrimp salad that my new friend and I had. Plus the Lobster Bisque was excellent.  However, the service was not. Closed to closing of the festival my friend who was a vendor there told me that they had lowered their prices. So I want over there. The manager told me the price for the shrimp salad went down to $7.00 from $10.00. So I purchased two of them. I should have been charged $14.00 plus tax. You would think so. Instead the manager charged me the full price and it came out to be $21.50. Big difference. I complained to the manager at the booth and she did nothing. I called the store later and spoke to Tim the manager on duty and explained it to him and he told me to call back on Monday. I call the store on Monday and spoked to the General Manager who happened to be the same person that was at the booth at the show and she said she would refund my money. I was only interested in the difference between what I was charged and what I should have been charged. The manager said she would give me a full refund. That was Monday. It is now Thursday and I am still waiting.

I have now visited this establishment twice and have thoroughly enjoyed them both. First the service is top notch and friendly. They are attentive but don't constantly bug you with questions. Both times I got great suggestions on the beer I might want to try and also on food.  I like their pizza a lot but their flagship for me is their Fish and Chips. The cod is top quality and you can tell as soon as you bite into it.  Cool Springs Brewery will definitely be one of our regular eating places.  Update 7/8/16 Have regularly visited this place now for 3 years and still love it. Besides the Cod and Chips I have grown quite fond of their Bangers and Mash. For appetizers I have yet to find one that I don't like. My favorites are Stuffed Mushroom and Calimari. Have never tried a desert because I have always been too full.

I had the special avocado eggs Benedict and it was delicious. It had a mashed avocado spread and bacon as opposed to a traditional eggs Benedict. It was very good and I recommend it. I also had the iced chocolate and it was delicious and refreshing. It tastes just like a chocolate milk with ice cream in it. I will definitely be back here again for brunch. Everyone was very friendly and it was a good experience.
Great local grocery store with affordable prices and friendly owners!  Go check them out and support a fantastic local business.

It is truly a shame! The food is great. I love their Chicago Style pizza and wings. The service is terrible. This has not just been a one time occurrence. We have had poor service the past couple times we have been there. It takes forever to get anything, and it seems like the staff is more worried about having a good time than serving the customers. You have to ask for everything at Uno Pizzeria & grill. The prices are relatively high considering the poor service we received. I do not recommend going to Uno Pizzeria & Grill in Oaks, PA.

The food, service, and management here are phenomenal. It's very nice to see a manager actually managing and directing, opposed to just standing around. The managers here also know how to handle a customers complaint properly and respectfully. This is the reason I gave five stars even though there was an issue with my visit. I came in for a late lunch on Sunday and sat at the bar. I was immediately greeted by a friendly bartender (Candy), and order a fried grouper sandwich with fires and coleslaw. The food came out very fast and everything was delicious. The grouper filet was huge, breaded properly, and not greasy ! The fries were seasoned, crisp, and fresh. The only complaint I had was a sticker being on some produce in the coleslaw (at least you know it's homemade). I brought this to Candy's attention and she immediately corrected the issue by notifying a manager. I was blow away at the level of service I received, it's a rarity and I appreciate it. Besides that little hiccup I was very pleased with my meal and would recommend this location to anyone. The beer is priced very well, and the food is excellent. The quality and service here is also better than the Dunedin location in my opinion. I'll be coming here more often.
Went to this place almost every day recently when I went for jazz fest. The oysters are really good and the gumbo was excellent, particulalry the first day! A must visit place!

A definite hidden gem in Indianapolis. We had never heard of this place. My husband was browsing to find things to do in Indy. This sounded unique and fun. It was wonderful! A lot like the old show "who's line was it anyways". The staff was very entertaining and courteous. There is PG show around 7:30pm. The adult show starts at 10 PM. They also were very well organized, and got the audience involves a lot! It was wonderful. Will be going back!
I love this place. .always Crowded and Great Food. The staff always take care care of their customers as well. And it's close to the Park to take a nap thereafter.

Yelp you've done it again! It was my birthday and we were attempting to eat at Acme but the wait was ridiculously long so we grabbed a table across the street...mistake. After ordering drinks we asked for the bill due to really poor service (think: server eating chips at the register next to our table). I searched on Yelp and discovered Luke so we took the short walk over. Upon arrival, we were pleasantly surprised. We were quickly seated and served water and bread. The servers were informative, patient (we may have had a bit to drink), and friendly, but more importantly, the food was delicious!  As others have said, happy hour is well worth it, we ordered fried, stuffed and half shell oysters along with a bowl of seafood gumbo for the table to share. BOMB.com!  I wish I had pictures to share, but the food was so good I forgot. I do have one though...

The mozzerella at the separate entrance next door is is good as I have had in a long time. Delicious, melt in your mouth and incredible. The deli next door has hard to find products I have not seen since visiting small towns in Italy. Great pasta selection, cheeses, olives and much more. The proscioutto was sliced razor thin and excellent.
Service was a little slow but building was cool, the drinks great and my fried chicken sandwich was literally the perfect bite.

I've been to my share of PF Chang's all around. Last night I was in the Plymouth Meeting one for the 1st time.  The menu has changed some since last being there with some good new additions. Had a fabulous meal, with hot and sour soup, steamed pork dumplings, spicy tuna roll and spicy chicken lomein. Wrapped it up with the banana spring rolls which were the best I've had at this chain.  The sushi was good, the hot and sour soup excellent, the spicy lomein was outstanding but the biggest winner was the dessert.  Service was nice as was the atmosphere. All in all only positives to say here.
Excellent! Original Chicago dog on the grill. Every bit worth the calories. Choice of fresh buns. I chose to have my poppyseed bun grilled. Quaint atmosphere and friendly staff.

Came here for Labor Day brunch with my boyfriend, and this place was not as busy so we were able to seat ourselves right away. However, service was absolutely terrible. We had to go up to the bar multiple times and talk to at least 3 different servers to even get waters. After putting our drinks in at the bar with the intoxicated bartender/manager, we had to wait another 20 minutes to receive these drinks and ask another couple of servers about the status of our drinks. The staff was less than apologetic about our wait, which frustrated us even more. After a while, I believe a bus-boy took our order and finally gave us our drinks. We ordered mimosas and coffee which were pretty good and decently sized. After the bus-boy took our order for the Piazza Omelette and the Breakfast Calzone, we received our food in about 30 minutes. The woman that ended up helping us said that she was a volunteer for the outdoor bar and didn't actually work at Cafe Piazza (ridiculous). Our food was a 7/10 with good sized portions and flavor. However, overall I was astounded how awful the service was. The only reason I give this place 2 stars instead of 1 is because the food and drinks were good. But that was also screwed up by the time and manner we received them. If your staff can't handle a half full restaurant, hire more servers or go through extensive training because this was the worst service I have ever received in my life.
$5 drinks is wonderful, if they didn't run out of ribs at 730pm on Thursday.  Wings and pazookie are good.  One time I got marinara instead of salsa lmao

Came here for breakfast as I was staying at the Royal Sonesta.   Went with the basics.  2 eggs, potatoes, bacon and a biscuit.  Food was done well and I really like the fact that peppers and onions were cooked on it.     I meant it come over during happy hour but never made it out of other bars.   Not a bad place for breakfast and want to try it for other meals.

Stopped by before the Blues game last night. There was a 30 minute wait but I think it was quicker. It was definitely hopping. Our waiter took our order right away because we wanted to go quickly so we could get to the game.   I started with a rosemary white peach boozy lemonade. It was delicious! Good sized and perfectly flavored. They have lots of variations of fruity boozy drinks, which I love. It was hard to decide what to get. My dining partners each got something different, and each liked theirs.   For my meal, I knew I had to try the veggie burger, which has changed since my last (dismal) review. Now it is described as "sweet potato patty, pecan butter, sage aioli, spinach." I wasn't so sure about the toppings but got it as is because I wanted to try how they had hopefully improved the flavors since last time. My husband and I also ordered a large order of fries with sriracha catsup and cheddar sauce.   Our drinks took awhile to come but the food was quick. And.... REDEMPTION!!!! This veggie burger was DELICIOUS. SO MUCH BETTER than it used to be! The flavors of the aioli and pecan butter were perfect with the earthy flavor of the burger. The bun was so soft and fresh. Just wonderful. I ate it all up. The fries here are really good, and the sauces also were. They bring bigger portions than they did the last time I went. The total bill for 2 drinks, 2 burgers and a large fry was about $40 without tip. A little pricey, but good for the quality and location.  The only bad thing was that one of my dining partners got a burger that was still mooing, but he sent it back and it was redone and brought back quickly. The others enjoyed theirs. I liked mine so much that I really want to come back soon to get another!
Awesome seafood. Great atmosphere. Line can get long and wait can get long but it's worth it. Best seafood in Kenner.

Came here for lunch with some of my co-workers.  They recommended the chicken tenders or shark on the lunch menu, so I ended up ordered the chicken tenders with Syberg's sauce along with a salad with Caesar dressing.  Service was fast and our food came out quickly. The salad was decent, nothing special and nothing to rave about. Kind of reminded me of one of those salad kits you could pick up at a grocery store. The chicken tenders were fried nice and juicy, but unfortunately lacked flavor. Even with the Syberg's sauce, I had to add quite a bit of pepper.  Quick food, but nothing spectacular.

We loved the cozy, welcoming atmosphere of this place. Brick wall filled with artwork, tables, couch, and a bar wrapped around the window with a bunch of art magazines lying around. The coffee was great as well. I can't believe I didn't realize this place was here. We'll be coming back often whenever we're in town.   I must say, they really do often have some of the most interesting artwork I've seen at coffee shops. Each time we've gone, it's been a new artist. We just purchased a few pieces from the current artist on display. I believe her name is Stevie something?
Fantastic choices of frozen yogurt with a nice selection of toppings. The staff was friendly,  the place was bright, inviting, and very clean.

It's been a little while since last I've visited The Depot, and this was the first time I'd visited for brunch.  Walking in, we were seated right away, got settled, and quickly put in our drink orders.  Half of our party opted for the Man-Mosa, a large serving of champagne and juice which is the perfect thing on a sunny weekend morning.  The other half of us, myself included, went for a tall mug of beer.  They've got a great deal on their American Pale Ale, where for just five bucks you can get a 32 ounce stein full.  Needless to say, I was immediately a fan.  Placing our food orders from their small but pretty awesome brunch menu, we were soon served up with all our tasty fare.  I will say this about The Depot; their food is top notch!  Every time I've visited, I have consistently been impressed with their fare.  A lot of their menu is for sure versions of pub favorites, but at a whole other level, and beyond these you can usually find something new and interesting to try out.  On this morning, my Fish n Chips were simply divine, and I was instantly addicted to their fries dipped in their house-made ketchup.  My companions likewise were blown away by their burger, and their pan-fried hash and eggs.  It certainly had me chowing down with gusto.  Now, let me contrast the food a bit with their drink menu.  It is great that they are a brewery and distillery, making them another great addition to our region's craft beverage scene.  I will say that all their beers are pretty darn good, even if they aren't really my favorite brews in town.  But hey, the stein special is hard to beat anywhere!  Their spirits are likewise good, and you can get them neat or in cocktails.  An addition they've made since last I was here is their Amer Picon liqueur, and seeing this, we of course had to order a round of Picon Punches, eager to see how it stacks up to the punches from places around town like Louis', Santa Fe, or 1864.  I'll admit, it was a really good cocktail, but seemed a lot lighter and just sort of different than what I tend to think of as a more standard form of our unofficial state drink.  But I applaud them for branching out with something not seen terribly often, and making it quite tasty to boot.  Should you desire, you can also purchase bottles of their liquors to go.  Another feature I must mention about this place is the building itself.  As the name suggests, it is the old railway depot, and they have done simply a phenomenal job updating and maintaining this historic building.  Open rooms, chique decorations, a great looking bar looking back into the brewery, and just in general capturing a warm, welcoming feel really makes this place a piece of eye candy.  The service here is always amazingly friendly and attentive, adding to that welcoming feel.  Parking can be a bit of a pain, though, as their lot is around back through an alleyway, a bit on the small side, and shared with the businesses next door.  Anyone exploring our local food and drink scenes should for sure add The Depot to their list.  Hopefully it won't be as long until the next time I visit.

I've been going here since they were basically a closet behind the Hub. Now being a Downtowner it's great to see how she has grown up to suit the locals needs. From pet food to pasta to great selection of beer and wine everything is there. Along with a moderate amount of fresh fruit veggies and herbs anything you need in case "you forgot" when you were at Publix. Now add in some gourmet chocolates and heat and eat meals that are well beyond your grocery freezer and cooler sections is why this place gets 5 Stars. If Publix is 4 Stars this place is a 5 :)

Worth the wait!! It is worth the wait!! The shrimp and grits made you want to swim in them they were so good. I would have licked my plate if I was at home. Yass!! Oh and the biscuit are so dense with a touch of fluffiness, you don't even need butter or honey. We tried the crab omelet and another item and they were excellent. Don't be discouraged by the wait. You are giving your tummy a treat.  What a reward !!

Had a reunion here - reunions make me feel old.   I have to admit that I wasn't TOO thrilled w/ the idea of the venue when I first Yelped the place and saw the bad reviews.   I shall be the first to admit I was wrong and that I was pleasantly surprised when I got there. The hostess was very polite (even though she was busy on that Sat. night) when she directed us to the upstairs area. The bartenders were friendly and paid attention.   The cost of one of these gatherings (top shelf liquor and 3 finger foods selected from 6 or more choices) was $40 per person. What really made me warm up to Paddy Whack's was that they were very flexible about the arrangement. If you couldn't/didn't want to partake in the food/drinks, you didn't have to. Those who did, got a green wrist band. Those who only wanted to eat could pay $20 and go at the food. Those who came late to the party ($40 for drinks lasted 3 hours only) could opt to do a cash bar only.  I will say that the food (in steaming trays) didn't look super amazing, but then again, Paddy Whack's isn't exactly advertising it as gourmet food either.  I didn't try it, so I have no opinion on whether they were yummy.  I'm very thrilled about the flexibility they gave our party and would definitely recommend them as a venue (provided the flexibility remains).  Thanks for hosting our fun evening!
Just average and the service isn't good. The older woman behind the counter is rude and pushy. Too bad as it used to be really good. Veggies were ok.  Broth was unseasoned.

I had much higher expectations. A couple flies were bothering me the whole time, so this place could use a few flytraps. Tried one of their signature cocktails called the Giovanni. The flavor was not entirely unpleasant but quite sour. The salsa was savory but i have a suspicion it's not made in house as ive tasted it before elsewhere. My entree was the Shortrib Chilaquiles and what a waste of good flavors it was. The proteins to corn chips was very scant. The egg whites were still translucent. Even with the tomatillo sauce it was way too much corn chip/carbs, made worse by the fact that i had chips and salsa prior to. Shocking, that such good presentation and decor are being let down.

One of the best, if not THE BEST, ice cream joints in the ville. You get SOOOO much ice cream, they have a ton of flavors (salted caramel is my favorite), and, for an ice cream joint, the price is alright (much better than the competition). The new location on 2nd Avenue is perfect if you want to sit and eat your ice cream or walk around and see Nashville with it. Loved coming here since I was in high school, will always be a fan!

Place is awesome for a local diner. Owner and staff are very friendly and food is very good. I tend to stick to sandwiches and breakfast so I don't have much input on their dinners. I will say that their burgers and turkey clubs ( type of food I would normally get at a diner) are the best around and are very reasonable.....try the cheeseburger deluxe or turkey club with a side of onion rings and you won't be sorry
Awesome bison burger, truffle fries, and sweet potato fries. Trevor was such an amazing server. We shall return!

Let's get this out of the way right now: it's pronounced "Ah-vuh" NOT "Ay-vuh" a fact that distinguishes the restaurant from the young lady it is named after.  I have been to Ava plenty of times but never wrote a review prior to now...it's time to spread the love!   My first experience at Ava was during Creative Loafing's Restaurant Week Tampa Bay in 2015. Since then I've been to Ava several times, mostly for dinner with family and friends and sometimes for lunch. I've also visited Ava to pick up food as an UberEats delivery person.    Having visited Ava several times, I am certain about a few loves:   1. The ambiance. It's perfect -- lively yet romantic; trendy yet comfortable. Ava offers al fresco dining (covered outdoor seating) as well as a variety of table styles inside (from classy high tops to traditional table seating). Be sure to ask about the "Secret Dining Room" if you're in need of a reservation for at least 10 guests!   2. The Casarecce al Pomodoro. I love to order this dish with extra ricotta and sometimes, if I'm feeling frisky, with the Nduja sausage (this is a spicy sausage which is different from the fennel sausage).   3. The Ava House Salad. It's a bit on the pricy side at $8.00, but the ingredients (mixed greens, cucumber, radish, tomato, red onion tossed in a house Italian vinaigrette) offer the perfect explosion of flavors pre-entree.  4. Joe Maddon. He is part owner of Ava and he is former manager of the Tampa Bay Rays...so duh, he is a love.  Also, for solo diners, Ava is one restaurant that has an incredibly welcoming bar that erases all of the unnecessary shame in dining alone. Trust me, I've done it time and time again.  :)

Came here for lunch, but it turned out to be only a brunch menu (1:15pm on a Sunday).  The menu was generally concise, but if I was in a brunch mood I think I would've been more excited.  I got burger which was okay.  The fries weren't very hot.  I also wasn't a fan of their "house ketchup," and they didn't have an alternative (Heinz, Hunt's, etc).  For comparison's sake, I know Burger Up nearby has their own house ketchup, but also "normal ketchup."  My gf got a squash dish, which she liked, but didn't love.  The service was friendly and professional and the decor was rustic and inviting.  I plan to come back another time when they're serving more than brunch -- our waitress mentioned that the dinner menu is great and changes regularly.  I'd expect my next visit to be 4/5, but it wasn't on this occasion.  Nonetheless, this seems like a clean, generally well-run establishment.  Check out the bathroom sinks while you're here.  Never seen one like that before!

The wait was very long to sit down, get order in, and get the food.  At the end of the day, the food was just okay.  We went there out of convenience from the hotel but next time we stay there we will go out and find a restaurant.

So many good breweries in Indy - and this is one of them! We ventured here with some friends for dinner and drinks. The atmosphere is nice and mellow, the place is cute and well kept, and there's plenty of parking in the lot and nearby. While I can't say it was the best beer I've ever had, I certainly did enjoy what I tried. Their food also didn't disappoint, as everything we tried was flavorful and fun (better than the reheated or refried offerings of many bars). The staff was also friendly and made for a good experience. This place is definitely worth a stop!

This is a super tourist spot, and the service kinda sucked. They know they'll likely never see you again. But the food was actually alright; a big portion for the price. And most dishes come with a salad and ice cream for dessert. Sorry my picture isn't too appealing, but this was the "stuffed pasta" dish.

Deeeeeeeeeeeeep Dish. No kidding-Like a swimming pool. Fun place. When we sat our waiter told us up front quite nicely that the deep dish takes 45 minutes to make. No solitaries. Cocktails and appetizers kept us happy. "Trendy" bar stuff with the latest labels. Black Magic instead of Captain Morgan etc. Still have no idea what the pop was (No Coke) it was good.  Mozzarella sticks a pretzels were delicious. The Deep Dish was as described. Thick heavy crust with cheese and toppings covered in Sauce.  Good pizza. Great service. Nice atmosphere. Definitely a fun place to hit!

When Freddy's opened I couldn't get enough. I think I went once or twice a week the months after it opened. They serve match stick fries, chicken burgers, melts, hamburgers and hotdogs. My favorite is the Freddy's Frozen Custard! I can't get enough, it's addicting for me. I see the monthly special flavors and just love every one. I especially love when they blend in the toppings to make a concrete, and this I will go through their drive through to get. The are thick and AS GOOD AS it gets. I would give this place a 5 out of 5 in the dessert category.    However, the food is OK. It's fresh and hot.  I just don't care for their style of burger nor their match stick fries; so the thought of eating of meal there, would for me, just be a means to an end to get dessert. I will profess I'm a picky fry person.  A huge plus at Freddy's are it's staff. They are always friendly and polite, the space is open and good for people with mobility issues. Also the location is clean and there is plenty of parking. So it's got a lot going for it.
Yummm their jersey tomato pie is AMAZING! The sauce was so flavorful it was unreal! I can't wait to try the margherita..mmm fresh mozzarella and basil. Price is great too! Definitely recommended!

Since we drank La Colombe Coffee at one of my Brunch places, curious to see where this one was, & maybe get a bag of coffee to brew at home. My Friend & I  walked around Rittenhouse square and there it was, right near the park.  As you enter, on left just little sitting tables, where you see people reading Sunday paper, couple had laptops. The coffee and tea were in these eclectic mugs with almost paisley design, very Parisian. As we looked, saw least 4-6 different versions of the La Colombe coffee ranging from Decaf to full bodied.  Corsica - Perfect for Filter Brew Nizza - Best Suited for Espresso Phocea - A French Press Favorite Monte Carlo - Full Body Decaf  We got the Nizza-It had a Caramel & nutty flavor. Can't wait to see how it tastes in my french press as well as my Brew machine!!!  It was in my car, left the most Scintillating smell that illuminated my car.The Woff it just consumed you. Mmmm-mmm!! They did have small case , couple pastries, but this place is mostly Coffee & tea.

Watched Logan on a Sunday night. It's pretty empty here, but maybe it was just Sunday. There wasn't any person there to purchase tickets, just teller-machines. I guess student discounts aren't a thing anymore?  Bathrooms are decent. Water fountains work. Seating wasn't cleaned for our movie. Popcorn buckets and trash left on seats around. I guess the movie industry around here isn't doing as well as netflix at home? I don't know. I don't watch movies often.

What happened Westmont Diner? Made a visit for dinner recently and I'm sad to say that everything was off. Waitress coughed in her hands, food was lackluster, burger underdone and needed to be sent back. It wasn't even a busy night. I'm sorry to say that it was such a disappointing experience that we won't be back for some time. I hope things turn around as we have enjoyed it in the past.

AWESOME espresso here! Came here to study, and loved the central Ybor location, and the open and spacious ambience. The staff was friendly, and helped me decide on getting the Espresso Bombón to help fuel my study session, and it was amazing - smooth robust espresso, velvety foamy milk, and just the right amount of sweetness to bring it together without overwhelming it...loved it! I also got a banana nut muffin, it was nice and soft and chewy. All in all, cool spot right in the heart of Ybor with great coffee and free wifi - def recommend!

We came here on a Saturday afternoon.  The place was busy but we managed to get a table outdoors. They have board games which you can take outdoors and play at your table. They have beer flight selection for $2/4oz. We ordered 5 each. They have some very good selection. We liked Tropical Thunder, CPA, Wrong way and MeTime. They keep introducing new flavors. We also ordered chicken sandwich which came with a side of fries. The sandwich was good but the fries were really very good -thin and crispy. The service was also very prompt and courteous. We got seating near the fireplace and had a very good time. Overall very good experience. I would recommend this place for evening dates by the fireplace or morning chill out brunches. Note: They have happy hours Mon-Fri with $1.5 flights and $10 pizzas. Will definitely try their happy hour and review it.

I have eaten here multiple times and really enjoyed it but my last visit was my worst experience. We got seated at around 11 when it wasn't super busy and after our waitress took our order it took 50 min to get our food. We actually hadn't seen our waitress at all and had to ask a different waitress where our food was. People that were seated after us got their food before us. When my pancakes arrived they were extremely cold and when I told the waitress she took them to the kitchen then came back to say : "what do you want us to do with them like microwave them or remake them im not sure what they can do" I was honestly so confused because if you as an employee don't know what the kitchen can do then why and how are you working here?? Once my eggs Benedict came they were alright, the bread was extremely hard and it took me awhile to cut through it. My boyfriend had the chicken and waffles which were good. At the end when we asked for our check we waited another 20 minutes for her to come tell us to stand in line and tell her what table we are in order to get the check. Truly an awful experience. I honestly think it was all because of our awful waitress who if I ever come back I will request to not have her.

Really good food and dog friendly!!! Absolutely loved the atmosphere and the quesadilla I got was really delicious. Some what pricey for what you get, but one should always expect to pay a little more when going out to eat. Good for families, friends, and dogs. Very casual and chill atmosphere.

I wound up at the Red Owl while hitting up the local tourist spots nearby. It is a charming and comfy little tavern with great service and a friendly atmosphere.   The lunch menu was extremely limited. I wasn't expecting anything extraordinary at a "tavern" but since it's connected to a hotel I thought there would be more options. The food was good - I had fish and chips, my boyfriend had a burger. Our server was fantastic - which really goes a long way with me.   My only complaint was the prices. I paid $20 for fish and chips that consisted of two SMALL pieces of fish and a plate full of shoestring fries. My boyfriend's burger was also $20 and was very average. I really feel like we paid $50 for a mediocre lunch only because it was "in" a hotel.   Unfortunately, I wouldn't come back - but it IS convenient if you are in the area.. and the service IS good.

The atmosphere is very modern without feeling cold. Good music, good vibes. The coffee is great and the gelato is to die for, so many great flavors!  I ordered a small gelato with blueberries and cream with coconut, and I think I could've eaten a warehouse full of it, so delicious! The iced coffee is refreshing and well-brewed.   The reason I will not come back here is the sandwich. I ordered a BLT, and watched the staff member cover it in aluminum foil and nuke it. Its the prettiest microwaved sandwich I have ever had, but I'm currently sitting here with a sandwich I've eaten 1/4 of because it's soggy and warm where it shouldn't be - the lettuce and the tomatoes are HOT. Not just warm, but hot. It's actually really vile.  So although the coffee and gelato are great, I'm concerned about the process of food preparation here if they're selling microwaved garbage for $7.50.
Little order at the window, sit outside joint. Bbq is great. Delicious ribs and pulled pork.
Reliable classics and good take-out service.    The only thing I had here that I didn't like was a watery wonton soup.

Good.  Not great.  Based on a friend's reccomendation i stopped here today.  Unfortunately for me almost every sandwhich he suggested has pork on it,  which I avoid.  I chose his non-pork recommendation, Italian beef.  While the sandwhich was very good, its not quite as good a those in Chicago.  Good: lots of meat, essential toppings were there (peppers, celery, carrots and peppers).  Bad: while I  like my sandwich dipped I prefer the dip, meat and bread seperated when the sandwich is to go.    I felt compelled to eat it in the car ride home before bread was too moist.   With all that said I will more than likely return.

I've been coming here for about a year now and they have my trust! I've had Tatiana and Patrick cut/style my hair. They're both awesome and have done a great job with my haircut. I've gone in with photos to show and they've worked well with me and given me advice. I've left the salon a happy gal every time :)   The staff is super friendly and they have skilled professionals who know what they are doing. The price is reasonable/affordable for the quality!

The quintessential after-work place to go If you like the people you work with. If you don't, go to some place like Scotty's where there's lots of distractions. This place focuses on beer and interpersonal communication around your table! There are TVs for sports but it's just the perfect place for the simple task of sitting down and drinking a beer. End of story.

I would say this is not the best BBQ in Tampa but it's not bad if your in the mood and in the area. There is no place to sit and eat so it's pretty much drive up, walk up and order your food. This is pretty much two trailer/out buildings set up roadside. As for the food ribs are fall off the bone but not really that much flavor. No dry or wet rub placed prior to smoking, and heck anything can become fall off the bone if you leave it on the smoker along enough...I don't mean this to come off that harsh but I am trying to be honest. I prefer my BBQ to have some sort of bark on the exterior with a noticeable smoke ring this BBQ is absent of the bark and only a light smoke ring. As for the sides they are just a ok in my opinion.. The beans say out inside individual packaged containers so there room temp, dry, and thick. The macaroni salad was the same lukewarm and bland. I guess in a pinch it fits the bill but if your in the mood for some finger licking BBQ you might want to look elsewhere..

I had a bad mead experience at a Ren Fair so lets just say I am totally late to the New Day party. Luckily they also have hard cider and innovative cocktails made with the aforementioned so I was in the clear when I attended an event at this bright and cozy Fountain Square location. I got the Gold Rush that was billed as an apple champagne quite rightly so. My guest got a bourbon barrel cider that was a head spinning 12%  and quite sweet. The cheese plates were stunning with many local products featured. A nice place to hang out, try a bargain $6 cider flight and peruse local artwork

Have not had the food here, but Smith's is a good place to get drinks and hang out with friends. Two bars! Smith's can be really random though. Some nights it is packed and people are dancing on the small dancefloor and other nights, it's totally dead.

Love this bar!  If you haven't been in a while, they have added new tables/seating near the front entrance.  There is a huge patio out front of the building with plenty of picnic tables.  If you happen to sit at a table instead of at the bar, remember you have to place your beer/food order at the bar.  Bartenders are always friendly.  On this occasion, I tried the Anniversaria 2015 and Regalia.  I would definitely buy a bottle of the Anniversaria to take home ($20).  The Regalia was by far my favorite (and my husband kept trying to steal it!).  During the winter, on Wednesdays, they have $3 pours of all beers (except Abraxas).

Mixed feelings. It has a very rich history, and is definitely a great place to visit if you're in town. It's an upscale place, but they have this indoor patio area that is not as fancy but the same menu. The indoor patio area might as well be outdoors because I was FREEZING. I could barely drink my water cause it was so cold.   Our waitress was also terrible. She was very inattentive, slow, and forgetful. We waited a ridiculous amount of time waiting to order, for our check, etc.   My friend got the Cajun chicken, and I got the pork chop. The food was GREAT. the bread they bring in the beginning was also out of this world, very unique. My friends wine glass was dirty, and they quickly got her a new glass.   Did definitely appreciate the sanitary door opener in the bathroom!

Beer was drinkable but probably doesn't inspire me to want to drink much of it.  The biggest challenge here is that the interior warehouse space gets very hot and uncomfortable.  So far this is probably my least favorite brewery experience in the NOLA area.

I have been here once before and remember not being that impressed. This time on the other hand we had a great time. I enjoyed all the dishes and ordered the lemon chicken and lamb. The first plate was excellent. I really enjoyed the eggplant dish with pita. My recommendation would be to go early and sit on the first floor it's much more comfortable.

Great family owned restaurant. Was a refreshing experience to meet the owner/chef Janise as she severed our plates. I enjoyed the reggae music throughout the meal and the upscale decor. Also has many big screens where they show soccer/rugby/cricket/basketball. Highly suggest it and get the brown stew chicken. Amazing comfort food.

We got off the 39hr Hubbard's fishing trip and had sometime before we headed back to Ohio.  We asked one of the mates where to get some great smoked fish dip.  Without hesitation he said "Ted Peter's".  He was right.  Man, was it good.  A few drops of Crystal hot sauce...good stuff.   A guy next to us was having a burger that looked pretty darn good too.  So, we split one.  Just a basic burger with American on it, but I don't think it could be made much better.  We order two tubs of fish dip to bring back to Ohio, since no one in Ohio seems to have a clue what it is. This is definitely a place we'll go back to when we're in town.

Pretty good breakfast/brunch place.  Honeybee Crepe was a great, balanced, sweet-savory treat -- if maybe a little too carb-y (crepe was pretty thick).  I also would have liked a side salad with that.  I stole my partner's and it was fresh and good.  I ordered a cortado and received something more similar to a latte -- espresso-milk ratio was too milk-heavy.  The space is nice, but was very smoky both upstairs and downstairs.  Don't come here if you have asthma?  Overall, a friendly, cozy place with good food.  Crepes (and omelettes) are pretty massive, so if i come back i'll likely get something to split.

Great little spot in Eagle in I believe and covered church. Recently attended out 2nd cigar & wine evening and again really enjoyed ourselfs. While my husband enjoyed his cigar I enjoyed the wine and a desert from the restaurant. Have been here for breakfast loved the French toast I had & his eggs Benedict also were very good.  Looking forward to stopping in for some coffee in the near future.

I was SO excited to try this place, but it was a Tuesday and we were leaving New Orleans on Wednesday. Imagine my disappointment to find out that they were closed on Monday and Tuesday! Well, we ended up staying another night and I just couldn't contain my excitement. We made our way over at around 7:45 and the place was packed!
