You can actually have a decent conversation without having to scream and yell. A nice change of pace from the rest of the bars in town.  Anyways, beer wise, they have a great selection. You can pick from their regular menu (100-400 level which is categorized based on intensity/level of flavour). Then they have their rotational menu with quite a selection to choose from. A buddy and I got there early so we had a few beers and drinks before the rest of our friends came to join us. So I'm just writing about what we had:  - Guldenberg ($8) - 12oz beer from their rotational menu. It is 8.5% and from Belgium. I'm no beer expert but it was a blond ale and was well balanced. - Wee Angry Scotch Ale (300 level) - dark ale that my buddy ordered.  - Lowrider Double Rye Ale (rotational menu) - I believe this was the other beer he ordered. I can't remember the name for sure but it was a 12oz and it almost had a bit of a wine aroma to it.   - Green onion cake sandwich ($13) - a friend that had gone before told me that this was pretty decent. Comes with pulled pork, suey choy, tomatoes and green onions with a green onion cake as the sandwich. They claim it is homemade but I wasn't too impressed with it. I've had better green onion cakes at Asian restaurants. And for the price, definitely would not order it again. For my side, I had the garden salad with lemon honey dressing. Nothing special again. - BBQ burger ($15) - 8oz beef infused with bbq sauce, cheddar and bacon. My buddy liked it but said he has had much better burgers. He upgraded to get the sweet potato fries.  Basically, good if you want to try beers. Mediocre for food (as you would expect for bar food in Edmonton). Overall, I'd give it 3.5 stars. Just go for a few beers.

So Fresh is my go to for easy healthy food to pick up. I always build my bowl and get as many veggies as I can and extra Zoodles. I love the Zoodles. They are probably my favorite thing. Gluten Free friendly and absolutely delicious and filling!

Been here a few times since my last review. Continues so have a solid burger and fries dish, but I have tried some of the vegetarian/vegan dishes and they just don't stack up. Veggie 'bacon' has been especially bland. Overall, I still like coming here!

Everything I've gotten here has been fantastic. My favorites have been the peanut butter candy cake and some strawberry shortcake I had awhile back. A lot of their baked goods are not overly sugary, but place more emphasis on the heavy cream/flavor like I prefer. They also have a large assortment of small cookies, which I like because you can get more variety without feeling the pressure of buying huge desserts.

I bit the bullet and bought a membership after seeing the 2015 pricing! I love this place. Peruse their website (imamuseum.org) or follow them on Facebook or Twitter for a bit about what they have to offer! The IMA is one of my favorite places in Indianapolis. :)  Considering a Membership? The Perks: - Membership gets you a discount in the cafe (its Kahn's so you know its good!) & free parking. - The dual membership option is the way to go! You can go-in w/any 2 adults & when one of you goes you can bring another adult w/you! I signed up my sister and I so either of us can go together or on our own w/our hubbies! - If you paid for parking on the day you bought your membership they'll take the $5 off your membership price!  Random Fugalista Tips: - If you don't have a membership and want to avoid the $5 parking fee, there is a lot to the left after entering via the Michigan Roan entrance.   - If you paid for parking on the day you bought your membership they'll take the $5 off your membership price! - Free refills on coffee purchased at the IMA Cafe! - Free coat check on the 1st floor near the elevators / welcome desk.  Random Mommy Tips: - There is a nursing mother's room on the first floor outside of the IMA Cafe. - There are free family friendly events on the first Saturday of the month - and they look amazing and engaging for kiddos of all ages! - Stroller friendly. And diaper bags are welcome throughout the museum!

First impression was not good. They were out of the signature appetizer, but sounds like this was a vendor issue. However, I ordered the Mahi with black beans and rice. Soggy, cold fries were substituted for the rice. I was not informed that there was no rice in the back, nor was I given a choice of what vegetable I could have as a substitution prior to my dinner being served. In addition, the servers and hostess crew were disorganized and confused. It took a long time to get out food. There were no apologies for the food or service from the staff.   Having said this, other than the fries which I substituted broccoli for, the food was good. I would try again, but I hope that our next time will be memorable for all the right reasons.//  Since original review have been back. I see some improvement has been made in service. Overall still about average on food, but glad to see them moving in the right direction.

Place is very nice and clean. Service is friendly and welcoming. We had about 3 different people serving us that made us feel very welcomed. The menu had plenty of choices for the two of us. We started the dinner with the burrata and risotto cakes. The cheese was decent. Lacked a bit of salt for our taste buds but we still ate every last piece. The risotto cakes were very heavy on the breading and lacked flavor. We next shared a salad and the tomato soup. The soup was some of the best we have ever had and was our favorite plate of the night. The salad was fresh and bright. The main dish consisted of Rigatoni for me and the wife had seafood paella. The chicken meatballs in the rigatoni were tough, dry, and not desirable. The pasta and sauce were great. The paella was tasty with a good selection of seafood. We finished the evening with a chocolate mouse tarte. The little dessert was full of great flavor. I would recommend Richard's to anybody looking for a great night out. Service is great. overall flavors are solid.  Cheers Always!

The food here is always hit or miss for me, and the last time I was there the service was extremely slow (2 hrs for lunch mid-week!). I think the atmosphere and taste of the food make the prices seem high, especially when there are other authentic New Orleans restaurants not too far away that do it better for cheaper. If you're in the area, and there's a table open, great. I wouldn't trek here, and I wouldn't wait for a table. The specials tend to be awesome, and rather pricey. Some of the main menu items can be total misses, and also pricey. The cocktails are generally the most consistent thing about the restaurant, so I don't mind the price quite as much, but they're not cheap. I've always had meh service on every visit, so nothing impressive there. Overall, at this point, I only go here when other people really want to go. If I can convince them otherwise, we eat elsewhere. Midcity has too many other great options at this point.

One of the best places in Indy.  So the food may not be 100% up to the expectations of what the Louisiana yuppies would call authentic Cajun... but it's good.  Damn good.  I have dreams about the Chili Cheese étouffée.  The staff is always helpful and they will let you try anything.  They are FAST and friendly and have a cool, mellow, hip vibe.  Love.......this......... place!

Recently tried this place on a Wednesday night for Geeks who Drink! Service was great - our server Melissa never let our drinks get too empty and always accommodating. I ordered the trident burger, MR with a side of sweet potato fries. The burger was delicious and cooked to order! The fries were a perfect texture, crisp and not soggy.   The venue itself is a little small for the amount of people that were there on a busy night as it got really stuffy and hot. They only have 2 stalls in their womens restrooms so there was almost always a line. Everything was very clean, however, despite the vast number of bodies in the room.
Nice quiet location. Good coffee. I got the mocha. It was excellent. Nice and friendly service. Mellow atmosphere. Stop by and check it out.

I like the chicken here. The taste is clean, yet moist and flavorful. The green sauce is the best. I don't care for the white sauce as much.  The Cilantro rice and beans I've had were decent as well.   The other meats are just okay. Stick with the chicken.  The location is in a weird spot on Hillsborough near the airport, so I always forget to eat here.

I liked staying at the peppermill.  The people were very nice that they upgraded my room to stay at the tuscany suites which wsa a very nice room. I got the room for $70 dollars a night. It came with a nice jacuzzi, shower and a great toilet.  The room was quite big that I could have stayed in there the whole day.  I really like the sheets as well and the bed was nice and comfortable that I could have fallen asleep in there all day long.  Room service was good and the food as well, but like in any hotel place it was quite pricey but then tip and everything is included so we did not have to worry about that stuff.  Anyways unfortunately I did not gamble there, but it was okay though. It was probably the nicest casino out in reno however.  The resort fee does suck it is $12 dollars a night.  But with it comes with internet though.

Tucked away in a very small and desolate mall is this little gem in Indy.  It's hard to find good/authentic Asian food in the Indy area, especially one that stays true to the flavors of Thai/Lao cuisine.  If you are looking for traditional flavors, check out this restaurant.  The papaya salad was great, crab paste base had a nice balance.  For some places, the crab paste can be a little too strong and lingers at the back of my throat but this one was balanced, not too dark!  The Laotian style wings were also great, the sauce had a nice sweet,sour,fermenty flavor to them. Wish I had more room in my stomach to order more from the menu. If it I was closer I'd come almost every day!!  Will definitely come back to try their Sunday pho and other items on their menu.
There's honestly nothing more to say here but HANDS DOWN THE BEST BAGELS EVER!!! stop in here for good service and great prices.

This is just a place to drink as the food is completely overpriced for the boring and typical Tex-Mex crap wrapped in a tortilla of some type.  Tex-Mex isn't complicated and nothing in it is very expensive so there is no way the outrageous prices this place charges for their food can ever be justified other than  they must get a lot of tourists and locals who don't know better so they just rip those clueless folks off.  Why even bother with the overpriced mediocre food here anyway when you can just go eat awesome food right across the street at Lola's!  The owners are nice people but what they charge for a freaking taco is insulting.  Do yourself and your wallet a favor and just booze it up here but never eat.

I love that we have place as a nice take out Mexican stand-by (you can eat in too).  It's close to our house on the west side.  They keep their prices low, and they're so good!  It's a family run long time business.  It's always packed, you can be guaranteed to be full and happy.  The rice and beans and meat are flavorful and delicious.  I like how they wrap my fork in a tortilla too!  It's a little on the dirty side inside, but I don't mind, I usually take the food out, and we're always super satisfied!
Awesome food, nice environment outside. Just a great experience. We will definitely be back!

Was it here that Abraham Lincoln asked, upon ordering coffee, "If this is coffee, bring me tea. If this is tea, bring me coffee." ?  Maybe the food is good, but don't buy the coffee unless you like over-priced hot brown water.

Jones is definitely average quality, not many complaints, but nothing to really rave over either. We went as a group of five with a reservation so we didn't have to wait long for a table. They have a limited menu during certain times, so be careful of that. It was very busy and loud, but this wasn't an issue for us. We would go back if there weren't any other options, but considering the area, there are many other better options.

I really enjoyed my tour Swamp Tour with Cajun Encounters, in fact this was the highlight of my trip! I called the day before to confirm my reservation just because I was worried about the shuttle remembering to get me. I had a pickup window of 8-8:30am at a hotel in the French Quarter for a 9:30 tour. Miss Josephine, the shuttle driver picked me up around 8:20. Then we made a few more stops to fill the bus and were on our way. Miss Josephine was very informative on the 45 minute drive, pointing things out as we drove along and talking a bit about her personal experience with Hurricane Katrina. We arrived with just enough time to get our wristbands inside the shop and take a quick bathroom break. Captain Bishop lead us to the boat and we were off! Thanks to the tip from another yelper I waited to board the boat last so I could sit up front (GREAT IDEA!). Though everyone gets a good view at one point or another, Captain Bishop was good about moving the boat from side to side for everyone to see. We saw about 10 gators, some raccoons, turtles, herron, and wild hogs too. The houses were interesting to look at and the swamp vegetation was really cool. Captain Bishop was both informative and funny. Great experience and highly recommended!

Great kale long life salad, Greek salad, hummus and chicken salad! Fresh. Fast. Come early and beat the crowd. Be prepared to be undecided about what to order!  Local fave and a must see and do. Serious foodies please consider!

5 of us gals had dinner here last night right before the Boz Scaggs concert at the Capital. The location is fab as it's right across the street & there is ample city parking within a block area. The front dining/bar area is very cool looking & pubby. Dark wood, classy, etc. We had requested a table in the garden room, which is totally different: very high ceilings with numerous skylights, plus big windows on the north side. There are faux windows with shutters on the brick interior that give the feeling of a garden and there is a lovely fountain on one wall where we were seated.   We were greeted by Rachquel (sp. is probably wrong) who was delightful & attentive. Unfortunately, they were slammed due to the concert no doubt as this was a Mon. night. They didn't have enough staff & the bar was really behind as it took a good 10-15 mins. to bring our drinks. We ordered our food quickly. I had the duck tacos. They were just ok as the sauce was mediocre. Two friends had the mushroom ramen. The taste was good, but the mushrooms were under-cooked & the ramen was mushy. The menu is quite eclectic & varied, but that's not always good as it takes a lot of effort to deliver a quality product. It's hard to give an accurate picture when a restaurant is so slammed, so I'm giving them 4 stars for now & will update if I return, which will only be when I go to another concert.   BTW, Rachquel comped our 1st round because we had to wait so long. Now that's the kind of customer service that brings people back!  Give this place a try & write a review.

Lama Dog Rocks! We visited on a Friday around lunch. It's not a huge place but has a good sized bar area along with a long and high community table as the main seating options. There is also a really nice outdoor patio that's away from the road. Lama keeps an eclectic mix of about 20 beers on tap at any given time spanning the gamut from light sours thru big chewy stouts. These days 20 beers is a solid number but not amazing. What is amazing about the 20 beers they had on when we visited was the quality level of the producers represented. A few great local brewers you don't see a lot around town, Draughtsmen and The Brewhouse as well as some fairly rare sightings from outside the area like Bottle Logic , El Segundo and 21st Amendment. Sour beer lovers take note, spots 11-13 on the board are reserved for sour beers.. Overall this is a great place. Staff are great, vibe is really good, it can get packed but on a not too crowded day it's a place you just never want to leave. Oh, did I mention that the Nook food bar is located right inside next to Lama Dog? And, on top of all that they have an excellent selection of bottle beers to buy and take home.
Love this location! Good bang for your buck, and the staff is really friendly! Love coming here with my husband for breakfast on the weekends!

Stopped by Olio around 5 o'clock on a Thurs before a class at the Botanical Garden. Sat out on the patio. It was a gorgeous day and the patio was wonderful - very relaxing. Service was excellent. I had the ceviche and my wife had the lahmacun. Both were excellent. My wife said it was the best ceviche she's ever had.   I'm torn between 4 and 5 stars. The reason I didn't give it 5 stars - we paid $36 for 2 small plates and we were starving 3 hours later.

I absolutely love going to New Day! They always are trying new things with infusions and the like. The choice and quantity of cocktails using their ciders are fantastic. My favorite one is the South Cider and the Breakfast Magpie. This place also has specials almost every day and the best one is on Tuesday's they have half price fills! Come support this local business! Love it!
OK, I'm off the hater wagon.  Izzo's does its job.  Good food, fast, and cheap.   I'll take a burrito any day.

I have recently become a vegetarian and as such have been desperately looking for places to eat that have good vegetarian options. My daughter suggested that we try Queen of Sheba Ethiopian restaurant because she and heard that they have many vegetarian items on their menu. Upon arriving at the restaurant I noticed the decor was very warm and inviting with lots of Ethiopian posters, artifacts, drums and aromas. Upon arrival I was immediately greeted and seated by a very nice gentleman The table was appointed with cloth napkins and tablecloth; very elegant for a midday meal. I asked the nice gentleman to recommend the best vegetarian option for someone new to Ethiopian cuisine. He showed me that there were several vegetarian entrees on the menu, but he recommended that I try one of the vegetarian combination meals.  The combination meal included a sampling of six of the top vegetarian options. I'm not sure exactly what I got,  I just know that there were collard greens, taters, carrots and beans and just about every other vegetable you could imagine. I have to say that the offerings were flavorful, colorful and delicious. You should know that, in this restaurant, you're encouraged to use your bread instead of utensils for eating. The bread is different, unlike anything else I've ever tried . It's sort of like a crepe,  but not really, a bit more spongy . Wasn't my favorite thing, but it was good to try something new. However, it was a wonderful experience trying vegetables in a new way. At one point in our meal a very merry woman came out to talk with us and see how we liked the food. She was very delightful, I assume she was the owner but I'm not sure. We really enjoyed meeting her and talking to her about ourselves and her restaurant. I encourage you to try Queen of Sheba if you're looking for something a little exotic and also yummy.

my first time here and i got the fried cheese  (because...fried cheese) and the blt. i think you can tell a lot about a place by their bacon to lettuce ratio.   this place didn't disappoint. embarrassingly, i snacked on the cheese on the way back to my office. they were delicious. the blt was stuffed full of bacon and equally satisfying.  the prices are a bit high,  but there's nothing wrong with splurging occasionally.  i would definitely go back.

Location: near the University, but I wish it was a bit more central, but the fact that I'll drive 20 minutes to North reno for this place should speak volumes. Ambiance: mellow and retro. What I expect from the late 80s early 90s California beach scene.  Food: I love the food, it's unique but so tasty with a large variety. There is literally something for everyone. I love the pig Kahana. And the surfing  cow. This place also has a good selections of local and micro brew on tap Price: Be warned it is a tad expensive, but the regular size is great for one person and you could share a large if you and a friend are craving the same thing. Artcade: yes I spelled that right, it an art gallery and throwback retro arcade games. It's enough to nerd out at for a few minutes. The best part is you can actually play these arcade games  Service: This is what drops us to the four star rating.. They are a bit slow and not the most friendly people, not mean or bad, just mediocre, doing their job. Overall: 4 out 5 and highly recommend with price and service in mind :)

I'm from Southern Cali and I always come here when I visit. I would never order crawfish back home but I always love the spicy crawfish here. It's pretty spicy but you can't stop because it's so good. My family and I were able to share 2 lbs between the 4 of us and we were fine for a small lunch. They don't have different spicy levels FYI. When we ordered we also got our food very quickly probably because all we got were the crawfish and it was already made.

Very nice service, and comfortable atmosphere.  Food is quite tasty and well-presented.  Location and setting is nicely close on the Mesa, but off the beaten path with good parking. Very close to the corner of Cliff Dr and Meigs Rd.

Average burger for $9 came out in 30 minutes, and my wife's salad hit our table 50 minutes after ordering. Clearly understaffed for a Saturday evening. They refunded a few customers. Luckily, we weren't in a hurry and had a great convo. On a positive note, beverages were served in a mason jar.

Yes, just yes. Because it's described as a wine bar, I really thought it'd be a stop on our way out to dinner, but it's so much more! It was SLAMMED on the (Saturday) night we went, but there were still plenty of places to sit. And the service- 5 stars FOR SURE. From the doorman to the entire staff really, they have it all flowing like a fine-tuned machine. Upon walking into the backyard of what once was a little bungalow, you'll see lots of tables to sit down at. It's all communal, so don't be shy! Share a table and your bottle of wine with your new found friends. Also, do yourself a favor and order the mussels and the pork shoulder as well...large portions and your stomach will thank you! Oh and did I say talented jazz musicians? Now if I could only transport this place to Austin....

Yep, definitely have been missing out on this place. Best gyro I have had in Tampa to date! I will have to come back again with the lady because I just know she would love the greek salad I saw a few people dining on!  I also liked the setup of the place...walk up counter to order so you can take it out or dine in, which is pretty nice and calm inside.  The greek fries were also a favorite when I brought the rest back home!
The girl that was working Saturday afternoon of July 22nd was rudeeeeee!!! It was pretty good coffee, just wish the service was more friendly.

Something I wish I had known prior to arriving here (which is super helpful to know) is that the non-pizza items here are somewhat tapas style. So that being said, this place ends up being somewhat pricier than you would think. We ended up ordering about 10 different ~$15 plates and 1 pizza for 4 people. We weren't planning on spending that much when initially looking at the menu online, but still glad we came because everything was amazing!  I drove by this place a million times on my commute along Kelly drive, and finally decided to give this place a try. I must say, everything was better than I expected it to be! We needed to order some veggie options so I reluctantly agreed to getting the pea ravioli... which actually blew my mind! Salads are fresh, flavors are incredible, and textures of all pasta and meat are perfect. You can pretty much order anything on the menu here and you will be happy... Even I, the meat lover, who also had to eat a vegan pizza that night, left more than satisified.

Nice clean store with great selection of organics. People at the register are always friendly too and love that their bakery super careful about cross contamination when it comes to peanuts...at least so they say when I expressed the concern.

While certainly not gourmet on NYC standards (the soft water in FL is somewhat to blame, I'm sure), the two pies we ordered were passable...delivery took quite a bit too long and the pies were close to cold on arrival, so the saice and cheese didn't hold up very well. Toppings, etc., will be exactly as requested, which in this day and age is a plus.
Local pizza joint with a great staff and great food. The Downieville pizza along with the apple/goat cheese salad and a beer is a hard combo to beat.

Ok so I went to this place several months ago, maybe January? And can't believe I haven't left a review yet. To be honest I don't remember the exact name of my dish but it was some type of truffle bucatini. There are no words. Just incredible. In full disclosure I'm a sucker for truffle anything, and pasta, so this was kind of a no brainer. Can't wait to go back now that it's nice out!

Druing this restaurant week, I reserved a table yesterday. The restaurant is located in walnut street and 14th street near by City Hall. Dark environment,  pretty waitresses and dressed up customers. I enjoyed the time. However, the food here is OK.
Great coffee.  I love this place.  Give me more coffee.

One of our favorite restaurants! Their red sauce is probably the best in town that we have found. Try their homemade house dressing- it's amazing. Everything we have had has been tasty and the portions are big. We always have enough left for lunch the next day. This is the place we bring friends to when they are visiting us from out of town instead and they want good food. It is also a frequent birthday or special occasion dinner location! We have been dining at the restaurant since it was located further south on 66th street. Love it.

I gotta give it three. First I have a sneaking suspicion that the same Asian family owns this and the restaurant next door....I could be wrong, but I doubt it. That place serves average american chinese. This place serves average Asian fusion...from a variety of off the shelf cook books. There is nothing original here, not even the Stephen Starr inspired music. Its like they went to POD and tried to copy it...poorly. You walk in to a chalk board in the vestibule. Take a picture of it because it is nowhere to be found inside. Look at the sushi counter. To the left is a few bits of shellfish on ice. I don't know if this is for show. The other two are filled with grocery store sushi makings. Why show off the kani? Is yours different? Homemade? Its krab stick from a box.  We ordered the left side of the menu. I told the waiter that I hate cilantro. Over 50% of the dishes have cilantro in the description. Almost every dish came covered in cilantro microgreens. It is still cilantro! Btw this is a steal from Bluefin in East Norriton.  They throw the food at you. Out of order and several at the same time. No sequence. No break. We got king crab croquettes, average with no noticable crab and wasabi crackers with spicy tuna. A hot app and a cold app at the same time. Before that was finished I got my soup put in front of me, a clam miso soup, with sandy inedibleclams. The broth was fabulous though. Japanese cuisine does not rely upon cilantro. Chinese and vietnamese do. So we aren't japanese. Do what you do best. Oh back to the menu. Its huge. Too huge. Cut it by 2/3.  The sushi was very sweet. Too much mayo. Nothing really popped. The tuna pizza sucked. Sweet. Too sweet. Unweildy to eat.  What are you trying to be?! Habachi?! No habachi tables. Korean...there's galbi and gochujjang, but no real spice to anything. Japanese..nope. I wouldn't go again.

Wow the pork chop was awesome and the scallops were cooked to perfection. Seriously those two items were awesome. Really is one of the best pork chops probably from anywhere. The duck egg rolls were good and the sauce is great.  Great stuff. The 201 drink was ok. Had some other drink that was too sweet and switched. Can't remember what it was. Still totally worth the price of scallops and chop.

I've been hearing this place was great, and have to admit that I was disappointed.   The food just ok. I ordered a frozen strawberry pina colada margarita and it was WAY too sweet, and my friend said her margarita on the rocks was just fair.   The dinning room is really large, and it seems like a good place for families since it was pretty loud, had huge menu and prices were reasonable.   As much as I hate to admit I like a chain rest better than independently owned restaurant...I'd go to Chevy's over this place. Not bad, just not inspiring either.

We enjoyed eating downstairs in the open area lakeside with a light breeze and lovely views. Everything and everyone working here is still relatively or brand new, so there are some glitches to be overcome. Friendly, affordable and comfortable - enjoy Felix's open air.

My review will be a tad bit one sided because my first visit was on a whim for the turducken croissant specialty. However, I will be returning for more treats.   Beautiful shop. The decor and the display cases are all arranged with loving care and definitely bump up the atmosphere to a sophisticated level.   My fellow foodies and I stood outside in the cold anticipating the open sign and did our best not to drool once the door opened. The smell wafting through the air is enough to cause a riot but we all behaved ourselves and queued up for the turducken experience. Standing in line taunted us all with the other glorious treats, sweet and savory, which were on display, but I resisted.  The staff was so friendly and helpful and quickly moved through the crowd which had gathered.   The turducken croissant.  Okay I know sometimes you wait in line for a "specialty" because it is part of the experience and try not to be let down on the actual item. The turducken croissant is special. A perfectly soft and flaky croissant stuffed with a turkey/duck/dressing/cranberry mixture you can dip in a side of gravy. Dear lord it was decadent. I will be back next year.

I went here for my boyfriend's birthday and our further anniversaries. I do not regret a thing at all! Although we had reservations, we still had to wait a couple of minutes. The servers are super kind and I always feel bad for them when they have to sing happy birthday songs or do that one dance routine when a certain song plays because they look soooo tired. Anyways, we ordered the raspberry lemonade and oh so gooddd! We got the boneless wings as an appetizer and we did get the sampler with a few of the other appetizers as well. We did not like those cheesy potatoes at all. The cheese was too thick and had turned to stone when it came to us. The jalapeno poppers are hooootttt but soo gooooddd. I forgot to mention the AMAZING bread rolls!!! Take advantage of it while you can!  The steaks were perfectly cooked and tasted wonderful. The green beans side tastes watery and not worth it unless you dump it in your mashed potatoes like I did. Nonetheless, will come back again!!
Same great Kung Pao with attentive service in a pleasant, upscale but casual atmosphere.

Chatham Tap is perfect for any occasion. The laid back atmosphere is comfortable for a working lunch, happy hour or dinner out. Stopped by today with a group of 5 and had awesome service and a delicious Southwest Cesar Salad, can't wait to get back and have it again!

I saw that the hours had been expanded so decided to stop in one afternoon. They've done a really great job remodeling the space; there's tons of seating and the decor is really cute. However, for the quality of the food, the price was really steep.   The cheese fundido w/ chorizo was probably the best thing we ordered, it was gooey and had lots of veggies; but it was not worth $10.   I also have a hard time paying $4/$5 dollars for a single taco unless they are just great... and these were not. Although the tacos were hefty and filled well, the lack of salt and lack of salsa selection left me unsatisfied. Just not worth $5. My boyfriend was also very underwhelmed with his selection of tacos, although he mentioned he liked the Idaho taco the best.   They did have a good local beer selection. you could tell the tortillas were fresh, and the workers at the counter were really nice.  Bottom line: If you're going to gentrify tacos, they need to have a lot more flavor. This place has lots of potential, but the college crowd who lives in the area will not be interested in paying $15 for a set of bland tacos.

Housekeeping prevents another star. Admittedly, the other reviews are spot on but one would thing the hotel has gotten the memo and talked to the housekeepers.   I stayed here a few nights during my last visit. I received a room that faced St. Joseph which is directly across the street from a popular night spot. I agree with one reviewer that says you need to be passed out to get a good night's rest. There is no way to sleep through the loud music that blasts on weekends until at least 6am. Geez!  To makes this worse, housekeeping knocked on my door at 8:30a on a Saturday. Really!!? I mean this is a late night city, in the heart of downtown. 8:30a? C'mon!! I told them to go away. At 10am, I received another knock. I answered and the housekeeper asked if I was ready for the room to be cleaned? I said no and she went away.   Fast forward to another day, when I put the do not disturb sign outside. They did not knock but when I did leave the room at 12p and tool it off, I should have known that the housekeeper would not clean the room even though a cleaner was a few rooms down cleaning someone else's room. What is sad is I returned to the room and there was voicemail left a 2:30p from another hotel room saying your room was not cleaned because of the do not disturb sign. What sign? Just say you did not want to clean the room.   The hotel rooms are really nice, very clean and are equipped with a microwave and refrigerator. The free breakfast was really nice, It included fruit, cereal, yogurt, juice, coffee and a few hot items.   I would stay here again but the housekeeping needs retraining. It is unclear why they should continue to derail the experience of most guests.

Chase's is a great hole in the wall. Very small menu, but everything is done correctly... especially the fries. The beer selection is excellent. Also, great selection of games to play while enjoying some adult beverages. No frills type of perfection.

I normally don't really care for fast food type chains but tagged along with my bf and his friend for a quick lunch here one day. They ordered burritos and I made my own steak quesadilla.   The burritos were massive. I had no complaints on my quesadilla. I liked that there were four different types of tortilla to choose from: white, wheat, cayenne and spinach. The girl working behind the register was also really nice. Personally, I prefer Chipotle if I were to eat Tex Mex type fast food because I feel their ingredients are more fresh and better quality... (Sorry, Izzo's fanatics).

My first visit to Cru Cellars was two years ago and that was the night I fell in love with a cheese, yes, a cheese.  Barely Buzzed, by the Beehive Cheese Co., is an espresso and lavender hand-rubbed cheese with subtle notes of butterscotch and caramel. Full-bodied with a smooth and creamy texture, Barely Buzzed will rock your world!  My dad lives about 20 minutes from the Beehive Cheese Co.(in Utah) and visits them often. He can't say enough amazing things about their wonderful staff and fabulous cheese.   So now back to Cru Cellars. After my first visit, I knew I had discovered something special. I took a wine class a month later and learned so much from owner Jennifer, and I met some wonderful people. In the spring, their new food menu made its debut and has been wowing food and wine lovers all over Tampa. This past weekend I met a few friends and we enjoyed a nice selection of wines paired with cheese and a few of their innovative dishes. We had a fabulous time and I can't wait to go back. Cheers to Cru Cellars!

Went here on a Monday with couple of friends for some drinks and small meals all the while also looking for a new Monday spot. I ordered two appetizers and a glass of pomegranate juice. My total came to $25!!! Whoa! That's a little pricey for small plates. Wings, good! Crab cakes, good! Cornbread fritters, good! Shrimp tacos, MEH!!! The texture was good, however, a whole shrimp is placed in middle of a taco shell. It'll be so much better if they were at least chopped up so it spreads in the shell more. The coleslaw was bland, not much flavor to it. Overall, the shrimp tacos just didn't cut it for us. I will not be ordering it in the future. Our server was good, but there were couple of times when he was nowhere to be found when we needed something. Needless to say, this won't be a regular Monday spot ;)

This place is so amazing! I enjoy my time here. It makes me feel like a Good Old Southern Belle!!  The bartenders are really crafty and serious to their craft! The Chef is so amazing! He's so accommodating and he actually take his time and asks guest how everything is. When me and cousin came here we were treated like Royalty. We were also asked how the food and our drink was as well as gained the scoop on what the next best appetizers were. We really enjoyed out time here! I enjoyed the waiter that made sure we were having a great time. The staff is just amazing! Everything was so awesome! Definitely worth the Buzz!

I am done with this place. While the coffee is excellent, the barista's are total a-holes. Yesterday, I was next in line, and the barista bypassed me, and placed the order for the guy behind me that appeared to be a regular. Really? I was in shock. There was no apology, nor was there any eye contact when he finally took my order. The barista's were more interested in chatting with the regular folks than finishing up with my order. This is not the first time this has happened, and I am not returning. Total morons running this place. I don't care how good your coffee is. If you don't know how to treat customers, there is no need for me to give you my business.

A nice spot for BBQ. Busy, but plenty of open tables. Food came out under 10 minutes. Giving four stars because the server rolled his eyes when we asked him a question. Besides that, a good experience.

Yum! This place is delicious! We went on a Friday night just after happy hour so it was pretty busy and there was a shortage of seats but it was quick moving and everyone was speedy and helpful. The Al Pastor was super flavorful. There are free chips and salsa and the chips are always fresh. We had classic margaritas which were good but not memorable. The Frescas were also refreshing. Great place for a quick bite to eat and a laid back atmosphere.
been on multiple occasions and its always as good as before. The wait can get a little long but its honestly worth the wait

Don't judge a book by its cover! Street tacos served on the corner outside of a truck  Always fresh, awesome customer service and the fish and shrimp tacos (can be separate) are amazing.   I spent some time in Mexico and have had street tacos while visiting in Mexico, this place is by far, the most authentic I've had in Tampa!

Decent but nothing special. If you're looking for a place to eat oysters and drink beer, come here for happy hour as Desire does have some good happy hour beer & oyster specials ($4 Abita drafts and $1 oysters if I'm not mistaken). There are many better tasting and cheaper options for food & drink outside of happy hour in the Quarter.

Easily and without a doubt my favorite food is pizza. Bury me with it. New Orleans is trying to get up in the pizza game and it's about time; we are way behind. Yes we have Pizza D and yes there's Mid City Pizza, Slice and let's not forget Theo's. However where each of those places excel in ingredients and tastiness, it's hard to find an excellent pie that is relative in price to the cost of what it takes to make each one. Blaze has that component down packed and for that, they're getting my #1 best pizza spot in Nola...for now.   You can get a nice sized pie for under $10 with however many toppings you want in any combination. It's like the Subway of pizza but I dare not put Blaze's quality on Subway's level. Nestled in a newer, edgier sector of downtown it's got that modern touch to its interior. You can choose from their specialty pizzas or build your own. Since it was my first time I went with a specialty as I didn't want the pressure of building something I'd regret.  I went with the Meat Eater, pause. Came out super quick, the only setback I had about it was when asked if I wanted a sauce as a topping, I went with the bbq which it was good, however I don't think I would have wanted it swirled over my whole pie. I should've asked if I could get some on the side. Wasn't expecting to commit my entire pizza to that, but hey, ya live n ya learn.   Super friendly and attentive staff, I also appreciated that the guy running the place went around and asked people if they were happy with their orders. A little bit goes a long way in hospitality with me. I'd definitely drop in here if I were you needing a downtown fix for a good pizza.

Dropped in Cheu on a Sunday afternoon and had no wait.  Enjoyed the Mojo Pork Dumplings while they prepared our ramen bowls.  The dumplings were outstanding.  The Miso Ramen and Brisket Ramen bowls we had were very good - rich in flavor, spiced just right, unique sauces.  Cheu is a funky place with a young, cozy vibe.  If I lived in downtown Philly, Cheu would be a weekly visit.

Went here today for the first time and ordered two different cupcakes.  There were many to chose from as well as homemade truffles, gelato, cakes, etc.  our cupcakes were very good but a bit to sweet for my taste. The atmosphere is comfortable and there are places to sit and relax while having coffee and a sweet treat.  The cake video running while we were eating was very intriguing to watch.  I would definitely go back but maybe this time try something other than a cupcake.

We wanted to listen to some music and have some beers on a Sunday night. TT was the perfect venue.  We only had beers so we can't speak to the food . But we had a really great time listening to an exceptional lineup of Blues artists for the Sunday night Jam.  There was no cover so we spent more money on the drinks and we had a great time. Was really cool to see the crowd up and dancing too. We will be back.

I just spent $25.00 for three COLD carne asada burritos!They charged me $1.20 extra for sour cream per burrito. I tried calling the phone number listed; to let them know how disappointing it is to have to eat a cold burrito, but it's not a working number.

Had an amazing private event here!! The staff was awesome (Taylor, Tom and Christina), the wine is excellent and the view can't be beat.   We would recommend for a private event or for wine tasting! One of the best views in SB, it won't disappoint.   Thanks so much Deep Sea!! We'll be back :)

I have been here a few times, but most recently the other night. Went with a party of 7 for a friends birthday. The food was delicious and the service was excellent. We had Noel and she was awesome! Ask for her. The place is a bit cramped, but the food and service made up for that.

Great dessert spot!  Very cool location and design, excellent desserts.  Parking is street only and can be a little crowded.  I don't believe they take reservations and I've had to wait every time I go.  I've never a meal there, but I assume it's great. Definitely worth going to and the waits aren't that bad.
Good. Not exceptional.  Large serving...almost too much.  Friendly service and quick.

Thrilled with the menu change and new chef. Hitting the breakfast venues on the weekend, it was great to see a menu with lots of gluten free options. The spinach frittata was delish- gluten free toast one of the best ever. And the preserves were amazing. The order of buckwheat pancakes was substantial- again gluten free- and tasty!  Open egg sandwich had great flavor; the andouille sausage really stood out. If you are an andouille fan, this is clearly for you. Great coffee- nice outdoor seating under a tree. Ok for doggies- enjoy!

We came here based off a recommendation from our hotel.  The food was fine but nothing impressive.  It was basic bar food.  The music was way too loud.  Impossible to have a conversation and we were there at 5:30 on a Saturday.  If you're in from out of town, try another place for dinner.
Great sushi. Nice service. Long wait if it's a busy weekend. Store is a little small but oh well. The Sushi a little pricey but I assume most sushi is now a days. I would recommend this place.

"Authentic" bordering on stereotypical, but the food is good and people seemed to be regulars but everyone was greeted like an old friend. Our handsome waiter was either authentically from Italy or doing the best exaggerated Italian-waiter schtick I've ever seen. (He's Italian).  The bread was ok, could have been better but at least they serve it hot. They bring a small place of (whatever the word for amuse bouche is in Italian I forget) which was just marinated vegetables - carrots, green beans with garlic, and grilled paper-thin yellow squash. They were featuring a special dish of  Linquini Pescatore - linguini, clams, mussels, crabmeat, shrimp, calamari in red sauce. Everyone was ordering it, and everyone who came in (including us) was "sold" just by the look of it - and it was delicious. The calamari was tender, there was ample amounts of seafood and pasta and excellent red sauce. We started with an arugula salad, which was dressed absolutely perfectly with lemon and oil. Couldn't resist dessert of "regular" (espresso) tiramisu (they also feature an amaretto flavor one). The food is pricey (the special pasta was $38) but certainly enough for 2 to split, even with my big appetite. This is definitely the kind of place to bring a nice bottle of wine and spend a relaxing evening with a nice meal. We'll definitely be back to try many other things so I will update my review.

I ordered some takeout from here as it was the closest sushi spot and had the highest rating. Their specialty rolls are a bit pricey but they do have some standard priced fare at the end of the online menu.  The sushi was fresh and tasty. They didn't skimp on the wasabi or ginger and I didn't have to ask for chopsticks.   Next time we will dine in as it looked intimate and a nice bar. I'm sure the is more noteworthy sushi in the area. They were our first try but not bad, not bad at all.

Had a craving for a burger and decided to try this place out since it's close to me, and it did not disappoint! Got the burger of the month and it was delicious along with some cheese fries and a beer. Everything here looked great and I will definitely be coming back to try more on the menu! Especially the milkshakes. The place itself is a small cute restaurant with an old feel to it and I would imagine its pretty busy on the weekends as there is not a lot of seating. Highly recommend!
This is the as good as any other  bbq I've had in Indianapolis.  Locally and family owned and operated.  A great business to support

My husband and I visited this place for the first time last weekend. I was in love with it the moment we entered. The restaurant is cozy and has a very classy decor. **Please make a reservation in advance** as this place fills up really fast.   Our server was very helpful in suggesting dishes as we had no clue as to what we should order.  Here's what we ordered : 1. Soup of the Day - a mushroom soup 2. Insalata Mista 3. Fusili Aristocratici 4. Salmone Al Limone E Capperi 5.Creme Brulee 6. Tiramisu  Everything listed up there was delicious. Do try their Tiramisu, I think it's the best I have ever tasted!   The entire experience was very memorable. Each dish is carefully crafted. My taste buds definitely enjoyed the entire gastronomic delights!

I feel like if Sheldon Cooper created a winter wonderland, this is just a small taste of what it would look like.    Jingle Rails is a yearly holiday tradition where they create different landmarks from Indy to beyond that are easily recognized. There are 9 model trains that traverse their way through the different landscapes.   Tips/Highlights:  +Tickets- You will need to purchase a ticket to the Eiteljorg Museum. This is considered an exhibit.  +Parking- Is free in the underground West street parking garage; however, make sure to get a validated ticket from the front desk.  As of Nov 2017- They are still working on maintenance in the garage, so is chunk of spaces blocked out.   + Stroller Parking - Please be kind to other sand park you strollers prior to entry into the exhibit in the marked area. The space inside, while ample, I could see getting extremely tight with a bunch of people and kids running around enjoying the exhibit  +Guides/ Find the "Hidden" Items - Upon entry there are large cards that are geared towards kids that gives them fun information and tries to keep them engaged with the exhibit by spotting specific items in each section  + The Displays - They are well crafted and you can tell the amount of time and attention to detail was extremely consuming.  The displays were but together using natural materials, so you will see a lot of wood.   + Noise - This may not be of concern to some; however, I could see certain kids, especially really young ones, possibly getting easily excited by the train noise and commotion of people taking photos possibly overwhelming. I would recommend going on a non-crowded day.

So I read that this place was a hit or miss. I have to say all the reviews were right. For the most part, I had a positive experience. The hotel was clean, no bugs, and no screaming kids or babies.Happy hour was nice as always. I was surprised how small the pool was and the beautiful bay view from my bedroom. I think this could be a five star hotel with some polishing. We paid $160 a night, so the price was ok. Only regret ---Mimosas for breakfast would have been stellar!    Here is a breakdown of my rating for Embassy Suites...    5 Stars  - Valet Service - Front Desk - Bartender - Server - Bay View - Housekeeping - Location / close to the casino and other attractions - church service on second floor on Sunday - cleanliness - great Starbucks inside hotel - peaceful in the morning - trolley service    4 stars  - breakfast - not the best yet good  3 stars  - not enough snack variety during happy hour  - signs for ice machine deceptive and we had to go to an odd numbered floor level for ice - loud music blared from convention center nearby till 3 AM and we could hear it clearly from a very high level floor ( no fault of the hotel yet it made our sleep unpleasant) - Internet not the best. - hot coffee in the lobby was not available until breakfast

Nice work here. Simple menu, decent prices, and good food. The mahi mahi was flavorful, sweet and tangy. The carne was very moist and succulent. The pork? A little bland, in my view. Good enough service, too.

Had a great meal at the bar on a Thursday night. Drinks are very reasonably priced and tasted great. Bartender was very nice- but used her phone on the bar to look at a drink recipe while making the drink, which kind of took away from her authority on beverages. At least one ice cube would come flying out of the glass when she stirred. Every. Time. Not exaggerating at all, at least 8-9 times while we were sitting there we had to block ice cubes from hitting us. It became funny and we'd laugh when inevitably she would use the same amount of ice as last time or stir the same way every time. Might want to change your approach next time! But it was a great atmosphere and the food was great. French onion soup was very very port-y but I really enjoyed it. Tons of flavor and would order it again. Burger was great. Fries were the best. Highly recommend and will be coming back (maybe with protective eyewear for ice cubes).

The New York style isn't really New York Style, it is better.  Perfect combination of crisp and chewy.  I like what they do with their beer options, for bottled beer, they have a beer fridege, and you pick what you want, and just tell the server.  **** awesome idea ****.  Our server was very good.  Accomodating, knowledgeable of the menu, and the right level of attentiveness and leaving us alone.  The booths off privacy with the high walls, but if you have a group, then long tables in the middle may be more suited.  Cheese bread was fantastic too.  Solid 4+*!  My wife would give this place a 5.0*, but this isn't the 1800's, she can get on Yelp and give her own rating now they women have suffrage as well.

The Quick: Fantastic hotel with incredible views of the water and an amazing restaurant (Oystercatchers)   The Details: I had a great stay at the Hyatt. The rooms were pristine, the view was gorgeous, and the staff could not have been more accommodating. Everything was great for a business trip as well as for enjoying some time at the pool and beautiful weather. The restaurant Oystercatchers was phenomenal. I cannot say enough good things about the food and the staff.   There's a fancy restaurant on the top floor as well. I did not eat there but I did enjoy a cocktail and the most amazing sunset. It's a great place to watch the sky turn rainbow colors. Overall a very nice experience at the Hyatt.   The Damage: I honestly have no idea since it was a business trip, but considering how nice and luxurious the hotel was, I expect it is not cheap.

I came here on a Saturday morning for brunch with my best friend because we saw a picture of the Drunken Monkey pancakes on instagram. The restaurant itself is adorable. The aesthetic feels cozy yet chic. We ordered the duck and sweet potato hash and this was definitely the star of the show (sorry, Drunken Monkey pancakes.)   I would come back again, its conveniently located off of South Street. Service was fine, nothing to write home about but altogether a solid experience.
Great spot in Chinatown. The food is excellent, and the prices are so cheap (especially if you order a bunch of plates as a group, rather than one meal per person). Definitely recommended.

Fancy, hip new burger joint.  I ordered the vegetarian burger, and LOVED it.  Seriously was bomb.  Drinks were strong and expensive.  And a little too fancy for me.  All of the reviews I heard about the food were amazing.  And I personally didn't think it quite lived up to the hype (aside from the veggie burger, of course).  I'm glad I went and had a great time.  I won't turn down an invitation to go again, but I also won't make a special effort to go back on my own.

My husband and I hyped up burger study thinking it was going to be a great place to eat, especially being in the St Elmo's family. We visited Burger study during devour downtown. We reserved our table on OpenTable and had no problem getting in. Our servers name was Justin and was really great to begin with. Once we ordered our burgers we continued to talk with our friends. About three minutes after our orders were taken, I completely forgot that I hadn't informed our server that I had celiac disease and needed a gluten-free bun. Justin immediately acted annoyed and frustrated with us. He went back to the kitchen to make sure to get this corrected. He did not return back to our table for over 20 minutes. He asked if a manager had come to speak with me and I said no. A manager never stopped by the entire night as Justin said they would. Our food came out great and we actually really enjoyed our meal. However the rest of our visit was really horrible. The server continue to treat us like total crap. It was frustrating for us because while we understood it was devour downtown and Justin was busy, we still figured we would be treated like human beings. My husband and I have both been in the service industry, and we understand it's frustrating when people forget to tell you things such as an allergy. We even apologized on her own behalf to Justin and said repeatedly that we completely forgot to tell him and apologized for any inconvenience WE caused HIM. However he still continue to treat us like absolute garbage. All this to say we definitely will never be back to Burger study. I'm not going somewhere I am treated differently because of an allergy that I can't help. We'll continue to go to St Elmo's, as they treat us with respect and are OVERLY accommodating when it comes to my allergy. It's a real shame that Burger Study can't be the same.

My brother in law is insane, no really he is. He will not eat anything unless he knows it comes from whole food. Now if that is not insane I don't know what is.  So we have been to this store a lot, sometimes just to buy one item to get the bag to fill it with cheaper stuff. I told you he was insane.  But at first I hated this place since I too am a bit insane and can not stand crowds of folks in small spaces and this place seems really small with tons of folks acting like it is a museum and just staring at the products that they have never seen before.  So we have been ;lucky in finding the store at times not too overly crowded and I can relax and shop, and when this happens I find some interesting products and great veggies and fruits along with some great cheese, even though they do not carry my favourite cheese, that being stinking bishop. Non the less I have found I do actually enjoy the store even when there are too many over processed folks shopping along side of me.  Oh but one time when looking at the cheese a female employee asked me if they she could help. I asked if the have any beer case cheese, now I realize it is a German cheese but have never heard it pronounced any differently than how I asked for it. So she asked me again and I said once again the same thing, she very haughtily said with a pointed finger, the beer cases are over there, of course pointing to the actual cases of beer. I then said no I am referring to the German pungent cheese. And then she once again in her best bitch voice said oh you mean and then pronounced it in the proper German. She of course knew what I was asking all along and it took ever bit of self control on my part to not crawl over the cheese counter and slam her head against the wall.   Yeah but I still like the place.

I came here tonight for dinner with four gal pals. It was my first visit to The Block. The experience was awful. I was the first of the group to arrive. Let me start by saying that the whole point of a hostess is to 1.) greet you at the door and welcome you to the restaurant, and 2.) select a table for you and seat your table. The hostess here did neither. When I approached her station at the door, the hostess seemed scared and nervous as if I had three eyes and was speaking a different language. I found this odd but told her that I'd like a table for five people. She seemed awkward and said "oh, uh, I don't know...um... let me check with the manager." Huh? Check with the manager? Aren't you the hostess?! This set the tone for the rest of our evening....  She went to get the manager, who also seemed jittery, and told me and a friend who had arrived that it should be ten minutes for us to get a table because they were waiting on a table who had paid their bill to leave. She told us that we could go to the bar while we waited.  We were standing at a long table in the bar area, which had no stools. The bar area waiter came by promptly to get our drink orders and we told him that we were  wating on a table. After more than ten minutes, the rest of our friends arrived and we were still not seated at a table. A friend who had just arrived asked the manager if we could order food and eat at the table that we were standing at, and she agreed and told us that we could pull chairs up to the table. Now, while it's good that she advised us that we could just sit there, why didn't she offer that to begin with?  Our entire group ordered the Block Burger medium well. The burger comes with fries and I asked if I could substitute my fries for green beans and was told by the waiter that it would be a $2 upcharge, which was fine. While ordering, I asked the waiter if the burger came with the traditional lettuce, tomato, onions, mustard, etc and was flatly told no. I asked if I could get that and he said sure. What he didn't say was that this addition of toppings would cost $2. I didn't find that out until I got my bill. SMH. I also should add, that the lettuce, tomato, and onions were chopped as though they were prepared for a salad and not a burger.  All of our burgers came out at different temperatures. Mine came closer to the well done side than the medium side (there was no pink at all in the burger), while a friend had a burger that was medium rare. It was completely pink on the inside. I didn't complain to the waiter about my burger, but my friend justifiedly alerted the waiter and he took her meal away to accomodate the error. He did not apologize. After the rest of our group was almost done with our meals, my friend still had not received her meal back so she went to talk to the manager about it. Shortly after, a brand new burger was brought out. It was cooked medium well, as requested. Right after she received it, the manager came to her and asked "how is your burger? good. good." and then she walked away. She didn't even wait for my friend to answer her question! My friend was quite upset about the long wait that she had for her corrected burger to arrive, as we were all finishing up when she finally got her burger. However, her bill was not accomodated for the inconvenience which my friend expected after talking to the manager. She asked the waiter, and was told "unfortunately we can't do anything about the bill because you ate the entire burger." Wow! I understand that not all restaurants/managers comp bills for ill-prepared meat dishes, but that response was strange.  While I've heard that the happy hour at the Block is pretty good, I don't think I'll ever return due to the poor service that we received tonight. From start to finish, the wait staff and manager seemed rushed and seemed like they did not care much for providing us with a good dining experience. I don't understand why the vibe was as strange as it was. Although the burgers and my green beans were all well-seasoned, I think the strange vibe of the staff, unspecified $2 upcharges, and poor service will prevent me from returning.

Been meaning to try for a while now, finally had the chance to stop in with bae.   Atmosphere is nice, music was great and staff was attentive and friendly!  I got the pierogis (apparently a new addition) and a side of braised green beans and tomatoes. Pierogis should have been great but I was underwhelmed. The meat inside was yummy, but the dish was cold when it came out and it generally lacked flavor. The green beans were also cold and tasted like frozen steamed green beans covered in Ragu sauce.   Bae had the burger and enjoyed it-nothing exceptional though.   I really wanted this place to be great! Hoping the menu gets better if I decide to stop in again.
Self service ordering and great tasting food. Service was great as well.  The dining area was more clean than I've seen in awhile.

Yuu-UM! Great food, upbeat ambiance, great service...overall an amazing experience and restaurant! I loved that they played latin/spanish music! It is definitely a lively environment---amazing complement to the mexican cuisine served!   For antojitos/appetizers:   First, get the guacamole ($9). Someone will come by and mix & blend the ingredients from scratch into a delicious dip!   The queso fundido ($9)---ok, it's simple in nature: melted cheese. But it is DELICIOUS! It is served with a side of warm, steamed tortillas. So good.   The pulpo ($12)---light and delicious! Highly recommend it!   Main entree:   The salmon ($22). It is served with green fava beans and some bites of fried yuca, over a sea of some tasty, green sauce. The yuca was a tad salty but still great. The salmon was beautifully broiled and flavorful. Overall, a mouth-melting meal!   No desert. But next time!

Nothing has changed since the last time I was in this drive thru, except at the moment I've been waiting for twenty minutes....thought I'd keep busy and update my review. I'm tired and hungry- next time I'll get some tacos from an actual restaurant and just call it in. This is completely ridiculous....waiting and waiting. Two cars ahead of me....last time I'll ever come to this place I really mean this now!

We love burger up! One of the first elevated burger places in Nashville Burger Up always has us coming back for several years. The marathon burger is the best burger and veg friendly! Great service and delicious beers and cocktails. What more could you want.

When contemplating a pregame spot for the Yelp 10th bash, I was glad when some good friends invited me to meet at black sheep for a number of reasons.  First it was spending time with good friends instead of drinking alone like an asshole. Second, in order to keep my elite status, I need to review things and in order to review things, I actually have to go somewhere, otherwise I'm reviewing another damn McDonalds.   Lastly, since I never go out, eating out is a treat I relish and Black Sheep didn't disappoint. I had a happy hour Guinness and 2 of their $5 apps ( wings and cheesesteak spring rolls). Both were out of this world. I'd go so far as to say that the wings rivaled the best I've ever had.   So mark Black Sheep as a new go to pub in Philly. They had me at $5.
If you love calzones then you will love this place. They are so big that I am never able to finish them. Best calzone in STL.

Oops I had written a review for the wrong place! So disregard previous review.   I know this place is popular in Bellevue but honestly I think it's just okay. The coffee is good (I like the maple latte) but the menu isn't clear and the desserts aren't usually very fresh. Maybe I've just had a bad experience but i'll try it again!

North 53.... The ideal cozy little late night date venue.  At least that was my first thoughts and impressions upon going to North 53.   The venue is small, dimly light and just cozy. North 53 has a signature smoke & oak drink that smells basically like camp fire and well smoke.. So if you're lucky enough to walk in when someone has just ordered it, you'll get this amazing waft of smoke as you walk through the door.   First off, if you go to North 53, definitely go with the intention of trying one or more of the hand crafted signature drinks. We had the Smoke & Oak, the London Calling (think a london fog with booze) and one other one (don't remember it). All the drinks were great. There's definitely a reason this place has been on yeg's list of best drinks in the city.   The menu has a few appetizers and a handful of main entrées. My friend and I each just ordered two appetizers and that more than enough for both of us. My friend ordered the salmon and the brussel sprouts and I ordered the fried chicken and meatballs. I can't speak for my friend's dishes, although she said it was tasty. My favorite of my 2 dishes was definitely the meatballs, although the chicken was moist/tender and came with a tasty dipping sauce.   Overall this place was great and like I initially stated, the perfect cozy date night venue. My guess is that they probably get pretty busy on weekends and during regular dining hours so I would suggest reservations or just go a little later in the evening. I will absolutely be going back to North 53 in the future.

We have been enjoying Enzo's on such a regular basis since it was reinvented out of the former Nabruzzi that I decided it was time for a yelp review. By now, we have tried everything from appetizers and daily soups to pizza, Strombolis, pastas and dessert. The portions are amazing and the pricing is family friendly. Tonight, we enjoyed fabulous service from Charlie. His knowledge of the menu and ingredients was excellent as well as we wanted to check out a few items that we had been wanting to try from our prior visits. We definitely are enjoying the improved service, generous portions and well priced menu from Enzo's and look forward to even more visits.

If you are visiting Nashville and want to hear live music at the famous Broadway area, this is a must visit!! The live music here was absolutely beautiful! There were so many places to choose from, but this place was the best choice we made.  I came here with my family on a friday night and enjoyed every single moment of the live music, food, drinks and atmosphere! The cheeseburger taco and their local nashville beers were very delicious! Staff was super nice and friendly.   Highly recommended!!

I'm always in the mood for sweets First time there though I was a little put off by the ordering system-picking from choices on a pad. You pick the donut, topping etc Not sure why you can't just place the order verbally-saves time, they say. After all that, the actual donut was ok!
Just had dinner there tonight. I had veal, my wife had chicken.  Both were excellent!  We will be returning for another dinner.

It is a super cute coffee shop. I stopped by the area while I was dining next door at OTB. I love the chalk board decoration with all the cute quotes. We saw other yelp review for their donuts so we decided to try one. Unfortunately, they didn't have a lot of options. We got tiramisu donuts and it was delicious...also it is gluten free. I love the environment and it is a very cute little coffee shop to spend some time to catch up with friends. The pricing is reasonable for coffee and their coffee is from Buddy Brew which is one of my favorite as well as Kaleisia tea. :]

The drinks were very creative and delicious. Food was good. The pig ears, pasta, and panna cotta were the best. I didn't really think the curry goat was good although it was the most recommended entree. Overall, pretty good dinner.

Forget the specialty donuts and just get one of the hot donuts, made to order fresh all day. You get a free honey donut with your chicken order, but I actually don't like that kind at all and usually give it to my daughter, who inevitably hovers over me waiting for my donut like a baby bird waiting for food to drop from its mother's beak.  The famous fried chicken is really really good, if you love crispy savory skin. In fact, I could just order their za'tar dry rub skin and be happy. While the chicken is also good, it's really just fried chicken, and when you order a "half chicken" you're not actually getting a half a chicken. You get four smallish pieces of chicken that are mostly bone. It's delicious, but it's not a lot of meat for the price.

Fairly good. The hype for Korean Fried chicken has finally made it to Nashville. Haha with that being said if you want to try it this place will be the spot. Get your quick fix for Korean style fried chicken! They have marinated and fried style. Also they have variety of Thai dishes and sushi. Owners are super friendly and very accomedating.

I've been going here for dinner and drinks with friends on Monday nights for quite some time and it's definitely become my regular pub of choice, due in part to these friends who go here frequently. As a non-drinker (for the most part), I am mostly concerned with the food quality, which is excellent! I adore their food specials for special events such as holidays (the Canada Day specials were delicious for example in 2014, as was the St. Patrick's Day special). The soups are consistently unusual but good. The Monday night steak special with all you can eat sides is a consistent favorite for our group.  My friends really enjoy trying the great drinks from their 72 taps and other drink menu, and I happen to like having a blueberry tea here on occasion, though it's rare to see me drink alcohol. We love the service we get when we come here!  Don't forget to order a yummy dessert if you do eat here: the tapioca dessert is especially good as is the Bailey's Creme Brulee and the Chocolate lava cake.

My grandparents were in town and I wanted a place with some southern charm. This place was perfect! We had to wait about 40 minutes for a table but Main Street Franklin is always fun to explore so the wait seemed like nothing. They have a great selection of southern favorites and their lunch special is a great deal. Cool atmosphere and at night they have live music. The food was delicious, the service was super friendly, and everyone left happy! Great experience overall.

Good eats!  Great service!! The crab cake eggs Benedict were amazing!!!  I also had the carnitas chilaquiles-- equally awesome!    Modest Mouse show last night was super; but this place for breakfast.... not sure which I enjoyed more!

Dave's chop house burger...amazeballs.  Loved the extra flavors for everything.   Power salad was deelish.  (-) Fried chicken salad was good except chicken was TOO fried.  Hard to eat too fried which cost them a star. Fried pickles...amazeballs  Loaded fries...amazeballs  Mint shake...amazeballs Butter pecan scoop...amazeballs   Ice cream tasted homemade.  Plus...they offer a military discount!  Call this 4.33 stars!  Forgot to add...service was terrific.  Let's make it 4.48 stars.

I've been a fan of the South Philly location for many years.  I'm happy to say that this location is every bit as good with much better parking.   This is the best roast beef sandwich I have had outside of the North Shore in the Boston area, and might even be better.

The gelato is obviously the highlight here. Every flavor I've ever gotten here has always been top notch. Great after dinner spot to wind down and relax with plenty of outdoor seating.  The guy who took our order was extremely friendly and very helpful describing all the different flavors to us.

Chick Fricken Fil A. Well, it's about time!  I luvitty-luv-luv Chick-fil-A. Their chicken is so good.  It's like candy. Sweet, breaded chicken candy.  Nuggets. Sammitches. Minis. Lemonade. Sweet Tea. It's all good. Really, literally all good.   As for the shiny new Reno location, I've only been once and that was a drive-thru venture just before noon. It was crowded, as I expected, but they had a small army of employees rocking it. Despite the throngs of chicken cravers I was in and out in short order and treated to the customary excellent service. CFA really sets the bar in courtesy, professionalism and helpfulness. I've never had a bad experience at any location and I've been to a few.   Hooray for CFA for finally planting their flag in the soil of Northern Nevada. Hooray also for the people of Northern Nevada for welcoming this establishment to our area.
Come here every couple weeks and load up on tacos. Ask for the salsa from the back its way hotter than the bar one

Have been here twice now and liked it both times. The place has a bar in front, restaurant in back. I bring my kids and don't love waiting in the bar for a table, but that's really about the only down side to this place. Good kids options and tons of food in the kids meals. Also great things for the kids to play with while you wait. Salad is the Gianino family recipe and done better than most of the Gianino family  places in town, but super big so it may fill you up. Great toasted ravs, really plump and fresh. I like the variety on the menu and my food was good and fresh both times. It would be nice if pastas came with a salad, but the salad is so big that my husband and I shared the one that came with his entree and both had plenty. I will be back for a great dinner where my kids are happy too.

Great place in the "middle district" of St Pete off 49th and 30th ave. Great Burg favorites, freshest breakfast in town, and they pay homage to the old Cuban Delight with a cool Cuban menu! Small space so get here early! Limited seating out front.

This place is amazing. If you like duck, you must try their infamous duck fries. It is sooooooo good. This is my go to spot when in doubt. The reason why it didn't get 5star is because food literally takes forever to come out and portions are kind of small. But other than that everything is amazing, interior is cute although can get a little loud during busy hours and it's hard to hear myself breathe.

Second run theater that still has the corporate backing of Cinemark, making it slightly nicer than your average second run theater. Nice because you can still find second run 3D movies here. This older theater has a fun retro vibe.

OK, I ordered their supreme pizza with a thin crust. If this is thin, I would hate to see their regular crust. There is nothing thin about it. The crust is quite similar to Papa Johns, a location that I have boycotted for years. The toppings are nothing special. Will not be returning and do not recommend. Go the NYPD (New York Pizza Department) or Broadway Pizza if you want good pizza.

John Besh delivers in a big way here. The $.75 raw oysters are amazing and the fried ones are soft, not greasy or chewy and just friggin delicious. Drinks are half price during happy hour and I'd suggest the French 75 or the bourbon smash with peaches and mint. I was looking forward to this as was my wife and let's just say, there was not a single drop of disappointment in this place. Just fantastic.

Food was great but our experience was a little odd...  I went for brunch on a Sunday with my roommates/friends. We had to wait outside for probably 15 minutes, not a biggie though. We were seated and when our waitress came to fill our waters she spilled one of the full glasses all over my friends phone/lap/shoes. She didn't really apologize which was weird... then about 10 minutes later she came to fill up the coffees and spilled my friends orange juice in the process of filling the coffees. It was 2 spills within 15 minutes and it was kindof crazy to be honest. Thank god it wasn't the hot coffee!  Otherwise, the food was very good. The atmosphere is very hipster but it was cool. I would go back, but I would be careful!

This brand new salon looks so chic inside. Everything is sparkly and fresh and just looks so new from the floors to the chairs to the giant flat screen and the trendy decor.   I had my eyebrows done and a basic pedicure and I've never had anyone take so much time with me at this price range ($30 for the pedi, $12 for brows). It's slightly more than other places in Old City but definitely worth it. I'll be back!
It was my second time eating here the first time was a very long time ago. Well the food was great and so was the service if nice portions. I had the seafood platter was very good

The whiskey is just coming of age. The brews are distinctive - this is not another Sierra Nevada imitation. And the food is both good and a good value. Upstairs for the non-family crowd and downstairs otherwise. Either way the restored depot architecture is worth checking out.

Awesome layout, great music, interesting drinks, and knowledgeable bartenders. I had the Clubber Lang which was Bourbon, bitters, and vermouth I think. They went down realllllly smooth.
