We sat upstairs but there's plenty of seating in the courtyard by the stage too.  Wine and cheese isn't my thing, but that's the main draw. I was happy to find out they were way more than just a place to get a wine and cheese tray.

Kyber Pass Pub is a great little bar and restaurant. I love all of the vegan options and the unique cuisine. I've tried both the vegan chicken po'boy and the vegan bbq pulled pork. Both were delicious! So far, my favorite side order has been fried okra, which was a weekend special. This place also has a nice craft beer selection, which I always appreciate. Warning: they're crowded! You'll probably need to wait a while for a table.

My family and I have been going to Nicks in South Philly since we were kids.  I am so happy they are in my local neck of the woods.  Their food is amazing every time.  Their service is hit or miss.  The food is the best!!
Our first experience at a Vietnamese restaurant and it was amazing.   You can taste the freshness of the noodles, the vegetables and the meat.   We are sold and will be back again.

I got the prime rib, it was okay. Nothing to write home about. I've definitely had a bigger piece of prime rib at other places but it wasn't bad.  The potatoes tasted a little like instant potatoes. The ceasar salad was good.  There was just nothing that stood out or made us want to come back.  Id rather go to longhorn.
The standout here was the crawfish pie. The creole was good, the pasta was solid and well prepared, the drinks were good. I would go back again and again for a crawfish pie.

Looking for Unique liquor, this is the spot to go. Unlike most liquor stores their stock here has more variety in choices. See photos. Although it can be quite pricey but they do carry the cheap stuff too. A bit smaller than most common liquor stores. They do have a very wide selection of wines so if that's your thing, check this place out. Maybe even if your cooking for that special someone for a perfect night. Staff is friendly, parking is moderately small. This place is also good for a quick liquor stop before house parties. Some of these alcoholic drinks look very foreign maybe that's why the price is high. After visiting this place, i'd come here for their rare drinks.  Recommended for the chefs looking for a unique wine to cook with or people seeking new liquor tastes.

OK food.  Horrible service.  We stopped in for a late lunch on a Sunday.  We ordered the dan dan noodles, braised beef noodle soup, Sichuan beef noodle soup, doubled cooked pork, and long pepper beef.  The food was pretty decent but, the service (from the host to the wait staff) was absolutely dismal.  It took forever to get seated, and even longer to get anyone to stop by the table and take our order.  The place was about 25% full so there was no reason why the staff could not pay attention to us.    Forget smile with a service.  I did really like the long pepper beef and if I want to try it again, I will make sure to get take-out.

Chill spot for hipsters and an okay happy hour for drinks, prices were affordable. We didn't get appetizers for happy hour, just a brownie sundae dessert which was super rich. If you're looking for a hipster spot and that's your scene, this place works.

We will not return to this place. Ordered Fried Green Tomato Sliders and mushroom Swiss sliders!  Bread was cold and hard and most of the sandwich WAS bread!  Meat was flavorless.... Fried Mac n cheese was ok but the onion rings were greasy.  Bill was $39 for two people! Crazy for not good food.  Sorry, Burger Up, we won't be going back.

Came in an placed a carry-out it has been 30 mins since it was put in one person has asked if I'm doing ok, looking around had I known the 3 tables I'm looking at that have been empty an dirty were still going to be here I probably would have stood up an left, it's a holiday an low on staff an poor on service this place has been going down hill for some time, take the time an drive extra few miles to another steak joint, it's time for the gm to go or clean house..... Pitiful

- TIP:  After you eat your meal -- they've got great prices on desserts ($1 baklava) and to-go containers of hummus.  - CON:  Closes @ 7pm most days.  - BOTTOM LINE:  Even if the food was mediocre, it'd be worth grabbing considering you get a big plate of mediterranean food for $8.  But the food is far better from mediocre -- and I would be ordering it instead of my other go-to take-out mediterranean spots (e.g. Hummus Grill, Al Zaytouna) if it was open later.

We visited on a weeknight, when there were only a few other parties dining. Service was fine, and prices on drinks reasonable. Some might debate the price of the tacos at $4/each, but think they're worth it. I had the carnes asada, and all around the table, everyone who had one, two, or three tacos, enjoyed them very much.   Based on Ian Froeb's glowing review of the bacon burger (STL PD 6.16.16) but not wanting the bacon, I ordered the basic Katrina's burger, medium. It arrived at my table pretty much well done, a disappointment. No dedicated fryer, so no GF corn chips, another disappointment. But I'd go back for tacos and drinks.

Recently stopped in during a bar crawl for some drinks and Battletoads. Couldn't eat the buzz away, but loved what I saw. Photos on walls showed the transformation from Tahiti Joe's/Latitudes into The Bricks of today, and brought back some old Ybor memories. The staff was kind, and the video game corner was a blast. Delicious coffee, and had an artsy feel as well. Every food item that passed our sniffers en route to their respective hipster pie holes was cause for comment. I will certainly be coming by for a sammich at this place.

Wow, what a disappointment. It's a pretty building in a pretty place, hence the 2 stars. The service was terrible. I very rarely leave a bad review but this place deserves it. When I booked the room it was for 2 people and their "bay breeze package" but when I got here I was told the reservation was only for 1 person. This resulted in an additional charge of over $50 to add my daughter back onto the package. Since it was my first time at the hotel and it was their error, I asked if there was a comp- 1 front desk person and 2 supervisors later I was able to get 1 free drink at the bar for a house brand drink. Really? All that work for a $4 glass of wine? When my daughter's cold got worse and then she couldn't use the additional service I just paid for, neither the hotel or the spa would work with us to rebook the service, issue a gift certificate or anything else. The room was ok, nothing special. The hot tub and pool were ok, but creepy later in the evening due to poor lighting and minimal staff. Save yourself money and stay some other place that is more customer oriented.

Not a bad place just a short walk from the ballpark.  They have a broad selection of beers and a small food menu.  I liked the chocolate milk stout quite a bit.  Service was a little lacking compared to the other breweries that I visited.  I'm pretty sure that I could have fit the bartender's personality and helpfulness into a thimble.
Fun little place! The bartender and regulars are a blast. Drinks ice cold. Will definitely be back

Many improvements found after a follow up visit months after their startup.  Lighting has improved, chips are much better, service was spot on and outside of a service error at the table next to mine, I'd call this location a solid three star location.

DeGrazia is a lovely gallery!  It had been years since my last visit to the gallery and was such a sweet treat today. A friend prompted the visit, as he had recently gone and sang its praises. So we made it a family affair and went for a morning visit.  Even if you are not a huge fan of his art or of Southwestern art, there are pieces to be found and enjoyed. My oldest son found a piece that he was thrilled to show us (of a reptile) and it was stunning.  Look for it on your next visit! The small gallery was my favorite part of our visit. They have guest artists displaying their work for two weeks. Today, Mary Alice Kellogg was the guest artist and her work was a stunning new approach to depicting the southwest. In the small gallery, visitors are able to meet the guest artist! Wonderful! It is a Tucson treat and a fantastic place to bring out of towners!
Very good , very limited vegetarian options , authentic and well done.        I visiting twice and was happy ..... and the prices are great ... you can have a great dinner below 20 bucks ...

I've been to the South Philly location twice and this was my first experience at the NoLibs location. I was satisfied as usual. I had the chicken fried steak with potato latke and corn muffin while my dining partner had the Eggs Benedict. It wasn't anything spectacular, but it's a consistently good meal. The atmosphere is cute, though the Northern Liberties location seems a lot smaller than the South Philly one. We were practically sitting on top of the people next to us.
The owner John and his manager have always been very nice to me.  Food is great and our server Cheyenne was hilarious and very good at her job.

Food was good. Would have appreciated a more detailed menu. The store was very dirty. Floors dusty, not just snow dirt. Napkins on floor. Tables not wiped. Drink area messy. Soda sprayed all over my hand when I dispensed it. Store was nearly empty at 1:30 pm and 4 staff members behind the counter with few customers.

Not a good burger ..... A great burger. An the draft beer selection rocks many on seen (& I've seen a lot). Shiracha, egg, avocado, on a great selection of ground beef. Sweet potato fries? YEP... Get them. On of my favored summer beers, the Radler on draft..... Sweet.  Off the path of downtown nashvegas but nonetheless worth it. TIP- Get the burger med rare or you'll miss out on the quality of the beef.

The food and service were excellent, great staff who was very friendly and helpful. We had the pasta and Korean chicken, chicken was a little burnt but i like it that way so no big deal. The beef tartar seemed to be a lower grade meat since it came in big chunks and was alright, the chips it comes with were over salted. I will say the cocktails were a bit of a let down, we tried 4 and only the one with the brandy and egg white was any good. We were really looking forward to the cocktails and they were just very bland and boring. A decent place but for what they are I think they could do better
I got the philly cheese steak with fried pickles.   Fried pickles were pretty meh, cheese steak was fine.   They messed up the order for the guy in front of me.

Snow was light and fluffy, and the creators behind the counter was more than generous with their syrups by dousing the ice with a second pouring of our chosen flavors.  We chose nectar cream and passionfruit.  Now here's the thing - I like my flavors pronounced and bold as much as the next person, but I was slightly disappointed with the somewhat artificial taste of the passionfruit syrup.  Comparing it to another place my husband and I tried on our first day here, this place's homemade grapefruit-basil syrup paired with their version of the nectar cream felt more light and refreshing; however because theirs did not have a second dousing of flavor, the syrup didn't get a chance to drizzle their way towards the bottom before we gobbled it up.  In the end ultimately, this place is clearly popular and you're not going to leave feeling un-refreshed.

Great pitchers of margaritas, food is alright. I wish the chips and salsa were better. I asked to pay the up charge for the spicy salsa and she brought me a bowl of green slimy looking sauce. Not too appetizing. The burritos were decent though.

Three stars for the pizza, four stars for the buffalo chicken wings. The pizza was good. The toppings weren't the best. But the dough, sauce and cheese were nice. I got a regular combination and that isn't the specialty here. They're best with the Californicated pizzas I don't like. The chicken wings were really good and that's what I get if I came back. Good meat flavor. Maybe some of the best quality chicken wings I've seen in the area. And the buffalo wings had a nice zing.
Very good pancakes and eggs! I knocked it a star because it was a breakfast place with bad coffee (burnt, flavorless). Can't have that.

Best italian food I've found in the area! A lot of it is made to order, and all of it is excellent. The fettuccine alfredo and white pizza are my favorite because they're super cheesy, but not overwhelmingly so.   It's also BYOB, so go in, order some delicious, homemade italian food and enjoy a bottle or two of wine!

Bait and switch! I have spent lots of time in piano bars all over the county, so I was really excited when I saw "the red piano" bar open on state street.  I took my girl and group of friends there with high hopes and we were all very disappointed in the fact that they have no piano. I mean they have a grand piano that hangs from the sign. After taking one step into the place I could hear without even seeing it that it was a cheap digital piano. Also I could see they spared no expense on the decor. I've been to really shabby establishments compared to this place that had real pianos (most have 2). I feel no self respecting piano bar should have a digital piano, and if they do it should at least be a high end one. We all left immediately feeling cheated. I guess if you just want to go to a bar this place would be ok. But if you are a musician, or just a music fan you will be frustrated and disappointed as we were. I will not go back to this fake piano bar.

Scallion pancakes were quite good. Pork chop soup had great flavor and crunch. We also opted for one of the Indonesian curries, which had a nice mix of flavors. Courteous staff. When we asked for our leftovers to be packed to go, we didn't specify that the rice needed to be packed as well, and ended up with curry sans rice as leftovers. Worth trying once, not sure that this will go into my regular rotation.

I was craving a salad, but I wanted a specific kind of salad.  A friend recommended we try Marathon.  I saw the menu and found a few salads that looked good.  Then, when we sat down, they handed us the "control freak" menu.  There are a few categories (burgers, salads, etc.).  In the salad section, there is a list of greens, veggies, and various ingredients/toppings/cheeses.  I was able to design the very salad I wanted: mixed greens, grilled veggies, feta cheese.  It was fabulous!      I went back another day for a hearty, yummy burger.  The fries were fresh-cut potatoes.      Marathon might become one of my favorite spots in Philly.  I'm a sucker for fresh, well-prepared food.  It need not be fancy.

I had a mixed experience here:  Donut: hubs and I agreed that this was one of the best donuts we have ever had. So soft. So good.  Everything bagel with jalapeño salsa cream cheese: the cream cheese was really tasty, not too spicy, and a generous amount. My bagel was a little tough but was generously coated  Iced coffee : it did the trick but wasn't special  One area they can really improve on is the ordering system. They should calls names or order numbers. Calling only what was ordered led to much confusion on the shop

i do not mind paying "high dollar" for top notch food, but almost 60.00 for what I had received, NEVER AGAIN!!!!!  The cake slices were so small, they didn't even stand up in the box. They were also dry. If a shop is going to have teenagers work there, they need to know how to cut a slice of cake.  One cookie for almost 4.00!  REALLY?  The search is still on for a great slice of cake!!!

Ended up having dinner here last Friday. Didn't think we could get a table without making a reservation, but fortunately they were not that busy that evening. Talk about food presentation, as the wife put it 'too pretty to eat". I was a bit skeptical at first after seeing the menu listing dishes with way too many ingredients. But it turned out great for the most part. Started with 3 small appetizers, shrimp corn dog, deviled eggs, and some fruit warp in bacon. They were all ok but the deviled eggs stood out quite well. They mixed the yolk I think with some pesto which made it green and gave it a different flavor to your typical deviled eggs. The wifey had the veggie plate which according to her is excellent while I had the short ribs. The ribs were tender and flavorful and the roasted veggies and mash potato made excellent sides. But my favorite part of my entree was the potato chip tube filled with mash potato, that was just awesome. They could put that in their menu as appetizer and I'd order it. Didn't care much for the other sauces and oils and dressing on my plate, the meat and veggies by themselves were good. For dessert, we chose the cheesecake in a jar. It's no junior's cheesecake but its still good and the ice cream on the side was quite tasty and the plate presentation was quite clever. Overall, it was an excellent dining experience, service was excellent, food taste great, food presentation was excellent, and price was pretty reasonable.

Really digging this plaza that they're building around Miniso - lots of Asian goodness next to each other and plenty of parking.  Miniso is a cute, bright store that was established in Japan, but the majority of its products are Chinese. (If you look them up, they're actually described as a "Chinese low-cost retailer" headquartered in China) There are lots of things to look at - think similar to an Asian dollar store. You'll likely find lots of cute things from purses/make up to stationery and kitchenware. Pricing is ok - I saw things between $3 and $20 ish (for some of the lamps and headphones.)  It's fun to walk around, and prices are a bit cheaper than a similar, bigger store in WEM.

Service was mediocre. Not at all impressive. Drinks were weak. Food was ok. Not exactly the best place for food in NOLA when there are so many other options around. And it's on Burboun Street which sucks. Burboun Street smells like piss and puke. It's disgusting. I would not go back to this spot.

I pick Edley's over any BBQ place in Nashville. No it's not like Kansas and no it's not like Texas but it's to me the best Nashville offers on the BBQ side. Even though ribs ribs ribs the brisket is everything. A tips:  the brisket usually sells out after the 12 noon rush.

This place is exactly as advertised: unique, healthy and delicious!   I had the backyard bowl and this thing was epic! You get your week's worth of antioxidants in one mouthful!  It seems a little pricey on first look, but when you take into acct the ingredients and the amount you get (you can make two meals out of the large), the price is on par!  Parking is a bit of a pain, but this place is well worth the effort.   I just wish they had a BYB in Austin!

I stopped at the 7th Sun Brewery for a beer, and was enjoying it outside when I caught a whiff of BBQ. I discovered the delicious smells were coming from Eli's BBQ, just up the road, so I changed dinner plans and went to Eli's. Eli's is nothing fancy, just an old-time BBQ shop. There ARE a few picnic tables under the shade trees, but it's mostly carry out. The food is all BBQ, smoked in house...or rather in the big smoker in the yard. Smoked pork, ribs, chicken, and a few sides is all that's on the menu, but it's all good. NOTE: They are only open TWO days a week, on Friday and Saturday afternoons, and they DO run out of food, so get there early to avoid disappointment!
Still one of my favorite places in town. I am loving their current tea selection. Jasmine key lime. This place is a must.

Had a great meal tonight at this establishment. We started with the potato skins which were amazing! I then had the veal tortellini which was very good. My only complaint was for 36 dollars they did not give you very much. My boyfriend enjoyed his meal as well and we would definetly return!
DELICIOUS LUNCH !!!!  Worth a try--their lunch specials include a LARGE variety of pasta dishes with soup or salad.  Cream Brule cheesecake is superb!  Very very good homemade Italian food.
I'm a first timer and was excited to stop in here for lunch today. The nuggets and sweet potato fries were overly greasy and soggy. I will not be back.

Absolutely delicious! I was led here through a random video ad on facebook, and I am so glad I followed the breadcrumbs (pun intended) to this macaroni spot. There is so much variety, that you could come once and then come again and again, even though everything is macaroni and cheese. I went with the cheesesteak mac because I was in Philly and was not disappointed in any way! The service is friendly and very willing to give advice and recommendations for the best options for you. Whatever you do don't skip getting their signature crumble, it is definitely the perfect topping, I wish I could have taken a cup home for my own mac and cheese. Now, the macaroni and cheese base is PRETTY good, if you make a strong baked mac and cheese this might not compete on purely base alone but once they make their combo magic you have what has earned this place a solid four stars. Limited seating so more of a quick eat, if that matters, but, if you are in Philly definitely stop in!

Would give a negative star if I could. Haven't had a good bar experience in about 4 attempts. Slow is an understatement. If they have more than 1 bartender working that's a good night. They should train all of the staff to at least pour a beer. Last but certainly not least... the entire building smells like a toilet. It's like they are trying their hardest to fail. From the day they opened its been terrible.

went for breakfast on a monday.  20 minute wait but bypassed that by sitting at the bar.  Great menu.  Biscuits were great.  Had biscuit egg/bacon and cheese and fried chicken/egg/cheese biscuit.  Coffee was great.  Nice environment
I love this place. The food and coffee are excellent. On the weekends, expect a bit of a line but as a local, It's great and I would highly recommend their "Build your own" hashes.

One of my favorites in town!  I've tried almost everything on the menu... and love it alllllll!  These are my favorite dishes:  *Starters  Shrimp and Pork Rolls Pork Belly Tacos STICKY CHICKEN WINGS  *Soups Wonton Soup Pho Brisket add Meatballs Tofu & Tomato Pho  *Entrees Lemongrass Chicken and Fried Rice (it comes with white rice so you'd have to sub it) Seasoned Noodles & Chicken (Pho Ga Co) Mint Moon Crepe (Banh Xeo) Chicken and Waffles  *Dessert  Pandan Waffle with a Scoop of Red Bean Ice Cream  *Drinks  Lychee Mule ------ something new and I LOVEEE it!  This sounds like a lot, but I promise you won't be disappointed!  For starters, I picked my three favorite but if I had to pick one, it'd be the sticky chicken wings! You must try how amazing it taste - fresh crispy fried chicken drizzled with this sweet teriyaki glaze and spicy mayo (sometimes I like to sriracha for another dimension of favor).   For soups, you can't go wrong with pho - especially to cure a hangover! I first had the tofu and tomato pho during lent season and didn't expect to like it, but I did. The broth in both has really good favor so you can tell they put some love in their pho!   For entrees, the most popular and my favorite is the Lemongrass Chicken - flavor, flavor, flavor! Most of the time sub the white rice for brown rice, but sometimes I treat myself and sub it for their deluxe fried rice! The Seasoned Noodles & Chicken is my go-to as well. It's basically the reverse of a chicken pho! You get a small bowl of broth on the side, so you can sip it in between eat bite. The bowl of noodles and sliced chicken is topped with lettuce and fried shallots/onion. There's a small sauce on the side (of soy sauce/sesame) that you can mix in your bowl to your likings.   For dessert, pandan waffle and red bean ice cream! If you don't like red bean, don't knock it til' you try it! The green tea ice cream taste just as good. Warm waffles and ice cream, mMm. You can't beat that.   For drinks, they have CHAMANGO smoothie! It's soooo good - you get: the tamarid candy straw, cubes of frozen mango, and apricot syrupy goodness!   My experiences here have always been great. The food is delicious and made with some mad love. Sometimes they are super busy so be patient because the food is worth it! I heard they're adding some new items to the menu and I can't wait to try them. Highly Recommended!!

Nothing fancy but it does have some delicious Vietnamese food.  My son loves it here especially the shrimp rockets and shrimp & pork noodle soup.  The beef pho is yummy.  Perfect on a cold day too!  Great service - quick and attentive but they aren't obnoxious about it.  Love coming here.
Place looks like a speak easy from outside. I went on trivia night, it was fun, didn't try the food.

The food was interesting and delicious but the server was quite condescending. He also said that their computer could not separate our order from that of our dining companions so we had to make a guess and divide it by percentage. I found that difficult to believe when most other restaurants have no such trouble. Also we were on the second floor which was less noisy than the first floor but the floor vibrated horribly anytime someone walked by. Since it was a busy Saturday night the shaking was pretty much continual. Not sure whether we'll give it another try.

Went here the other night for the first time and was pleasantly surprised! The menu was so unique and I can't wait to try more of the entrees when I return. :)   I had the Margarita Pizza and it was fantastic! It wasn't greasy or sloppy, but perfectly crunchy with a nice balanced flavor and cheese perfectly cooked.   They also have some amazing gelato that we had the pleasure of tasting. Although, I'd prefer if they had an option to order a smaller amount of the gelato. They only offer it in one size and it's far too much for me, one scoop is plenty!  Overall, I highly recommend!
This place was recommended by locals to me. Definitely a must-go restaurant if you like sea-type meals. The service was excellent the shrimp plate is exceptional!

my first time going to a NOLA market; it was alright. was there for mardi gras, so my senses were in overdrive. king cake was OK, in comparison to others.  had an ok variety of stuff, but the liquor selection - they should put a sign that the good stuff is up front!  reasonable prices, good customer service.   would return, if (WHEN) I move to NOLA.

While in town and reading all the good reviews we decided to try this place out. We stopped in around lunch time, it was not busy so we were able to sit at the pizza bar and watch them cook. Now just to forwarn anyone we are not big fans of Neapolitan pizza just because it's hard to find a place that really knows how to cook it. It's always very burned and very soft amd wet in the middle. The staff was great, very friendly and the menu had great meat and veggie options.   Today we started with their meatball and rotolo (pizza dough, mortadella, ricotta and pistachio pesto) appetizer. The meatballs were very tender, had great flavor and the sauce had great flavor. The rotolo was a nice treat it had a crispy exterior, soft interior filled with tasty mortadella and creamy ricotta and the pistachio pesto was a nice compliment. Next we tried their shaved cauliflower salad. The greens were fresh it just a bit to tart of a dressing maybe to much vinegar. Next we tried their Tonno Pizza (Sicilian tuna, onion, mozzarella and parmigiano). The toppings were very tasty, the dough had great chew and texture and the sauce was sweet and tangy. Next we tried their Pastinaca Pizza (roasted parsnip, charred brussel sprouts, brown butter parsnip sauce, mozz, parm and lemon zest. The flavor combination was outstanding very different but a great veggie option. While the dough had great flavor and texture it was very burned and very soggy in the middle.   I would have to say if your a fan of Neapolitan pizza this would be a great place to check out. The menu is very nice and plenty of options for everyone.

Not a whole lot to say here. The food is fantastic. I haven't tried anything that I wouldn't devour again. Brisket, pulled pork, smoked wings...yes please on all! All the employees are extremely friendly, service is fast, and this is the best Texas style bbq you will get in the Tampa Bay area. If you want good bbq, this is your place. Maybe next time I won't eat so much of the smoked meat that I can actually try one of the dessert offerings...
Great service experience. They make it simple and affordable. And they value my business. I just came from the Arizona MVD.  It's great to leave the Soviet Union and br back in the US of A.

Chinks (now known as Joe's) may be the best-kept secret in Northeast Philadelphia.  These are arguably some of the best cheesesteaks in the city. The meat is great quality and always cooked right with a perfect meat-to-cheese balance and the onions are cooked perfectly too.   This is definitely a place where you want to eat in, as long as you aren't a large group. It has a great 40's diner vibe (because that's when they opened) and the servers are all really efficient and nice.   Also, don't leave without getting a milkshake or ice cream soda. I always go for the cherry ice cream soda, and it never disappoints.  They have great specials too, including my favorite- $6.50 large cheesesteaks all day on Wednesdays.  The prices are really good and they do take credit cards.

The concept of this restaurant is pretty simple.  Tacos made fresh and to order and cheap.   The tacos were pretty good.  I can see where this would be a good place to go when it is warm enough to sit out on the deck and enjoy some tacos and beer.  The inside is ok but think outside would be better.  I tried a couple of fish tacos. One was good, the other had mayonnaise on it, even though I didn't request it.  I just don't get putting mayo on Mexican food.  Second time it happened in a week.

One of if not the best sushi restaurants in all of Nashville. Don't let the line at the door deter you. Just get online and chat with some of your fellow diners until it's your turn to sit down and eat some really amazing sushi.  We are most impressed with the fact that despite a line out the door the service is very unrushed. While the shift prepare sushi at a speed I don't think I've ever seen we were welcome to come back and put in the second round of orders after we ate the first batch of really incredible    Don't hesitate to step out of your comfort zone here and order something you've never tried. It's really that good. If I'd recommend one thing it would be the fried oyster roll which was out of this world.

Is Mongolian food Comfort food? Can it? Screw it, let's roll with that.  It's a build your own bowl. Buffet for dinner, one bowl or buffet for lunch. They also have soup, rice, and sesame bread offered. You'll be look at $17 for all you can eat with a soft drink, which isn't bad.   You start by getting what you want, then you drop it off at the cooking station, then you get your sides, then you pay. This confused me the first time I went so y'know, just an FYI folks.   The quality is generally fairly good. Sometimes the vegetables are left out a little too long, and sometimes the meat available is the shredded remains and not actual slices... but when drowned out in strong flavors as is common here it is less of an issue. For food that's cooked on the spot, it comes out pretty quick. I generally will get my bowl before I have finished up my soup, which is pretty good. You get to choose how you want your sauce done and they do recommend combinations of flavors, but I generally will do my own thing. Their soups are generally pretty solid, and they will provide as much soup, rice, and bread as you want.   Mongolian BBQ isn't necessarily something that is in the forefront of American eating, but this is certainly worth trying if you're unsure or curious about it. This place isn't fancy, but there's a good variety of strong flavors and enough meat/veggie options to build something you'll certainly enjoy.

I would actually give them 4 1/2! Had the meaty meat, chicken BBQ and cacciatore - all delicious!  The yellow curry one with chickpeas was a fail for me, but perhaps I should have known better given all the other delicious options.  Looking forward to trying some more combos!

Worth trying at least once.  Simple menu, not a whole lot of options.  Coffee was basic. Chicken was tasty and donuts were authentic.  Prices were a bit steep.  Might return if traveling with others but once was good enough for me.

I came to this restaurant with very high expectations and was sadly disappointed. First, the positive: the food was delicious (esp. the sweet potato mezze was exceptional and the hummus was tasty). The venue was nicely appointed for group pictures, etc.   However, parking was a pain (only available on the street in the area). The food was very overpriced (a $19 salad with chicken that was truly minuscule, for example, and $5 for 6 slices of rather odd tasting parsnips). The service was also perfunctory; we were first seated right by the door somewhere very cold and then moved to a table awkwardly adjacent to the hostess stand, not private at all, and the waitstaff was clearly annoyed that we needed to be in and out (for a flight) in 45 minutes.   For a large group wanting to brunch together, I can see how this would be a nice spot, but generally I would not return unless a friend was desperate to try it.

Dewey's is a solid choice for pizza and salad. I am currently digging their harvest salad which has tasty garlicky boursin cheese with figs, bacon, and pumpkin seeds with just the right amount of vinaigrette over field greens. That and sharing a pizza with my husband leads to both full tummies and lunch for the next day. I also love that you can split the pizza in half and we each get our own half to do whatever we want with while still only having to order one reasonable size pizza. They don't skimp on pizza toppings here on their speciality pizzas and every single pizza I've tried I have liked. The crust is a more medium crust and they brush some sort of buttery ness on the edges that makes it taste even better.   Service is super efficient and very friendly here. If you're wanting to check them out on a busy night you can check the wait and check in from home on NoWait too. They also do a brisk take out business that seems pretty organized too.   While I realize the cost is more than say the major national chain pizza places I do feel like the quality is substantially better here. I'd choose Dewey's any day.

Great little family run Italian restaurant..Lunch was great,all homemade dishes.We had classic dishes as I always do my first time in a restaurant,eggplant parmigiana,spaghetti and meatballs and chicken piccata!!Each dish came out piping hot,enough to split,we all had leftovers and they were very very good!!Bread and soup or salad comes with your entree,$9.99 is a great deal considering the quality/portions..I will be back this weekend for dinner!!Chris the owner Chef was very gracious and knows his stuff!

This update is dedicated to the PTR burger. It  Its technically the first of my burger reviews -  prior to deciding that its time to work my way through every phila taproom burger i can find.  This unusual take on the burger works: its served with an egg, truffle oil, gruere & arugula on a french roll. Not your usual burger combo, but really unique flavor. Hope they make it a staple. $10.  Burgerlicious rating = 7

Beers are different than any brewery I've been to previously. Very good selection, always. The service is always friendly and good. The food is great, chicken sandwich for lunch is the best. Finally the space is big and can handle lots of people if you have a big party. Great spot !

I enjoyed the Blue Duck.  I sat at the counter and found the atmosphere to be enjoyable, I always like watching the chefs cook when there's an open kitchen like that.  They were playing awesome music when I was there.  It was almost like they had a playlist of my favorite songs prepared.  I got to hear Toto, Men At Work, Quiet Riot... good choices!    The entrees and specials all sounded totally awesome, but I was hankering for a burger so I got one of those.  It was nicely cooked and came on a really good roll.  I have to admit however that I found the combination of garlic aioli and red onions to be way too pungent and overpowering.  I would skip the garlic aioli next time, it was all I could taste.  The fries were pretty decent.    Service was excellent, almost too good actually, so I left a fat tip.  I like that the place is BYOB, so be sure to pick yourself up some beers before you come here.

If what you're looking for is unlimited sushi at a great value, this is the place to come. At around $16 per person, you really can't find a better price to have all the sushi you want. Service was pretty good. The servers were attentive and food came out at a good pace. You feel like you have enough time to finish your orders before a new wave of food comes at you.   In terms of quality though, don't set your expectations too high. For what you're paying, you are getting a decent variety of rolls, nigiri, and various appetizers and soups. Michelin Star and Haute Cuisine dining this is not. You might say this is about as average as average can be, not bad and will satisfy your cravings at the right time. Blow you away it probably won't.   Will I be back? Yeah, more than likely. One can never ignore the call of unlimited sushi. But just remind yourself that you're coming for quantity. Quality can be found elsewhere.
I went here for my graduation dinner with my family (table of 16) back in June. Food and service was great! The view was amazing and I got the best photos.   I would come back again!

I work in finance so money interests me. It's pretty cool to see the "factory" where our coins are made.  We arrived on a Friday and unfortunately the machines weren't running at that time but the exhibits and videos gave us a good idea of how everything works. There's a fun gift shop where you can buy enlarged replicas of our coins. This is a fun place to kill half an hour.
Great great oysters. Some of the best I've had in the Tampa/St Pete area. Casual. It's easy to go right by.  Stop if you can.

If you are ever in Conshohocken and are craving italian, this is the place to go. My god this place is delicious.   I went on a double date during restaurant week, so I had a set menu to choose from. However, their menu compared to others during restaurant week was pretty large with several options. I even asked for no  mushrooms and they were able to accommodate. Not all places do that during restaurant week.   The restaurant is a quaint area with food to die for. Fresh pasta, bread, and BYO! They were a bit busy as it seemed like there was a large party, however, the waiters handled it with ease. My boyfriend and I are excited to go back and take a look at the whole menu.

A little on the pricey side, but this is such a cute and "sweet" little candy shop! Pun Intended. The staff was very friendly and there are sooooooo many good looking sweets to choose from. As soon as you enter the shop, you will be overwhelmed by the smell of sugar and chocolate in the air. It's the most glorious aroma smacking you in the face ever! I purchased pre-packaged cajun pecans, glazed pecans, and pecan popcorn to take home with me for my family. I don't eat nuts, but there are so many varieties of prailines here, it almost made me want to try some. Almost. Still, for a nice treat while on Decatur St, do yourself a favor and step into this shop. There's something for everyone here!

I can't even count how many times I've been to Cheddar's, butI don't think I've ever been disappointed once.    Sometimes, the place is pretty busy, but its understandable.  You really get the bang for your buck here.    Good food.  Huge portion sizes.  Super decent prices. Friendly wait staff.    Some of my favorites are: Buffalo Chicken Wrappers Asian Shrimp Salad Apples &Yogurt Honey-Butter Crossaints!

I can't think of a better place to try when in Tucson. I enjoying being with the bright white interior and the cool feeling it brings on a hot day( which is a large part of the year). This gelato shop has 20 or so flavors, which can be served in a cup or a cone. Also they offer  shakes and Affogatos (Espresso & Gelato) which I am fond of. My favorite is their Stracciatella (chocolate chip). However when I'm getting an Affogato the dark chocolate gelato is the perfect choice for me.
Half of their beers were just okay, the other half were solid beers (best one I had was the Training Day) but I've had better, laid-back industrial interior, TV's for sports and nice bartenders.

Wonderful Experience. This review is a bit long but covers everything.  Reputation Smiling Elephant proudly displays many local awards throughout the years. They are known for their Pad Thai and they have a daily rotation of curries.  Food Overall, excellent food that is not typical of other Thai restaurants. All original recipes of favorite dishes some nice new recipes as well.  Tom Yum soup was a pleasant surprise. It contained large pieces of vegetables in broth, as opposed to a typical bowl of broth with tiny pieces of diced veggies. I added shrimp, which was one jumbo prawn with a little too much TSP, but tasty none the less. I needed chop sticks to eat the veggies and could have used a knife for the shrimp.  Pad Kra Pao was a new dish for me. It is chopped Thai pork barbecue with a unique sauce. IT was a vinegar type yet sweet. It had some heat and was well balanced. The blend of mushrooms, basil and cilantro was wonderful. It was chopped just right to eat with chop sticks. The rice was great, but just too much for my blood sugar levels.  Drinks This place is a tea heaven. For the first time in my life, I tried Thai iced tea UNSWEETENED and without the "milk". It was so refreshing. There were many varieties of teas served both hot and iced.  Menu Layout/Pricing Even the server admitted it was hard to read. All meals are Prix Fixe, but that price is hidden on one page of the menu. So, as you read the menu, you do not find any prices except add-on prices for meat or shrimp.  Some descriptions mention types of meats you can add, but do not describe them. You have to hunt them out later in the multi-page menu.  My major disappointment was pricing on the drinks. I am used to getting free refills on hot tea. All drinks are single serving and there are no refills. hrmph  Decor Wonderfully done with traditional Thai prints and artwork. The wood (bamboo?) treatment on the walls made me feel as if I were in a Thai mountain house, even though I've never been to one.  Service Prompt, and polite  Parking Now, this is interesting. There is a small lot out front that is not very well laid out. I squeezed my SUV into a space and hoped I could get it out. If not, I could have driven over the sidewalk. There is a lot of congestion in the small lot. It was not until after I parked and walked to the door that I saw there was additional parking in the rear.

Top 5 restaurant experience, anywhere, ever.  Perfect.  Spectacular.  The food is magical.  Eating the pasta with morel mushrooms was a religious experience.  I had high expectations and this exceeded them.  My husband and I both agreed that everything was perfect.  Our waitress was great, food came perfectly spaced out and timed.  The bread and butter basket was the best ever.  I mean ever.  The duck was the best I've ever had.  I am usually disappointed when I order duck, but this dish was on a different level.  My husbands chop was the best he's ever had.  The desserts were divine.  The vibe and the energy in the restaurant was awesome, I love the open kitchen concept.  I can't put into words how absolutely amazing this place is.  I really don't understand how anyone can give this place less than 5 stars.  I am so excited that a restaurant of this caliber opened up in Collingswood.

Great portions. Kid friendly. Tasty food and awesome staff. My kids loved the carnitas tacos and mexicano hot dog. And they didn't make a big deal about my daughter wanting all the toppings on the side. Oh and don't forget the guacamole. Yum!

Carmine's Steakhouse provides a reasonable Steak along with a admissible ambiance in DTSTL.   After the raucous afternoon at the Rams game I decided that an agreeable meal was in order. Eschewing the Ruth Chris chain experience, I figured that Carmine's Steakhouse would offer a more uniquely "Saint Louis" feel. To be forthcoming it really feels like a Hotel Restaurant (as it is) which in and of itself is not a particularly bad thing, it is just the service was a bit stiff and forced for my liking. Also, the interior is quite dated.  The menu is about as stereotypical as Steak House dining gets and all of the usual suspects in Midwestern dining are well represented. The local favorite "Toasted Ravioli" was my first order in and is came to the table at breakneck speed. Basically it is the same as any Ravioli, just twice the size and Breaded and deep fried. Considering the great hype surround the dish i was honestly underwhelmed. It basically tasted like an amalgamation of Ground Beef, grease and Marinara with a bit of crunchy mouth feel mixed in. Ho hum at best but I came for steak, so it was not a deal killer at that point.   As for the Steak, Carmine's does put out a very solid 21 day aged Bone-In Ribeye when they get the temperature right. On the kitchen's first attempt my Steak came to the table still eating the salad. After returning it to the kitchen (I loathe doing this), a perfectly marbled 20 ounce piece of charred delight came to my table seared to order (medium rare) along with Steak Butter on their second try. Returning meat to a kitchen in a Steakhouse always irks me, but at least they erred to the side of caution.    As I side I ordered the Twice Baked Potato which was rather uninspired and boring thanks too being under-seasoned.   In closing, Carmine's Steakhouse is probably a pass for future visits if and when I get back to Saint Louis as there are several other Chop Houses in the area that deserve a fair shake before a return trip. 2 stars and moving on.

I've been here a few times.  It's just OK.  I like watching the sports, but around 8:00, they turn off the TV volume and crank up the hits of the 80's. bah The chipotle steak salad is the best.  The burgers are killer.  Endless beer selection. We like to sit at the bar.  Bartenders don't always make the best food servers. LOL.  Dude removed my hubby's plate before he was done.  Ooops.  He put it back but since he had touched the fork, hubs requested a clean one to finish his meal.  This caused confusion.  Poor guy got flustered.    Nothing is ever really wrong here, it's just never great.   $7.00 pints and up.  Kind if pricey for sitting in a basement.

A fantastic surprising discovery! The last time I got takeout pizza a couple years ago, this place just had a counter. Now it's a beautiful dine in restaurant complete with large local draft beer menu, a varied menu, and...gluten free crust! So good! Charming old b&w family photos on all the walls. Delightful. Service excellent. Pizza, great. We'll be back.

The restaurant is really nice, the food is okay, the waitstaff isn't good at all. Took so long to get one drink that we didn't even have time to order a second (ordered apps and food, and half way through our main course we got our drinks)  It was okay. I wouldn't recommend it, but it's okay.

Alway's very friendly staff and clean environment with a nice patio. I always order the turkey (gobbler) burger which is very good. I would rate this place a 5* but there's nothing else I like. If your into salads, organic food or a vegetarian you'll probably love it here.

Fantastic sushi takeout. Beautifully packaged sashimi with lemon slices between the pieces, and a naruto rainbow roll with an orchid, and colorful roe and scallion on top for visual appeal. Wait was short for pickup. Will definitely return for this again.

The sandwiches are ok and all, but the best thing here is the She Crab soup. Holy moly it's to die for and made the way it should be. Lots of generous chunks of crab, rich and creamy and a touch of sherry. You can get it to go in multiple sized containers.  Another winner is the unique vanilla bean lemonade. It's really different, but really good.  They have a lot of desserts, and especially a lot of gluten free desserts. Everything has been pretty tasty. I appreciate the ritz crackers with pimento cheese and jelly for the complimentary starter. It's a cute lunch spot, but gets busy. Looks like they've added a coffee shop next door, which has made the parking even worse. They did add a valet, which seems a bit overkill for that area, however there are signs out for reserved parking for nearby businesses. Go early to avoid the crowds.

I'll start by saying I haven't searched high or low for Boba in Tucson. However I have enjoyed trying the various drinks at Bing's Boba tea, which is located next to Lee Lees market. They offer a ton of flavors: with milk, blended with ice(snow), shakes, tea's, lemonade and any drink you choose can have extra's added boba, jelly, popping boba(today I tried popping boba which rather than a gummy tapioca pearl has a flavored liquid inside of various selected flavors) , etc. There is a charm to this place that makes me giddy. The drinks are prepared and then topped with a special sealing film with cute cartoons, which allows your drink to be encapsulated until you pierce the top with your boba jumbo straw. I love bursting the seal. I have tried the Taro (yam) shake based on a recommendation from YELP and it was purple yummy treat. Today I asked if there was any way they can make anything sugar free. Initially they said only the tea could be prepared sugar free, but in fact they tried and successfully make a Taro shake with splenda for me. SO it's not on the menu but can be ordered. It worked out great, since my husband can't have sugars without getting a headache. Today I had strawberry lemonade with strawberry popping boba added, it was great! Another plus is, if you check in on your YELP application and show them, they offer a free add in! Just another plus to give Bing's Boba Tea a try.

So roommates and I were feeling lazy and not like cooking tonight. Instead we used UberEATS to get food delivered to us. None of us had tried Bab Cafe yet so we figured why not. We made the right decision.  I ordered the beef bowl with the spicy sauce. I didn't realize it came with miso soup. It was a very pleasant surprise. The bowl itself was very good the meat tender and the verifies crunchy. I had upgraded to the brown rice which gave it good flavor.  We also ordered potstickers which were flavorful and the filling was very tasty. The sauce it was served with had a slight spice to it which made it even better.  While I haven't been to the restaurant yet, after tasting their food I will definitely try it out. The food was flavorful and well cooked. Being lazy tonight definitely worked out in our favor.

I really want to love this place. I want to be able to come here and know I'm going to get the best service and what I actually ordered. I'm not sure why or how hard it is to remember two menu items. I used to serve, I know the deal. And if you can't remember two things, please just write it down. Example- if I say no olives, I mean no olives.   The bartenders are friendly and made an effort to remember my name which I appreciate, makes you feel welcomed. They have many tvs to watch a good game and any place that has Goose Island on tap I'm a fan of.

Had a very tasty lunch here today! The quesadilla was super tasty as well as the salad with chicken. The chicken was surprisingly plentiful and very thick and juicy. The dressing choices could be better but regardless I would come here again. Also the margaritas were on par.

We have had many friends visit this shop so we wanted to go and try it out.  Saturday night after a concert at USF. There was a really long line!  30 people? But we were determined to try it. The line actually went really fast. And it gave us time to decide what we wanted.  Even with the time in the line, we hadn't really decided! My daughter went the fruity route while I went with chocolate. It was fun to watch them create the ice cream and roll it up.  I would come back with friends from out of town.  It's a unique experience.

I'm really at a 3.75 stars here.....I've been here twice.  Once for a private baking event - for that I give it 5 stars.  And then I was here for a little coffee meeting....I give that like 2.5 - 3 stars.  During the baking event, we had a group of about 12 and we were making babkas.  I wish I remembered the chef's name because he was fun and fantastic.  We learned how to roll out the dough, a little help from the fancy rolling machine and how to knead it properly, how to load it up with filling, how to cut and fold it and then they baked them for us.  They turned out lovely and delicious.  For storage purposes, they also told us how to package them so we could keep them for Thanksgiving 4-5 days later and mine kept perfectly!  My family enjoyed it!  The event itself was fun and doing the work and baking was fun.  We even got to help with the next day's croissants - rolling them...  They laid out some nice snacks and drinks for us after the baking was done so we could chat with one another until the baking was complete.  Very nice small baking event for a very reasonable price.  That was 5 stars!  On a separate occasion I met a friend for coffee and had a breakfast sandwich.  I was happy that they could accommodate my need for non-dairy in my special drink and the croissant with egg and bacon was nice...however, I could not taste the caramel in my caramel macchiato...I went up to request more and she loaded it in there and I took a seat and again, still could not taste the caramel.  The coffee did not taste nice.  I was VERY sad about that.  My croissant was very large (update to include that this large croissant was not a negative thing!  I liked that it was large), the egg was decent and bacon was thick...but a lot of fat.  I peeled off a lot of fat from the 2 slices and left it on the side of the plate.  You're going to get fat on bacon, yes, but it was kind of a lot of fat...  And the flavor was plain.  I think this place is a bit over-rated, as much as I enjoyed the baking class...but the baked goods are very good.  It's just not a great place for coffee or to eat here unless you are having one of their baked goods.

Not happy. Although I typically go to the Italian market or Callowhill I thought I would come to Collingswood with friends who were having their first experience here. I called ahead at 1015am and was told there would be a half hour wait plus 15 minutes when I arrived. When we got there I was told they had no notice of my call even though I received a text. Now it is 1150am and we are still waiting for a table. Unacceptable and technology is no excuse. Very disappointed that I decided to bring my friends here.  After being seated managers apologized and brought out French toast. The food was as good as it usually is but some kinks need to be worked out. Service was mediocre. I'll be back in a few months when things are running smoothly and noise machines are in place.

I went on a Monday with my kids we took about a 40 min drive to get there and it was well worth it. We were greeted by Sophie who was an absolute gem. She was very attentive, patient, and knowledgeable. I ordered chips, an xtreme taco, black bean taquitos, buffalo chicken taco, and elote in a cup. Everything tasted great! The elote in a cup especially. I am not a fan of corn it was for my son but one taste of their corn and I was in love! The reason for the 4 stars is due to my chips being a tad burned and my bean taquitos as well. This wont stop me from going back however. Even if you are not in the area this place is worth the drive!

This place is awesome!! Right when u walk in ur overwhelmed with the many options available. It's so hard to choose. The guy that helped us was very friendly and made polite conversation. Now let's talk about the actual gelato... Very good! The quality is amazing. I especially liked their lotus cookie and mango. Outside the place was hopping. Makes u feel like ur somewhere other then Tucson. Will come again.
Best prime rib I've ever had and best carrot cake I've ever had. Regardless of restaurant, skip the seafood tower, it's a trap. Fantastic food here folks.
Amazing dining experience all around. Unique meal. Good portions. I look forward to coming back.

Very good experience. There were five of us. Everyone enjoyed the appetizers, main entree and drinks. Service was excellent and personable. The prices good.  Ambiance is pleasant and relaxing. Cost is reasonable. Looking forward to our next visit!

A friend of mine and I had dinner few nights ago and was really impressed.. we ordered quite a bit & everything we tried was fantastic .. must try if you're looking for me Benihana style restaurant in Santa Barbara

I only found Old Glory thanks to a local's recommendation of "you have to check it out".  Now I've heard that statement before and was disappointed in some other visits. That wasn't the case with Old Glory!  Like everyone has said it's a bit tricky to find but it added to the appeal of the lounge. Right after walking in you descend down the amazing gold staircase that mimics the gold triangle on the door. Ascetically it feels like an industrial speakeasy.  Then as the night goes on the entire lounge is illuminated by candlelight. Now the staff and cocktails are both amazing!  Our waitress was fantastic and was able to provide her own recommendations for us!  I was surprised to see the large selection of gin and gin based cocktails. It was very refreshing!  I had an amazing time here and I can't wait to come back!
I love this place. This is my #1 spot for sushi. Food  is fresh and the services is excelente.

I like to use uber eats but the draw back is you can't speak with the person that made your sandwich. This is unacceptable. Worst club ever. Your are a sandwich shop. It's what you do!! Make a great sandwich especially for take out! Never again.

iiiiiiiiiiiii love this bar. I've only been here a few times, twice to pregame while waiting for a table at Cafe Lift, and once to postgame brunch at Cafe Lift and ended up staying for several hours.   Haven't had the pleasure of trying the food yet but it's definitely on my list. The bartender dude is super friendly and helpful. One of those people that knows when to leave you the fuck alone but is also more than willing to engage in witty banter.   The beers on tap are all unique and range from low to high %, and the staff knows how to recommend a winner based on your personal taste.   I just really like the vibe here, it's hard to put into words exactly what I mean. The kind of place that it's easy to lose track of time in. Gotta come here yourself and I'm sure you'll see what I mean!

This theater has a huge variety of screens with 24 options. The theater is clean and the concession stand is amply stocked with a variety of food. This theater has more than just popcorn, pretzels and candy. You can actually get chicken nuggets, french fries, mozzarella sticks, ice cream and more at the concession stand. This is the place to go if you need something a little more for you or your little ones. There is ample parking here as well. I frequent this theater, which is across the street from the Cherry Hill Mall :).

Awesome burgers. Calamari steak sandwich is on point as is the friendly service and comfortable atmosphere.  A lifelong resident of SB and this was my first visit here.  Will definitely come back again!

Ehh I'll round up from 3.5 stars. This place was alright. Didn't knock my socks off, but it was serviceable. The garlic naan was kind of a let down in comparison to the pictures I saw online. Didn't seem to match that but was moderately tasty. I had the lamb Marsala which was, again, ok. The meat chunks where a bit more fatty then I'd like. It's pretty fancy and the service was good. Can't complain about the price either. So overall, it's good for a meal but I wouldn't take a date there or anyone I want to impress.

We went to Chick-fil-A last night for Valentine's Day. They did a reservation dinner. It was $20 for two people and that included an entree, 2 sides, a drink and dessert. Plus you had a server. Each table had a goody bag with some chocolate and a mini moo in it. They also had a photo booth and a group of gentlemen singing. It was a super cute date night with our 8 month old son. I hope they do more events like this!

I wish I could give this four stars. I loved the food, the pizzas were charred and crusty, the cheese gooey and delicious, and even the tap list was half decent. The rotolo was to die for, almost too decadent. And the fried pizza dough for dessert? Da bomb. Great location, great vibe, even great option to donate to a good foundation at the end of the meal.  But the service was, in a word, indifferent. A few others: inattentive, idle, isolated.  Couldn't get the servers' attention to order more food or drinks.Might be two stars if that rotolo wasn't so darn good.  I was frustrated, but some spots have their off nights. I'll be back for a re-review in time.

Insanely good! This is the first time I've ever been in St. Louis and Yelp helped me find this gem! I'm so happy that I found Ted Drewes because BOY IT WAS GOOD! My dad who normally eats insanely healthy had to have one of their concretes! I had "The Christy" and it was absolutely delicious! It had hot fudge, caramel and brownie mixed together as a concrete. It was so good! The brownie was soft and fudgy and the custard was very creamy and flavorful.   If you're in St. Louis, you need to stop here asap!

Best bread in town!  In addition to the staples, Mazarro's sells different specialty loaves each day of the week.  Saturday is Sour Dough, which is my favorite!  Throughout the week, they also sell jalapeno, rosemary and sun dried tomato, to name a few.  I also buy all my meat here.  It's not too expensive and is fresher and better cut than meat at a standard grocery.  They do their own dry aging and will accommodate specialty orders, such as course ground beef for chili. The wine and cheese room smells awful but it's a must see!  They have a huge variety of cheese on offer and a very nice selection of wines. I love shopping here, however, it's become a very popular spot with tourists which can make for long lines on the weekend.  It's usually best to avoid peak times such as breakfast and lunch.

So maybe this shouldn't bother me so much but we are here at lunchtime. Our waitress is currently at a table fooling with her phone and occasionally making some coffee or leaving her table and coming back all while we are in our 20th minute waiting for our food.   I don't know if the food is all made so fresh that I'm supposed to be grateful that they even let me eat here but I feel like appearances matter. She's not been back to ask how we are, or to indicate that our food will EVER be ready. I can only assume they are back there cooking it. There may only be one person cooking.   Maybe I'm not hip enough for this new style of eating  I'll report back when the food actually arrives. Unless I die of starvation.   27 minutes.  When the food finally did arrive it was very good. A significantly large burger for the price but I'm not sure the wait and sense of isolation would lead me to return. I would at least recommend the staff appear busy when they are within view of the patrons.

I have had good and bad experiences with espresso, and this is definitely one of the better ones. I got the espresso bombón, which comes with condensed milk at the bottom, and it surprisingly was the perfect sweetness. I like my coffee still a little bitter and it was perfect. The place itself is nice and quiet and the staff is very friendly.

I love this farmers' market for the variety and the location. There is always a nice selection of produce, but there are also artisan breads and cheeses, herbs and sprouts, and plenty of homemade goodies. Prices are what you'd expect (you get what you pay for), and people are always friendly. I love their expanded hours, and Tower Grove Park is a beautiful spot to spend a couple of hours!

I seldom get on this side of town, so I spent an evening just going to many places, ordering 1 item and then on to the next destination. I found the place almost empty. Service was slower than slow. I tried the California rool. I found it a bit lacking & tasteless. Overall not impressed. Not my type of place for fresh, innovative sushi. What's a girl to do? Go where you know exactly  what you are getting, especially  when it comes to raw fish.
Tex-Mex food. If your looking for a good Margarita and some Tex-Mex appetizers give Don Pablos a try. Unlimited warm nacho chips and sausa. Great Service.
Anyone who feels like driving 15 min over the bridge it is a great restaurant i go with my family all the time .Food is great atmosphere is great, fun place.

The only reason, and may I say it's irrelevant of the actual experience... Is why I'm not giving this place 5 stars is because the place is a little dated, and lacks modern decor. Other than that... I LOVED the experience! The drinks were great; the food was great; the service was great; the chairs were ridiculously comfortable; the screen play of the movie was as and their other theater. I'm going to be so bummed ever going back to a regular movie theater! The prices are a bit more for a regular ticket (around $12) but totally worth the comfort! I highly recommend everyone try this at least once!

Went to this restaurant on recommendations for lunch on a Wednesday by family. We were seated immediately, which was great... received our water and iced teas quickly, which was also great. Everything else went downhill from there very quickly. I have a very serious allergy to avocados wich end in anaphylactic shock, which I mention immediately upon arrival to any restaurant. So far I never had any issues about restaurant kitchens dealing with this until yesterday. The waitress advised me that they would NOT be able to produce avocado free sushi for me in their establishment. The cooks would NOT clean the surface  properly  to ensure that no avocado would make contact with my food. Slightly shocked about the nastyness of the dirty surface they prepare their sushi on. I also did not receive any salad since they chop the greens on the same surface as the fish... hopefully a food inspector will read this!! GROSS, stay away if you have any allergies or a sensitive stomach...

This was my second restaurant week stop this go-around and tbh I was v disappointed. It was a true bummer!  So, my qualms were mainly the food - for starters it was four courses. The first course was an antipasto board and let me tell you, it was a sad excuse for an antipasto board. I was looking forward to it most, but it turned out to be dry and bland. There were little cheese balls and olives and I don't even know what else. I can't remember because it was so forgettable.   Second, I got the verde salad and it was eh. It didn't have enough avocado and it was also way too bitter. I was so excited for the pasta I ordered, but was again...sorely disappointed. The servings were small and it was extremely garlicky. For dessert I got the "chocolate cake." It came out and my friend said, "is this cake for ants." It was a tiny little circle brownie that you can get pre-packaged at Costco.   I would totally understand if this spot had small portions cus the food was decadent and luxurious, but it wasn't at all.  It was nothing special or tasty.   I did like the aesthetic, although casual, it was also modern and chic. It was located, however, in a weird spot attached to a We Work on 24th and Walnut.   In all, I probs won't be back and 24 has gotta step up their restaurant week game!

Venue: clean, large, with indoor and outdoor seating. Plenty of room for large and small groups. Even areas with a pretty romantic vibe.  Service: horrendous. Our server literally hated her life or her job. She came out to "greet" us and already was annoyed that she was about to serve 13 people who would've tipped her more than 20% had she showed some enjoyment out of her job. She only got 20% because of that reason. She was miserable, we don't even know why, we paid with 1 card, we all ordered all at once. We only asked for additional beers, it was the easiest table to serve. I just can't imagine why she is working in the service industry when she clearly does not enjoy it. Hurts the company and restaurant. Nonetheless, this is about the food more than the service. Just had to mention it though.  Prices: over the top pricey. Drinks to food, small portions, barely any alcohol in the mixed drinks. Just poor choice in costs when it comes to such poor qualities. Although the pizzas were enjoyed by everyone around the table, but the other ancillary dishes were subpar, the poutine, not with poutine cheese. The wings were bland.  Beers: pricey, but again, had plenty of selection so everyone eventually found their top choice. $2 tiny tiny flights. There are many other breweries in the area that have better pricing and beer selection.  I will say we ended up sitting outside in the humidity even though there were 3 large tables inside that had no one sitting at them. They refused to let us sit there, by the time we left, 2 of those tables were still empty and remained open. I don't understand their thinking behind refusing us. We were a mix of all ethnicities and everyone was dressed well, so I don't know how they judged us to stick us outside rather than let us opt for the inside table.  Unfortunately, it was such a poor experience, will not be back. Hope for future customers to beware and proceed with caution. Happy eating and drinking!

I used to dine at Albasha weekly in Baton Rouge. When I found out they were also in Metairie, I made my fiancée abandon our old middle eastern favorites. Albasha is all we eat now! It's worth the drive. Typically do take out. Love the big mezze platter of everything . Or the combo chicken schwarmA and gyros is my favorite ! We love Albasha!

Village Inn is exactly what it advertises to be. A bit nicer than a dinner. Good for them, good old American food. Breakfast, brunch, lunch, dinner or coffee and conversation, it can't be beat. Staff is okay, not overly friendly,  but usually alert, get orders correct and ready for refills. Unless it is a busy time, they literally don't mind if you stay for hours. Great place to meet someone for the fi rst time. Great after a night out on the town. Food is usually consistent, some items better than others. One thing I love is they listen to their customers. Hence the vip or is it vib menu selections, they are great, separately they would be a fortune. Here your choices from super healthy to cholesterol city are all laid out for you. Most of the salads and sandwiches are good or better. Most burgers very good. Breakfast skillets, omlets, waffles, and even, whole wheat pancakes. .. all very good.  I have not had many dinner entrees, so won't address them. By gourmet standards they aren't very good, but they are great for what they are & we have a place for them in our lives. I love the carafe of coffee on the table and the sugar free syrup, because they listen to their customer.  A sign of a great reastaurant is the ability to meet your customers needs!

Really 2.5 stars.  Arrived shortly before 1 on a Saturday to an empty hostess station and not a server in sight.  There were three tables with customers so not busy.  We waited a few minutes and the bartender saw us and seated us in the bar.  She was fairly friendly.  As stated in other reviews the chips are not complementary.  I had a craving for nachos as my meal anyway so I didn't care.  The bartender recommended the buffalo chicken nachos so that is what I ordered.  You do not want the nachos.  The tortilla chips are too thick, under salted and boring.  The toppings were quite good.  Hubby ordered the restaurant's equivalent of a pulled pork sandwich and fries.  Sad fries... too greasy so they lack crispness.  The sandwich he found to be just ok.  The pork in my opinion had great flavor.  We were underwhelmed and in a town with lots of Mexican food we will not be back.

i really wanted to like this place. I got the Philly style one. it was soggy and super buttery for some reason. maybe it was the mustardy mayo sauce that was on it. pretty bland. I ended up picking through the other half after eating the first and just ended up throwing away half of it. I'll have to try the Komchi version next time...if I go back.

We tried Cafe Ponte based on a recommendation, and the reviews. The food and service exceeded my expectations! My husband and I like different qualities in our servers, but ours impressed both of us. To the point my husband actually wanted to leave a nicer tip! Kudos to the service because that doesn't happen too often! The Steve McQueen cocktail impressed me more than a standard cocktail. Amazing bread choices. Unique beet salad that I'd eat every day if I could but even I'd say this one is strictly for foodies. I'm all about a good steak and my hubby's NY was tasty, but I had hands down the best Sea Bass I've had in years. The only other close second would have been at Oyster Catchers and even there's wasn't this good from recent memory. Can't wait to go back to Cafe Ponte!!!
I'm a big fan of dives but this was just dirty looking.  I didn't even want to touch the doors to enter.   Everything had a nasty layer of greasy scum.  Food was good though.

Delicious experience here at Harry's! I ordered the chicken sandwich, which included sauteed spinach, roasted red pepper, pesto and mozzarella on a extremely fresh bun. This sandwich was accompanied by sweet potato friend and kale slaw. What a combo!! I will be back to try the tacos. Thanks for the great experience Harry.
Yay!!! I love this place! There are so many more delicious options than any coffee place in town! The staff is easy going and put a special touch on everything.

Very nice spot with a balcony overlooking the main street.  Really nice ambiance inside and outside.  Wine variety quite extensive - I had thought it would have been more local stuff but it is really a broad variety with many wines from CA as well as beer from the West coast.  The cheese platter was a nice accompaniment and much needed after a flight of tastings...  Tried the flight of wines (2 whites and 3 reds) and also a beer and a glass of wine.  The flight was a nice balance and ranged from the viognier to the shiraz.  All were reasonable and the ladies behind the bar were very helpful and accommodating, helping us to choose some wines by the glass and also some beers.   Great spot and great service.  Will be back.

Incredibly excited to have this a couple blocks away! Extensive menu with lots of different options all reasonably priced. We were there for lunch on a Sunday. Seated quickly, maybe a bit of a wait to order but nothing bad. Everyone was very friendly and it has a nice atmosphere and outside area. I had the lunch taco salad and I was honestly amazing. The shell was fantastic- crunchy but flakey, I'll probably get it every time I go for lunch! My fiancé got the pollo sabrosos and he said it was really flavorful. We need to come back on margarita nights but definitely a place to check out! I was surprised by the negative reviews here since it's been getting glowing reviews on the nextdoor site.

I wanted to write a review today because of Colin in the meat department. This guy knew his stuff! I was one of those annoying customers asking the difference between farm salmon, ocean salmon, organic etc ... And he patiently talked to me about all of the above plus more. Super helpful! He also helped instruct me on how to cook the fish on the grill vs oven. In regard to the rest of the store, it was clean and well stocked. Very nice grocery store.

If it were just based on their sushi, I would give them a 5. The new york roll and eel avocado roll were fantastic! Presentation of the food was beautiful too. Service was poor, I'm not even sure who my server was, each time someone came to the table, it was a different person and not friendly. Didn't ask what we wanted to drink, and once ordered no one came by to refill our drinks or waters. The dine in atmosphere is a bit awkward too, I would take the food for to-go next time.

Well, I've been there for breakfast with their Bloody Mary bar and it was good. The food was great but the Bloody Mary bar has spice girl figures on it which i don't understand. Tonight, I went to the bar and ordered a Bloody Mary. It was white. That's right. It has so much horseradish in it that it was white and not red and they served it that way. It wasn't drinkable sadly.

Was pleasantly surprised with the Naf. Ordering was quick, easy and they had a friendly staff. Overall the food was delicious. Tasted very fresh and was very reasonably priced. They give you perfectly baked pita bread with your order which was a great addition to the meal.  Naf, definitely will be back very soon

One of our fave coffee spots! Chill, relaxed setting. Always offering a variety of house blends and always taste amazing! We've tried all the food on their menu and it all taste so fresh! Our favorite is by far THE BBC CLUB. The pickled green tomato taste like heaven on earth!   P.S I just wish they had chai tea

This is a perfect date night if your looking for something fun and unique. My husband and I had a blast. The great staff were all so helpful and fun. The atmosphere is fun, young, and very upbeat. We've been married 26 years and have never done anything so stress relieving and crazy. The price is right and the experience is priceless! They also have leagues and contests for more serious throwers and it is a BYOB establishment. I think this is the perfect adventure for couples, friends, and even co-workers. Book this place for your next outing!!

I used to work on this end of Carondelet (the dead end, lol) and realized quickly that we didn't have much of an eatery choice on this side. So, when the sign of Whitebox shot up, I was excited. A new place to try and some foot action.  ***Stratton's used to be in the space where WB is now.   Whitebox has a sleek setup. Clean lines and finishes. Bright decor, few flatscreens. Very minimalist.  Lowkey, it reminded me of IKEA. I dug it, tho. The staff was always nice and upbeat. The BFF and I researched the menu online before going. I decided on the grilled cheese for lunch. It comes with a pickle and kettle chips. The sandwich was really good! Bro, the sweet tomato jam on top?! Yes! I've also tried their roasted chicken sandwich. Really good, light and fresh.   Even though the prices are a little steep (*side-eye*), I enjoyed my meal nonetheless.    The customer service was great and awesome dining area. I don't tip through Clayton like I once did, but I'm not opposed to stopping in, if I'm in the neighborhood.
