Love this place! My husband and I needed a kitchen getaway on an early Sunday evening so we headed to the Depot. Happy hour was in full swing so we sat at the bar and had Depot craft cocktails with the Shishito peppers as a snack. The peppers were a little greasy, but a so tasty and just spicy enough! I always have a hard time picking my favorite craft cocktail so I tried 2 this time, the Depot Manhattan (always a winner) and the Old Fashioned Cacao. Both were made with the Depot's own distilled whiskies and are superb. My husband tried the Amaretto Sour, and even though he proclaims not to like "sweet" drinks, this one was just the right mix of sweet, sour and great whiskey! We moved into the dining room for our dinner and had wonderful service from Bethany! Mussels were great, the turkey sliders a generous portion (3) for an appetizer and my husband loved his Depot burger with pastrami, swiss and kraut added to a beef patty. We struck up a conversation with the couple next to us visiting from SoCal, and shared our mussels for a taste of their artichoke kale dip. All in all, a wonderful evening with great food and drink.
Loved the steamed salmon with black bean sauce - amazing flavored broccoli! Great sushi and fantastic service!
Great bacon jam app.   Could not truly judge the taste of the ribeye because it was drenched in house steak sauce.

We went to Hendel's for lunch on Wednesday and were very pleased. There was a wait--about 20 minutes before a table became available. They had several large groups that kept sitting and visiting even after their plates had been removed. Two of these groups could see people waiting for tables---really rude to keep occupying a large table when everyone has finished eating! It was a beautiful day and Hendel's has tables and chairs outside--they could have moved their visiting out there! After we were seated, we were greeted quickly and our beverages arrived promptly. I ordered the Salmon BLT with Pasta Salad. My husband ordered the Hendel Burger without cheese and fries. We both enjoyed our sandwiches very much. I ordered the Key Lime Pie for dessert and, while good, it was not very lime-y....not tart at all. The lady at the table beside us had the fish tacos and they looked and smelled divine! I would definitely try those the next trip. The place is noisy, so don't plan on a quiet, intimate meal.

Stopped in for an after school snack. Parking in the back. The staff was all smiles and friendly. My daughter was thrilled to see bakers in action through the looking glass. We purchased cookies with yummy Nutella and apricot centers, a delicious buttery croissant, and a tasty sweet zucchini muffin! Enjoyable

Beer is decent and the outdoor seating is nice, but the service was off when I visited the tasting room, and that just made for bad experience. The bar area got crowded and no one knew how to get the bartenders' attention, although once you got it, they were pretty chill.   The food area can get crowded if someone is picking up their food and you're trying to order. The guy working the food prep area seemed annoyed at me for standing around waiting to order. The food itself was decent (Peacemaker), but pricey for what it was. $15 for mussels, $10 for a tiny portion of blue crab dip w/one pretzel stick.  Eh, it's fine. I'll keep drinking the beer but won't go out of my way to return here.

I'm a fan. Service was excellent, food came out very quickly, and the portions were massive. I was a bit disappointed by how things weren't very spicy (even when ordered on a 8 spice level), but everything still tasted great.  My friend and I ordered the dan dan noodles, the chili-oil wontons, and the double-cooked fish. To get it out of the way, the chili-oil wontons were the biggest disappointment - no flavor, super greasy, and not spicy at all. The dan dan noodles were great, though, and the double-cooked fish was the star of the meal. The fish was fried perfectly and tossed with chilis, leeks, and garlic. It also came with a large bowl of rice on the side - more than enough food for two people to share.  Han Dynasty is definitely worth coming to, although I would stop by during their lunch specials because the regular menu can be fairly pricey.

It's a wonderful place. My friend who lives in NOLA brought me here to hang out as the locals do. The food is not traditional "touristy" NOLA food, but it's simple, clean, light, local, and absolutely delicious. I love that there's live music, but it's a bit hard to find a place to hear it depending on what time you come. Come here for the food, come here for the wine, come here for the music - it is ALL good.

Atmosphere: Interesting crowd. Very young and trendy. Likely very privaledged people and that are local or college students. Not diverse at all. Most seem to be beach style (trendy but rough like you just got out of bed but your look is premeditated). Comfortable seating area, very busy which is probably great for business. Pretty sure I saw a dude with beach clothes on and a gold chain. Also saw some women in emo style but they appeared to be privaledged as well (obviously not certain of any of this but I want to paint you an accurate picture).   Food: Pretty good and likely considered healthy (I can never really tell). Lots of Acai and other styles of bowls to include oatmeal and quinoa. I got the quinoa bowl and my wife tried the muesli bowl. The food was pretty good.   Service: Nothing special. Good service.   Overall: Probably worth going back based on the healthy option route. Not bad.

We stopped in for dinner during a weekday to a nearly empty restaurant.  Waited 10 minutes for someone to greet and seat us.  We ordered the 'customized' guacamole.  The portion size is admirable but the guacamole was basically bland despite the additions you can select.  Chips were crisp and tasty though.  We order Tamales which were very disappointing.  The masa needed salt and the fillings are served along side the tamale, as a restaurant means not to have to make four kinds of tamales.  The mushroom mix was bitter tasting.  The pulled pork was dry and flavorless.   I also ordered a Gin and tonic (a pretty weak pour) and when I tried to order another drink they said they were out of gin.  Perhaps that was the reason for the weak pour, but what bar/restaurant runs out of gin when you serve umpteen kinds of tequila.  Pretty disappointing all the way around.

Not a great experience!!! I've been here a couple of times and haven't had much to complain about, I've really enjoyed their food and services, but that changed when I planned a larger group event. With the increase in guests their care of us got exponentially worse. Every other time I've been here there has only been two ladies and they seemed to handle us well no hick ups. This time I made a 15 person table the first thing that happened was when we arrived they miscounted our table setting and it took about 10 minutes to get it handled and with three different servers that were not communicating to each other we kept getting more chairs and more plates and settings that had to be removed or brought back. The service this time seemed subpar and almost crass. I don't know if it was because they knew we had to pay a 18% gratuity anyway, but they were not very hospitable and I even felt ignored, they barely looked at us and often overlooked us for other smaller groups. One serve even said "I barely saw you out of the corner of my eye" because they never looked at our table directly. The food was sloppily presented Family style which I did not expect considering it was always plated individually before, I don't mind the sharing but the way it was arranged made my event feel slapdash and childish. We were constantly having to reorder things from different servers because the other would forget. What really pushed the Brunch to a bad place was that a plate of half burnt scones was dropped off, not only did it look bad, but it smelled worse and to have that placed in front of us was unappetizing and a huge oversight by the staff. One of my guests was also horribly talked down to by the staff in a very berating way when she asked for butter for her scone the server which I think was the owner stopped and said "you are in a tea house and you don't want butter, here we use Devonshire cream and not butter for our scones" this I feel was not the way to talk to a customer and embarrass them in front of a large group.  I had high hopes for this Brunch, but my hope was sorely misplaced. I don't think I will be hosting a get together here again because the service and attention to detail was so poor.

Mexican restaurants are ubiquitous inTucson. El Charro differentiates itself as being the oldest continuously operated restaurant in town. Recently visited their Oro Valley location. Had the chorizo rellanos (sp?) and pollo y burro. Both tasty. Homemade salsa good and spicy. Tres Leches Tini for her, cervasas pour moi. I liked the interior, open, with alcovey tables against the wall. Bar and TV within easy reach and viewing. I definitely liked the modern interpretation of the tin plate lights. i've heard some folks say it's too noisy, the concrete floor doesn't help, but the noise level the night we were there wasn't bad. We'll keep it on the list for a return visit.

The food was fine. Some of it was even GOOD.  But the MANAGER...creep fat man in a lime green dress shirt.  Came by EVERY table to  make sure this were going well but the table my Mother and I were at.  Went by us several times (7 AFTER I started counting), even walked right towards us, only to suddenly veer off at the last minute.  If this is who is running things there, I will not be going back.  Don't get me wrong...our server was tops!  She got a great tip for the service she gave us.  But I was made to feel VERY unwelcome by the Manager.  And the comments I heard him make to others as he made his rounds ("Better chug that beef before I get to it!") were not what I might consider appropriate.    Creepy Sizzler Manager, you have failed to being me anything approaching decent customer service!

We had a great experience here recently, and very much recommended this for others.  First, the service was great.  Robin, our waitress, was great.  Timely with attending to us without being overbearing, had no issue with the additional questions & requests.  Thank you Robin, you rock!  Drinks were also very good.  I had a great whiskey sour that was spruced up with some Syrah and Egg White.  The dinner was good, not great, but certainly a fine experience.  I recommend the salmon burger.  My only comment would be that they might need to look at adding 1 more vegan item to the menu.  Will definitely be back!
This place is great.  Michelle is the best hostess.  What I really love is all the food combos.   It's amazing!  The beef bulgogi is the bomb!

The fiesta buffet is always really good! We typically go on Seafood Thursday it is really important to get their early or else you are standing in line for 30+ mins to get a seat.  However, when you get it the staff never make you feel rushed and allow you to get you $30 worth of food.

Stopped in for lunch with coworkers on a Thursday. Place looked moderately busy. The menu looks great offering breakfast all day and your typical lunch items. There is also a decent dessert menu. Only street parking is available. We waited almost 20 minutes before someone took our order. Our waitress was slow but the food was decent. As I was waiting to pay, I commented on the assorted desserts they had displayed behind the glass. A staff member was very generous as to offer me one on the house. I had to refuse (watching my diet haha). Items were priced okay.

This is our go to wing place. Been there 100 times and never had food that disappointed. Always fresh crispy saucy piping hot delicious wings and sides. Try the onion rings too! Don't let the divey style of the place deter you... It's just super casual they aren't focused on the building they are all about thier food and service.

Great lunch deals on weekdays. I got this lunch combo for $7.50.  A choice of entree, soup, an egg roll, a piece of fruit and  salad.     I don't think any other restaurant can beat this deal. Food and service was good.

I'm the duchess! Chao roll is bomb diggity dawg! I've had like a hundo check ins. Plus free sush on your bday is totally rad. And free beer and sushi mid week is nuts and balls! Super awesome! Grub here.

A Carrollwood hidden gem. Retro vibes with modern amenities. This theatre has a full bar and restaurant. Service is very good, the ticket agents are friendly and the bar staff polite. Cold beers & refreshing cocktails, the black bean burger is okay...not great but okay. The garlic fries are phenomenal. Great for date night or outing with friends.

This is our go-to place in Broad Ripple. Our last visit was to support Dining Out For Life, an annual fundrasier for the Damien Center.   I was surprised to see the menu had recently changed, and the beloved (by me, and perhaps not many others apparently) dolmades are off the menu. So, on this visit I tried something new! I ordered the veggie quesadillas and added spinach. Good choice, and this might just be my new staple! This dish packs heat, so beware.   Other things I love about the Brew Pub: -Plentiful outdoor dining space -Interesting and truly homemade menu options -Beers available by the pint, or in smaller portions (this was much more appealing to me when I was a breastfeeding mom and just wanted a smidge of my favorite Porter) -Great kids menu (go for the Shepherds Pie, every time!)  Things that add to the charm of the Brew Pub, but might be irritating to some: -Slow-ish service -Cigarette smoke wafting over from the fire-pit area IF you choose to sit outdoors on that side of the building (not a fan of smoke? Request to sit on the street side and you'll be in the clear)

A well kept secret of Indy. These actors (they're known as "actletes") are HILARIOUS. They're very talented. I would give it a 5 if tickets were a little cheaper. It can be a fun date....unless the audience is packed with obnoxious, screaming teenage girls, which happens from time-to-time. Downside: the food is not good.

Been going to Jones on and off for about 18 months since we live in the area. No more.  No one seems to attend to the complaint that this place is deafening. In fact we left early on one visit because the noise level was so aversive that our ears were ringing and it was impossible to carry on a conversation. The food continues to be predictable and OK but so overpriced for essentially diner food. The service is chaotic and staff is not trained competently. Recently our party of 8 tried to get in on a weekday at 5 pm and the host was so scattered that he barely knew what he was doing with a line that was out the door. He looked like a deer in the headlights. Not confidence inspiring. And the people kept waiting while he dithered. Then come to find out that they NOW have changed to taking reservations. Way to go Jones -- should have done that years ago not changed the rules midstream. Needless to say, we did not have reservations and they missed out on feeding 8 hungry people. This place is on its demise I think and I further think that Starr needs to attend to the complaints that people who know food and service and ambiance are posting here and on other venues. I don't see that happening.

Okay so I have to start this review by saying (1) that the food was actually really good and (2) that this was one of the weirdest experiences of our entire lives.  We came here to use my $30 off birthday certificate and it was also our first time at Benihana.  We'd been to Hibachi before so somewhat knew what to expect but went into it with no expectations.  We sat down with two other couples and immediately knew this was going to be a weird night.  The couple next to us told us that they were regulars and came roughly once/week so we leaned on them for guidance and expertise.  I ordered the Rocky's choice and my husband got the teriyaki steak - both were good but the problem was our experience.  Immediately my water was overfilled and the woman to the other side of the table made a huge deal about it and we knew that they were going to be persnickity and make all of the servers and chefs very flustered and because of that my husband and I just tried to smile, laugh and enjoy our date night and not pay attention to them.  They went on to correct the chef multiple times during his presentation, tell the servers that they were getting them the incorrect sauces, extra dressings, multiple waters and sodas even if they weren't finished with the ones that they had (people, i tried to tell you that this was the weirdest experience we've ever had).  With how shaken the staff was, our presentation was not the best and our food wasn't cooked properly because the chef had to cater to this couples ridiculous demands (more butter, cooked more, i want my vegetables and meat mixed together, cut my meat in the opposite direction).... and then the weirdest part was that the couple ordered an additional steak which when the server went through to ensure that our orders were accurate was never brought up but then when the couple didn't receive it at the end of the meal were OUTRAGED.  To the point where the manager came over and assured them and guaranteed that he put the steak on the plate himself (he didn't) and they called his bluff and everyone got even more uncomfortable.  phew.  Now they forgot my white rice as well as the entire table's ice cream and tea and gave us the incorrect check twice.  The bathroom floors were incredibly sticky and gross and two people at our table had to request new flatware or plates because they weren't cleaned properly.  This review is after a dinner on a Monday night so maybe it was just an off time for this crew and I know that the customers were not helping the cause but we're not willing to try again to find out if it was just a fluke.

The food is good but this experience had the worst service. Waited for basically 30 minutes to get our change/cards back from our waitress while we could literally see her just talking with other tables about her fanny pack pins. Super disappointed and a real waste of my time. .... update now it has been an hour

I like this place a lot! Far better than some of the other Mexican places in Springfield. I love the tamales, I've had a few flavors and they don't disappoint, but my favorite is pork with green sauce. My husband had nachos with some kind of beef. THAT was delicious. They also have Mexican Coke in bottles, which is a favorite treat of mine. The flan was good. Not the best I've had, but still satisfying. I wish they had horchata! The atmosphere is nice, very casual. I would go for lunch not a romantic date. The menu is pretty large and I'm excited to keep trying new things.

Decided to go here for breakfast yesterday. Overall it was a good experience. My only, and I guess it's most important, complaint would be the food. I ordered the creamed chip beef and got a side of scrapple. The chipped beef was very VERY bland. Had no taste whatsoever.  I've made better at home from the frozen packages you get at the grocery store. The scrapple was fine, but a little pricy for the portion.  For what it's worth, my gf enjoyed her meal. I forget it's name, but it was an open faced sandwich with some sauce on top.   I'm not sure if it's worth the trip to just get okay food when there are better breakfast spots closer.

Everyone here is so nice. We got their pudding and a little cupcake once which were both delicious. We also got a large (20 people) custom buttercream birthday cake for our baby's 1st birthday and couldn't have been more pleased. The cake was fun and beautiful and soooooo yummy. I ate it for breakfast, lunch, and dinner for several days. They're so easy to work with. I never had to stress about the cake. We also bought separate cake toppers from Amazon and the bakery incorporated them so nicely into the cake. We will see them for our next bday cake!

This brewery was awesome. It is incredibly large with plenty of picnic tables and games. Bunch of friendly folks too! Their beer selection is good. Based on the banners that were hung, they've won some awards too.  I got Blueberry Lemon Gose which I think was a special they had at the moment. That and the Lime-Cucumber Gose both had the tart flavor I was craving. Ugh this place was so good.
Always a fun and interesting  place that offers the unusual combination of creative cocktails, great food and decadent deserts.  We come here often.
Very good falafel, fries,and baba ghanoush.  Not very good shawarma though, it was light on meat and a little dry.  Quick and efficient service.

IMA comes with a bit of a sticker shock for a single day admission. On its face, it seems expensive as it is right up there with some museums in Chicago. The catch there is other museums charge for specific exhibits but once you're in the IMA, you're good to go.  If you go at least twice a year, you should just spring for the $75 membership. You also get discounts for special events and a few other benefits as well.  IMA is a beautiful facility and have really done a lot to modernize over the past few years. In addition to the current Audubon exhibit, the IMA also has mini golf and a beer garden. You can explore the nearby greenhouse while drinking beer or wine. Or enjoy a King David's hot dog or some great iced coffee. And considering you're at a museum, the prices aren't marked up too much.  I'm glad I'm a member and will likely renew next year.

I had never been inside the IMA before this week. SHAME, I know. How had I not yet explored this incredible place practically in my own backyard??  It was a beautiful fall afternoon so I decided, what the heck, let's go explore. I was on the hunt for a free annual membership using my handy dandy student ID - students attending Butler, CTS, IUPUI, Marian, Martin, and UIndy all get a free membership for a year. Crazy, right?! All you have to do is go to guest services inside the main entrance and fill out an application. Your official membership card comes in the mail a few weeks later.  Parking was easy (and free!) to find in the paved surface lot off the 38th Street entrance. There is also a parking garage.   Before I even went inside, I strolled around the front lawn area and marveled at the gorgeous flowers and landscaping. Wooden benches beneath shaded trees? Check. Big beautiful fountain? Check. The requisite visit to the iconic Robert Indiana LOVE sign? Done. It's worth it alone just to go there and sit on a peaceful sunny afternoon!   The inside though? IT'S SO BIG. Goodness gracious, it goes on forever. Three floors of gallery space that wind and weave to more rooms. You're like "there can't be more..." but there is, and then you're lost. But fear not, ask for a map at guest services or ask one of the friendly workers in the rooms. I had no idea the IMA had such an expansive collection - and everything from artistic paintings to ancient relics and fashion pieces. Something for everyone with any interest!  I also just really loved the modern vibe - high ceilings, bright, spacious. The escalators and elevators make it very accessible for all visitors.  I was impressed with how many staff members were spread in the gallery areas. They didn't bother me, but one kind fellow noticed I had stopped to ponder a crisp, photograph-like painting and informed me that Napoleon's brother used to own it. So cool! They are there to enhance the experience and answer questions but not bug you.   According to the website and brochure, photography is allowed, but not for commercial use. I didn't take many photos because I thought it wasn't allowed and that's why the staffers were there - to watch us like hawks and snatch my camera. Guess not!  Don't forget the Lilly Gardens (need a membership to explore there), the IMA Cafe, and 100 Acres (which is free and open dawn to dusk - take entrance off of 38th Street).   You can spend an hour here or a whole day here - it really depends on what you get out of the experience. There's a handy guide at guest services called "I have an hour" with recommendations on what to see to make the most of your visit!  TL;DR do yourself a favor and check out this state of the art museum in Indy!

Stopped in for lunch today with 2 other friends. This was the first time any of us have been to this restaurant and we found it by accident, it was the only restaurant open for lunch on Monday. Boy we sure were glad we found us and it's going to be a new favorite. I had the Punkys house salad with blue cheese dressing and it was wonderful. It came in a large wooden bowlwith a lot of toppings. My only complaint would be the wooden bowl had some chips around the edges. And probably should've been thrown away. My one friend had the grilled cheese sandwich which was made with Bree, fried green tomatoes, and bacon. We all three loved this sandwich, unbelievable. The best grilled cheese I have ever had. Our other friend ordered the Cobb salad with a side of spicy waffle fries. He also really enjoyed his meal. We really enjoyed eating here, the atmosphere is super cool, my only suggestion would be to turn the music down a bit. We were there on a business lunch and the song crazy bitch came on. The song was blaring and it was not bleeped for the inappropriate words. So if not for the wooden bowl having chips around the edges and the loud somewhat vulgar music this would be five stars.

Holy boba! Some great stuff in a cup!!! Gotta get that halo halo all the toppings were yummy and their flan is so tasty!!! Try the a chocolate-taro smoothie aka the "Johnny" or at least they should call it that cause I order it too much.

I have been going to veekoo forever. The food is delicious and fresh the only problem is they get so crowded between takeout orders and people waiting to be sat that it's almost like waiting in a crowded subway car. They should expand the sushi bar so you can actually sit at it and expand the waiting area to make customers more comfortable. They have already remodeled once, it's time to do it again.

Nice place. They seem to be going for Guiness world record for most Lassi and Chaat flavors. Not only that, the food is also good. the Chaat looks a little strange. I must admit I was apprehensive, but once I tried it it was quite good. I had the Kunna lamb Chaat and my wife had the Chicken Keema. We also had a Lychee Lassi and a Date Almond Lassi, along with a sample of one that had strawberry in it. Only downside is this place is a bit of a hole-in-the-wall with very little seating. Probably better for takeout, although we were able to get one of the three tables when we were there.

For a smoked meat house this placed rocked the Green Dragon Vegan sandwich (disclaimer: there is honey in the dressing) I popped in after a float session at Modern Gravity cause I was too relaxed to drive.   The space is very rustic, trendy and in it's own right had an individual flare of a backyard picnic with white string lights, an old bike and white washed wood. Black marker words of inspiration, love and admiration towards the restaurant were scrawled in front of the kitchen. The unique charm of this establishment is the homemade perserves they offer for sale and in their dishes. They use local producers and bring things back to basics. They do add a sense of adventure with items like pickled daikon.   The place closes at 3pm for high tea. But at 2:30 they still allowed me in. All of the staff were authentic and really warm to be around.   I choose the Green Dragon veggie sandwich on rye with a side order of fries and a coffee. The coffee was rich and flavourful. The sandwich was kale and sprouts tossed with a honey vinegrette dressing layered with pickles and jalapeno hummus on rye. It was a brilliant sandwich with a compliment of sweet, sour, astrigent and bitter flavours. The fries were excellent. Deep fried shoe string coated in cayanne with home made Ketchup. It could have used a bit more salt. But that's just me.   Overall it was an excellent lunch. I left feeling full, happy and relaxed. I really enjoyed all aspects of the restaurant.

This place rocked during the time they were at the house instead of the strip mall. It was the best in town for BBQ. I was there with my wife one night and the sausage, baby back ribs were cremated...it was so dark, dry and had the scent of burn gasoline. The next time I came it was great, then another time it was the same dry meat again. Hit or miss or what is the deal?
Basically, some of the best Mexican food in St. Louis.  Always  good, fast and friendly.  Often see a large Hispanic crowd enjoying a meal so you know it's the real deal!

Like this location for the convenience, but I prefer the Butler location.  Same food, but the atmosphere at Butler is better.  This location is more like a place where you see, and stop in, whereas Butler is more like a neighborhood feel.  However, the service here (esp Slap) is great.  Food served hot, quickly and they check on you just enough.  I was reading and she just came by to clear the plates and leave more coffee and cream.  Either place is great -- give it a try!

As always I rate by similar types of places.  This place caters to teenagers.  Somewhat expensive.  Medium sized portions and long waits even if it is not busy.  If you have never been here you will need to find a local middle schooler for a tutorial.   Pretty sure the menu is in some sort of teenager code. Taste is good and it is fun to visit once.   Most adults this will be a one time kind of place.

HOLY WOW!!!   This is a fantastic dinner spot!  I had the Shrimp and grits for dinner and started with the crab cake!  I really enjoyed this  experience.  The  service is spot on - the food is excellent!   The desserts makes this fat kid happy!!  The bread pudding is really excellent!

It was not bad, I had the jerk chicken dinner platter and my friend had the mango chicken dinner platter. We also ordered jerk chicken wings for an app. The jerk chicken holds a very nice kick and I enjoyed it tho the jerk wings were very burnt and dry. The dinner platters were more moist and tasty and over all very good.

Loved it!!!  We've been twice now and both times the pizzas were great. One time we ordered in and everyone order personal size pizzas. It was great that everyone could customize their pizzas and get just what they wanted. The second time we ordered online. The ordering process was very easy and again the pizzas were tasty!  We will definitely be going back!
Screw Pat's and Geno's! Joe's is the best by far! The fries are always crispy and I personally prefer larger chunks of meat in my cheesesteaks. The milkshakes are incredible also!

The Bourbon Street Breakfast is really good and a great value. It's basically a dish of red beans and rice topped with a fried egg and split-and-toasted corn muffin. I had one today and it really hit the spot! Great comfort food. I also tried the gator sausage on a stick which was really good.
This restaurant is a neighborhood gem. You can get really delicious and healthy food fast here. The owners are also super sweet. It's a perfect place for takeout.

There is no doubt that the food here is first rate.  The flavors are fresh and authentic Sicilian, and the antipasto is possibly one of the best I have ever eaten.  There was sweet, spicy, savory cheeses - it was phenomenal, and artfully put together.  I could have licked the plate of the lamb ragout that accompanied the pillowy light potato gnocchi, and my husband reminisced about our own trips to Italy with the grilled sardine appetizer her enjoyed.  But the dining experience here was dreadful.  My husband and I couldn't hear each other talk across the table, so we just didn't talk through most of the meal, which made us look like a couple on an awkward first date.  You don't have an assigned server, instead, the multiple servers in the dining room flit around like vultures waiting to snag an empty plate.  There's no warmth, no friendliness, no Sicilian "family" feeling.  The gentleman at the table next to ours tipped our bread basket forward every time he leaned back in his chair, but we left the basket there instead of switching it with the drinks on the other side.  As the evening wore on, the restaurant became quite dark, and some white, bright table cloths would have gone a long way to add warmth and light to the place.  Again, if you are going for delicious food and ambiance is not a priority, definitely go to Zeppoli to enjoy the amazing food.  But after waiting a couple of months to snag a table, we wanted a whole wonderful dining experience.  We definitely didn't get that.
It's still no bastion of authentic Mexican cuisine by a long shot, but dare I say it?  El Arado is now actually passable. A solid three stars. Hurrah!

I stayed at the Gaylord a few years ago and visited it this May. The place has not changed much and is beautiful as ever. Their Rainforest and other plants are breathtaking. This is a busy place that has lots of things to offer and the Grand Opry is next door. They have a great pool and gym, both we were able to check out.

Yum yum yum!!!!!!    Had the buffalo chicken sandwich and it was outstanding - just the right crispness and tenderness!  And their dressings are homemade, and me being a dipper :) that made the experience that much better....  French fries and tea were superb as well..  I'll be back for sure!
Great little hole in the wall sushi place. Great value for lunch. Best sweet potato tempura roll I've had outside of Orlando

Super solid choice any day of the week.   My favorite thing about Twisted Tail is that it's always a good choice. Whether you've got some foodie friends in town and want to impress (but not quite looking for Vetri-level shenanigans) or just looking to catch a great happy hour deal, Twisted Tail should be your go-to place.   I've enjoyed their lovely dinner menu and service  but more often you'll find me at the bar, enjoying live music and munching on yummy "social hour" bar bites (get the crawfish Mac n'cheese and oysters!!) and drinking super reasonably priced cocktails and $3 beers. They also give you complimentary savory popcorn at the bar.   They have "social hour" seven days a week and a nice lounge on the 2nd floor if you're really feeling mellow. Just do it.

Having not been in the Marigney for a couple of years this was a welcome new find.  Small and intimate, there was a traditional style ballad jazz band performing.  I do not know how else to describe this, the music was low and soothing and we could eat, drink, converse and listen all at the same time which is rare.  It was late and the place was packed, lovely.  The drinks were so-so, they seemed to have their own twist on what a cocktail should be so I had a glass of wine instead.  The food was A-ok, Lamb sliders and a clam vongole (although it had bacon and parmesan on it, not what I was looking for but delicious)- tapas portions.  We just happened in on the the exact atmosphere we wanted, a beautiful thing indeed.

A few friends and I came on a Saturday morning for brunch. I was surprised by how empty the restaurant was, given that it was prime brunch time. The inside of the restaurant is modern looking, but with exposed pipes and a hipster twist. We were seated by the large windows in a booth overlooking the street. With the warm September light streaming in, it was the perfect brunch atmosphere.   I ordered the 24 Brunch, which consisted of two eggs (any style), potato hash, focaccia bread, and bacon. Though the individual components of the meal was typical diner-brunch fare, the quality of the ingredients was high and relatively affordable ($10.00). The focaccia bread, shaped into sticks, was thick and pillowy with nice herb flavoring and a toasted shell. The eggs were cooked medium-hard, like I'd requested, and the potato hash was solid, but not anything special. In hindsight, I wish that I had ordered my eggs sunny side-up like my friend, so that I could dip the focaccia in the egg. Relative to the rest of the meal, there was a lot of focaccia bread. The portion sizes were substantial, but not generous. What surprised me the most about the meal was the bacon. The bacon wasn't cooked in any special way that I was aware of, but it was delicious. Bacon has a leg up in being delicious, given that its bacon, but this bacon was melt-in-your-mouth delicious. My other friend ordered the mixed berry waffle, which he reported as "fruit on top of a waffle." He enjoyed it, but wouldn't necessarily pay $11.00 to eat it again.  Overall, we had a nice time. The service was very attentive while we were there and the atmosphere was relaxed. I would come back again for the atmosphere and try some of their other brunch foods, such as the pizza, or their regular entrees.

I doubt if I'll visit Fulton Alley again. I went there Saturday night with some friends around 12am (so I guess that would actually be Sunday). We had no intentions on bowling; we only wanted to grab some drinks and food and chill. My friend went to the front counter to get a menu. She asked if the kitchen was open. First she was ignored by two people standing up there, and after she had to repeat herself, a girl (young, black girl, skinny, some type of scarf thing on her head) finally gave her a menu, but she just held it out without even acknowledging my friend or answering the question! She was unnecessarily rude.  It took us about 5 minutes to decide what we wanted, then we went to the bar to order. The bartender was polite, but he told us the kitchen was closed. I was understanding, but I wanted to know why the front counter girl even gave us a menu if she knew the kitchen was closed. I went to the counter asked what time the kitchen closed and she told us 1. It was 12:40. Fulton Alley, if your kitchen is supposed to close at 1, it needs to close at 1. When I told her the bartender just told me the kitchen was closed she just changed her answer and said "oh, well I guess it's closed then" and took the menu out of my hand and threw it on the counter. Excuse me?! What type of night was this chick having that she had to be so rude? Whatever, I wasn't leaving, so I stood there and asked "so is it closed, or is it not?" There was a guy (short, white guy, blonde hair, might've been in a mohawk type thing if I remember correctly) that heard the exchange, so he said he'll go check because he wasn't sure if the kitchen was still cooking after some private party that was going on. When he came back out, the girl told us that the kitchen wasn't cooking anymore after the party, so that was the only food they had. I told her that we weren't with the party; we came here on our own and did she expect us to go and take those people's food? All she said was "well the kitchen's closed" and turned her back on me!   My friends and I immediately left. We wanted to stay; the DJ was awesome and we were really having a good time, but that front counter girl ruined it for us.

I was beside myself taking in all the gorgeous treats in here!  It is a stunning shop,  with high ceilings and light and space to showcase the delectable baked goods.  Loved trying samples of the gluten-free peanut butter brownies which were moist and gooey and amazing.  Picked up a vegan chocolatey-carmel dessert that was utterly amazing.   And,  they have incredible dog biscuits which my fussy furry daughter gobbled up,  looking for more!  The woman at the counter helping us was beyond accommodating and pleasant and knowledgeable.   A friend wanted to meet Becca (primary pastry chef/owner?) and she came out and hung for a bit and posed for pics ... a lovely soul.  This place is awesome!!!

I have been anxiously awaiting a visit to Burger Republic and finally had the perfect opportunity on Sunday morning at about 11:30 am. This location is not very large, and there are some long waits during prime dining time.   BF and I were super excited to try them out, and we decided on our first go around to order the Tennessee burger and I ordered the Taco Truck on the house made veggie patty, which is a mixture of quinoa and awesomeness. Seriously one of the top veggie patties that I've had in my life. For real. Also, you can sub out any of their burgers with beef, chicken, ground turkey, or the house made veggie patties. As someone that is not a huge burger fan (unless it's In-N-Out) I LOVE the variety and options that they provide!! Wow.   The list of burgers is crazy, and there's seriously something for everyone. We also had the misfortune of sitting by the milkshake window and I drooled over them being made. Those things are trouble. I resisted this time around, but next time I'm definitely going to cave. Especially since they offer spiked versions of shakes...Uh oh.   Our waitress couldn't have been nicer, she discussed the menu in detail and was quick with suggestions and was delightful all around.   Delicious all around. I can't wait to try them all!

So many positive reviews. unfortunately, what a big disappointment. The drink was so bland that our group had ordered four different drinks and not one taste good/ok. The atmosphere is nice and relaxing but to go hang out and grab a drink, not a place to go to.

I want to like the Helena Bakery, but they don't offer the yummy bakery items they provided at Lucky Penny, so I miss Lucky Penny because they had better food. I especially miss the wood fired eggs and financiers. Every time we order for here, they give us our drink in to go cups and it takes a long time to get the drinks. Today the music was really bad late 70's early 80's music. I'm done with this place.
We had the best seat in the house, and it was a real pleasure eating here. The shrimp and grits was outstanding. Every bit of our experience was top notch, and was a real treat.

Perfect lunch place and great for vegetarians because there is a build-your-own salad and sandwich bar. I created a veggie sandwich which was very tasty. Since it's also a market there is a large selection of drinks and snacks.
Never stayed here in a room before but have been to the bar many times. The carousel bar is very cool and historic. Even if you're walking by stop in and have a drink

A local and tourist favorite near the Vanderbilt Campus in Nashville. Lots of street parking nearby, but we snagged a spot in the small lot behind the building (still had to pay though). We arrived around 9 on a Tuesday and there was no wait, we were seated immediately. Their website also has a pancake cam to check the line before you venture out! As much as I wanted to love their food, the pancakes did not stand out to me. I will admit I had the pigs in blankets so I didn't try any of the gourmet flavors but the plain buttermilk were standard pancakes. My sisters had the small appetite plate and it was just the right portion.

All I have to say is BEST FRENCH TOAST EVER. Seriously. If you think you need their pancakes, you don't. You need their French toast. Also the service is wonderful and quick since everyone is basically ready to order as soon as they sit down. And again. THE FRENCH TOAST.

Huge space with plenty of tables and games. We came for a birthday party and it was great. I loved the Charming Wit beer, it was light and easy to drink. My girlfriend liked the Delta Mama pale ale. Their cans are really cool. It was a really fun place to celebrate our friends birthday. They let us bring in food and cake and move tables around.

we were told over and over how great the chili is... but guess what, it's not that great. maybe because my mom and my fiance make an amazing chili, so i am used to theirs. fun place to go with friends, and i thoroughly enjoyed the margaritas. the fried jalapeno popper app was the highlight for me. this place is a-ok!

After all the hype I had to try and my chicken salad sandwich was really good! I was not a fan of the duck fat fries or the quack and cheese. The taste of duck fat was strong and left a bad taste in my mouth. My bf had the fried boneless chicken breast sandwich which was also just ok. I probably won't go back.

Have gotten a few cakes for birthdays and such well in fact several over the years. The last three have been less than yummy, this last one the cake it self was dry, very dry.  Guess it is time to find some place else huh.

Don't shop here very often due to it's distance from home. They do have many items that you just can't find elsewhere. They have a fantastic bakery and prepared foods operation. Prices can be a bit higher than the competition. For example, a filled king cake was priced $18, while the same flavor was $8 at Winn Dixie.

GREAT taco truck food. We always get the Sonoran hot dogs, carne asada tacos and my husband likes the Carmelos. They are so delicious and the best in town for sure. It is outside so we normally get our food to go but it's still just as good when we get home.

I like this place, a lot!   I felt as if I was transformed to a different place in time. My first thought that flooded my mind when crossed the threshold was,"wow!" The initial atmosphere is breath taking.   There are low sitting tables, and a high bar community table. Each table has a specific name so should you find a quaint place you want to eat on your next romantic outing, you can asked to be sat at your own private bubble of romance. There are several tables that will up the romance and once you visit, you will know what I mean.  Back to my experience. Great! Logan, the Director of Capital Cellar's Operations and our server, was happy, helpful, and fun. After we flooded her with several questions she offered to give us a small tour of the underground food haven.   My favorite room is the large group room in the back.  It has walls of wine and a live, dancing light show in the ceiling. (Nothing overwhelming, it is just simple and adds to the experience. )  My friend and I left wanting more. We both had a glass of wine and some delicious croquettes with a pea purée.   I will be back very soon because it doesn't have to be a romantic stop, it can be a breakfast, lunch or coffee stop!  Go, go! Stop walking by ten times and wishing you had gone there like I did. I waited too long to find this spot!

I would give the food a "4 star." So if you want delivery or pick up, definitely come here. The food was great and the cook was wonderful! This review is on their bartender and set up. This business is split half and half. One side is a bar and one side is a pizzeria. No one was on the pizza side when we came in and so we sat on the bar side (We just wanted pizza). The bartender greeted us as we came in. We went to sit at a table and she said why do you come sit at the bar? We didn't reply yet and she says yah, you'll sit up here. Then, we tell her we want to sit at a table and under her breath she says whatever, you get better service at the bar. So we sit down and she asks us what we want to drink and we say water and lemonade she looks at us crazy and repeats our drinks back to us rudely. We get up and walk to the other side. We didn't want a bar experience while we were there to eat pizza. So she follows us over and says why did you leave? We told her we just wanted to sit in the restaurant side. She goes to our new waitress and says have fun with them. Our new waitress was good.  Then this same bar tender comes over to the pizza side yelling to the cook saying you cock sucker and fuck you. Really loud. It was sooo annoying. They have this place set up so you can dine in or go to the bar. It was awful. This bartender ruined our dining experience. We were there at 9pm on Friday to eat pizza and we were not at the bar drinking and this was our experience. The food is great. The cook is great. Order take out or delivery. Do not dine in, do not bring your kids in. I cannot speak for the bar services. Just the pizzeria side.

Philly local and I was not impressed with the taste of this cheesesteak. It's underseasoned/bland, the bread is way too chewy.. Not a good chewy but the type you can still be chewing way after the meat is gone. They give you very little meat. Maybe this is a great cheese steak to the hipsters? My sister did enjoy her vegan cheesesteak but I will stick to Carmen's Cosmi's or Jim's... Sidebar:They do have good wiz sauce though.

I'm confused by the other reviews. Perhaps it was an off night? I ordered the goat roti and oxtail entree.  My two favorite dishes! The goat was very tough and the curry was flat. Similarly the oxtail was tough and the flavors were flat. I really wanted to like this place because of the location and the service was so friendly. But I was really disappointed with the food. There are better places in Philly if you're willing to drive.  Maybe I'll try again in the future to see if it was just an off night.

Got to say I always have loved the Ben franklin bridge even when I did live by it and had to travel yo Philly.  First and foremost it is a beautiful constructed bridge with a view like you wouldnt believe going across.  Noe if you are a runner you will get something special out of this as running the bridge gibes you an even better view plus you have the luxury of taking pictures.  The bridge is 5$ to get over which isn't great compared to the other bridges into Philly are actually cheaper but it still isn't as bad as the gwb in NY which is like 15$  so keep that in mind.  Plus this bridge doesn't open up for boats so you don't have to worry about that either.  Lastly they have been doing construction lately so there are sometimes delays especially in the early morning and at night.  The commuting hours to factor that in.

UPDATE from previous review: Their manager Ali contacted me privately and assured all concerns were addressed and offered her apologies.   Hoping to see the results in a positive way.  I'll give this place a second chance.  WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE. EVER!!!! Not once was I asked how my food was, coffee was served once, with no cream and sugar. I was there for 40 mins and not ONCE was I asked for a refil. My order was wrong.  I left to pay inside at the cashier, since I had been waiting 20 mins for someone to get my bill, only to walk in on my servers CHIT CHATTING like they had no job to do!!!  There was a server who was on his smoke break, smoking outside in the public in front of me eating; went back inside and started bussing tables WITHOUT HAVING WASHED HIS HANDS AFTER SMOKING!!!! I left and called to talk to their manager Alli - but while talking to her she sounded more ANNOYED at me than concerned and understanding and said, "OK SIR, I GOTTA HANG UP NOW....."  All I wanted was a nice place to have breakfast; but TBH, after today, I would rather eat off Roseanne Barr's underboob than have to eat here and be treated like this ever again!!!
St. Louis stable! So yummy and reasonably priced!!

Stayed here with my mom and sister for a girls weekend in the city, and picked this place for its well designed lobby and pretty guest rooms. There is another Kimpton in the city that was a bit cheaper and more central depending on your plans, but it didn't look quite as cute! After studying the photos prior to arrival, all was as great as I had hoped: Complementary champagne upon check in, an incredibly decorated lobby, and super cozy guest rooms. Location was walkable to South street, city hall, Walnut and Chestnut streets shopping areas etc. We had a great room overlooking Independence mall park, which was nice and remote as compared to a lot of the cityscapes I'd expect you'd see at other Philly hotels. They have a cute restaurant attached on the corner,  The Red Owl Tavern, but we didn't venture in this trip because we were on a mission for some of the other local favorites! They also have a nightclub on the 10th floor, which was not our thing either, however it has some outdoor space that would be nice on a warm day

My boyfriend and I had dinner here a few weeks ago.. it was amazing! Their house pop corn is super delicious ..we ordered the punch bowl, octopus, fried chicken, wings , and corn bread... we loved it all! Also, the service was great!
Great beer selection. Park paradise is a delicious Pilsner. Gose blueberry lemon is my fav

Omg omg omg get their nachos!!!! They are called habanero nachos, so you know they have a lil kick to them. The nachos are so fresh, they taste like no nacho I've ever had before. I swear they make them fresh, it's no way they come out of a plastic bag. I've tried other food from here but I can't remember, didn't have a lasting impact on me. Drinks are drinks, noting special. But those nachos though, try em!  Oh yeah they have a lil dj booth and one side of the bar turns into a dance floor for ppl to get their jam on once nightful hits. Music wasn't really my type but the atmosphere is cool.

This is a great little place! Gyros, Greek salad with chicken, hummus and pita all delicious! At other places at times salads are lacking enough meat, but not here! Plenty of chicken and the salad is enough for two meals. I always leave with a box and have it for a second meal. The pitas are some of the best that I have had! They also do catering and have a television screen with pictures of all of the yummy platters they are able to do. Each time service has been friendly and welcoming. You can get your food to go or eat inside at one of the several tables.

The place is nice, the service was friendly and efficient and the owner is a pretty nice guy! They don't serve food, but had a food truck on site, so sustenance is available if needed! The beers were well made and free of off flavors, but nothing wowed me! Although the El Heffe was quite tasty! Definitely worth trying out, plenty of house beers on tap, and nice people working there! If you like craft beer you should definitely stop by!

I don't know how long I've been saying that if the world was ending or if I was dying, the first thing I'd do is rush to Vetri. Thankfully, neither scenario happened, and I was privileged to finally dine there last night.   With that being said, I obviously preemptively put Vetri Cucina on a pedestal surrounded with unrealistic hype. I had butterflies the moments leading up to my visit of combined sheer giddiness and nerves, that I would somehow be let done. I'm ecstatic to report that my dining experience at Vetri was one of the top dining experiences of my lifetime, and I found it to be truly everything everyone says it is, and more.   Upon entry to the restaurant, I was actually a little uncertain. The hallway was narrow, and I had to walk through a crowd to get to the hostess stand. That literally was the lowlight of the evening, and that's not even horrible. Every aspect of the evening was stellar from there-on-out.   Where to begin? Well, to start, I wish I was taking notes. Our server was so knowledgeable that I wish I could've written down everything he said about the food and the wine. The wine paring was extraordinary from the thorough to description to the actual pairing. I had the six-pour pairing and I'd absolutely recommend going that route. I didn't view the actual list until after the meal, I didn't realize that they literally listed the wines and dishes they paired with on the page. This is a great keepsake, and guide for my own future pairings.   After our aperol spritz, we started with a few "snacks" and a vegetable crudités platter for the table. We passed the veggies and helped ourselves to the snacks from the center of the table. Our next course, after the bread, was our antipasti. I didn't realize the plate in front of me was my tuna crudo, and mine alone. Theoretically, a group could share and pass everything. We did, to some extent, however we mostly ate the plate in front of us. Based on specific wine pairings, this does make sense.    Taking a step back, before our tasting journey began, our server gave us some time to discuss the menu and our preferences. I was told to "not be shy" if there was something I really wanted to try. While the tasting menu had the element of surprise at every course, the meal was centered around that initial conversation with the server. From just looking at the menu, there were a few standouts that I simply needed to try.   After the antipasti- we moved on to multiple rounds of pasta. Mamma mia! The swiss chard gnocchi with brown butter was one of the most exquisite things I've ever eaten in my entire life. The gnocchi melted in my mouth as the flavor of the brown butter sauce lingered. This was served with the tortellini with truffle sauce that would've been a knockout on its own. I received the mafaldini with lamb shank ragu as my second pasta course. This was my must-have, and while delicious, the gnocchi still takes the cake. Special shout out to the delectable seafood in the tonnarelli al Frutti di mare that I sampled for one of the other plates  At this point in the meal, I was starting to hurt, however there was no looking back. No food down, no food left behind. Of course, I had to sample the next round of bread that came out- the sourdough. No regrets either.   I rallied in time for our secondi; in my case, the milk fed veal with asparagus- yet another standout from my initial menu review. I've said this before about veal, however, we have a new winner. This was the BEST veal I've had in my life. We had two different preparations of this tender, tender meat. One was more tender and filet-like, the other was delectably fattier with a slight crisp. There was a dollop of sauce, however I preferred the veal in its own juices.   Finally, we reached dessert status. Based on our groups eagerness to try everything, our server ended up bringing us one of each dessert option. The pistachio flan was placed in front of me, and the pistachio that oozed out of the flan upon dipping my fork was a highlight. We literally based the plates around the table and it was great to try a little of everything. I hit crazy status when the little pastry tray was placed in the center. Did I need to try one of everything? Probably not, but when at Vetri, you try to eat it all.   While the food was phenomenal, the staff added to the entire experience. Aside from our fantastic server, the team work and collaboration of guest service was apparent. We had so many people interacting with us throughout the meal that were aiming to please and perfectly harmonized with the actions of their colleagues.   I was smiling on the outside and smiling on the inside. This meal is a true highlight in my culinary life.  So, I'm so thrilled the world isn't ending and I'm not dying. While I've checked a major item off my bucket list, it's only left me with the taste for more of the finer Vetri Cucina dining experience.

I live around the corner from here and it has become my go to place for myself and guests.  I have had many of the items on the menu (both sweet and savory) and have not been disappointed.  The staff is friendly and the service is efficient.  The coffee is hot and specialty drinks are well made.  There is limited seating on the first floor and outside, and also seating upstairs.  I will continue to come here!

Great place!  They do steaks really well here.  Many sauces to choose from for the steaks and I like that they bring it on the side instead of covering the steak with it.  You get to try the steak first with nothing except the grill flavor which is really good.  The only knock is it is a small place with limited parking but this is good in a way because it allows them to focus on the people that are seated.  If you are not in a hurry and don't mind waiting it will be worth it.  The staff here is so helpful and good at what they do.  The bartenders were some of the best I have seen and offer great feedback on the beer and wine choices of which there are good ones!  I also think for the amount of food you get the cost is quite reasonable.  I was amazed at how many people were getting their tomahawk cut for one!  That is one large steak!!

Chili's has become that restaurant that we only go to when we are super bored with the other late night restaurants AND enough time has passed to forget how awful they are. They may have a new decor and some new menu items, but this is still the same old crappy Chili's with the same problems:  1- rarely a hostess to greet you 2- mediocre to awful service that takes FOREVER 3- disappointing (sometimes disgusting) food (I have even been served moldy cheese at this location..eww)  Our most recent visit reaffirmed that we do not like Chili's and we will not be returning soon. We arrived a little before 9pm and there was not an employee in sight. Since we were hoping to make this a quicker visit, we seated ourselves at the bar area along with 90% of the people that were in the restaurant (I'm guessing there was not hostess for them either).   We were asked for our drink orders rather quickly. We ordered our drinks and took a few minutes to decide on our dinner. You are supposed to be brought free chips and salsa in the bar area...never happened. To make matters worse, the two tables right in front of us both received their chips and salsa. I guess they just didn't like us?  After we ordered we waited...and waited...and waited. It took about 35 minutes for one mushroom swiss burger and a carnitas fajitas. When the food arrived the burger was smaller than normal (we have ordered this burger here before), overcooked and SUPER dry. On top of that, my Husband's fries were about 1/4 of the normal amount they serve. He literally had about 7 overcooked tiny fries on the plate. Pathetic.   My fajitas were served with a huge amount of onions, green bell peppers (which were undercooked) and carnitas. Unfortunately, I was only given a few tiny tortillas. It also came with some ridiculous tree of condiments (guac, some sort of taco sauce, sour cream, cheese, tomatoes, green chilis with pickled onions and a few other things). The meat was BLAND. How do you even make carnitas bland? The meat naturally has so much flavor. I also had a HUGE piece of stringy fat. Disgusting. We both ate our entrees feeling more and more gross as we ate. We left feeling sick. We probably would have complained if there had been anyone around.   After waiting for awhile and having our credit card on the table, we finally had our server come back and say "Ohh sorry, you pay with the Ziosk now" and show us how to pay. That would have been nice to know at the beginning.   Needless to say, we will not be back anytime soon. We will stick with BJ's for late night meals.

Went here for game 7 where the Warriors pulled it off against the thunder. Olivia made beer drinking an act of tribute to the beer deity, citing viscosity and aromatic natures. I highly suggest going there and having her describe each and every beer in detail and then pair with menu. Have a ball!

The pizza is always good. We loved almost everything about this location. The parking was a bit insane. Good food, beer and atmosphere. The hostess was a bit rude. Aside from that another win for FPP.

Love LOVE love this place. We've been here several time and could never go wrong. They always busy but its all worth it. Their rolled ice cream very different compare to other locations I've been too. Their toppings is perfect for your craving and unlimited too. The best thing we love to go is right across where there is a beach that you can enjoy your ice cream roll with a beautiful scenery.

If I could rank this place with six stars, I would.  I had the Shrimp and Cochin au lait sandwich.  My wife had the Cajun Cuban.  Great sandwiches.  This is a very busy place, so don't be surprised if you have to wait.  Well worth it though.

There are some places that just catch your attention.  Some places that you walk out saying,  I've got to come back.  I didn't have that feeling here.   Two chicks is a nice little cafe offering a little more than standard fare.   $7 for a breakfast sandwich without side, not even fruit, is steep.   I got the barnyard.  Which seemed a bit too simple for my liking but they had me at herb cream cheese so I went with it, indeed it's simple.  The turkey is super thick sliced like country ham.  Wasnt expecting that and honestly could have been better had it been normal deli sliced.  I added tomato and it was a good addition.  It's just too plain.  It needs Arugula or something to perk it up.  Thick sliced turkey with a fried egg slapped on some bread is reminiscent of "what's in my fridge that I can make this morning because I'm too lazy to go to the store". Sounds harsh I know. .. I'm sorry,  well not really.  Honest feedback.    I would not hesitate to come here if you're in the area but don't go out of your way to try this new establishment.    I may have to return with a greater appetite for some shrimp and grits.   The staff are sweet and service was quick even during a relatively busy time.    I like the layout and appreciate the bar seating for single people like myself.  I left paying $14 and some change + tip, for a breakfast sandwich and a mimosa. I feel like I can get more bang for my buck elsewhere in this city.

My son has been bragging about this restaurant since visiting last summer during a band camp.  The first time I visited I ordered a macaroni and cheese with bacon slice and a veggie one with artichokes while my son ordered pepperoni.  On Wednesday, I ordered one with fresh mozzarella and tomatoes.  One slice is enough since they're so large.  At our last visit, we shared a brownie.  Each time we've sat in large bench seating. It's comfortable though it can be a bit loud.  Delicious pizza; will definitely visit, again.

This was our first visit and won't be our last! We ordered an appetizer of queso with andouille sausage and it was good. I would've liked more cheese than the sausage, but I think that it was a personal preference. The chips were crisp and not stale and the salsa had the right amount of heat. I ordered the burrito Mexicana and it was delicious. It came with lettuce, sour cream and tomatoes and it was just the right portion. It was topped with a red sauce that mixed well with the sour cream. The menu was expansive. They especially offered many combinations, which is what my husband chose. He ordered a taco, enchilada, rice, and beans. He also finished everything. He got 2 margaritas and said that they tasted very good.  The service was also good. We were seated and then greeted immediately, and he checked up on us frequently.  I found the prices to be just a tad bit higher than other places, but didn't balk since the experience was a good one.

Woohooo we found this awesome park after multiple visits to NOLA.  We went unprepared to walk much though so will have to come back when we are better prepared.  Beautiful setting and no homeless like so many other places in Nola.  Right along the mighty Mississippi and beautifully landscaped.  My legs got an amazing working on the bridge crossover!

This place is right off the main tourist drag in Nashville. You know that whole area no one who lives in or near Nashville actually goes to unless there's an event? Yep. Swarms of people, overpriced food and drinks, loud music from wannabe country stars doing 90s cover songs. We were going to a concert at Bridgestone and wanted grub first but not bar food when we found this place which serves breakfast and sandwiches all day. I'm a sucker for good chicken and waffles so I ordered that. Sun's version is unique in that they cover it all in sausage gravy which I've never seen done. The chicken was good, the waffle was delicious, and the gravy was pretty good. However I think the chicken and waffles would've been better with the gravy separate, maybe with a biscuit. It wasn't bad by any means, just that it kind of takes away from the chicken -waffle combo to overpower it with sausage gravy. My wife had chicken and fries and the fries were great- almost unsalted but very crisp and I'd much rather have to add salt then have something served too salty. Other menu items like the patty melt and some of the desserts looks good but it is a relatively small menu. A few beers in bottles if you need a drink and off the main drag where bars litter the landscape it's a pretty safe bet that all day breakfast is a good racket to be in. '50s music and a plethora of Sun record memorabilia are in abundance. Kind of a surprising theme for a place with prime Nashville real estate since sun records is a Memphis rather than a Nashville thing. All in all great service, pretty good food, and a bit pricey for what it is but not devastatingly so.
Celebrated by daughter's 21st birthday here.  The pasta dishes were absolutely delish.  Prices are reasonable and it is bring your own bottle.

I try not to go to too many chains.. But I will literally be searching for this place everywhere I go now. I went for the baba ghanoush bowl, chicken Schwarma, and literally every topping they offered.. Plus the two hottest sauces of course. Everything went together so well. The sumac onions especially were crunchy (hopefully not poisonous), and flavorful. And all the slaws made me mucho happy. It's literally a Middle Eastern Chipotle, and I cannot wait to find the rest of them.

Although this location is small and could use to be expanded, I love it here. The staff is friendly and the store is always clean and organized. This is the closest TJ to me and I always make a point to stop here when I can. My only wish is that they would be able to carry alcohol!
The coffee is OK and it's fun to get a shot of booze in it. I'm never really excited by what I drink here, but it's enjoyable when I'm relaxing in the Quarter.

We went here for brunch and prices were 20 dollars a head.  We used our comps and got our meal for almost free.  The selection is pretty good as there is a salad bar, roast beef and ham station with sausage.  They had a place to make pho as well.  Thr breakfast was okay and the lunch portion.  Like the fried chicken too.  Overall not too bad for the price.

Ordered a family meal to go... & first let me comment on how RUDE the guy was when we called in. You would think I was placing a to go order close to closing time. I get that's annoying and reason for an attitude. But I called around 6 he proceeded to say hold on hold on let me check to see if we can complete your order... come in to pick up my order and it is COLD af! And cold BBQ is not appealing at all. Not to mention our ribs were pretty crispy. Needless to say this business will not be seeing me or my money again!! Horrendous service.

Chains like this should drive Pizzahutdominoslittleceasarspapajohnsetc right out of business.  The secret is to keep your eye on the name when you order.  Barro's Pizza.  Not Barro's Pasta.  The pasta is bland... you can beat their pasta sauce by using a can of crushed tomatoes and skipping the spices.  This is ok, though, because this is Barro's Pizza, and the pizza is solid!  I order thin crust, pepperoni and either sausage, onion, mushroom, or peppers, and the haven't missed yet.  Delicious every time!  I'd go as far as saying it's my second favorite in the area.  The cheesy bread sticks are good too.  It's also a nice place to eat in, and they can easily accommodate groups from the look of it.  Just tell your group to stick to the pizza and pizza-like bready things, and save the pasta for the 90yo grandma in the group who can't have anything with even a little spice.

The Service was superb there was this rising star and the young man was on point! He was polite, observant, and very accommodating! For his third day and handling a large table with the young lady he should be at Cracker Barrel for a long time!  The food was good I couldn't decide between fish or shrimp so I went with my staple catfish and it didn't disappoint! We had a large table of about 15 people and a whole lot of food!

Came here with a Groupon, which was incredibly easy to redeem. The service was prompt and welcoming and was probably the highlight of my visit.   My date and I ordered phyllo cigars, which came out piping hot and tasted pretty good. I ordered the lamb kofte and piyaz, which also tasted good but didn't wow me or seem worth what they were charging. My date got the seafood linguine, which did not taste like anything special.  I did really enjoy the complimentary olive bread!  Anyway,  the happy hour specials looked good. I would come back for those but otherwise wouldn't pay full price for a meal here.

We went for our anniversary. Wow!! Best steak we ever had! We had the ribeye with crabmeat and bearnaise. Perfectly cooked and seasoned! Service was impeccable, busy Saturday night but functioned like a machine! Anthony, our amazing server recommended we get different side dishes because they are large enough to share. He was spot on! We shared mushrooms and onion rings. I didn't care for the onion rings. I guess I thought they would be the thick rings. They were stringy. My husband enjoyed them. We also got the famous St. Elmo shrimp. Sooooo good! Building was beautiful, barhrooms were clean. Great time! Can't wait until we go again. I was to try the prime rib pizza.

Cute little bakery that offer up some pretty good pastries, cookies, and cakes. Their German Chocolate cake and petit fours are my favorites! Their decorated sugar cookies are tasty, but wish they were a little softer. They have great selection of coffee and tea drinks, and a vast variety of lunch entrees. They are a bit pricey, but overall have some tasty treats :)
Chicken kabob pita with a side rice hit the spot. They're always timely with having orders ready. Oh, and the hummus is really great. I'd also recommend picking up a smoothie.

Step out of the ordinary and enjoy some extraordinary Italian food!  This is not not the typical Italian. Katie's unique twist to her dishes knock those restaurants out of the park!  We tried the Pear Pizza and the Fig and Squash Pizza (without pancetta). Both were delicious. The table is centered with extra olive oil and chili oil, which you can add to enhance or spice up your meal.    Always remember with better ingredients come elevated prices.  Expect to pay a little more for these fresher, healthier dishes.   Katie's also delivers excellent service that is knowledgeable, quick and accurate.  We were not disappointed in the service.  They seated us quickly and served us efficiently.   What stops this place from being a five? The prices did effect my rating.  While seafood and fresh ingredients are frequently listed on the menu, I have experience fresh, larger (in portion size ) meals, that were lower in price. My own personal bias, also effected the rating. Italian is not my favorite, so it is based primarily on my personal taste.  If this review were over Italian food, it would be a five.     So next time you want to take an afternoon or evening and step out of the traditional St. Louis Italian, try Katie's Pizza.  You'll take your taste buds on an adventure!
I ordered the Rittenhouse roll and geisha roll on the special menu! I really enjoyed both. They were very fresh and flavorful. I also liked the environment.

I love Livery. It's one of my favorite spots in Indy, mostly because of their rooftop patio, but their food is also awesome. The food is mainly appetizers and small plates, so it's not a place to go if you're starving, but the atmosphere is lovely and the menu has some unique (and, more often than not, delicious) items that are fun to try. It's a great spot for drinks and catching up, as it's a little quieter and cozier than similar places in the area. Highly recommend!

I checked out EWC because I changed jobs and worked on the other side of the city from my normal waxer. I try to stay away from chains but I needed somewhere with flexible hours (and open on Sundays!)  Long story short I'm very happy with my decision. I received the 50% discount for first timers and even their full prices aren't that bad, $47 for a full Brazilian in the city is very cheap. They were professional, clean, and walked me through the whole process. I already made my second appointment!

I went on a Sunday around noon and there was a line to get a table. Apparently, that's how the crowd is all the time. I'd heard good things about Biscuits, but I would end up being disappointed.  I ordered a breakfast burrito ($8.00). The menu stated that it would be filled with chorizo, potatoes and eggs, but half of the tortilla was filled with onions and peppers, which I do not like. There were hardly any eggs in the burrito. The chorizo was bland - the worst chorizo that I've ever had.  The server was super nice, but the food was blah. I won't be going back.

Despite the ongoing construction around this location, we stopped in for some lunch.  This Chili's is located at the northwest corner of East Bay and US19.  Over the years, Chili's has changed it's menu a multitude of times to keep up with current food trends.  In my opinion, they need to stop it!  Also, as a heads up, they no longer offer free chips and salsa if you sit in the bar area.  Bummer.  For lunch, we ordered the Quesadilla Explosion Salad and the Pork Carnitas Tacos.  The salad was huge awesome and fresh.  They are now using a better grade of chicken that was moist and flavorful.  The tacos were a pleasant surprise.  They are soft tacos and they were tender and tasty.  The tacos come with rice and beans.  I would recommend both of these dishes.  When you sit in the bar area, the service can be a little slow because the bartender is your waiter but we had pretty good service.  Sadly, they no longer offer either the Chicken Fried Steak or the Old Fashion Burger but their new menu is huge and I'm sure that everyone in your party can find something they might like.

I had a gift card for this place so I thought I would take a nice motorcycle ride across the Skyway bridge and try it out. I must say, I don't understand all of the negative reviews. Yes it is cafeteria style but it's not like they hide that from anybody. Is it pricey? It's not "cheap" but it certainly is a fair price for the food. $11 for a pulled pork plate, two sides, a roll, and ice cream. And the quality of the pulled pork was very good. A very good hue, Nice and tender, and your choice of three barbecue sauces. I had green beans and bacon, and baked beans for my sides and both were excellent.  The staff was very friendly and it was worth the trip. If you're expecting a relaxing sitdown meal with a waitress, this isn't it. But if you're looking for lunch with very tasty food, this is it.

Traditions in place, I want to love this place.  I watched it grow from ground up.  As a longtime native, El Charro was "the place" to take visitors, and to just get yummy Mexican.  This is the 5th time here, with lackluster results.  Salsa, has lost the yummy factor, chili rellenos lacking any real flavor, etc. for beans, even chips!  If the goal is to reach out to the general tourist  public, this is the p,ace.   I will say house margaritas are delicious, and service is spot on.

It's hard to believe it's taken me so long to write a review... I go here at least once every couple weeks.  I used to recommend people try the steak sandwich, but there were a couple complaints from friends/co-workers about the toughness of it. So now I recommend the chicken sandwich, since it's consistently good.  Recently, I've been getting the chicken salad. It's a MASSIVE bowl of salad with the rotisserie chicken on top. Very tasty.
